title: Devin Nunes officially resigns from Congress, clearing way to helm Trump's social media company
summary: Devin Nunes officially resigned from Congress to become CEO of former President Trump's media and technology company, Truth Social. Nunes, a staunch ally of Trump, will lead the new platform which aims to be an alternative to mainstream social media. His move signifies his continued loyalty to Trump and his transition from a congressional career focused initially on local issues to national security and defense of the former president.

title: Smart Water for a Smarter Home: Moen Leads the Way with Industry's First Whole-Home Connected Water Product Suite
summary: Moen introduces the industry's first whole-home smart water ecosystem, the Moen Smart Water Network, which allows consumers to control, monitor, and conserve water throughout their homes using a single smartphone app. The network includes new and expanded smart products like the Flo Smart Water Monitor and Shutoff, Smart Leak Detector, Smart Sump Pump Monitor, Smart Faucet with Motion Control, and Smart Shower, all designed to provide peace of mind, security, and personalized water experiences. These innovations offer automated water security, water usage education, personalized settings, and voice activation capabilities for a truly connected home water system.

title: Investor Michael Seibel Explains Google’s Search Quality Decline And How It’s Creating Opportunities
summary: Investor Michael Seibel, a partner and CEO at Y Combinator, has voiced concerns about the declining quality of Google Search results, particularly in categories like health, product reviews, and recipes, attributing the problem to Google's focus on short-term ad revenue. He believes this opens opportunities for disruptive startups, despite Google's dominant market share. Seibel highlighted that while DuckDuckGo offers an alternative, the fundamental issue lies in Google prioritizing monetization over user search experience.

title: 3 BYOD security risks and how to prevent them
summary: The increasing adoption of Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) policies introduces significant security challenges for organizations, despite offering cost savings and flexibility. Key risks include data theft stemming from personal logins and cloud synchronization, unclear security protocols leading to shadow IT and employee-driven breaches, and the prevalence of mobile malware. Effective prevention requires robust mobile device management policies, including encryption, application blacklisting, regular data backups, and thorough employee security training.

title: $20 Billion In 2022: Google Pays Apple For Not Launching Its Own Search Engine?
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Apple and Google, alleging an illegal agreement where Google pays Apple billions annually to remain the default search engine on Apple devices and prevents Apple from developing its own search engine. The lawsuit claims this arrangement violates US antitrust law, stifles competition, and inflates advertising rates. While both companies have been discreet about the exact figures, estimates suggest Google could pay Apple around $20 billion in 2022, accounting for a significant portion of Apple's annual earnings.

title: Iberdrola optimizes working capital with SAP® Real-Time Treasury
summary: Iberdrola, a global leader in renewable energy, has partnered with J.P. Morgan to implement the SAP® Real-Time Treasury app. This integration provides Iberdrola with real-time visibility into their global cash positions, streamlines treasury operations, and enhances their ability to respond to market changes. The solution, powered by J.P. Morgan's Treasury Services APIs, has migrated Iberdrola from daily reporting to instant access to financial data, optimizing their working capital management.

title: Google users fume as smart speaker features disappear following Sonos patent ruling
summary: Following a ruling by the United States International Trade Commission that Google violated five Sonos patents, Google has removed certain smart speaker features, specifically related to group volume control. Nest smart speaker users are frustrated by these changes, which now require individual volume adjustments for speakers in a group. Google is facing a potential import ban on products infringing on Sonos's patents, while Sonos suggests Google could pay royalties.

title: Google loses Sonos smart speakers patent fight
summary: Smart-speaker firm Sonos has won a major legal battle against Google, with the US International Trade Commission ruling that Google infringed on five of Sonos' patents related to multi-room audio technology. This decision could lead to a ban on importing infringing Google smart home tech into the United States, and Sonos suggests Google may have to "degrade" its products or pay licensing fees. Google disputes the ruling, stating it has already developed workarounds and does not expect the decision to impact its ability to sell products.

title: Santa Barbara-Based Company Wins Legal Battle Against Google Over Patent
summary: Santa Barbara-based company Sonos won a legal battle against Google, with the U.S. International Trade Commission ruling that Google infringed on five Sonos patents related to smart speaker technology. This ruling includes a cease-and-desist order and an import ban on infringing Google products, specifically concerning Google's Nest speakers and their volume adjustment capabilities. Sonos has two additional patent-infringement cases against Google pending in federal courts.

title: Apple Could Reinvent Money Management, Take on PayPal and Robinhood
summary: Apple has the potential to significantly disrupt the financial technology (fintech) sector by expanding its Apple Card offerings, introducing checking accounts, and developing new money management tools. This strategic move could position Apple as a direct competitor to established players like PayPal and Robinhood. The author suggests Apple is poised for a major impact in this space, with further developments expected in 2022.

title: Akron Marathon registration details, sponsorships announced
summary: Registration for the 20th Akron Marathon Race Series opens on January 20, with early registration for eligible participants starting on January 18. Key sponsors FirstEnergy Corp., Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co., and National Interstate Insurance Co. have renewed their support through 2024. The article outlines the dates and locations for the various races, including the National Interstate 8K and 1 Mile, Goodyear Half Marathon and 10K, and the FirstEnergy Akron Marathon, Half Marathon, and team relay.

title: PacBio and Google Collaborate to Use Machine Learning to Optimize Long-Read Sequencing Data Analysis
summary: PacBio announced a research collaboration with Google to use machine learning to optimize long-read sequencing data analysis. This collaboration aims to improve PacBio's HiFi sequencing accuracy and data output by integrating Google's genomic analysis, machine learning, and algorithm development tools. The partnership is expected to further enhance variant calling and unlock more insights from PacBio sequencing data for various applications.

title: Welcoming the Post-Zoom Era
summary: The article discusses the impending end of the "Zoom era," noting a waning enthusiasm for constant video conferencing, even among those previously reliant on it. Author Jonathan Bartlett observes that people are increasingly opting out of Zoom meetings and classes, highlighting the draining nature of online interaction compared to in-person engagement. He suggests that organizations must soon decide whether to be primarily remote or in-person, and for remote setups, managers will need to minimize Zoom time and find alternative ways to foster team cohesion to prevent a decline in loyalty.

title: J.B. Hunt, Waymo expand partnership on self-driving truck technology
summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services Inc. and Waymo Via are expanding their strategic alliance to integrate commercial autonomous driving technology into transportation and logistics, aiming for fully autonomous transport in Texas within the next few years. This long-term partnership builds on a successful 2021 pilot and will include multiple trials to analyze the operational capacity of Waymo Via's autonomous Class 8 trucking unit. The collaboration also seeks to merge autonomous driving technology with J.B. Hunt’s digital freight marketplace and refine the technology's commercial readiness.

title: Here’s How Warren Buffett Persuaded Google’s Reluctant Founders to Go Public
summary: Google co-founders Sergey Brin and Larry Page were initially hesitant to take their company public due to fears of losing control. A serendipitous meeting with Warren Buffett, who shared his strategy of a two-tier stock structure used at Berkshire Hathaway, convinced them that they could go public while retaining control. This led Google to adopt a similar stock structure for its IPO and publish an "Owner's Manual" outlining their long-term philosophy for shareholders.

title: A Full List Of Uber Subsidiaries [& How They Benefit Uber]
summary: This article details Uber's various subsidiaries, including Uber Eats, Uber Freight, and UberWAV, alongside its corporate structure and key leadership. It also outlines significant acquisitions such as Jump Bikes, Careem, Drizly, and Postmates, and discusses previously owned entities like Raiser LLC and Xchange Leasing. The piece highlights Uber's expansion into a global transportation and e-commerce giant, aiming for a future with driver-less transportation.

title: D-Wave increases European presence with quantum computer in Germany
summary: D-Wave Systems Inc. has expanded its European presence by providing an annealing quantum computer to the Forschungszentrum Jülich Supercomputing Centre in Germany, claiming it's the first such device in Europe. This move is part of D-Wave's strategy to accelerate commercialization in the growing quantum computing market, despite facing significant competition from tech giants and other quantum startups. The company, a long-standing Canadian firm, aims to leverage its new association with Germany to capture a larger share of the market for solving complex optimization problems.

title: Chip giant Nvidia expands R&D operations in Israel
summary: Nvidia is expanding its R&D operations in Israel by establishing a new design and engineering group focused on next-generation CPUs for AI, robotics, and autonomous vehicles. The company, which acquired Israel's Mellanox Technologies in 2020, plans to hire additional engineers in Israel, further solidifying the country's role as Nvidia's largest R&D hub outside the US. This expansion intensifies competition for tech talent in Israel, where both Nvidia and Intel have significant R&D presence, and Google is also entering the chip-making sector.

title: Employee surveillance is exploding with remote work—and could be the new norm
summary: The COVID-19 pandemic significantly accelerated the adoption of employee surveillance software, with 60% of large companies using these technologies by the end of 2021, compared to 30% pre-pandemic. This expansion of monitoring into employees' homes raises significant concerns about privacy, trust, and the blurring of work-life boundaries. Experts warn of "function creep," where temporary surveillance measures become permanent, and highlight the urgent need for new legal protections for workers' privacy, as existing laws are largely weak and inadequate for the evolving landscape of remote work.

title: How to Invest in the Top 100 U.S. ESG Companies
summary: The article discusses the 2022 list of top 100 U.S. ESG companies compiled by Just Capital, highlighting major movements like Alphabet's rise and Meta's fall. It explains the methodology behind the rankings, which prioritize issues important to Americans, and introduces the Goldman Sachs JUST U.S. Large Cap Equity ETF (JUST) as a way to invest in companies demonstrating "just business behavior."

title: Certification programs build digital skills
summary: The Economic Development Corporation Michigan City (EDCMC) and Goodwill Industries of Michiana are collaborating to offer certification programs for digital skill enhancement. These online courses, including Facebook Social Media Marketing and Google IT Support, aim to help community members earn stackable certificates in high-demand fields. The programs are designed to assist individuals in overcoming barriers to education and employment by providing coursework, mentorship, and job placement services.

title: What is HPC-as-a-service and why does it matter?
summary: High Performance Compute-as-a-service (HPCaaS) is emerging as a critical cloud service, offering hardware, software, and expertise to process demanding workloads like big data analytics. This service addresses the challenges companies face with in-house HPC, such as high costs and specialized staffing, by leveraging cloud infrastructure from providers like IBM and Microsoft Azure. While offering benefits like faster technology integration and dynamic management, HPCaaS also presents challenges related to security, licensing costs, and data transfer, though experts believe these will be overcome by continued innovation, with the market projected to reach $17 billion by 2026.

title: Microsoft secures IPs like Call of Duty and Candy Crash to boost its metaverse ambitions, analyst says
summary: Microsoft's $68.7 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard is seen as a strategic move to bolster its metaverse ambitions, according to analyst Joost Rietveld. The deal secures popular gaming franchises like Call of Duty and Candy Crush, potentially differentiating Microsoft's Game Pass service and influencing cross-platform availability. While Microsoft has committed to honoring existing agreements, allowing Call of Duty to remain on PlayStation for now, the long-term impact on platform exclusivity for other IPs remains uncertain.

title: SRE: Benefits and Business Impact of the SRE Mindset
summary: This article discusses the benefits and business impact of adopting a Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) mindset. It explains how SRE, originating from Google, offers a concrete model to implement the DevOps philosophy by balancing feature development velocity with operational reliability risks. The SRE model aims to raise operational excellence, foster unified engineering vision, and redefine operations as a value creation center through concepts like SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets.

title: Intel scores major win as court scraps $1.2 bln EU antitrust fine
summary: Intel has successfully overturned a 1.06-billion-euro ($1.2 billion) EU antitrust fine that was imposed 12 years ago for allegedly stifling rival Advanced Micro Devices. Europe's second-highest court annulled the fine, criticizing the European Commission's analysis as incomplete. This ruling is a significant setback for EU antitrust regulators and could impact similar cases involving other major tech companies.

title: 11 Undervalued Mega-Cap Stocks to Consider
summary: This article identifies 11 undervalued mega-cap stocks that investors should consider, defining mega-caps as companies with market capitalizations over $200 billion. It highlights that the recent market downturn has brought many such stocks into buying range, including detailed analysis for Alphabet, Amazon.com, and AT&T.

title: Neil Young’s dispute with Spotify over Joe Rogan increases pressure on streamer to monitor content
summary: Neil Young's dispute with Spotify over vaccine misinformation on Joe Rogan's podcast has put the streaming giant under significant pressure to monitor its content more closely. Spotify, which removed Young's music but kept Rogan's podcast, announced it would add content advisories to all COVID-19 related podcast episodes. This incident highlights the challenges platforms face in balancing free speech with preventing the spread of misinformation, especially given Spotify's substantial investments in podcasts for business growth.

title: Google (GOOGL) announces 20-for-1 stock split
summary: Google's parent company Alphabet announced a 20-for-1 stock split proposal, aiming to make Google stock more affordable and abundant for investors. This news followed an impressive Q4 2021 earnings report where Google beat Wall Street expectations, driven by investments in AI and strong Pixel phone sales. If approved, existing shareholders by July 1, 2022, will receive 19 additional shares for each original share by July 15.

title: Google parent Alphabet announces 20-for-1 stock split
summary: Alphabet, Google's parent company, announced a 20-for-1 stock split following a surge in its stock price. This move aims to make shares more accessible to a wider range of investors by significantly lowering the per-share cost. The split requires shareholder approval and will involve Class A, B, and C shares, with existing holders receiving 19 additional shares for each one they own by July 15.

title: Alphabet brings big stock splits back to market
summary: Alphabet Inc. announced a 20-for-1 stock split, aiming to make its shares more accessible to small investors by reducing the per-share price. This move is also seen as a step towards potential inclusion in the Dow Jones Industrial Average, which has historically been a barrier due to Alphabet's high stock price. The announcement led to a 10% jump in shares during premarket trading.

title: Alphabet Stock Split: A Win for Retail Investors … And the Dow?
summary: Alphabet's 20-for-1 stock split aims to make its shares more accessible to retail investors and potentially opens the door for its inclusion in the Dow Jones Industrial Average. While fundamentally inconsequential, the split could provide a tailwind for GOOGL stock. Its impact on the Dow would also be significant, altering sector weighting and index composition considerations.

title: Alphabet eyes $2 trillion value after blowout results
summary: Alphabet Inc. is on the verge of joining Apple and Microsoft in the $2 trillion market valuation club after a strong quarterly report led to an over 8% surge in its shares. The company also announced a 20-to-1 stock split, aiming to make shares more accessible to retail investors. At least 20 brokerages raised their price targets on Alphabet's stock.

title: Alphabet Stock Is Splitting. Why It Still Matters in the Age of Fractional Shares.
summary: Alphabet, Google's parent company, announced a 20-for-1 stock split alongside a robust fourth-quarter 2021 earnings report. The article discusses why this stock split is still relevant, even with the increasing availability of fractional shares.

title: KeyBank taps Google Cloud and Deloitte to become cloud-first lender
summary: KeyBank has partnered with Google Cloud and Deloitte to transition its banking operations to a cloud-first model by 2025. This multi-year collaboration aims to migrate primary platforms and applications to Google Cloud infrastructure, using its data solutions and AI technologies to enhance personalised digital banking experiences and secure client services. Deloitte will serve as the systems integrator for this migration.

title: News Corp’s deal with Google and the Melbourne Business School questioned by journalism academics
summary: Journalism academics are questioning the newly announced Digital News Academy, a partnership between News Corp Australia, Google, and the Melbourne Business School. Critics argue that housing the academy within a business school rather than a journalism program undermines academic rigor and independent criticism, especially given News Corp's historical animosity towards university journalism education and its reliance on Google funding. The academics emphasize the importance of distinguishing between skills training and comprehensive education that includes ethics and critical thinking within the industry.

title: Opinion: $1.4 trillion? Big Tech’s pandemic year produces mind-boggling financial results
summary: In 2021, despite supply-chain disruptions, labor shortages, and rising prices, major tech companies including Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, and Microsoft achieved staggering financial results. They collectively saw over $320 billion in profit on $1.4 trillion in sales, with profits increasing more than 55% and sales growing 27% from an already record-setting base. This performance highlights Big Tech's resilience and expanding economic influence, making their combined sales comparable to a top-15 global economy.

title: Amazon hiking base salary cap to $350,000 for corporate, tech workers
summary: Amazon has announced a significant increase in its base salary cap for corporate and tech workers, raising it from $160,000 to $350,000. This move aims to enhance the company's competitiveness in a challenging labor market and attract and retain top talent. The company indicated that these increases are "much more considerable" than in previous years, aligning its pay structure more closely with other major tech firms.

title: Nvidia’s $40bn takeover of UK chip designer Arm collapses
summary: Nvidia's $40bn takeover of UK chip designer Arm has collapsed due to insurmountable regulatory hurdles, leading Arm to pursue a stock market flotation instead. The deal, which had been valued as high as $75bn, faced fierce opposition and regulatory scrutiny in multiple regions, including the US, UK, and China. Softbank will now prepare Arm for an IPO and receive a $1.25bn break-up fee from Nvidia.

title: Want Exposure To Google? 2 Communication Services ETFs Holding Alphabet Shares
summary: This article discusses two Communication Services ETFs, the Fidelity MSCI Telecommunication Services Index ETF (FCOM) and the iShares Global Comm Services ETF (IXP), that offer significant exposure to Alphabet shares. It highlights Alphabet's strong Q4 and FY 2021 results, including a 20-for-1 stock split, and reviews the holdings, performance, and key metrics of each ETF for investors looking to capitalize on the growth in this sector.

title: Anger after News Corp and Google Australia set up journalism academy at university business school
summary: News Corp Australia and Google Australia have partnered to establish the Digital News Academy, offering a nine-month journalism course through the University of Melbourne's business school. This initiative has sparked controversy among academics at the university, who express anger and concern over not being consulted and the choice of the business school over traditional journalism programs. Critics view this move as a reflection of News Corp's historical antagonism towards university journalism and question the focus on "commercial journalism."

title: PEPSI® Develops New Platform with Verizon to Immerse Fans into Pepsi Super Bowl Halftime Show with Live 360-degree Mobile Experience
summary: Pepsi and Verizon have partnered to launch the "Pepsi Super Bowl Halftime Show ULTRA PASS Powered by Verizon 5G Ultra Wideband," an immersive mobile experience for Super Bowl LVI. This platform will provide fans with live 360-degree on-field and on-stage access to the halftime show through a dedicated app, utilizing Verizon's 5G technology. The initiative aims to enhance the broadcast viewing experience by offering exclusive vantage points and bringing fans closer to the performers.

title: Corporate lobbying ETF seeks to profit from influencing politicians
summary: Strategas Asset Management has launched a new exchange-traded fund (ETF), the Strategas Global Policy Opportunities ETF (SAGP-A), designed to invest in companies that demonstrate a strong return on their lobbying efforts in the U.S. federal government. The ETF aims to capitalize on corporate lobbying as a means for companies to gain an earnings advantage, especially in a politically volatile environment. While the existing portfolio has underperformed in recent years, the new ETF includes small and mid-cap companies and non-U.S. firms, which are believed to achieve better returns on lobbying spend, despite some ethical concerns raised by analysts.

title: YouTube floats ideas of verifying NFTs, watching games in metaverse
summary: YouTube is exploring using blockchain technology and NFTs to help creators find new ways to monetize content by offering verifiable digital assets. The company also suggested ideas for the metaverse, focusing on enhancing social viewing experiences, particularly for gaming content, to allow users to watch videos together in a virtual environment. These initiatives aim to cut down on fraud in the digital art market and improve user engagement.

title: Dutch judges deny Google injunction request against Sonos
summary: A Dutch court has rejected Google's request for an injunction against Sonos regarding an alleged patent infringement related to smart speaker technology. Google sought to prevent Sonos from infringing on its "Domain-based digital-rights management system with easy and secure device enrollment" patent (EP 621) in the UK, Italy, or Finland, and also requested Sonos inform customers of the alleged infringement. The court dismissed Google's claim, stating a lack of specification in the writ of summons, found that Sonos did not infringe the patent, and ordered Google to pay costs of €200,000 to Sonos.

title: Copyright case: Google LLC v. Oracle America Inc., USA
summary: The U.S. Supreme Court ruled in favor of Google LLC in a significant copyright case against Oracle America, Inc. The Court determined that Google's use of approximately 11,000 lines of code from Java SE API for its Android mobile operating system constituted fair use and did not infringe on Oracle's copyright. This decision, dubbed "the copyright case of the decade" by Google, was a six-to-two ruling.

title: Sonos IKEA Symfonisk Table Lamp Speaker with WiFi (Gen 2) review
summary: The Sonos IKEA Symfonisk Table Lamp Speaker with WiFi (Gen 2) is a stylish second-generation lamp speaker offering improved audio and a modular design with interchangeable shades. While it delivers solid trebles and mid-range sounds comparable to other Sonos devices, bass performance can suffer at high volumes. Despite being a little pricey and lacking built-in smart assistant functions, its elegant design and multi-room audio capabilities make it a practical choice for Apple users seeking a discrete speaker solution.

title: Billionaire George Soros reveals stake in Rivian, sells some tech shares
summary: Billionaire George Soros' investment fund, Soros Fund Management, acquired a significant stake in electric-pickup maker Rivian Automotive Inc., purchasing nearly 20 million shares worth approximately $2 billion in Q4. This move was disclosed in a recent SEC filing. At the same time, Soros' fund also trimmed positions in major tech companies like Amazon and Alphabet.

title: John Deere and Brazilian bank team up to equip farmers deforesting the Amazon
summary: An investigation has revealed that farmers in Brazil, some with properties embargoed by environmental authorities for Amazon deforestation and outstanding environmental fines, have been accessing government-subsidized loans to purchase John Deere tractors. These loans are administered by the state-owned Brazilian Development Bank (BNDES) and underwritten by John Deere Bank, a subsidiary of the U.S. manufacturer. This practice highlights a loophole in Central Bank rules intended to bar such credit, raising concerns about the misuse of public funds and the enforcement of environmental regulations in Brazil.

title: Tesla Stock vs. Alphabet Stock: Which Is the Better Buy Today?
summary: Morningstar compares Tesla (TSLA) and Alphabet (GOOGL) stock using proprietary metrics to determine which is the better investment. Alphabet is found to be undervalued and has a wider economic moat and lower uncertainty compared to Tesla, which is currently overvalued. Based on their ratings, Morningstar identifies Alphabet as the overall better buy today.

title: Akamai pays $900M to acquire AWS cloud infrastructure rival Linode
summary: Akamai Technologies Inc. has announced its acquisition of Linode LLC for approximately $900 million. This strategic move aims to transform Akamai into a highly distributed compute platform, enhancing its offerings for developers by combining Linode's cloud computing capabilities with Akamai's edge platform and security tools. The acquisition follows Akamai's strong fourth-quarter financial results and is expected to add about $100 million in revenue by year-end.

title: JPMorgan Chase spent $2 billion on brand new data centers last year
summary: JPMorgan Chase invested $2 billion in new data centers last year, even while actively migrating data and applications to public cloud platforms like AWS, Google, and Microsoft. This investment is part of their broader annual technology spending, which exceeded $12 billion, focusing on cloud capabilities, digital consumer experience, and data analytics. CEO Jamie Dimon emphasized that the new data centers incorporate "cloud capability," and the firm plans to move 30% to 50% of its applications to the cloud this year, driven by the significant value seen in risk management, fraud prevention, and customer satisfaction.

title: Waymo partners with logistics giant to run self-driving trucks between Dallas and Houston
summary: Waymo, Alphabet Inc.'s self-driving technology unit, has partnered with logistics giant C.H. Robinson to pilot autonomous freight hauling between Dallas and Houston on Interstate 45. These self-driving trucks, which will include a safety driver, aim to address long-term driver shortages and improve the carrier and driver experience. Waymo has established a 9-acre hub in South Dallas for its autonomous trucking operations, leveraging Texas's status as a major freight hub.

title: Jaguar Land Rover, Nvidia ally in vehicle software arms race
summary: Luxury automaker Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) has partnered with Nvidia to develop advanced computer brains and software for its vehicles launching from 2025 onwards. This collaboration aims to accelerate JLR's in-vehicle software development, enabling features like autonomous driving and parking, and providing more digital functions to drivers. The deal is a significant win for Nvidia in expanding its automotive industry presence and helps JLR compete with rivals like Tesla in digital technology.

title: Google Creates $100 Million Fund for Skills Training Program (Published 2022)
summary: Google has created a $100 million fund to support skills training and job placement for low-income Americans, aiming to help those without a four-year college degree find middle-class jobs. The initiative partners with non-profit groups like Year Up, Merit America, and Social Finance, with a unique financing model where students repay interest-free loans only if they secure jobs paying at least $40,000 annually. Google hopes this project will create a template for other companies and policymakers, ultimately fueling $1 billion in wage gains for 20,000 graduates.

title: Pinterest General Counsel and Google Alumna Set to Step Down
summary: Pinterest's General Counsel, Christine Flores, who was hired from Google in 2017, is set to step down on October 1. Flores will assist with the transition as the company searches for her replacement. She had previously sold off approximately $18 million in Pinterest stock over the past year.

title: Kraft Heinz Invests In Consumer Insights With Kraft-O-Matic Data Platform
summary: Kraft Heinz is enhancing its consumer insights capabilities by launching a new data platform called Kraft-O-Matic, in partnership with Google Cloud. This initiative leverages AI and machine learning to generate real-time data for product innovation and personalized consumer experiences, supporting Kraft Heinz's broader transformation strategy initiated in 2020. The company aims to improve speed to market, optimize its e-commerce marketing, and strengthen retail partnerships through these data-driven efforts.

title: Salesforce ties executive compensation to ESG goals
summary: Salesforce announced that a portion of executive variable pay for those at the executive vice president level and above will be tied to ESG measures, starting this fiscal year. These measures will focus on increasing representation for diverse employees and reducing air travel emissions, as well as increasing spend with suppliers signed onto Salesforce's Sustainability Exhibit. This move reflects a growing trend among major companies to link executive compensation to diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) and sustainability goals.

title: Kraft Heinz Names Miguel Patricio as Chair Following Alex Behring's Retirement and James Park's Election to Board of Directors; Greg Abel Comments
summary: The Kraft Heinz Company announced leadership changes to its Board of Directors, with CEO Miguel Patricio slated to become the new Chair following Alex Behring's retirement. Additionally, technology entrepreneur James Park will be nominated for election to the Board. These changes aim to continue strong leadership and integrate technology expertise into the company's strategy.

title: Trump's new social media application 'Truth Social' begins gradual rollout
summary: Donald Trump's "Truth Social" app began a gradual rollout on Sunday, with the aim of being fully operational in the US by the end of March. The platform is intended as an alternative to major social media sites that banned Trump after the January 6, 2021 Capitol assault. Former congressman Devin Nunes, CEO of Trump Media & Technology Group, stated that the app would be available on the Apple App Store, celebrating the return of free speech for those who felt their voices were canceled.

title: Kraft Heinz taps Google for multiyear transformation push
summary: Kraft Heinz announced a multiyear strategic partnership with Google to accelerate its digital transformation, leveraging Google Cloud's AI and ML to drive food innovations, build a customer data platform, and improve media efficiency. This initiative is part of Kraft Heinz's broader strategy to reinvest in its brands and achieve cost savings by 2024, enabling data-driven decisions as consumer behavior shifts online. The partnership aims to enhance personalization at scale, optimize media activations, and address changing market challenges amid the pandemic.

title: LG AI Research forms 'Hyperscale' AI alliance with 13 companies
summary: LG AI Research has formed an alliance with 13 companies and organizations to expand the use of its hyperscale AI engine, Exaone. The alliance aims to apply Exaone's capabilities, which include 300 billion parameters and multi-modal functions, across various sectors like IT, finance, and medical fields. Members will benefit from technologies such as Exaone Tuning, API access, and Exaone Playground, while contributing to the AI's further development and adoption.

title: Travelers Acquires Tech Assets of Insurtech Trōv for Personal Insurance
summary: Travelers has acquired the technology assets of insurtech Trōv, an early innovator in micro-duration and embedded insurance. The deal, whose financial terms were not disclosed, will see Trōv's team and technology integrated into Travelers' Personal Insurance segment. This acquisition aligns with Travelers' ongoing strategy of investing over a billion dollars annually in technology to enhance customer, agent, and partner experiences.

title: Exclusive: Singapore flags concern to India over ban on Sea's game -sources
summary: Singapore has expressed concerns to India regarding its ban on the popular gaming app "Free Fire," owned by Sea Ltd, marking the first diplomatic intervention since the ban. The move significantly impacted Sea's market value, and investors are worried about potential extensions to Sea's e-commerce app, Shopee. India had banned the app among others, citing data transfer to Chinese servers, a claim Sea refutes, stating it is a Singaporean company compliant with Indian law.

title: Snowflake and Databricks back $222M round for dbt Labs at $4.2B valuation
summary: dbt Labs, a fast-growing data management startup, has secured $222 million in funding, valuing the company at $4.2 billion. Notable investors in this round include Snowflake, Databricks, Alphabet’s GV fund, and Salesforce. The company plans to use the capital to hire 300 new employees over the next year and expand its offering as a popular data transformation tool used by over 25,000 data scientists and 9,000 companies.

title: CRM giant Salesforce acquiring B.C.’s Traction on Demand
summary: Salesforce.com Inc. is acquiring B.C.-based software firm Traction on Demand, which specializes in implementing marketing strategies using Salesforce's CRM services. The Burnaby-based company employs about 500 workers across British Columbia, and its founder had previously resisted selling the company. This acquisition follows Salesforce's recent spree of purchases, including Mobify and Slack Technologies Inc.

title: Qualcomm Inc. stock rises Friday, still underperforms market
summary: Qualcomm Inc. (QCOM) stock rose 1.76% on Friday, closing at $170.93, despite underperforming the broader market. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both saw larger gains. Qualcomm's stock remains below its 52-week high and showed mixed performance compared to competitors like Alphabet Inc. Cl A, NVIDIA Corp., and Cisco Systems Inc.

title: WPP's Mark Read on why Coca-Cola, Google and Unilever are pivotal to the network's future
summary: Mark Read, CEO of WPP, discussed the company's strong 2021 performance, driven by growth in digital marketing, media, e-commerce, data, and technology. He highlighted the importance of strategic partnerships with major clients like Coca-Cola, Google, and Unilever for future growth and client-agency collaboration. Read also addressed WPP's response to the situation in Ukraine, its hiring plans, and adjustments to workplace strategy.

title: KYNDRYL REPORTS FOURTH QUARTER AND FULL-YEAR 2021 RESULTS
summary: Kyndryl Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: KD) reported its fourth-quarter and full-year 2021 financial results, with full-year revenues totaling $18.7 billion and a net loss of $2.3 billion. The company is strategically positioning itself for growth through new partnerships with cloud hyperscalers and key initiatives focused on increasing signings, transforming service delivery, and addressing substandard margins. Kyndryl also announced significant strategic partnerships with major cloud providers and an expanded customer base following its spin-off from IBM.

title: How Does Google Make Money?
summary: This article explains how Google, now Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ: GOOGL), generates revenue primarily through advertising on its search engine and web tools, as well as through its Google Cloud services and its "Other Bets" segment for new technologies. It details the financial performance of these segments and discusses Google's stock, leadership, and future plans including a stock split and AI expansion.

title: Google, Meta face penalties in Russia as deadline passes to open local offices
summary: Google and Meta are among several internet companies facing potential penalties in Russia for failing to establish local offices and comply with a new communications law. Russia's state communications regulator, Roskomnadzor, had set a deadline of the end of February for these companies to open local offices or face restrictions. The move is part of Russia's broader effort to control internet content, with fines previously issued for failing to censor anti-government content, and recent curtailments of access to platforms like Facebook in retaliation for censoring state media.

title: AMD, Arm, Google, Intel, TSMC and others join Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) group
summary: Leading semiconductor companies including AMD, Arm, Google, Intel, Meta, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Samsung, and TSMC have formed the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) industry consortium. This group aims to establish an open chiplet ecosystem by standardizing die-to-die interconnects, with the UCIe 1.0 specification already ratified. This initiative leverages existing PCI Express and Compute Express Link standards to enable easier mixing and matching of chiplet components from multiple vendors for System-on-Chip (SoC) construction.

title: Tripadvisor, Google Maps Suspend Reviews of Some Russian Listings
summary: Google Maps and Tripadvisor have temporarily suspended new user reviews for certain listings in Russia, Ukraine, and Belarus. This action was taken in response to a flood of political statements posted as reviews, driven by a social media campaign urging activists to communicate with Russians via these platforms about the war in Ukraine. The companies aim to prevent the publishing of off-topic and abusive content.

title: Spotify closes its office in Russia in response to attack on Ukraine
summary: Spotify announced the indefinite closure of its office in Russia in response to the country's attack on Ukraine. The music streaming platform stated its priority is its employees' safety and ensuring access to information. Spotify has also restricted the discoverability of Russian state-affiliated media and removed content from RT and Sputnik in several markets, while matching employee donations for humanitarian aid.

title: DigitalOcean vs Cloudflare — which stock can take advantage of cloud expansion?
summary: This article compares DigitalOcean Holdings (DOCN) and Cloudflare (NET), two cloud computing companies, to determine which is the better investment. DigitalOcean serves developers and SMBs, while Cloudflare focuses on web security and content delivery. The analysis concludes that Cloudflare is the less risky and better investment due to its continued large client growth and potential for SMB expansion, despite DigitalOcean's strong performance and hold in the SMB market.

title: How Hyperscale Data Centers are reshaping India’s IT
summary: Hyperscale data centers are critical infrastructure for companies like Amazon, Google, and Microsoft, providing dedicated computing resources to handle massive user demands. These facilities are essential for the digital economy, supporting everything from emails to online shopping, and their importance has grown with trends like remote work and emerging technologies such as AI and IoT. India is poised for significant growth in its data center industry, driven by government incentives and an increasing need for robust data storage and processing capabilities, despite environmental concerns related to energy consumption.

title: Sonos Roam SL vs Sonos Roam: which Bluetooth speaker will be the best?
summary: This article compares the Sonos Roam SL and the Sonos Roam, two portable Bluetooth speakers from Sonos, to help consumers decide which one best suits their needs. The key difference lies in the Roam having a built-in microphone for voice controls, Auto Trueplay, and Sound Swap, features absent from the more affordable Roam SL. Both speakers share the same design, battery life, and core audio technology, making the choice dependent on budget and the importance of smart features.

title: Kraft Heinz Partners With Google; Sanjiv Gajiwala and Giusy Buonfantino Detail
summary: Kraft Heinz has formed a multi-year partnership with Google to leverage Google Cloud's data and analytics technologies, enhancing its marketing strategy and digital transformation. This collaboration aims to deepen consumer understanding, foster personalized engagement, and accelerate product innovation through data-driven insights. Key figures Sanjiv Gajiwala (Kraft Heinz) and Giusy Buonfantino (Google Solutions) emphasized the strategic importance of this alliance for future growth and consumer connection.

title: Primeprint Solution by Dentsply Sirona: The Medical-grade 3D Printing System for Dental Practices and Labs
summary: Dentsply Sirona has launched the Primeprint Solution, an automated, end-to-end, medical-grade 3D printing system designed for dental practices and labs. This system aims to simplify and enhance the manufacturing of patient-specific and biocompatible dental appliances through DLP technology and automated post-processing. It integrates with Dentsply Sirona's digital ecosystem, developed in collaboration with Google Cloud, to offer a seamless and efficient workflow, maximizing productivity and ensuring high-quality, regulated results.

title: Alphabet’s (GOOGL) Stock Price Has Fallen 12.8% in 2022. Should You Buy It?
summary: Alphabet's stock (GOOGL) has dropped 12.8% in 2022, leading many to question if it's a good buying opportunity. Despite plateauing ad revenues, Google is expanding into cloud services and cybersecurity, highlighted by its acquisition of Mandiant. The company is considered attractive at current prices, though antitrust concerns remain a risk.

title: Our Ultimate Stock-Pickers' Top 10 Buys and Sells
summary: This article identifies the top 10 high-conviction buys and sells among Morningstar's Ultimate Stock-Pickers for the fourth quarter of 2021, based on an aggregate analysis of their holdings. It highlights key companies like Microsoft and 3M as top buys and Marsh & McLennan as a prominent sell, detailing the Morningstar analysts' fair value estimates, economic moats, and investment theses for these firms. The report assists investors by providing insights into attractive investment ideas and valuation considerations.

title: Amazon announces 20-for-1 stock split, $10 bln share buyback
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has announced a 20-for-1 stock split and authorized a $10 billion share buyback plan, causing its shares to rise by 7% in extended trading. This marks Amazon's first stock split since 1999, aimed at making shares more accessible to investors and increasing flexibility for employees managing equity. Trading with the new share price will commence on June 6.

title: CrowdStrike’s Crowd-Pleaser; Build-A-Bear’s Blunder; Asana’s Attitude Adjustment
summary: This article discusses the financial performance and market outlook for several companies. CrowdStrike reported impressive earnings and strong guidance, positioning it well in the growing cybersecurity market. Build-A-Bear Workshop issued a cautious 2022 outlook due to inflation, leading to investor concern, while Amazon announced a 20-for-1 stock split, mirroring Alphabet's earlier move. Additionally, Asana reported significant losses despite revenue growth, and PayPal faced a downgrade from BofA Securities due to concerns about the Russia-Ukraine conflict's impact.

title: Amazon announces 20-for-1 stock split
summary: Amazon announced a 20-for-1 stock split, following similar moves by other major tech companies like Alphabet, Apple, and Tesla. The article explains that a stock split divides existing shares into multiple, smaller-priced shares without changing the overall value of an investor's holdings or the company's market capitalization. Stock splits are often done to increase a stock's liquidity and make it more accessible to a wider range of investors, though in an era of fractional shares, some of the practical impact is diminished.

title: Russia says it will block Instagram
summary: Russia announced it would block Instagram, blaming a recent change in Meta's hate-speech policy that temporarily allowed some users to call for violence against Russians in the context of the Ukraine war. This move is part of Russia's broader effort to control information flow and prevent its citizens from accessing uncensored news about the invasion. The ban is expected to affect 80 million Russian users.

title: ‘The Dirty Dozen List’ – Corporations Enable and Profit from Sexual Exploitation
summary: The National Center on Sexual Exploitation (NCOSE) has released its annual "Dirty Dozen List," highlighting twelve mainstream entities accused of facilitating and profiting from sexual abuse and exploitation. The list includes technology giants like Google, Meta, Discord, and others, pointing to various ways they enable access to harmful content or profit from commercial sex industries. NCOSE details specific examples for each company and offers ways for the public to take action against these practices.

title: 5 Artificial Intelligence Growth Stocks to Buy Now and Hold
summary: The artificial intelligence (AI) industry is projected to grow significantly, from an estimated $93 billion in 2021 to $997 billion annually by 2028. This article highlights five AI growth stocks—C3.ai, Upstart, Splunk, Alphabet, and Lemonade—that are positioned to capitalize on this expansion by leveraging AI in various sectors, from enterprise solutions and lending to data analysis and insurance, offering investors opportunities for long-term growth.

title: Instacart Unveils Shoppable Recipes, New Product Integrations With TikTok, Tasty, Hearst Magazines And More
summary: Instacart has launched "Shoppable Recipes," a new feature that allows users to seamlessly purchase ingredients for recipes found on platforms like TikTok and Hearst Magazines' food properties (e.g., Delish). This initiative aims to transform online recipe browsing into immediate action, providing a new avenue for food creators to monetize content and for Instacart's retail partners to reach new customers. The integration leverages Instacart's extensive grocery catalog and AI to enable quick delivery of in-stock items.

title: Musk’s Starlink Brings Internet to Ukraine, and Attention to a New Space Race
summary: Elon Musk's Starlink has provided crucial satellite broadband service to Ukraine, particularly as Russian forces target infrastructure. This assistance highlights the growing importance of low-Earth-orbit (LEO) satellites, championed by companies like SpaceX and Amazon, in a new space race for global internet connectivity. These LEO satellites offer a smaller, more affordable alternative to traditional geostationary satellites for delivering internet access.

title: Why banks and NATO are worrying about a future ‘Quantum attack’
summary: The article discusses the growing concern among banks and organizations like NATO about the potential for "quantum attacks" as quantum computing technology advances. Andersen Cheng, CEO of Post Quantum, warns that powerful quantum computers capable of cracking current encryption methods may be closer than initially thought, posing a significant threat to critical communications, financial data, and cryptocurrencies. The piece highlights how various nations are investing heavily in quantum technology and the commercial sector is beginning to prepare for this shift.

title: MSG Entertainment Names Lucas Watson President, MSG Sphere
summary: Madison Square Garden Entertainment Corp. announced the appointment of Lucas Watson as President, MSG Sphere, effective March 28. In this role, Watson will lead the business strategy and execution for MSG Sphere, the company's planned immersive entertainment venues, including go-to-market planning and bookings. The first MSG Sphere venue is scheduled to open in Las Vegas in the second half of 2023, and Watson will also oversee the global development of the MSG Sphere brand.

title: Lawsuit Accuses Google of Bias Against Black Employees (Published 2022)
summary: A former Google employee, April Curley, has filed a class-action lawsuit against the company, alleging systematic discrimination against Black workers. The lawsuit claims Google places Black employees in lower-level jobs, underpays them, and denies them opportunities for advancement, echoing longstanding complaints about the company's diversity issues. Google's 2021 diversity report showed Black employees make up 4.4% of its U.S. workforce, which is below the national average for digital publishing and search companies.

title: GM bets $3.5 billion more on self-driving tech unit as SoftBank exits
summary: General Motors (GM) is increasing its investment in its self-driving car subsidiary, Cruise, by approximately $3.5 billion, as SoftBank's Vision Fund exits its stake. GM will pay $2.1 billion to acquire SoftBank's share and make another $1.35 billion investment committed by SoftBank in 2018, bringing GM's total stake to 80%. This move raises questions about Cruise's near-term revenue generation and the financial commitment required from GM and its partners to fund the unit's development.

title: Racism at Google? Alphabet Inc's Google discriminates against Black people, former staffer claims
summary: A former Google employee, April Curley, has filed a lawsuit alleging that Alphabet Inc.'s Google discriminates against Black people by pigeon-holing them into low-paying, dead-end jobs and subjecting them to harassment. Curley, who was fired in 2020 after advocating for reform regarding the treatment of Black employees, claims that Google's leadership lacks Black representation, leading to biased hiring practices and limited advancement opportunities for Black professionals. The lawsuit highlights Google's slow progress in diversifying its workforce and its struggles in retaining Black employees, despite efforts to increase hiring after 2020.

title: Jury: Employer’s DEI training didn’t conflict with worker’s religious beliefs
summary: A federal jury ruled that a born-again Christian employee's religious beliefs did not conflict with his employer's mandatory Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) training. The employee, who was later fired, had refused to complete a course question about identifying harassment of a transgender co-worker, claiming a conflict with his beliefs. The jury found that while he had sincerely held beliefs, they did not conflict with the company's employment requirement, highlighting the employer's appropriate response of focusing on workplace behavior rather than personal beliefs.

title: Google’s revamped Contact Center AI Platform provides tighter integration with CRMs
summary: Google Cloud has updated its Contact Center AI Platform to offer a comprehensive, out-of-the-box solution with tighter integration with popular CRM software. This aims to provide businesses with cloud scalability, unified data across sales, marketing, and support, and more personalized customer experiences. The platform also expands its partnership with Salesforce.com Inc. to integrate with Service Cloud Voice.

title: The BlackBerry veteran who took on Google and won
summary: Patrick Spence, CEO of Sonos, a veteran of BlackBerry (formerly Research In Motion), successfully led Sonos in a patent infringement lawsuit against Google. The U.S. International Trade Commission ruled in favor of Sonos, affirming that Google infringed on five of Sonos's patents related to smart speaker technology. This victory demonstrates that smaller companies can win intellectual property disputes against large tech giants, emboldening Sonos and potentially setting the stage for future actions against other major players like Amazon.

title: Alphabet spins off its Sandbox AQ quantum computing unit
summary: Alphabet Inc.'s quantum computing unit, Sandbox AQ, has spun off into an independent company after raising a "nine-figure" funding round from prominent investors like Eric Schmidt and Marc Benioff. The new startup, led by CEO Jack Hidary, aims to develop commercially viable quantum technologies, focusing initially on post-quantum cryptography software and quantum simulation in healthcare and materials science. Sandbox AQ already has customers, including Mount Sinai Health System and SoftBank Group Corp.'s telecom business, and plans to work with multiple cloud providers, including Google Cloud.

title: Independent Sandbox AQ Launches, Prominent Investors Including T. Rowe Price, Eric Schmidt, Breyer Capital, Guggenheim Partners and Thomas Tull, and Customers
summary: Sandbox AQ, an enterprise SaaS company leveraging quantum tech and AI, officially launched with significant backing from prominent investors including T. Rowe Price, Eric Schmidt, Breyer Capital, Guggenheim Partners, and Thomas Tull. Eric Schmidt will serve as chairman of Sandbox AQ’s board of directors, and the company plans to hire additional AI experts, physicists, and engineers. Sandbox AQ is developing commercial products for various sectors, focusing on post-quantum cryptography, drug development, clean energy, and data security, and has already secured global customers like Vodafone Business and Mt. Sinai Health System.

title: Towards The Distant Virtual World: Vitalii Vashchuk Shares Insights
summary: Vitalii Vashchuk, Director and Gaming Technologist at EPAM Systems, shared his insights on the metaverse's evolution and the underlying technologies. He describes the metaverse as a concept aimed at enhancing communication and experiences, offering greater freedom than Web2.0. Key technologies enabling the metaverse include Blockchain, NFTs, AI, and IoT.

title: Mizuho plans to tie up with Google to serve clients better
summary: Mizuho Financial Group Inc. announced a strategic alliance with Google to enhance its client services. The Japanese financial firm aims to use Google's expertise to analyze client transaction data for improved customer service quality and to offer other financial services through the Google Cloud platform.

title: WT Dining Services Launches Mobile Ordering
summary: WT Dining Services, in partnership with Aramark, has launched mobile ordering for Chick-fil-A, Which Wich, and Pony Express Burritos at West Texas A&M University. The new service, available through the Transact Mobile Ordering App, allows students, faculty, staff, and community members to order and pay using various methods, including Dining Dollars, Buff Cash, and credit/debit cards. This initiative aims to enhance customer service by providing a convenient way to access menu items.

title: Netflix buys another video game studio as it builds out its gaming business. (Published 2022)
summary: Netflix announced it acquired Boss Fight Entertainment, a mobile game developer, marking its third studio purchase as it expands into the video game market. This move is part of Netflix's strategy to differentiate itself by leveraging popular shows for game development and eventually creating original games, aiming for a careful expansion after seeing other tech companies struggle in the gaming industry.

title: Brookfield agrees to buy Hibernia REIT for $1.2 billion
summary: Brookfield Asset Management Inc. has reached an agreement to acquire Hibernia REIT Plc for approximately 1.09 billion euros ($1.2 billion) in cash, causing Hibernia's shares to surge to a record high. The deal, which offers a 33.9% premium over Hibernia's average share price, sees Brookfield expanding its real estate portfolio in Dublin, a market where tech giants are rapidly growing. Brookfield is actively investing in European office landlords, capitalizing on market uncertainty and discounts to asset values.

title: Amazon Stock Rally Erases Losses for the Year
summary: Amazon.com Inc. shares rallied, becoming the first megacap tech stock to recover its losses for the year,

title: Toyota, Aurora Test-Drive Autonomous Ride-Hailing Fleet In Texas
summary: Toyota and Aurora Innovation Inc. have begun testing an autonomous ride-hailing fleet in Texas using Toyota's Sienna minivans equipped with Aurora's self-driving system. The tests, conducted with two safety operators and no passengers, will cover highways and suburban streets in the Dallas-Fort-Worth area, including routes to the airport. This initiative aims to generate revenue from significant engineering investments in autonomous vehicle technology and prepares for a commercial launch on ride-hailing networks like Uber.

title: Best Global Company Culture 2022
summary: Comparably’s annual Best Global Company Culture list for 2022 ranks the top large companies (over 500 employees) with international operations based on anonymous employee feedback collected over the past year. The list highlights companies with strong cultures across nearly 20 categories, including compensation, leadership, and work-life balance, making it a relevant resource for job seekers worldwide. Microsoft, IBM, and Google took the top three spots, respectively, among the 50 recognized global companies.

title: Google Play Store App practices: France slaps $2 mn penalty on Google
summary: The Paris Commercial Court has fined Google 2 million euros ($2.2 million) for abusive practices against developers on its Play Store. Google is also required to amend seven clauses from its contracts from 2015 and 2016, which included a 30% commission on developer revenues. The French Ministry of Finance initiated the legal action in 2018, citing a significant imbalance in rights and obligations for app developers.

title: IonQ Named As One Of TIME’s 100 Most Influential Companies
summary: IonQ (NYSE: IONQ), a leader in trapped-ion quantum computing, has been recognized on TIME’s annual TIME100 Most Influential Companies list for its leadership in commercializing quantum computing. The company was included in the "New Frontiers" category, acknowledging its extraordinary impact and recent achievements, including the introduction of IonQ Aria, the world's most powerful quantum computer. This recognition follows a year of significant business momentum and technical breakthroughs for IonQ.

title: GameStop Soars on Plans to Extend Rebound With Stock Split
summary: GameStop (GME) shares soared by as much as 22% in extended trading following the announcement of plans to propose a stock split to shareholders in the form of a dividend. The company intends to ask for approval to increase its Class A shares from 300 million to 1 billion at its upcoming annual meeting. This move led to a significant jump in the stock price, reaching $203.98 after closing at $166.58.

title: Nova Labs surges to $200m series D
summary: Helium, rebranded as Nova Labs, secured $200 million in Series D funding, leading to a $1.2 billion valuation. The funding round saw participation from corporate investors like Alphabet, Goodyear, Liberty Global, and Deutsche Telekom, alongside lead investors Tiger Global Management and Andreessen Horowitz and others. Nova Labs develops a blockchain-backed decentralized peer-to-peer wireless internet-of-things network.

title: Why Big Companies Like Tesla and Amazon Are Splitting Stocks
summary: This article explains what a stock split is and why major companies like Tesla, Amazon, and Alphabet execute them. It delves into the financial and psychological reasons behind stock splits, such as making shares more accessible to a wider range of investors and signaling positive future performance, despite the total market capitalization remaining unchanged. The piece also discusses the impact of stock splits on retail investors and highlights the strong post-split performance of such stocks.

title: Expedia Hires Google Travel Advertising Director Rob Torres
summary: Expedia Group announced the hiring of Rob Torres, Google's former managing director of advertising and marketing, as senior vice president of Media Solutions. Torres previously worked for Expedia from 1999 to 2006. This move comes as Expedia aims to reduce its reliance on Google for customer acquisition, a goal that was temporarily set aside during the pandemic.

title: Startup Warp raises $23M to reinvent the venerable command-line terminal
summary: Warp, a developer productivity startup, has successfully raised $23 million in funding, including a $6 million seed round and a $17 million Series A round. The company aims to modernize the command-line terminal, which it believes is an outdated tool despite being used daily by developers. Warp's new terminal is built with the Rust programming language, offers a modern interface, and includes features for improved collaboration and intelligent command suggestions, moving the terminal experience into the modern era for developers.

title: Alphabet’s Pichai Freed From Questioning in Google ‘Incognito’ Privacy Suit
summary: Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai has been excused from questioning in a consumer privacy lawsuit against Google. The lawsuit alleges that Google collects user data through its Chrome browser even when users are in "Incognito" mode. A California judge ruled against deposing Pichai regarding the Chrome feature, though Google's marketing chief will still face queries.

title: Boeing and Google Cloud Partnership Takes Flight, Aims to Transform Aerospace Industry
summary: Google Cloud and Boeing have announced a partnership to migrate hundreds of Boeing's applications to Google Cloud, enhancing scalability, flexibility, and leveraging advanced data analytics and AI/ML tools. This collaboration aims to modernize Boeing's IT infrastructure, accelerate software development, and improve manufacturing efficiencies while supporting Boeing's commitment to net-zero carbon emissions by utilizing Google Cloud's renewable energy-matched infrastructure.

title: Dreams of green data
summary: This article discusses the efforts by companies like Apple, Google, and Equinix, alongside Santa Clara officials, to make data centers more environmentally friendly. The primary focus is on reducing energy consumption and shifting towards carbon emission-free power sources for these energy-intensive technological backbones. The article highlights the increasing importance of cleaner power sources and less electricity use for data centers.

title: Evolution of GIS: How Maps Have Progressed from a Mapping Pancake to a Soufflé
summary: The article discusses the evolution of Geospatial Information Systems (GIS) from rudimentary mapping to sophisticated platforms integral to daily life and various industries. It highlights how digital maps and smartphone integration have transformed navigation and planning, expanding GIS applications beyond traditional uses to include military, agriculture, retail, and logistics. The piece also addresses challenges in real-time data and information authenticity while forecasting future trends, including AI integration, disaster management, and smart city applications.

title: HP Buys Poly (Formerly Plantronics)
summary: HP Inc. announced its agreement to acquire Poly (formerly Plantronics), a Santa Cruz-based workplace collaboration solutions provider, for $40 per share, totaling $3.3 billion including net debt. This acquisition aims to capitalize on the growing hybrid work trend, strengthening HP's portfolio in peripherals and workforce solutions. The transaction is expected to generate $500 million in revenue synergies by 2025 and improve Poly's operating margins.

title: These Black Women Launched An AI-Powered Beauty Tech And Secured Backing From Serena Ventures
summary: Parfait, an AI and facial recognition-powered beauty technology, has successfully closed a $5 million Seed funding round led by Upfront Ventures and Serena Ventures. Founded by four Black women with extensive tech backgrounds, Parfait aims to revolutionize the wig market by applying AI to solve problems like AI biases and the fragmented wig buying process for women of color. The funds will be used to enhance production and automate supply chain capabilities to provide personalized experiences for customers in the rapidly growing wig industry.

title: Pay-as-you-go MongoDB Atlas now available on Google Cloud
summary: MongoDB Atlas has launched a new pay-as-you-go offering on Google Cloud, simplifying subscription for developers by charging only for resources used without upfront commitments. This initiative aims to align with modern application development and follows significant revenue growth for MongoDB Atlas, partly attributed to its partnerships with cloud providers. The move also integrates MongoDB Atlas more tightly with Google Cloud services, making data analysis and application development more efficient.

title: MU professor says Google v. Oracle case leaves fair use ‘muddy’
summary: A University of Missouri law professor, Gary Myers, argues that the 2021 Supreme Court ruling in Google LLC v. Oracle America, Inc. has left the concept of "fair use" in copyright law "muddy" for software development. The ruling, which stated Google did not violate copyright by using Java code in Android, failed to clarify what aspects of computer code are protected and could introduce uncertainty for future software innovation. Myers highlights specific areas like intent, market impact, harm to the public, and transformative use that require further clarification from the courts.

title: UK gamers prepare for ‘post-console’ future, finds Amdocs
summary: Amdocs research indicates that UK gamers are increasingly ready for a "post-console" future, driven by cloud gaming and 5G connectivity. The study found high enthusiasm for cloud gaming and the metaverse, with many gamers willing to pay more for these services and forgo new hardware purchases. Key priorities for gamers include content quality and the ability to stream from any device, while concerns about the metaverse mainly revolve around its early stage, hardware costs, and internet connection capabilities.

title: How technology is transforming the future of performance marketing
summary: Technology is becoming increasingly vital in performance marketing, offering benefits like omnichannel mapping, real-time data, mobile-first strategies, powerful AI-driven ad stacks, and robust fraud protection tools. These advancements help brands connect with audiences more effectively, optimize ad spend, and maintain ROI in an evolving digital landscape. The article highlights how ad-tech ensures campaigns are impactful and efficient.

title: The Top Funded Quantum Security Companies and Who’s Backing Them
summary: This article lists the top-funded quantum security companies, detailing their services, funding, and key backers. It highlights eight companies including Arqit, IDQuantique, ISARA, Quantinuum, Quantum XChange, Quintessence Labs, PQShield, and Sandbox AQ, with a focus on their various approaches to quantum-safe encryption and cybersecurity. The piece emphasizes the growing landscape of quantum security and the significant investments flowing into these innovative firms.

title: Federal antitrust lawsuit filed against Google over Maps monopoly and Waze deal
summary: A federal class action antitrust lawsuit has been filed against Google, alleging it leveraged its dominance in the GPS navigation mapping market through Google Maps and Waze, improperly locking app developers into its ecosystem. The lawsuit, filed by Dream Big Media, Inc. and Getify Solutions, Inc., claims Google engaged in anticompetitive practices, including imposing restrictive terms, acquiring monopolies through acquisitions like Waze, and using data from its services to disadvantage rivals, violating federal antitrust laws and California state law.

title: FCC Mapping Data Open, Google-Waze Challenged in Court, Florida Co-op and Conexon Connect Team Up
summary: The FCC has opened preliminary serviceable location fabric data to fixed wireless providers, enabling them to align their availability data before the full fabric rollout on June 30, 2022. Concurrently, Google faces a class-action antitrust lawsuit over its alleged dominance in the GPS service market through Waze and Google Maps, while Florida's Tri-County Electric Co-op partners with Conexon Connect to deploy a 2,400-mile fiber-to-the-home network to serve rural areas.

title: LinkedIn was the most exploited brand in phishing attacks last quarter
summary: LinkedIn became the most exploited brand in phishing attacks during Q1 2022, accounting for 52% of all analyzed attempts, a significant jump from 8% in the previous quarter. Cyber threat intelligence provider Check Point Research reported this surge, noting that phishers are increasingly leveraging social networks. The article details examples of these attacks, including spoofed LinkedIn and Maersk emails, and offers tips to avoid falling victim to phishing.

title: Apple Spends Record High on Lobbying Amid Pressure from Congress
summary: Apple Inc. spent a record $2.5 million on lobbying in the first quarter of 2022, marking a 34% increase from the previous quarter. This significant expenditure comes as the company faces growing pressure from the U.S. Congress regarding antitrust concerns. Other major tech companies like Alphabet, Meta, and Microsoft are also heavily investing in lobbying efforts as Congress considers antitrust legislation that could impact the technology sector.

title: Cloud Architect: The Tech Skill Everyone Wants to Learn?
summary: Cloud architecture is rapidly becoming the most sought-after tech skill, with a 285% jump in class participation on Udemy. This demand is driven by the widespread adoption of cloud infrastructure by organizations, leading to a significant skills shortage. Cloud architects command a median annual salary of $131,513 and require expertise in AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and continuous learning of diverse technical and soft skills.

title: What is fintech and why does it matter?
summary: Fintech, a combination of “financial” and “technology,” refers to software that makes financial processes more accessible and automates traditional financial services. Kirsty Rutter, Fintech Investment Director at Lloyds Banking Group, explains how fintech is transforming banking by improving customer experiences through technologies like AI and machine learning. Lloyds Banking Group is actively investing in and collaborating with fintech companies to enhance its services and maintain a competitive edge.

title: Tax Breaks Cushion Tesla’s Texas Landing
summary: Tesla received approximately $64 million in state and local tax breaks to build its Giga Texas factory in Austin, sparking debate over the efficacy of such incentives in generating promised economic benefits and jobs. The article highlights concerns that communities often give up more than they gain from these deals, drawing parallels to Tesla's Buffalo factory where job creation targets were not met. Community groups and local residents have raised issues regarding the deal's lack of oversight, potential for wage theft and unsafe conditions, and the challenge of local hiring due to language barriers, leading some local lawmakers to question the wisdom of future incentive deals.

title: WPP merges Essence with MediaCom to form EssenceMediacom
summary: WPP's GroupM is merging Essence with MediaCom to create EssenceMediacom, aiming to combine Essence's digital and data-driven expertise with MediaCom's multichannel planning for client growth. Nick Lawson, formerly global CEO of MediaCom, will lead EssenceMediacom as global CEO, while Kyoko Matsushita, global CEO of Essence, will become WPP Japan CEO to focus on high-growth markets. This merger is part of broader WPP initiatives to enhance integrated services and leverage technology for outcomes-driven marketing, including the integration of Mindshare with Neo and the formation of GroupM Nexus.

title: Award-Winning Actor Michael Greyeyes Joins Directing Team for CTV Comedy Channel’s Original Series, ACTING GOOD
summary: Award-winning actor, director, and choreographer Michael Greyeyes is set to direct five episodes of CTV Comedy Channel's new original series, *ACTING GOOD*. The half-hour scripted comedy, loosely based on the life of Anishinaabe stand-up comedian Paul Rabliauskas, is currently in production in Winnipeg and surrounding areas. Darlene Naponse, Amber-Sekowan Daniels, and Eric Toth will also direct episodes for the debut season.

title: Why AWS, Microsoft, Google, Visa Are Setting Base In Kenya
summary: Major tech companies like Amazon Web Services, Microsoft, Google, and Visa are establishing operations in Kenya due to its growing economy and increasing client demand for cloud computing services. Kenya's strategic location as East Africa's largest economy and a hub for Fortune 500 companies makes it an attractive market for expansion. This move is driven by the need to meet local data residency requirements and capitalize on the burgeoning African cloud computing industry.

title: China Grants First Driverless Taxi Permits to Baidu, Pony.ai
summary: China has issued the first permits for driverless ride-hailing services to Baidu and Pony.ai in Beijing, marking a significant step in the country's pursuit of autonomous driving technology. These permits allow the companies to offer rides to the public without a safety driver behind the wheel, though a safety supervisor is still required in the front passenger seat. Baidu commenced its driverless services in a 23-square-mile suburban Beijing area on April 28.

title: MOTO & MRAD Rise on Tech Stock Rally
summary: U.S. stocks, particularly technology stocks, climbed on Thursday, with Apple and Amazon's earnings calls anticipated. Meta Platforms' strong first-quarter earnings report, showing an increase in users beyond expectations, boosted the Nasdaq Composite Index and the S&P 500 technology sector. The SmartETFs Advertising & Marketing Technology ETF (MRAD C) and the SmartETFs Smart Transportation & Technology ETF (MOTO) offer exposure to different segments of the technology sector, with MRAD focusing on advertising and marketing technology and MOTO investing in smart transportation and related tech.

title: Ford finds $130m road to Cavnue
summary: Ford Motor Company co-led a $130 million Series A funding round for Cavnue, a US-based road technology startup. Cavnue, formed by Sidewalk Infrastructure Partners, is developing technologies to integrate roads with advanced automotive features like autonomous vehicles and is exploring its first automated vehicle corridor in Michigan. Alongside Ford, other strategic partners including Globalvia and Landstar also invested, contributing their expertise in digital road technology and freight delivery improvement.

title: Top 10 Growth Stocks by Market Capitalization
summary: This article defines growth stocks and explains how they differ from other investment types, highlighting their potential for capital appreciation and typical reinvestment strategies. It then presents a list of the top 10 large-cap growth stocks as of April 2022, detailing their market capitalization, forward P/E ratio, dividend status, and specific growth drivers for each company. The article also touches on general market performance of growth stocks and factors influencing their volatility.

title: Crypto Logs: Crypto Goes from Meme to Mainstream
summary: This article discusses the increasing correlation between Bitcoin and broader equity markets, particularly tech stocks, despite a weaker start to 2022 for crypto and blockchain equities. It highlights growing institutional interest in cryptocurrencies, exemplified by Silvergate Capital's customer growth and Fidelity's announcement to offer Bitcoin investments in 401(k) plans, suggesting a move from "meme to mainstream" for crypto. The author posits that increased institutional participation could lead to more efficient trading and research, ultimately benefiting Bitcoin and related equities.

title: Two Chinese driverless robotaxi services approved in Beijing
summary: Beijing has approved autonomous rideshare companies Pony.ai and Baidu's Apollo Go to operate SAE Level 4 driverless robotaxis on public roads within a designated 60 square kilometer area. While no operator is required behind the wheel, a safety supervisor must still be present in the vehicle.
