This marks a significant step for China's driverless future, with both companies planning to expand their robotaxi fleets after passing rigorous safety tests.

title: With interest rates rising, it's time to focus on MANG stocks instead of FAANG, according to Jefferies
summary: Jefferies analysts recommend focusing on "MANG" stocks (Microsoft, Apple, Nvidia, and Alphabet) instead of the traditional "FAANG + Microsoft" group as interest rates rise. They suggest that MANG stocks present a safer investment strategy during the Federal Reserve's monetary policy tightening. This advice was issued prior to a Federal Open Market Committee meeting.

title: Stock Splits Lead to Split Results in Trading
summary: This article analyzes the impact of stock splits on market structure, liquidity, retail investor participation, notional exposure, trade size, odd lots, and volatility in both equities and options markets. Using data from 61 stock splits since 2020, including a case study on Apple (AAPL), the Cboe team found that stock splits do not necessarily increase liquidity proportionally to the split ratio and can lead to a decrease in overall investor capital exposure, though they do increase retail investor participation. The study suggests while splits enhance accessibility, they may not always benefit existing shareholders.

title: Google Cloud and SAP Expand Partnership to Enable Native Integration Between Google Workspace and SAP S/4HANA® Cloud
summary: Google Cloud and SAP have announced an expanded partnership that will integrate Google Workspace, specifically Google Docs and Google Sheets, directly with SAP S/4HANA® Cloud. This integration aims to enhance collaboration and efficiency for businesses by allowing users to collaboratively edit documents with SAP data, ensuring data integrity and version control. The initial features are expected to be included as a standard functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud later this year, built on SAP Business Technology Platform.

title: DevOps: Why it is now the only way to develop software
summary: DevOps is presented as a cultural shift essential for modern software development, moving beyond traditional "silos" and "waterfall" approaches. It emphasizes collaboration between development and operations teams and leverages automation to achieve frequent, seamless updates, meeting high end-user expectations set by major tech companies. The article argues that without DevOps, it's impossible for applications to remain relevant in today's fast-paced digital environment.

title: 2022 Selloff Has Left the U.S. Stock Market Undervalued
summary: The 2022 stock market selloff has pushed the broad U.S. equity market into undervalued territory, trading at a 12% discount to fair value according to Morningstar's equity research. While value stocks remain attractive, growth stocks, particularly small-caps, now offer significant discounts after substantial declines. The article highlights undervalued opportunities in the Communications, Consumer Cyclical, Financial Services, and Technology sectors, despite ongoing headwinds like slowing economic growth, Federal Reserve tightening, rising interest rates, and inflation.

title: Vodafone, Cardinality.io and Google Cloud build smarter European network performance platform
summary: Vodafone, Cardinality.io, and Google Cloud have partnered to develop a smarter, pan-European cloud-native network performance platform. This platform will enable Vodafone to manage and enhance its mobile network across eleven European countries more efficiently, using AI to analyze billions of network data points daily. The collaboration aims to accelerate network automation, improve customer experience, and optimize network investments.

title: Google Cloud: Unifying Manufacturer Data
summary: Google Cloud has launched new solutions, Manufacturing Data Engine and Manufacturing Connect, to help manufacturers unify data from disparate assets and improve visibility. These solutions enable AI- and analytics-based use cases such as manufacturing analytics, predictive maintenance, and machine-level anomaly detection. Early customers like Ford, Kyocera, and Phononic are using these tools to enhance data transparency and optimize production.

title: 7 Charts for Making Sense of These Markets
summary: This article analyzes current market volatility through seven charts, highlighting the Federal Reserve's aggressive interest rate hikes as a primary driver. It discusses the impact on both stock and bond markets, the underperformance of growth stocks versus value stocks, and offers insights on potential opportunities for investors amidst the market adjustments.

title: Class Action Complaint Claims Apple, Google, Snap Liable for Facilitating 'Sexual Grooming' of Minors
summary: Apple, Google, and Snap Inc. are facing a class action lawsuit in California Southern District Court for allegedly enabling the sexual grooming of minors. The suit stems from a criminal case where Snapchat was used to coerce a minor into pornographic acts. The plaintiffs seek to compel the defendants to enforce policies to prevent such criminal conduct and comply with child abuse laws.

title: Korean Webtoon Companies that are giving the Audience Addictive Content
summary: The Korean webtoon industry is experiencing significant global growth, driven by the success of K-culture, despite challenges like increased competition and a decline in artists' average income in 2023. Key players like Lezhin Comics, Naver's Webtoon and Line Manga, KakaoPage, Ridi Corp's Manta, Toomics, and Tappytoon are expanding internationally, leveraging diverse genres, free-reading models, and innovative technologies like AI and NFTs. These companies are not only captivating worldwide audiences but also transforming the global entertainment landscape through partnerships and strategic investments.

title: Meta is opening its first store as VR headsets inch closer to mainstream reality
summary: Meta Platforms is opening its first physical store to sell VR headsets like the Oculus Quest 2, despite its Reality Labs division facing significant losses. The company is betting heavily on the metaverse, viewing it as the solution to boost virtual reality adoption beyond the current low engagement among teens. Experts suggest that widespread VR adoption could be triggered by one or two "killer applications" and that Meta's strategy is to build the foundational platform for this new computing paradigm, even if profitability remains years away.

title: Walmart, J.B. Hunt Take Lead in Driverless Technology
summary: Walmart Inc. and J.B. Hunt Transport Services Inc. are leading the adoption of driverless truck technology to enhance supply chain efficiency. Walmart has partnered with Gatik for "middle-mile" deliveries, achieving 200,000 accident-free miles, while J.B. Hunt is collaborating with Waymo on long-haul routes in Texas, delivering 862,000 pounds of freight without incident. These initiatives aim to address increasing consumer demand, driver shortages, and the overall safety and efficiency of freight movement.

title: Tinder owner Match sues Google in 'last resort' to avoid app-store booting
summary: Match Group Inc., owner of dating apps like Tinder, has sued Alphabet Inc.'s Google, describing it as a "last resort" to prevent its apps from being removed from the Google Play store. Match is contesting Google's policy of demanding up to 30% of sales from apps and threatening to block downloads unless Match uses Google's payment system exclusively. The lawsuit accuses Google of violating antitrust laws and seeks to protect Match from potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in revenue payments to Google.

title: Tinder's parent company Match is suing Google over in-app billing
summary: Match Group, the parent company of Tinder and other popular dating apps, is suing Google over its in-app billing policies on the Android platform. Match Group alleges that Google's requirement for apps to use Google Play Billing for all in-app transactions, along with Google taking up to a 30% commission, constitutes antitrust and unfair competition. Google defends its policies, stating that its fees cover platform development, security, and distribution services, and that Match Group is trying to unfairly leverage Google's investments.

title: Online dating giant Match Group sues Google over app store
summary: Match Group, the company behind popular dating apps like Tinder and OkCupid, has sued Google, alleging an illegal monopoly in the Google Play Store and anticompetitive in-app payment policies. Match Group claims Google's 30% cut of in-app purchases and its mandate to use Google's payment technology are harmful, especially as Google threatened to remove Match apps from the Play Store if they don't comply with new rules. Google, in response, argues Match Group is trying to avoid paying for services and that the majority of apps on its platform do not pay fees.

title: Google Cloud launches AlloyDB, a PostgreSQL-compatible database for mission-critical workloads
summary: Google Cloud has introduced AlloyDB, a new fully managed, PostgreSQL-compatible relational database service designed for mission-critical workloads. Unveiled at Google I/O, AlloyDB is built on open-source PostgreSQL and Google Cloud's infrastructure, aiming to offer superior performance, scalability, and reliability compared to standard PostgreSQL and AWS Aurora. It emphasizes transparent pricing without I/O charges and includes AI/ML capabilities for management and analytical acceleration, positioning it as a strong competitor to Oracle and AWS in the database market.

title: Rackspace for sale, looking for privatization
summary: Rackspace Technology, which went public less than two years ago, is seeking privatization due to its market capitalization not reflecting the company's true value. The company, which helps businesses manage cloud environments, needs to make strategic changes to better compete in the growing multicloud market. Although profitable, Rackspace's growth isn't fast enough to satisfy stock market analysts, making privatization an attractive option to allow for necessary repositioning in the market.

title: Improving PPA With AI
summary: This article discusses how Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) are increasingly being used in Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools to enhance chip design by improving Power, Performance, and Area (PPA), reducing design errors, and accelerating time to market. It highlights that while AI can't replace the need for human designers to make critical decisions, it significantly streamlines tedious processes and optimizes designs that would be too complex for humans alone. The integration of AI promises greater efficiency, cost reduction, and the ability to design highly complex chips, as demonstrated by examples from leading technology companies.

title: Google’s New Wallet Digitizes Payment Cards Plus Drivers’ Licenses And Other Credentials
summary: Google has launched a new Google Wallet, aiming to digitize not only payment cards but also various other credentials like driver's licenses, vaccine cards, transit passes, hotel keys, and office badges. The new wallet is a core part of the Android mobile operating system and integrates with Wear OS, with plans to expand its capabilities and availability. Key features include ease of access, rich customer experiences, and enhanced security through biometric authentication or PINs.

title: WD Targets Cloud Challenges
summary: Western Digital (WD) is launching new SSDs and HDDs to address the exponential data growth and performance demands in cloud environments, particularly for hyperscale customers. The company's new 22TB and 26TB UltraSMR HDDs leverage OptiNAND and enhanced SMR technology, while their Ultrastar NVMe PCIe 4.0 SSDs target disaggregated storage in public clouds to improve utilization and density. WD also notes a strong demand for client SSDs driven by remote work and gaming, reinforcing the critical role of storage innovation across the board.

title: QVC in the digital age: Tech giants like YouTube embrace livestream shopping
summary: Tech giants like YouTube are embracing livestream shopping, reimagining the QVC model from the 1990s. YouTube aims to streamline the existing organic behavior of users seeking product reviews on its platform by integrating live video shopping experiences with influencers. This move comes as livestream shopping has become a multi-billion dollar industry, particularly significant in China, and presents a win-win opportunity for creators and viewers by making the process easier and more interactive.

title: SK Telecom's latest virtual assistant is more than just a voice
summary: SK Telecom has launched "A.", a new virtual assistant app that differentiates itself from others like Siri and Bixby by featuring a customizable character. This app aims to be a personalized daily companion for users, offering content curation, schedule management, and conversational capabilities, while integrating various services and utilizing the GPT-3 language model.

title: How to Send Money: The 9 Best Ways to Transfer Money
summary: This article details nine of the best ways to send money, catering to various needs from instant peer-to-peer transfers to large international sums. It compares options like Zelle, Venmo, Cash App, wire transfers, and money orders, highlighting their fees, transfer limits, and transfer times. The guide aims to help users choose the most suitable method for their specific financial transactions.

title: With multi-device FIDO credentials, you can now go all-in on passwordless | CXO Revolutionaries
summary: Apple, Google, and Microsoft's expanded support for multi-device FIDO credentials is set to revolutionize passwordless logins, addressing previous limitations of FIDO authentication like device-locked cryptographic keys and complex re-enrollment processes. This development, coupled with the Federal Zero Trust Strategy requiring phishing-resistant authentication, positions FIDO to become a prevalent, high-assurance authentication method for both consumers and federal use cases. The update promises seamless and secure access to FIDO credentials across multiple devices, even after device replacement.

title: Google 'private browsing' mode not really private, Texas lawsuit says
summary: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has amended a privacy lawsuit against Google, claiming the search engine collects user data even when users are in "private browsing" or Incognito mode, which implies anonymity. Paxton argues that Google deceptively tracks personal data, including sensitive information, while Google counters that the claims are inaccurate and outdated, asserting its strong privacy controls. This amendment is part of broader lawsuits filed by several states regarding Google's alleged deceptive location-tracking practices.

title: International Business Machines Corp. stock underperforms Friday when compared to competitors
summary: International Business Machines Corp. (IBM) stock fell 0.91% to $128.48 on Friday, May 20, 2022, underperforming the broader stock market, which saw the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average rise slightly. This marked the third consecutive day of losses for IBM, leaving its stock $17.51 below its 52-week high. While IBM declined, some competitors showed mixed performance, with Apple Inc. (AAPL) rising and Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) and Alphabet Inc. Cl A (GOOGL) falling.

title: Music festivals are a UX nightmare. Here’s how Snap and Live Nation are fixing them
summary: Snap and Live Nation are partnering to use augmented reality (AR) to enhance the music festival experience. New Snapchat lenses will debut at the Electric Daisy Carnival, offering features like 3D maps, set lists, directions, and a "FriendFind AR" tool to locate friends in the crowd. This collaboration aims to transform how attendees navigate and interact with large-scale events, going beyond mere selfies to provide practical utility and new engagement opportunities.

title: Trump sues Facebook, Twitter, Google for ‘censorship’, launches his own social media platform
summary: Former President Donald Trump filed class-action lawsuits against Facebook, Twitter, and Google for alleged "censorship" after his accounts were restricted or suspended following the Jan. 6, 2021 Capitol attack. The lawsuits claim these platforms, by coordinating with government entities, act as "state actors" and thus violate his First Amendment rights, though legal experts and court rulings have largely dismissed these arguments. Concurrently, Trump launched his own social media platform, "Truth Social," designed to be a "Big Tent" alternative to mainstream platforms.

title: Interxion pauses plans for $300 million expansion in Dublin
summary: Interxion has paused a $300 million expansion plan in Dublin due to a de facto moratorium placed on new data center developments in the greater Dublin area by EirGrid, Ireland's national grid operator. This moratorium, which could last until 2028, has led to calls for a nationwide ban and has prompted criticism from various industry bodies, despite local council efforts to prevent further data center construction. The situation highlights the growing pressure on Ireland's energy resources from data centers, which now consume 11 percent of the country's total grid-generated electricity.

title: Gartner: Marketing Budgets Increase To 9.5% Of Overall Company Revenue 05/24/2022
summary: Gartner's 2022 CMO Spend and Strategy Survey indicates a significant rebound in marketing budgets, which have increased to 9.5% of overall company revenue from 6.4% in 2021. Despite global economic challenges, 70% of CMOs reported budget increases, with digital accounting for 56% of the spend. Financial services recorded the highest budget at 10.4%, while consumer goods marketing spend slightly decreased.

title: Our Ultimate Stock-Pickers' Top 10 High-Conviction Purchases
summary: The article highlights the top 10 high-conviction purchases by Morningstar's Ultimate Stock-Pickers, focusing on significant additions to portfolios. Despite market volatility and inflation in early 2022, managers found value across various sectors, with Sherwin-Williams, T-Mobile, and Home Depot being particularly notable. Many of these high-conviction and new-money purchases received positive economic moat ratings from Morningstar analysts.

title: L’OFFICIEL Makes Its Arrival in the Metaverse with Virtual Land on The Sandbox
summary: Fashion magazine L’OFFICIEL has launched its presence in the metaverse with virtual land on The Sandbox, inspired by sci-fi films like Dune and Total Recall. This "L’OFFICIEL Land" is designed as an open-air exhibition and digital theatre to showcase the magazine's 100-year content history and future editorial focused on Web3 fashion. The initiative, led by Benjamin Eymère, CEO of L’OFFICIEL and Chief Metaverse Officer of AMTD group, marks a significant move into immersive digital experiences for the brand.

title: Pony.ai's permit to test autonomous vehicles revoked in California
summary: The California Department of Motor Vehicles has revoked Pony.ai's permit to test autonomous vehicles with safety drivers due to the company's failure to monitor the driving records of its safety drivers. The DMV found numerous violations on the driving records of active Pony.ai safety drivers, emphasizing the critical role of clean driving records for safety drivers. Pony.ai, while stating pride in its safety record of over 6.8 million accident-free autonomous miles, had previously lost a permit for driverless testing after a collision and subsequently recalled vehicles due to software issues.

title: How Chatbots are Revolutionizing Customer Experience
summary: Modern AI-powered chatbots are transforming customer experience by handling various tasks from ordering taxis to booking appointments, driven by advancements in AI, Machine Learning, and Natural Language Processing (NLP). Unlike traditional rule-based bots, NLP allows chatbots to understand complex conversations, retain context, and interpret human sentiment by integrating with external knowledge sources. Conversational AI, built on neural networks, enables chatbots to learn and improve continuously, process vast amounts of unstructured data to predict user behavior, and perform repetitive tasks, making them indispensable for businesses and freeing up human employees for more complex issues.

title: Global Data Annotation Tools Market Size, Share Forecast to 2033
summary: The global data annotation tools market was valued at USD 2.87 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 23.82 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 26.50%. Key growth drivers include the rapid penetration of AI in healthcare and massive data generation via advanced technologies, while the inaccuracy of some tools remains a restraint. Asia Pacific is expected to dominate the market, with North America also showing significant growth.

title: iRobot Unveils iRobot OS - The Most Thoughtfully Intelligent Operating System for the Home
summary: iRobot has launched iRobot OS, a new operating system that enhances the intelligence of its home robots. This OS integrates advanced computer vision and machine learning to offer features like improved pet-centric cleaning, object avoidance, and enhanced voice command capabilities for Roomba j7 and j7+ robot vacuums. iRobot OS is designed to make robot cleaning more effective and adapt to user preferences and home environments.

title: Apple Inc. stock falls Tuesday, underperforms market
summary: Apple Inc.'s stock (AAPL) fell 0.53% to $148.84 on Tuesday, underperforming the broader market as the NASDAQ Composite Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also declined. This marked the end of a three-day winning streak for Apple, and the stock closed $34.10 below its 52-week high. Competitors showed mixed results, with Microsoft (MSFT) and IBM (IBM) also falling, while Alphabet (GOOGL) saw a gain.

title: To Promote 'Stranger Things,' These Businesses Developed an App That Lets You Order a Pizza With Your Mind
summary: To promote the fourth season of Netflix's "Stranger Things," Domino's partnered with WorkInProgress and Unit9 to launch a new "mind-ordering" app. This app allows users to order pizza using head movements, mimicking the telekinetic powers of the character Eleven. It also features a "Just for Fun" mode to explore the show's world and discover Easter eggs, building on Domino's history of innovative ordering methods.

title: Ottawa should reject the Murdoch plan
summary: This article argues that Canada should reject Rupert Murdoch's plan to force tech companies like Google and Meta to pay news publishers for content, as successfully implemented in Australia. The author contends that the plan is a self-serving cash grab by large newspaper chains, particularly Postmedia, which is largely American-owned. The piece highlights Murdoch's history of media manipulation and questions the premise that Google and Meta are "stealing" content, while suggesting that Canada adopting this plan would create a domino effect for the UK to follow.

title: What Does Amazon's Stock Split Mean for Investors?
summary: Amazon is executing a 20-1 stock split, its first in over two decades, effective June 3. The split will adjust Morningstar's fair value estimate for Amazon to $192 per share from $3,850, but senior analyst Dan Romanoff's long-term view remains unchanged, anticipating continued growth in e-commerce, AWS, and advertising. Other major tech companies like Alphabet and Tesla are also planning stock splits.

title: Google Cloud Partnership to Bolster XPO’s Advanced Capabilities
summary: XPO announced a multiyear partnership with Google Cloud to enhance its supply chain capabilities through advanced AI, machine learning, and data analytics. This collaboration aims to improve visibility, optimize logistics in both truck brokerage and less-than-truckload (LTL) services, and support XPO's upcoming spin-off of its brokered services business. The partnership also emphasizes sustainability by reducing XPO's carbon footprint through more efficient transportation technologies.

title: Lawyer David Boies bills $1,950 hourly in Google case, court filing shows
summary: Veteran trial lawyer David Boies is billing $1,950 an hour in a lawsuit alleging privacy violations against Google. This billing rate is part of a fee petition filed after a judge sanctioned Google for litigation misconduct by failing to timely disclose key evidence. The lawsuit claims Google secretly collected user information while they were in "Incognito" browsing mode.

title: Oracle closes $28B deal to buy EHR giant Cerner
summary: Oracle has finalized its $28.3 billion acquisition of health IT company Cerner, marking its largest acquisition to date and a significant move to expand into the healthcare market. The deal, which involved Oracle finalizing a stock tender offer with 69.2% of Cerner shares, aims to leverage Cerner as an "anchor asset" to scale Oracle's cloud business in hospitals and health systems. This acquisition positions Oracle to transform healthcare delivery through new information systems and addresses the growing trend of big tech companies investing heavily in the healthcare IT sector.

title: Google partners up with Figma to bring the popular design tool to Chromebooks
summary: Google for Education is partnering with Figma to bring the web-based design tool to Chromebooks, allowing school districts to manage free licenses through the Google Admin Console. This initiative, starting as a beta for U.S. High Schools this summer, aims to equip students with visual communication and collaboration skills using accessible hardware. Figma's co-founder noted increased student engagement and interest in design careers due to the platform's trial in classrooms.

title: Verizon's cloud continuum meets MongoDB's multicloud deployment model (it's all supercloud, really)
summary: Verizon and MongoDB have partnered to simplify software development and networking, especially for mobile edge applications. They aim to abstract away the complexities of 5G and multicloud deployments through what Verizon calls a "cloud continuum" and MongoDB supports with its multicloud deployment model. This collaboration facilitates easier development by allowing developers to use familiar tools to interact with robust network APIs across various cloud providers.

title: MIGHTY MORPHIN POWER RANGERS #100 Announcement
summary: BOOM! Studios has announced the upcoming release of MIGHTY MORPHIN POWER RANGERS #100, a special oversized issue celebrating the milestone. Written by Ryan Parrott, this issue will culminate years of storytelling and feature a host of returning artists, along with collectible trading cards and an exclusive art print. It marks Parrott's final issue on the main series, promising an epic and climactic battle for the Rangers.

title: Fortinet (FTNT) to Split Stock in 5 to Attract More Investors
summary: Fortinet (FTNT) announced a five-for-one forward stock split to make its shares more accessible to employees and retail investors, with stockholder approval scheduled for June 17. If approved, the stock price will drop from approximately $300 to $60 per share. This move follows similar stock splits by other major tech companies like Alphabet, Amazon, and NVIDIA, aiming to boost demand and potentially increase share prices.

title: Trudeau meets Google boss Sundar Pichai, but no word on whether the tech CEO chewed out the PM over Bill C-18
summary: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau met with Sundar Pichai, CEO of Alphabet (Google's parent company), but it's unclear if Bill C-18, a controversial Canadian media compensation bill, was discussed. The bill, criticized by experts like Michael Geist as a "shakedown," aims to make platforms like Google pay for linking to news content, giving the CRTC greater oversight. Google's Canadian head of public policy, Colin McKay, has stated that while Google supports journalism funding, Bill C-18 "would fundamentally and negatively impact millions of Canadians."

title: Google Agrees to Pay $118 Million to Settle Pay Discrimination Case (Published 2022)
summary: Google has agreed to pay $118 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging systematic underpayment of women. The settlement covers approximately 15,500 women employed by Google in California since September 2013 and also mandates external oversight of its pay practices. Google denied wrongdoing, stating the settlement was in the best interest of all parties involved after five years of litigation.

title: Google Engineer Claims AI System is Sentient; China's Tech Giants Flourish as Crackdown Eases
summary: A Google engineer has been suspended after claiming that the company's LaMDA chatbot system shows signs of sentience, which Google denies, citing confidentiality breaches. Meanwhile, China's major tech companies like Baidu, Tencent, and Alibaba are experiencing a surge in share value as Beijing eases its regulatory crackdown on the sector. Twitter has also introduced a new "people-first" content moderation process to improve reporting of offensive content, especially amid Elon Musk's previous concerns about fake accounts.

title: Oracle women stumble in pay bias suit while Google cuts a deal
summary: Women at Oracle Corp. face a setback in their pay disparity lawsuit as a judge tentatively revoked their class-action status, making it more difficult to proceed. Meanwhile, Google agreed to pay $118 million to settle a similar lawsuit alleging pay disparities for more than 15,000 female workers. The Google settlement includes provisions for independent oversight of hiring practices and pay equity studies.

title: Newmark Completes Sale of Moffett Green, Fully-Leased Trophy Campus in Silicon Valley
summary: Newmark has successfully completed the sale of Moffett Green, a 719,037 square-foot, fully-leased trophy office campus in Sunnyvale, California, to CommonWealth Partners, LLC. The transaction, which represents the largest single-asset investment office sale in Silicon Valley history, involved Meta Platforms, Inc. as the long-term tenant. Steven Golubchik and his team at Newmark facilitated the deal, highlighting continued investor demand for prime assets in high-barrier-to-entry markets and the strong performance of Moffett Park.

title: C3 AI & Google Cloud: Optimizing Supply Chains
summary: C3 AI announced a new supply chain software product developed in partnership with Google Cloud to address disrupted global supply chains. The C3 AI Supply Chain Suite leverages Google Cloud AI, infrastructure, and Supply Chain Twin to improve demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and supplier lead time visibility, leading to better on-time customer delivery. This collaboration integrates C3 AI's AI capabilities with Google Cloud's powerful infrastructure and data organization for resilient enterprise AI supply chain solutions.

title: C3 AI Receives Google Cloud AI/ML Partner of the Year Award
summary: C3 AI has been awarded Google Cloud's 2021 Technology Partner of the Year for AI/ML, recognizing the success of their partnership launched in September 2021. The alliance focuses on enabling customers to rapidly deploy enterprise AI applications across various industries, utilizing Google Cloud services like Cortex, Supply Chain Digital Twin, Vertex AI, and LendingDoc AI. This award highlights their joint efforts in delivering innovative, industry-specific AI solutions and accelerating customer value.

title: L3Harris in talks to buy Israeli spyware firm NSO - reports
summary: U.S. defence contractor L3Harris is reportedly in talks to acquire Israeli spyware firm NSO Group, a deal that would require approvals from U.S. and Israeli authorities and L3Harris's board. The potential acquisition raises concerns in the White House due to NSO's controversial Pegasus software, which has been used by governments to spy on individuals, leading to the company being blacklisted by the U.S. Commerce Department. If approved, the deal could potentially remove NSO from the banned list and allow its assets to be exclusively used with the United States and its allies.

title: McDonald's agrees to pay $1.3 bln to settle French tax dispute
summary: McDonald's has agreed to pay $1.3 billion in fines and back taxes to resolve a long-running tax dispute in France, ending a probe into whether the company properly declared its income. The settlement, which covers the years 2009-2020, does not constitute an admission of guilt and concludes both tax and criminal cases against the fast-food giant in France. McDonald's stated it paid over 2.2 billion euros in taxes and created nearly 25,000 jobs in France over that period.

title: Walmart enters shoppable TV via exclusive deal with Roku
summary: Walmart has partnered with Roku to introduce shoppable TV, allowing customers to purchase products directly through their Roku remotes while streaming. This collaboration aims to simplify the shopping experience on the largest screen in the home and integrates with Roku Pay for seamless checkout. This initiative is part of Walmart's broader strategy to expand its livestream shopping efforts across various digital platforms.

title: A closer look at P&G’s ‘water positive’ pledge
summary: Procter & Gamble has announced a "water positive" goal by 2030, aiming to restore more water than its operations and products consume. The initiative includes two new restoration pledges focusing on water consumed during manufacturing and by household product use in 18 water-stressed regions, primarily Los Angeles and Mexico City. P&G is investing in various restoration projects and integrating sustainability metrics into executive compensation, while experts commend the company's rigorous approach to consumer-use impact but call for more engagement with public policy for broader impact.

title: Oracle introduces lower-cost version of its full on-premises cloud stack
summary: Oracle has introduced a more affordable version of its on-premises cloud stack, Dedicated Region Cloud@Customer, alongside a new rack-level offering called Oracle Compute Cloud@Customer. The new Dedicated Region Cloud@Customer reduces the required space and annual cost significantly, making Oracle's full public cloud services, including Fusion applications, accessible to a broader range of global companies for workloads that cannot fully migrate to the public cloud. Oracle Compute Cloud@Customer provides managed on-site computing and storage resources for smaller workloads, similar to offerings from AWS, Microsoft, and Google.

title: Microsoft stops selling emotion-reading tech, limits face recognition
summary: Microsoft Corp announced it will stop selling emotion-reading technology based on facial images and will also limit unfettered access to its facial recognition technology. This move aligns with efforts by leading cloud providers to self-regulate sensitive technologies as lawmakers consider new legal limits. Existing customers of emotion-reading tools will have one year before losing access, and facial recognition services will now require approval for use.

title: How Comscore and YouTube Unlock the Power of CTV
summary: Comscore and YouTube have partnered to measure the effectiveness of YouTube advertising across various platforms, especially connected TV (CTV). Their findings indicate that YouTube provides significant unique reach beyond traditional linear TV, with nearly half of target audiences watching YouTube ads on CTV. This partnership aims to offer advertisers greater transparency and efficiency in cross-platform campaign measurement through Comscore's Campaign Ratings™ (CCR).

title: MinIO extends Snowflake analysis to any data location
summary: MinIO has partnered with Snowflake to enable Snowflake analytics on data stored in any location, not just within Snowflake's own environment. This partnership allows customers to leverage external table support in MinIO for cost-effective analysis of large datasets without moving them to the cloud. This provides flexibility similar to recent Snowflake partnerships with Dell and Pure Storage, offering various options for on-premises or cloud-based data analysis.

title: How T-Mobile's 3G shutdown will impact Google Fi and older Pixels
summary: T-Mobile's upcoming 3G shutdown in July will impact Google Fi subscribers, especially those using older devices. While newer Pixels received a VoLTE roaming update, Pixel 1 and 2 users may experience connectivity issues when roaming, and non-VoLTE phones will lose all voice call ability, including emergency services. Google Fi is offering a $200 service credit for affected customers to upgrade to newer devices.

title: Companies paying for abortion-related travel include Dick's Sporting Goods, Google, Disney
summary: Following the Supreme Court's decision to overturn Roe v. Wade, several major corporations, including Dick's Sporting Goods, Google, and Disney, are offering to cover abortion-related travel expenses for their employees. This move comes as at least 26 states are expected to ban or significantly restrict abortion access, prompting companies to take a stance on reproductive rights despite previously avoiding the issue.

title: Por qué un empresario propone colocar "estatuas de Cristina Kirchner en cada rincón de Uruguay"
summary: A Brazilian venture capitalist, Paulo Passoni, has provocatively suggested erecting statues of Cristina Kirchner across Uruguay. He argues that her policies have inadvertently driven a significant number of Argentine entrepreneurs and tech companies to relocate to Uruguay, appreciating its stable economic environment and clear rules. This migration has fostered a thriving tech ecosystem in Uruguay, benefiting the country by attracting talent and investment.

title: T-Mobile is now selling data on which apps you open
summary: T-Mobile has launched its new "App Insights" program, which sells app usage data to advertisers, allowing them to target users based on the apps installed and engagement patterns. While the program focuses on Android devices, Apple's strict privacy guidelines largely protect iPhone users from data collection, though T-Mobile notes that it is "kicking the tires" on incorporating iOS data in the future. Android users can opt out of the program through the "Magenta Marketing Platform Choices" app.

title: J.B. Hunt, Waymo Add Wayfair to Autonomous Trucking Program
summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services Inc. and Waymo have partnered with Wayfair to expand their autonomous trucking program. This pilot initiative, taking place in July and August, will involve autonomous trucks delivering Wayfair shipments between Houston and Dallas to evaluate logistics, freight loading, and autonomous performance. This collaboration marks a significant step towards fully autonomous commercial transport and the first in-depth use of autonomous technology for home furnishings retail freight.

title: News | Google in Talks To Buy Chicago’s Thompson Center
summary: Google is in negotiations to purchase the James R. Thompson Center in Chicago's Loop business district, a move that could significantly boost the area. This deal is the latest in Google's history of strategic real estate acquisitions in Chicago, and it could provide space for thousands of additional employees. The acquisition could also influence Prime Group's redevelopment plans for the building and the state of Illinois's prior agreement to buy back office space.

title: Self-driving truck firm Waymo to deliver goods for Wayfair on Interstate 45
summary: Waymo, Alphabet Inc.'s self-driving technology unit, is partnering with J.B. Hunt Transport Services to deliver Wayfair goods on Interstate 45 between Houston and Dallas. This six-week pilot program, starting in July, will involve autonomous Class 8 trucks with specialists monitoring operations in the cab. The partnership builds on previous successful tests and aims to integrate autonomous freight movement into supply chains to meet capacity needs.

title: J.B. Hunt releases metrics on self-driving truck pilot
summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services Inc. has released positive metrics from a self-driving truck pilot program on Interstate 45 between Dallas and Houston, in collaboration with Waymo Via. The pilot successfully moved over 860,000 pounds of freight with excellent safety records, leading to a long-term strategic alliance. J.B. Hunt also announced an expansion of its autonomous trucking program with Wayfair for a six-plus week pilot project on the same corridor.

title: Cloudera embraces Apache Iceberg as cloud data lake evolves
summary: Cloudera has announced support for Apache Iceberg, an open-source cloud data lake table format, within its Cloudera Data Platform. This move signifies Cloudera's continued evolution from its Hadoop roots towards modern cloud data lake technology. The company believes Iceberg enhances its ability to work with various cloud providers and analytical engines, addressing the growing need for flexible and structured data lake solutions in enterprise environments.

title: How Nuuly is using technology to aid in sustainability tracking
summary: Nuuly, a clothing rental service by Urban Outfitters, is leveraging cloud-based technology, specifically Google Cloud, to track clothing items and enhance sustainability efforts. By understanding customer behavior and garment lifecycles through data analytics, Nuuly aims to reduce waste and improve efficiency. This initiative aligns with a broader retail trend where companies are prioritizing ESG efforts, though many struggle with implementation and measurement of sustainability.

title: What are FAANG stocks?
summary: FAANG is an acronym for five leading technology companies: Facebook (now Meta), Amazon, Apple, Netflix, and Google (now Alphabet). These companies are known for their consistent growth, innovation, and significant impact on the American stock market, particularly the NASDAQ 100 and S&P 500 indices. While they have shown strong performance and market capitalization, there's an ongoing debate among investors about their valuation.

title: Up in the Cloud!
summary: This article by Uma Iyer discusses the evolving concept of cloud computing, moving beyond its abstract perception to its tangible impact on technology and data management. It explores the differences between cloud storage and cloud computing, highlights real-world applications and the benefits of scalability and ease of use, and addresses the critical issue of trust and security in cloud technologies through various models and programs like CSA STAR. The article concludes by looking at emerging trends like edge computing, indicating a bright future for cloud technology.

title: Enterprise AI and Machine Learning for Managers​
summary: This article introduces an eBook titled "Enterprise AI and Machine Learning for Managers," designed to provide frontline managers with a practical understanding of AI/ML. The book aims to equip managers with the knowledge to identify high-impact AI use cases, collaborate effectively with data scientists, and deploy AI/ML into production to gain a competitive advantage. Written by former McKinsey consultant and professor Nikhil Krishnan, the guide focuses on helping managers understand AI science, measure success, and build strong data science teams.

title: Android Automotive is taking over, but what about Google Automotive Services (GAS)?
summary: Google's Android Automotive OS is rapidly gaining traction in the car infotainment space, with a projected 18% market share by 2027. A key component of this growth is Google Automotive Services (GAS), which offers integrated Google apps like Maps, Play Store, and Assistant for a licensing fee to automakers. While many car manufacturers are adopting Android Automotive, not all are integrating the full GAS suite, leading to variations in user experience and competitive strategies in the evolving automotive infotainment market.

title: What the Alphabet Stock Split Means for Google Investors
summary: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) announced a 20-for-1 stock split scheduled for July 15, aiming to make its shares more accessible and liquid for investors. While the split is a cosmetic change that doesn't alter fundamentals, it could increase trading activity and allow smaller investors to buy shares. The article advises investors to consider their personal strategy when deciding whether to invest before or after the split, noting that the current market dip combined with the split could make Google more affordable.

title: Amazon and Microsoft deny using Flickr pics for facial recognition, as suits test limits of privacy law
summary: Amazon and Microsoft are facing lawsuits alleging they violated the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act by using Flickr photos for facial recognition development without consent. Both companies deny using the IBM "Diversity in Faces" database for their AI algorithms after initial downloads. The cases highlight broader issues concerning the lack of a national privacy law, the trade-offs between privacy and AI accuracy, and jurisdictional complexities in the cloud era.

title: Gurus foresee 'virtual humans' enhancing many industries
summary: Experts predict that virtual humans, fueled by AI advancements, will revolutionize various industries such as retail, finance, and education. Production costs and development times for digital avatars are expected to decrease significantly, leading to widespread adoption. Companies like Baidu are already investing heavily in this technology, with the market in China projected to reach 10.24 billion yuan ($1.5 billion) by 2026.

title: PLDT Home, Google launch Be Internet Awesome online series
summary: PLDT Home and Google have partnered to launch the "Be Internet Awesome" online video series, aimed at teaching Filipino children internet safety and digital responsibility. The series combines animation and live-action, featuring characters who guide kids on how to be smart, alert, strong, kind, and brave online, based on Google's global program. This initiative is part of broader efforts by PLDT and Google to ensure online safety, especially regarding child sexual abuse material, and will premiere on PLDT Home's YouTube channel.

title: NetApp Ontap to be sole vSAN storage alternative in the cloud
summary: NetApp's Ontap-based cloud storage services will become the exclusive vSAN storage alternative officially supported by VMware's vSphere instances in the cloud. This partnership allows enterprise customers to extend VMware data stores in AWS, Azure, and Google clouds using Ontap as an external NFS data store, offering a more cost-effective solution than traditional vSAN. The move aims to provide a familiar and integrated storage experience for VMware customers migrating to the cloud.

title: 5 Ways to Play the Next Big Thing in Telecommunications
summary: The next significant trend in telecommunications will be the "fiber rollup," where large telecommunications companies acquire local fiber networks. This trend is driven by broadband subsidies and the desire of existing players to upgrade from obsolete copper infrastructure. The article highlights five companies – Alphabet, Verizon, Frontier, Comcast, and Lumen Technologies – that are well-positioned to benefit from or participate in this fiber network consolidation, offering various investment opportunities including dividends and potential capital gains.

title: What Does Google’s Stock Split Mean for Investors?
summary: Google's parent company, Alphabet, is undergoing a 20-for-1 stock split, which will adjust Morningstar's fair value estimate per share to $180, though its competitive advantage and 4-star "undervalued" rating remain unchanged. Analyst Ali Mogharabi identifies YouTube monetization, Maps monetization, and the cloud business as key growth drivers, with strong confidence in Google's continued dominance in the online search market. The article also notes other companies like Amazon, Shopify, and Tesla undertaking similar stock splits.

title: Stock-Split Watch: Is Adobe Next?
summary: Adobe could be the next high-profile company to announce a stock split, following a trend set by others like Alphabet and Amazon in 2022. While stock splits don't alter a company's fundamental valuation, they can make shares more accessible to retail investors and help companies attract/retain talent. Adobe has a history of 2-for-1 splits, with its last one occurring 17 years ago, and its strong financial performance in Q2 2022 suggests a healthy business that could support such a move.

title: YouTube settles moderators' case over graphic videos for $4.3 mln
summary: YouTube has agreed to pay $4.3 million to resolve a lawsuit alleging it failed to protect content moderators from psychological harm caused by exposure to violent and disturbing videos. The settlement, which includes counseling services and peer support, still requires federal judicial approval. This follows a similar lawsuit settled by Facebook for $52 million last year, and a pending case against TikTok.

title: How to Buy Google Stock After the Split: GOOG vs. GOOGL
summary: Alphabet (Google's parent company) completed a 20-for-one stock split on July 15, 2022, making its shares more accessible to retail investors by lowering the per-share price. The article explains that Alphabet trades under two ticker symbols, GOOG (Class C, non-voting) and GOOGL (Class A, voting rights), both of which were affected by the split. Existing shareholders did not need to take any action as their accounts were automatically updated to reflect the new share quantity and price.

title: Google Parent Alphabet Names Goldman Sachs Veteran to Board
summary: Alphabet Inc., Google's parent company, has appointed Marty Chavez, a veteran from Goldman Sachs, to its board of directors. Chavez brings extensive finance and technology experience, having served as a vice chairman and partner with Sixth Street Partners and previously spending two decades at Goldman Sachs. This appointment marks Alphabet's first board change since 2020.

title: In Just 3 Words, Google CEO Sundar Pichai Taught a Leadership Lesson to Every CEO
summary: Google CEO Sundar Pichai’s recent memo to employees, announcing a slowdown in hiring and cost-cutting measures, is highlighted as a model of leadership and emotional intelligence. Amid economic uncertainty, Pichai framed these challenges as an opportunity to focus on what truly matters for Google. The article praises his approach, contrasting it with other tech companies that have also announced layoffs or hiring freezes.

title: Verizon Communications Inc. stock rises Friday, still underperforms market
summary: Verizon Communications Inc. stock increased by 1.05% on Friday to $51.02, despite a strong day for the broader market indexes. The stock's rise ended a two-day losing streak, yet it still closed short of its 52-week high and underperformed key competitors like Apple, Alphabet, and AT&T. Trading volume for Verizon was lower than its 50-day average.

title: Google hit with $971,000 sanction for litigation misconduct in privacy suit
summary: A U.S. judge has ordered Alphabet Inc.'s Google to pay over $971,000 in legal fees and costs as a sanction for litigation misconduct in a privacy lawsuit. The misconduct involved Google's failure to timely disclose certain evidence in a $5 billion lawsuit alleging unlawful tracking of users in "incognito" mode. Plaintiffs' lawyers, including David Boies, sought over $1 million, while Google's lawyers questioned the billing rates.

title: What Does GameStop’s Stock Split Mean for Investors?
summary: GameStop (GME) is undergoing a 4-for-1 stock split, its first in 15 years, which will increase the number of shares and decrease the price per share without affecting market capitalization. Morningstar gives GME a 3-star quantitative rating, indicating it is fairly valued, but with extreme uncertainty due to high volatility. This split follows similar actions by other tech and consumer companies like Amazon, Shopify, and Alphabet.

title: Google faces $1 bln UK trial over app store pricing
summary: Google is set to face a London trial over a £920 million ($1.1 billion) class action lawsuit alleging it overcharged 19.5 million customers for app store purchases. The lawsuit claims Google abused its dominant position by charging up to 30% commission on apps in its Play Store since October 2015, breaching European and British competition laws. This action is part of broader global efforts by regulators and consumer groups to curb alleged anti-competitive behavior by Big Tech companies like Google and Apple.

title: Amgen exec describes generative biology’s promise to unveil sequence, structure and function
summary: Amgen is heavily investing in generative biology, an emerging field that combines AI, machine learning, and high-throughput automation to accelerate protein drug discovery. Alan Russell, VP of biologics at Amgen, leads the Digital Biologics Discovery Group and emphasizes the importance of integrating wet lab and dry lab approaches to understand the links between protein sequence, structure, and function. The company's strategy involves leveraging decades of data generation, strategic investments in AI-ready infrastructure, and partnerships like the Teneobio acquisition and collaboration with Generate Biomedicines to overcome the challenges and timelines associated with traditional drug discovery.

title: 3 Reasons Why Investing in the Nasdaq-100 Is Effective
summary: This article highlights three core reasons why investing in the Nasdaq-100 is effective for long-term asset management. It explains how index investing, particularly in the Nasdaq-100, offers exposure to rapidly growing, innovative global companies with strong brand power and historically high growth rates. The article details various investment products available, such as mutual funds and ETFs, and discusses strategies like currency hedging and leverage.

title: Baidu unveils autonomous vehicle without steering wheel
summary: Baidu Inc. has unveiled a new autonomous vehicle (AV) with a detachable steering wheel, planning to deploy it for its robotaxi service in China next year. The new model will significantly reduce costs to 250,000 yuan per unit, enabling a larger deployment across China and aiming to make robotaxi rides half the cost of traditional taxis. This Level 4 autonomous vehicle features advanced sensors, and Baidu is awaiting regulatory approval to operate it without a steering wheel.

title: iRhythm Technologies receives another FDA 510(k) for AFib monitoring system
summary: iRhythm Technologies has received another FDA 510(k) clearance for its ZEUS System, developed in partnership with Verily, which monitors atrial fibrillation. The latest clearance is for an algorithm that characterizes the amount of AFib a patient experiences over time using PPG-based bio-signals. The ZEUS System is not yet commercially available but is expected to have a limited market release in 2023.

title: GM trails far behind Tesla in EV sales — CEO Mary Barra bet the company that will change
summary: GM CEO Mary Barra is betting the company's future on electric vehicles, despite currently trailing far behind Tesla in sales. GM plans to invest $30 billion in EVs by 2025, launch 30 electric models globally, and believes its Ultium platform will enable it to surpass Tesla by mid-decade or 2029. Barra's legacy is tied to transforming GM into an electric leader, building on past efforts to make the company leaner and more agile.

title: iRhythm, Alphabet unit Verily get FDA clearances for Zio Watch and software system
summary: iRhythm Technologies and Alphabet's Verily have received FDA 510(k) clearance for their new AI-powered Zio Watch and Zio ECG Utilization Software System (Zeus). This system is designed for continuous monitoring of cardiac arrhythmia, particularly atrial fibrillation, and aims to assist clinicians in diagnosis by characterizing the amount of AFib over time. While not yet commercially available, the collaboration aims to compete with existing wearables like the Apple Watch in the cardiac monitoring market.

title: Top stock reports to watch this week (25.07.2022)
summary: The article highlights the key earnings reports scheduled for the week of July 25, 2022, focusing on major US tech companies like Alphabet, Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Amazon, and Apple. It notes that the Wall Street earnings season is in full swing, with several Dow Jones members also reporting their financial results. This week is expected to be significant for traders due to the high-profile companies announcing their earnings.

title: Google Has a Genius Method for Discovering Top Talent -- No Degree or Experience Required
summary: Google is attracting top talent using an innovative method that doesn't require traditional degrees or prior experience. The company offers professional certificate courses, such as the Google Project Management Certificate, which open doors to roles for successful participants. This approach allows Google to assess aptitude based on coursework quality rather than conventional educational or professional backgrounds.

title: Alphabet earnings miss estimates, ad revenue beats, stock pops
summary: Alphabet reported Q2 earnings that missed Wall Street estimates, with adjusted earnings per share hitting $1.21 against an expected $1.32. Despite the overall miss, ad revenue surpassed expectations at $56.29 billion. Shares rose 4% in after-hours trading, offering some relief to investors following concerns about the digital advertising market.

title: WSJ stands by Elon Musk affair report: “We are confident in our sourcing”
summary: The Wall Street Journal is standing by its report alleging an affair between Elon Musk and Nicole Shanahan, Sergey Brin's former wife, despite Musk's denials and Shanahan's lawyer calling the report false and defamatory. Musk has publicly refuted the claims, stating that he spoke to both Brin and Shanahan, who denied being sources for the story, and that he does not plan legal action against the Journal. The WSJ maintains confidence in its sourcing and reporting.

title: Construction begins on 100-MW solar project for Google data centers in Tennessee Valley
summary: Construction has commenced on the 100-MW Skyhawk Solar project in Obion County, Tennessee, a collaboration between the Tennessee Valley Authority, Origis Energy, and Excelsior Energy Capital. This facility will provide carbon-free energy to Google's data centers in Tennessee and Alabama, supporting Google's goal of operating on 24/7 carbon-free energy by 2030. The project, developed through TVA's Green Invest program, is expected to create 300 construction jobs and generate significant local economic benefits.

title: Xfinity Mobile and Comcast Business Mobile Will Offer the Google Pixel 6a
summary: Xfinity Mobile and Comcast Business Mobile are offering the Google Pixel 6a "On Us" to new and existing customers who activate a new line, transfer a number, and purchase the phone on a 24-month Device Payment Plan. This promotion runs from July 28 to August 9, while supplies last. The Pixel 6a features Google Tensor and Titan M2TM security, and customers can choose between by-the-gig or unlimited data plans.

title: Google and U.S. chip maker SkyWater expand open source chip design platform
summary: Google Public Sector and U.S. chip manufacturer SkyWater Technology Inc. are expanding an open-source platform for chip design, with the U.S. Department of Defense funding $15 million for the development. This collaboration aims to lower the cost and increase accessibility for chip design and production for both national defense and commercial markets. The platform will be powered by Google's cloud and focuses on analog-digital mixed-signal chips using a 90-nanometer process.

title: KT ups ante with $1.5 billion spending on public sector cloud service
summary: KT Cloud Corp., a subsidiary of South Korea's telecom giant KT Corp., plans to invest up to 2 trillion won ($1.5 billion) over the next three years to expand its presence in the domestic public cloud service sector and enhance its internet data center (IDC) capacity. This move comes as competition intensifies in the Korean cloud market, with local rivals like Naver Cloud and NHN Cloud also aggressively expanding, and foreign players such as Snowflake, Amazon's AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud Platform dominating the broader market. However, local companies are seen to have an advantage in the public sector due to meeting specific government security requirements.

title: Lessons from Target’s cloud journey, a decade in the making
summary: Target embarked on its cloud journey a decade ago, moving beyond a simple "lift and shift" to a hybrid multicloud architecture supported by Google Cloud Platform and Microsoft Azure. The retailer modernized its application stack with a microservices approach and open-source tools, overcoming initial cost challenges through significant investment in engineering and FinOps. Target plans to continue its hybrid strategy, leveraging public cloud for scalability and cost arbitrage, and private cloud for data-intensive and core business applications.

title: Webull Launches Expansion into Singapore
summary: Webull Corporation has officially launched its expansion into Singapore and the Asia-Pacific region. Through Webull Securities (Singapore) Pte. Ltd., residents can now trade U.S. and Hong Kong-listed equities, ETFs, options, and fractional shares with low commissions. This move follows Webull's success in the U.S. and Hong Kong, aiming to capture the growing mobile-first brokerage market in Asia-Pacific.

title: The Neocolonial Game Behind Musk-Bolsonaro Rendezvous
summary: This article criticizes Elon Musk's meeting with Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro, framing it as a neocolonial act driven by resource acquisition and political manipulation. It highlights concerns about Musk's Starlink project in the Amazon, linking it to Brazil's nickel and lithium reserves, and questions the integrity of Bolsonaro's government in its dealings with foreign interests given its past dismantling of environmental monitoring bodies. The author suggests the collaboration serves to undermine Brazil's democratic processes and exploit its natural wealth for corporate gain.

title: Music Stocks List 2026 - Best Publicly Traded Companies
summary: This article identifies top publicly traded music companies for 2026, including Spotify, Apple, Sirius XM, Google, Tencent Music Entertainment, and Warner Music Group. It discusses their market positions, growth opportunities, and business models, highlighting shifts towards streaming, podcasts, and diverse media portfolios within the evolving music industry. The author emphasizes that investing in the music industry offers various choices, from streaming giants to production houses, with significant future growth potential.

title: Cohesity names former VMware COO Sanjay Poonen as CEO
summary: Cohesity Inc., a data management provider, has appointed Sanjay Poonen, former COO of VMware Inc., as its new president and chief executive officer. Poonen replaces Cohesity founder Mohit Aron, who will transition to chief technology and product officer. This leadership change comes after Cohesity surpassed an annual revenue run rate of $300 million and boasts over 3,000 customers, including major financial institutions and Fortune 500 companies.

title: Oracle Reportedly Initiating Layoffs in Bay Area, Other Locations
summary: Oracle has reportedly begun laying off thousands of employees in Silicon Valley and other global locations, targeting a billon dollars in expenses. These cuts occur despite strong profits and revenues, as Oracle is heavily investing in becoming a major cloud-services player to compete with Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud, while simultaneously continuing to pay specialized technologists competitive salaries. The company's strategy involves significant investment in cloud infrastructure while also reducing its workforce in other areas.

title: How the economic downturn is affecting the AI sector
summary: The economic downturn is impacting the AI sector, with enterprises cutting budgets and slowing AI projects, despite AI being an integral part of the economy. While some vendors and venture capitalists are feeling the squeeze through stricter investment processes and hiring freezes, others remain insulated due to prior budgeting or long-term investment strategies. Experts suggest experimental AI projects will be paused, but the long-term outlook for next-generation AI technologies remains positive.

title: Google Sued for Nixing Free Workspace Software to Early Adopters
summary: Alphabet Inc.'s Google is being sued by an early adopter of its Workspace cloud productivity software. The lawsuit claims that Google reneged on a promise to provide free lifetime access to the program, which includes services like Gmail, Calendar, and Drive. While some basic features are free, enterprise features such as custom email addresses typically cost extra.

title: Naver says no growth sacrifice to cut costs with record sales
summary: Naver, South Korea's largest online portal, reported record quarterly revenue driven by its webtoon and fintech services, despite a sharp drop in net profit due to aggressive global marketing. CFO Kim Nam-sun stated the company is not sacrificing growth to narrow losses and hinted at further M&A in commerce and B2B solutions. The CEO aims to achieve global operating profit margins for its content business similar to the domestic market's 20% within two to three years.

title: Putting the emphasis on IEC 62368-1 Overvoltage Compliance
summary: The article discusses the importance of complying with the IEC 62368-1 standard for electronic equipment, especially concerning overvoltage and overcurrent protection in universal power adapters. It highlights how this newer, hazard-based standard is more flexible and future-proof than its predecessors, requiring careful consideration of protection components like varistors, GDTs, and fuses. The authors, from Littelfuse, Inc., detail the scope, criteria, and testing functions required by IEC 62368-1, offering guidance on selecting appropriate protection components for reliable and safe electronic designs.

title: Cybersecurity skills shortage provides rare cross-training opportunities
summary: The article highlights the significant cybersecurity skills shortage in the U.S., presenting it as a unique opportunity for individuals to cross-train and enter a high-paying field. It discusses the attributes that make a good cybersecurity analyst, dispels the myth that a college degree is always required, and outlines various certification paths, including those for cloud security and zero trust frameworks, emphasizing the benefits for both individuals and global security.

title: Chuck Schumer’s Family Is Getting Nice Work on Wall Street
summary: The article highlights how Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer's family members and former staffers are being hired by major corporations like Blackstone, Amazon, Intel, and Google to lobby on legislation before the Senate. This raises concerns about potential conflicts of interest, especially regarding Schumer's role in advancing or delaying legislation that impacts these companies. Specifically, it details Blackstone's hiring of Schumer's son-in-law around the time a tax loophole benefiting private equity was being debated and Schumer's delay in bringing antitrust legislation against tech monopolies to a vote, given his daughters' employment by these tech giants.

title: 10 malicious Python packages exposed in latest repository attack
summary: Check Point Research has discovered 10 new malicious packages in PyPi, the official Python repository, which could lead to malware downloads or credential theft for unsuspecting users. These supply-chain attacks are becoming increasingly common, prompting repositories like GitHub to implement digital signing systems for packages to help verify their authenticity. One such package, "ascii2text," mimicked a legitimate library and downloaded a malicious script to steal tokens, passwords, and crypto wallet information.

title: Ant Group IPO: Everything you need to know about Ant Group
summary: Ant Group's IPO plans are currently on hold as the company focuses on business rectification and optimization, with previous attempts to go public having been shelved. Despite a significant drop in its valuation from $315 billion to $180 billion, Ant Group remains profitable and generates revenue primarily through its lending, investment, and insurance products, with Alipay serving as a crucial entry point for users into its extensive ecosystem. The company faces stiff competition from Tencent's WeChat Pay in the Chinese digital payment market and is undergoing regulatory changes, including an anticipated shift in control from Jack Ma and increased oversight from the People's Bank of China.

title: How Microsoft secures its spot among the world's most valuable brands
summary: Microsoft has become one of the world's most valuable brands, with its brand value reaching $661 billion in 2022, securing the number four spot in Kantar BrandZ Most Valuable Global Brands. The company's success is attributed to its meaningful, different, and salient offerings, including superior cloud, software, and hardware products, as well as its commitment to sustainability. Under CEO Satya Nadella, Microsoft has focused on specific market segments, leading to a tenfold increase in brand value and solidifying its position as the world's most valuable business services brand, further boosted by the continued digital transformation and remote work trends.

title: Download and Play Spellslingers Now!
summary: Wizards of the Coast has soft-launched Spellslingers, a new digital collectible card game set in the Magic: The Gathering multiverse. The game features fast-paced, hero-centric combat, allowing players to choose from sixteen unique Spellslinger characters, each with their own playstyle and starter deck. Spellslingers is available for download on Android, iOS, and PC, inviting players to customize their decks, form teams, and trade materials.

title: 'Big Short' Hero Michael Burry Dumps Most of His Stock Holdings
summary: Michael Burry, known for predicting the 2008 financial crisis, disclosed that his firm, Scion Asset Management LLC, significantly reduced its stock holdings in the last quarter. As of June 30, Scion Asset Management held shares in only one company: private-prison operator GEO Group, compared to over $165 million in various stocks at the end of March. This move suggests Burry's bearish outlook on the market.

title: Michael Burry's Hedge Fund Added One Stock And Dumped All the Rest
summary: Michael Burry's Scion Asset Management drastically reduced its holdings in the second quarter, exiting 11 US equities including Alphabet and Meta Platforms. The hedge fund added Geo Group Inc., making it the sole long stock holding by June 30. This move signals a significant shift in Burry's investment strategy.

title: Saudi PIF buys shares in Alphabet, Zoom and Microsoft in U.S. shopping spree
summary: Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) has significantly increased its U.S. stock holdings, acquiring shares in major tech companies like Alphabet, Zoom Video, and Microsoft. The sovereign wealth fund also invested in JPMorgan, BlackRock, Starbucks, and several other prominent companies. These investments are part of the PIF's strategy to diversify Saudi Arabia's economy away from oil and build an international investment portfolio.

title: Saudi PIF buys shares in Alphabet, Zoom and Microsoft in US shopping spree
summary: Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) significantly expanded its US stock portfolio in the second quarter, investing approximately $40.8 billion. The fund acquired shares in major tech companies like Alphabet, Zoom Video, and Microsoft, alongside holdings in JPMorgan, BlackRock, Starbucks, Adobe, and others. The PIF, managing $620 billion in assets, is a key instrument in Saudi Arabia's strategy to diversify its economy away from oil.

title: Capital Budgets Fall to 13% of IT Spending
summary: Capital budgets now constitute only 13% of IT spending in North American companies, a significant drop from 24% in 2013, as businesses shift away from on-premises infrastructure towards public cloud and subscription-based applications. While total IT spending is projected to increase, the allocation is largely towards operational expenditures like cloud infrastructure and applications rather than long-term capital investments. This shift brings flexibility but also risks of being locked into difficult-to-negotiate operational contracts.

title: R|T: The Retail Times – Groupon to focus on ‘mission critical activities’ following staff cuts
summary: Groupon has cut over 500 staff and plans to focus solely on "mission-critical activities" due to underperforming business. This move is intended to improve its trajectory and generate positive cash flow by the end of 2022, following similar workforce reductions by other tech giants like Alibaba and Amazon. The article also provides updates on various other tech companies, including funding rounds, layoffs, and strategic partnerships.

title: Aflac virtualizes the customer enrollment experience
summary: Aflac successfully virtualized its customer enrollment experience during the pandemic to support its independent agents, who were facing significant business risks due to social distancing. By combining existing technologies and building a new enrollment platform on AWS, Aflac enabled agents to conduct virtual meetings, co-browse with clients, and process policy purchases more efficiently. This digital transformation, which included the development of microsites and a "bionic" agent approach, earned Aflac a CIO 100 Award and lays the groundwork for a full migration to the cloud and further intelligent automation.

title: 15 Best Apps That Can Pay You Real Money
summary: This article lists 15 best apps that can help users earn real money, ranging from market research apps like Money App and iPoll to freelance platforms like Upwork and Fiverr, and social media platforms like YouTube, Facebook, and Instagram for content monetization. It explains various methods to leverage these apps for income, such as affiliate marketing, selling products, participating in surveys, and investment savings, while also highlighting general ways to earn extra income and the benefits of having multiple income streams.

title: These Wide-Moat Stocks Are Gaining Ground
summary: Quality stocks with wide economic moats, including Microsoft and Amazon, have outperformed since the bear market low in June, despite still being undervalued. While narrow-moat stocks have seen stronger recent gains due to market-cap weighting of companies like Apple and Tesla, wide-moat companies are better positioned to handle inflation due to their pricing power. Overall, Morningstar analysts consider wide-moat stocks to be deeply undervalued compared to overpriced narrow-moat stocks.

title: Making AI Run At Any Scale But Not At All Costs
summary: This article discusses the challenges and solutions for scaling AI applications, highlighting how companies like HPE and Lenovo are developing accessible AI products. It focuses on Anyscale, a company founded by UC Berkeley researchers, which commercializes the open-source Ray framework to simplify distributed AI computing and make it easier for enterprises to develop and deploy AI models without extensive infrastructure expertise.

title: Rackspace’s intention not to sell leaves Wall Street wary
summary: Rackspace Technology Inc. has decided against selling the company and will instead pursue a restructuring plan, disappointing some Wall Street analysts. The cloud-computing services provider, the largest tech company in San Antonio, will realign into separate business units for private and public clouds. This decision led to a significant drop in share prices as analysts downgraded the stock, expressing skepticism about the standalone strategy's ability to create shareholder value quickly.

title: China's Baidu reveals its first quantum computer called Qianshi
summary: Baidu, the Chinese search engine giant, unveiled its first quantum computer, "Qianshi," featuring a 10-qubit processor, making it available to external users. The company also developed a 36-qubit quantum chip as it joins the global race in quantum computing. This technology, seen as a cornerstone for future global supremacy, is attracting significant investment from governments and major tech companies like IBM and Google.

title: Eyeing expansion, Infosys leases 5 lakh square feet in Bengaluru
summary: Infosys has leased approximately 5 lakh square feet of office space in Bengaluru to support its expansion plans, which will help the company spread its presence across the city. This move is part of Infosys's strategy to expand beyond its existing campuses and set up new offices in tier-2 cities like Kolkata, Nagpur, Coimbatore, Vizag, and Noida, aiming to tap into a wider talent pool and accommodate its growing employee count, which reached 335,186 by June 2022. The new facility, located at Northgate, will provide space for around 1,000 employees as part of the company's hybrid work model.

title: Plask Launches Namesake Cloud-Based Motion Capture Tool
summary: Plask, a 3D animation startup backed by major Korean companies, introduced its cloud-based motion capture and animation SaaS tool after six months of beta testing. The new MoCap Pro premium tier offers advanced features like multi-person mocap and foot locking, aiming to make 3D animation more accessible. The tool uses AI and camera-based mocap to convert movement into 3D animation data, catering to the growing market of metaverse platforms and Web3 applications.

title: How Royal Caribbean set up a seafaring cloud strategy
summary: Royal Caribbean Group has adopted a multicloud strategy to manage the complex IT needs of its cruise ship fleet, leveraging Google Cloud, AWS, and Microsoft Azure for different workloads. This approach helps address the unique challenges of seafaring operations, such as unreliable satellite connectivity and the need for robust hybrid solutions. The company emphasizes the importance of balancing internal expertise with external partners and carefully considering cost and talent access in its vendor selection process.

title: Google Says Trump’s Truth Social Must Scrub Violent Content to Join Play Store (Published 2022)
summary: Google stated it would not distribute Truth Social on its Play Store until the platform removed content inciting violence. Google identified physical threats in user content from Truth Social's Android app submission, while Truth Social's parent company, Trump Media & Technology Group, said it is working with Google to comply with policies without compromising free speech. The social media app is available on Apple's App Store and could bypass Google by offering direct downloads or using other app stores like Samsung's Galaxy Store.

title: Google-Sonos patent litigation an early case dealing with internet-of-things, says law prof
