summary: A Federal Court of Canada decision found Sonos Inc. did not infringe Google’s patent on adaptive echo and noise control technology, marking an important early case in internet-of-things (IoT) litigation. The case is part of a broader, multi-jurisdictional dispute between the two companies over smart-speaker technology. Professor Norman Siebrasse highlights that IoT devices, much like smartphones, are complex products involving numerous patented technologies, making patent litigation in this area a relatively new and evolving field.

title: Garmin Vivosmart 5 vs Fitbit Charge 5 vs Inspire 3: Fitness band face-off
summary: This article compares three popular fitness trackers: Garmin Vivosmart 5, Fitbit Charge 5, and Fitbit Inspire 3, examining their design, hardware, and health tracking capabilities. It highlights that while smartwatches are popular, fitness bands offer similar metrics in a less bulky design with better battery life, making them a cost-effective alternative. The comparison concludes that the Fitbit Charge 5 generally offers more advanced features like built-in GPS, ECG, and a superior AMOLED display for the same price as the Vivosmart 5, while the Inspire 3 is a discreet, budget-friendly option.

title: Truth Social: content moderation issue keeps Trump app off Android phones | Donald Trump
summary: Donald Trump's social media platform, Truth Social, has not been approved for distribution on the Google Play Store due to insufficient content moderation, a Google spokesperson announced. Google cited concerns about violations of its policies, including those prohibiting physical threats and incitement to violence. This delay prevents Truth Social from accessing approximately 40% of the US smartphone market, a setback for the platform.

title: DAX-ETF statt MSCI-World-ETF? Was der Fokus auf deutsche Aktien wirklich bringt
summary: This article examines whether a DAX-ETF is a viable alternative or supplement to an MSCI World-ETF for investors. The author argues that while German companies in the DAX have a long history, focusing solely on a DAX-ETF has historically yielded poorer returns compared to the more diversified MSCI World. Key reasons cited against a DAX-ETF include its historical underperformance, regional concentration, lack of tech companies, and limited number of constituent stocks.

title: Friday Q&A: iRhythm CTO Mark Day talks Zio Watch, Verily partnership, Big Tech in healthcare
summary: iRhythm Technologies CTO Mark Day discusses the new Zio Watch, a diagnostic smartwatch for cardiac monitoring developed in partnership with Alphabet's Verily. Day emphasizes that the Zio Watch is a prescribed diagnostic tool, distinguishing it from consumer smartwatches like the Apple Watch, and addresses the strategy for clinical evidence, reimbursement, and market evaluation. He also shares his views on Big Tech's complementary role in raising awareness for conditions like atrial fibrillation.

title: Amazon discriminating against Asians, whites in grant program, class action lawsuit says
summary: A class action lawsuit alleges that Amazon discriminates against Asian and white applicants in its "Diversity Grant" program, which offers stipends to Black, Latinx, and Native American entrepreneurs. The lawsuit, filed by Crystal Bolduc, claims the program violates the Civil Rights Act of 1866 by forcing Asian and white hopefuls to cover their own startup costs. Bolduc seeks to end the program and obtain damages for those who have experienced racial discrimination due to it.

title: ADT Draws More Than $1.5 Billion of New Investments From State Farm, Google
summary: ADT has secured over $1.5 billion in new investments from State Farm and Google. State Farm will invest $1.2 billion for a 15% stake in ADT and an additional $300 million for product innovation and customer acquisition, gaining a board seat. Google will commit an extra $150 million towards product development and marketing, building on its previous investments.

title: Jim Chanos Takes Aim at Data Centers and Their High Valuations
summary: Noted contrarian investor Jim Chanos is challenging the high valuations of data center REITs, despite their apparent growth potential. He argues that major cloud providers building their own facilities pose a competitive threat, potentially limiting future growth for companies like Equinix and Digital Realty Trust. Chanos and other analysts also raise concerns about rising costs, intense competition, and opaque valuation metrics for these firms.

title: Google CEO wants company to be 20% more efficient, hints at cuts
summary: Google CEO Sundar Pichai has indicated that the company needs to become 20% more efficient, hinting at potential cuts. This comes after Google previously announced plans to invest over $75 million locally. The move suggests a focus on cost-cutting measures, following a period where the company had added 10,000 employees in the second quarter.

title: The Head of Google Says Future AI Must Align with Human Values
summary: Google CEO Sundar Pichai emphasized at the Code 2022 conference that the future development of artificial intelligence must align with human values. He highlighted Google's strides in AI, including large language models and AlphaFold, but acknowledged that true "conversational AI" is still some years away and that Google needs to improve its handling of AI ethics. Pichai also discussed competition in the tech industry, antitrust concerns, content moderation, and the company's approach to issues like data privacy post-Roe v. Wade.

title: Single-Stock ETFs Are Mushrooming. What to Know.
summary: Direxion, a fund company, is expanding its single-stock ETF offerings with six new funds. These ETFs will allow investors to make short-term bullish or bearish bets on major tech companies like Amazon, Alphabet, and Microsoft. This move signifies a further expansion in the fast-growing market of single-stock funds.

title: Buy stocks with wide moats to protect your profits
summary: The article discusses Warren Buffett's "economic moat" concept, highlighting companies with sustainable competitive advantages. It outlines five key characteristics of such companies—intangible assets, cost advantage, switching costs, network effects, and efficient scale—and provides examples like Coca-Cola, Salesforce, and Alphabet. The piece concludes with advice on how investors can identify and invest in these wide-moat companies, including building a personal portfolio or using ETFs and investment trusts.

title: Google, Apple facing anti-competitive complaint in Mexico
summary: Apple and Google are facing an anti-competitive complaint in Mexico filed by former telecommunications chief Mony de Swaan Addati. The complaint alleges that both companies exploit their app store monopolies by forcing the use of their proprietary payment systems for in-app purchases, charging commissions of 15%-20%, and thereby inflating prices. De Swaan Addati hopes Mexico's telecommunications regulator (IFT) will investigate and address these practices.

title: NetEase Games and Shueisha Games Announce unVEIL the world, a Tactical Hero Summoning RPG for iOS and Android
summary: NetEase Games and Shueisha Games announced "unVEIL the world," a new tactical hero summoning RPG for iOS and Android, at Tokyo Game Show 2022. The game features an epic story centered around a gigantic tower, with character designs by Posuka Demizu, known for "The Promised Neverland." This collaboration aims to deliver an unparalleled gaming experience by combining Shueisha's content creation expertise with NetEase Games' development strengths.

title: News | Tech Companies Still Need Offices. But Here's How Their Look and Location Are Changing.
summary: Tech companies are rethinking their office real estate strategies, moving away from large, centralized headquarters to more flexible, dispersed models. Influenced by the pandemic shift to remote and hybrid work, companies like Salesforce and Google are opting for smaller, more purposeful spaces, often with enhanced amenities, or expanding to satellite offices in various cities. This change reflects a balancing act between cost-saving, attracting talent, and recognizing the value of in-person collaboration, while also acknowledging a broader geographic distribution of the tech workforce.

title: Google Completes Acquisition of Mandiant
summary: Google LLC announced the completion of its acquisition of Mandiant, Inc., a leader in cyber defense, threat intelligence, and incident response services. Mandiant will join Google Cloud, retaining its brand, to deliver an end-to-end security operations suite for customers. This acquisition aims to enhance cybersecurity solutions by combining Mandiant's frontline expertise with Google Cloud's security capabilities.

title: Google and NIST Partner to Open Source Nanotechnology Testbed Wafers
summary: Google and the US National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) have partnered to create an open-source, wafer-scale testbed for nanotechnology research, built on SkyWater Technologies' SKY130 process node. Google will distribute these nanotech test wafers to universities and release the designs under an open-source license. This initiative aims to facilitate research, improve access to semiconductor technology, and streamline the transition of innovations from lab to practical application.

title: Palo Alto Networks 3-for-1 stock split: What to Know
summary: Palo Alto Networks completed a 3-for-1 stock split, with shares gaining slightly on the first day of trading post-split before moving into negative territory. Shareholders of record as of September 6 received two additional shares for each one held, distributed after the close of trading on September 13. The company stated the split aims to make shares more accessible to employees and a broader base of investors, following significant stock price increases since its IPO.

title: Essent Group adds to board of directors
summary: Essent Group Ltd, a mortgage re/insurer based in Bermuda, has appointed Anu (Henna) A Karna to its board of directors. Ms. Karna brings extensive experience in digital, data, and analytics innovation from high-tech, consumer goods, risk management, and insurance industries, including previous roles at Google Cloud, AXA XL, and American International Group Inc. Chairman and CEO Mark Casale highlighted her in-depth knowledge of innovation driven by data and technology as invaluable to the company's future endeavors.

title: What’s New in Artificial Intelligence from the 2022 Gartner Hype Cycle™
summary: The 2022 Gartner Hype Cycle for Artificial Intelligence highlights key AI innovations expected to drive significant business benefits and will see mainstream adoption within two to five years. These innovations are categorized into data-centric, model-centric, applications-centric, and human-centric AI, with a focus on impact on both business leaders and engineering teams. The article emphasizes technologies like composite AI, decision intelligence, edge AI, and responsible AI as crucial for competitive advantage and solving complex business problems.

title: Essent adds digital expert to board of directors
summary: Essent Group has appointed Anu (Henna) Karna to its board of directors, bringing over 25 years of experience in digital, data, and analytics innovation across various industries. Karna currently serves as the global general manager of industry solutions for insurance at Google Cloud. Her expertise is expected to be invaluable in enhancing Essent's use of data and digital transformation initiatives.

title: S&P 500 Buybacks Decline 21.8% as Financials Pull Back; 12-Months Buybacks Pass $1 Trillion for the First Time
summary: S&P 500 stock buybacks decreased by 21.8% in Q2 2022 to $219.6 billion from Q1's record $281.0 billion, primarily due to a pullback from Financials and Health Care sectors. Despite the quarterly decline, the 12-month expenditure ending June 2022 surpassed $1 trillion for the first time, reaching $1.005 trillion, a 65.0% increase from the prior year. An anticipated 1% excise tax on net buybacks in 2023 is not expected to significantly impact overall buyback activity in 2023, though some 2023 purchases may be accelerated into Q4 2022.

title: Never Mind Growth vs. Value Stocks, Look to Beta
summary: This article argues that investors should focus on stock volatility, represented by beta, rather than the traditional growth vs. value distinction, especially given the current economic uncertainty. It highlights that high-beta stocks, despite their risk, are currently undervalued, particularly in cyclical sectors, presenting potential opportunities. The author also suggests that low-beta stocks can offer a haven for risk-averse investors in uncertain times.

title: Herbalife Nutrition Survey Reveals Top Ten Modern Day Entrepreneurs and What Traits are Needed to Join the List
summary: A Herbalife Nutrition survey identified Bill Gates, Jeff Bezos, and Steve Jobs as the top three most influential modern entrepreneurs, followed by Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg. The study, involving 2,750 respondents across the U.S., Mexico, and Israel, also explored traits essential for entrepreneurial success, highlighting creativity and intelligence as most important. Herbalife Nutrition emphasized the ongoing strength of the entrepreneurial spirit and the company's commitment to fostering it.

title: Robert Kyncl Named CEO of Warner Music Group Starting January 1, 2023
summary: Robert Kyncl has been appointed CEO of Warner Music Group (Nasdaq: WMG), effective January 1, 2023, succeeding Steve Cooper. Kyncl, currently Chief Business Officer at YouTube, will serve as co-CEO with Cooper for January 2023 before becoming sole CEO on February 1, 2023. Cooper, who has been the longest-running CEO in WMG's history, will transition out after over a decade of leadership marked by growth and innovation.

title: 7 Stocks to Buy During a Stock Market Crash
summary: This article identifies seven stocks that investors should consider buying during a stock market crash, arguing that such downturns offer opportunities for long-term gains. It discusses the academic likelihood of crashes versus investor fears and presents companies like Alphabet, Intuitive Surgical, Exxon Mobil, NextEra Energy, Toyota, Walmart, and Coinbase as strong candidates for investment once deep discounts become available. The author emphasizes that while investors often over-anticipate crashes, it's prudent to strategically prepare for potential market pullbacks.

title: Google Cloud and Travelers to Create Connected Data Ecosystem to Enhance the Commercial Insurance Underwriting Process
summary: Google Cloud and The Travelers Companies, Inc. are partnering to develop a cloud-based data ecosystem that will streamline commercial insurance underwriting for large and middle market businesses. This platform will integrate diverse data points and leverage AI to provide underwriters with a clearer risk profile, potentially reducing the quoting process from weeks to minutes. The initiative aims to enhance underwriting efficiency and accelerate interactions with agents and customers.

title: Wisk CEO predicts air taxi industry shakeout next year
summary: The CEO of Wisk Aero LLC, Gary Gysin, predicts that only four or five air taxi companies will survive out of the over 100 currently operating. This forecast follows the closure of Kittyhawk, a joint venture backed by Boeing and Google co-founder Larry Page. Gysin's prediction highlights the anticipated consolidation within the burgeoning air taxi industry.

title: Budget cuts will spare IT heading into 2023
summary: Despite widespread concerns about a prolonged economic downturn and anticipated budget cuts in 2023, IT spending is expected to remain robust. A survey of over 1,400 IT professionals by Spiceworks Ziff Davis indicates that most companies plan to increase their tech budgets, with only a small percentage looking to reduce IT spending. This trend suggests that companies view technology investments, particularly in modernization and cloud services, as crucial hedges against economic instability, even as they trim non-essential spending elsewhere.

title: SK Telecom, KT raise Galaxy Z Flip 4 subsidies ahead of iPhone 14 launch
summary: South Korean mobile carriers SK Telecom and KT Corp. have increased subsidies for the 512-gigabyte model of Samsung's Galaxy Z Flip 4. This move comes ahead of the iPhone 14 launch next month, as the carriers aim to strengthen the competitiveness of Samsung's foldable smartphone amidst anticipated competition from Apple. LG Uplus, the third-largest carrier, did not adjust its subsidy program.

title: Amazon.com unveils device that tracks breath while sleeping
summary: Amazon has announced a new contactless sleep-tracking gadget called Halo Rise, priced at $139.99, which monitors room conditions and breathing patterns to provide insights into sleep quality. This device, not FDA-approved, is part of Amazon's broader investment in consumer health technology. The company also unveiled updates to its Kindle e-readers and voice-controlled devices.

title: Lazard Asset Management: 3 undervalued global equities
summary: Warryn Robertson from Lazard Asset Management identifies three undervalued global equities: eBay, Intel, and International Game Technology (IGT). He explains that eBay and Intel are attractively priced after significant declines, having refocused their strategies and invested heavily in R&D, respectively. IGT is highlighted for its strong, underappreciated earnings growth and low valuation compared to its peers.

title: Microsoft Corp. stock outperforms market despite losses on the day
summary: Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) shares decreased by 1.48% to $237.50 on Thursday, still outperforming the broader market which saw the S&P 500 fall by 2.11% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average fall by 1.54%. The stock closed significantly below its 52-week high, but performed better than some competitors like Apple, Alphabet, and SAP SE ADR. Trading volume for Microsoft exceeded its 50-day average.

title: Lloyds Bank closing Copley data center in 2025
summary: Lloyds Banking Group plans to close its Copley, West Yorkshire data center in 2025 as part of a modernization strategy and ongoing cloud migration. The bank had previously announced a £60 million plan for its Halifax Bank unit's HQ, which included the closure of the Copley facility. Lloyds has been moving workloads to the cloud, signing a large deal with IBM in 2017 and later with Azure and Google Cloud.

title: TTAC: Overweight Tech & Energy, Underweight Industrials & Communication
summary: The FCF US Quality ETF (TTAC) focuses on free cash flow to select approximately 150 stocks, aiming to outperform the Russell 3000. As of late August, TTAC was overweight in technology and energy sectors and underweight in industrials and communication services. FCF Advisors prioritizes free cash flow metrics, which they believe are more difficult to manipulate than GAAP earnings, especially in the current challenging investment environment.

title: AI-based clinical assistant Kahun raises $8m
summary: Israeli AI-based clinical assistant Kahun has secured an $8 million seed financing round, bringing its total raised to $13 million. The company's unique approach involves an algorithm based on professional medical literature rather than patient records, allowing doctors to trace diagnoses and treatments to their sources. Kahun aims to assist doctors, particularly in telemedicine, by providing a questioning tool for patients that saves time and improves accuracy, with current agreements with telemedicine companies and medical content platforms.

title: Q3 2022 Market Performance in Charts
summary: The third quarter of 2022 saw significant market challenges, with stocks returning to bear market territory despite an initial relief rally. Both stock and bond markets were negatively impacted by high inflation, aggressive interest rate hikes from the Federal Reserve, and rising recession fears. The article details various market segments' performance, the strengthening dollar, commodity changes, and increased volatility, suggesting continued difficult market conditions ahead.

title: Waymo, C.H. Robinson Driverless Trucks Haul Beer to Houston From Dallas
summary: Waymo, Alphabet's driverless-vehicle unit, and C.H. Robinson have completed a pilot program hauling over 1 million pounds of Modelo and Corona beer from Dallas to Houston using Class 8 trucks with Waymo's self-driving software and a safety driver. This test demonstrates the system's maturity and performance, potentially addressing the long-haul driver shortage for C.H. Robinson and commercializing Waymo's autonomous vehicle technology. The companies are working towards deploying service without a test driver in the coming years.

title: Google Cloud unveils medical imaging suite with Hologic, Hackensack Meridian as 1st customers
summary: Google Cloud has launched a new medical imaging suite designed to make imaging data more interoperable and useful through AI, with Hackensack Meridian Health and Hologic as its first customers. The suite aims to accelerate AI development for faster, more accurate diagnoses by addressing challenges like image annotation and data silos, leveraging partnerships with Nvidia and Monai. This initiative focuses on standardizing imaging data around DICOM to improve diagnostic accuracy and ease the burden on radiologists.

title: Driverless Waymo Trucks in Texas Transport Over 1 Million Pounds of Modelo, Corona
summary: Waymo's driverless trucks have successfully transported over one million pounds of Modelo and Corona beer across Texas as part of a pilot program with Constellation. This initiative, which began in April, involves autonomous vehicles driving over 220 miles daily between Houston and Dallas. While a safety driver is currently present, Waymo anticipates fully automated trucking could be a reality in the coming years, following successful test runs with no reported issues.

title: Google Settles Consumer Privacy Lawsuit For $85 Million
summary: Google's parent company, Alphabet Inc., has agreed to pay $85 million to resolve a consumer privacy lawsuit filed by the state of Arizona. The lawsuit alleged that Google continued to collect user location data without consent, even after users had opted out of tracking, in violation of the state's Consumer Fraud Act. Google, while agreeing to the settlement without admitting wrongdoing, claimed the lawsuit was based on outdated policies.

title: Fiserv, LG back Korea Credit Data as the SME-focused fintech startup raises another $24.7M
summary: South Korean fintech startup Korea Credit Data (KCD) has raised an additional $24.7 million in Series D extension financing from strategic investors Fiserv and LG Uplus, bringing its total Series D funding to $70 million and its overall funding to $112 million. This latest round values KCD at approximately $776 million. KCD provides digital financial services, including its bookkeeping app Cash Note and SME-focused lending services, to 1.7 million registered merchants in South Korea, aiming to address critical pain points for small and medium-sized enterprises.

title: FuboTV vs. YouTube TV: Which streaming service should you choose?
summary: This article compares FuboTV and YouTube TV, two live TV streaming services, highlighting their pros and cons to help consumers choose. It delves into pricing, channel offerings, sports coverage, simultaneous streams, DVR capabilities, and available add-ons. While YouTube TV is praised for its unlimited DVR and simpler pricing, FuboTV is recommended for sports enthusiasts due to its extensive sports coverage and customization options.

title: Lumen (LUMN) Completes Sale of ILEC Business to Brightspeed
summary: Lumen Technologies (LUMN) has completed the sale of its incumbent local exchange carrier (ILEC) business in 20 states to Brightspeed. This strategic move allows Lumen to focus on its national fiber routes, ILEC operations in 16 other states, and invest further in its Quantum Fiber and Enterprise businesses. The transaction also aims to support its accelerated Quantum Fiber deployment plan and reduce debt.

title: Instagram, TikTok teen addiction lawsuits grouped in northern California
summary: Over 80 lawsuits alleging that social media platforms like TikTok, Instagram, and Facebook intentionally design their services to hook young users, potentially harming their physical and mental health, will be consolidated for pretrial proceedings in a federal court in Oakland, California. The Judicial Panel on Multidistrict Litigation decided on an industrywide MDL, despite objections from some platforms, citing overlapping allegations of "indivisible injuries from multiple products" and common legal defenses. The cases have been assigned to U.S. District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers in the Northern District of California.

title: Bumble cuts annual revenue forecast as Ukraine war, competition bite
summary: Bumble Inc. has reduced its full-year revenue forecast for 2022 due to the impact of the war in Ukraine and intense competition from rivals like Match Group Inc. The company expects revenue between $920 million and $930 million, lower than previous estimates. While the Bumble app saw a surge in paying users, its Badoo app, primarily used in Western Europe, experienced a decline in users after Bumble exited Russia and Belarus.

title: California Gov. Newsom receives $14.9 million, with First Foundation Bank as top donor
summary: California Governor Gavin Newsom has raised $14.9 million for his re-election campaign in the 2022 election cycle, making him the second-highest fundraiser in California state politics. While First Foundation Bank is listed as the top donor, an editor's note clarifies that this amount represents interest payments, not traditional campaign donations. The article details Newsom's top donors and expenditures, and compares his fundraising to other U.S. governors with available data.

title: Sirius XM (SIRI) Introduces Grown Folk Jamz for R&B Fans
summary: Sirius XM has launched "Grown Folk Jamz" on channel 362 as part of its Project Amplify initiative to diversify its programming with R&B, Gospel, Hip-Hop, Smooth Jazz, and Classic Soul music. This move aims to attract new subscribers and enhance content discovery, building on recent successes like increased self-pay subscribers and subscription revenue growth in Q2 2022. Despite these efforts, Sirius XM faces pressures from rising subscriber acquisition and music licensing costs, as well as competition from other streaming services like Apple, Spotify, and Alphabet.

title: State Street Hires New Compliance Chief From Google
summary: State Street Corp. has appointed Yvette Hollingsworth Clark as its new global chief compliance officer. She joins the financial services company from Alphabet Inc.'s Google, where she was the head of compliance for Google’s consumer trust business. State Street believes her experience in risk compliance management and developing anti-money-laundering and compliance frameworks will enhance its compliance functions.

title: Google Cloud and Snap Inc. Expand Partnership to Power Growth and Innovation for Snapchat
summary: Google Cloud and Snap Inc. have expanded their partnership to enhance Snapchat's growth and innovation, leveraging Google's data, analytics, and AI/ML technologies. This collaboration, building on a decade-long relationship, aims to transform the customer experience and deliver more personalized solutions for Snapchat's 347 million daily active users. Snap's use of Google Cloud's data cloud services and Vertex AI will enable it to scale efficiently and continue providing popular experiences while focusing on its core business.

title: Google’s ‘Incognito’ Mode Inspires Staff Jokes — and a Big Lawsuit
summary: Google's "Incognito" mode has become the subject of internal jokes among staff and is now at the center of a significant lawsuit regarding user privacy. An email to Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai from Google's marketing chief highlighted that Incognito mode isn't truly private, leading to "fuzzy, hedging language" in its marketing. This situation underscores the ongoing debate and legal challenges surrounding the privacy claims of the popular browsing feature.

title: Cadence and Google Cloud Collaborating to Advance Chip Design Ecosystem
summary: Cadence Design Systems Inc. and Google Cloud are partnering to enhance system and semiconductor design by making Cadence's cloud-ready tools, part of the Cadence Cloud Passport, available and certified for Google Cloud. This collaboration promises up to a 10X improvement in design and verification throughput, with Cadence tools showing performance gains of up to 25% on Google Cloud C2D instances compared to on-premise infrastructure. Customers like Recogni have already experienced significant benefits, including scalability and accelerated project timelines for their Vision Inference Silicon development.

title: The CEO of OpenTable says there's no such thing as a 'wrong turn' in your career: 'You really don't have to stress out'
summary: Debby Soo, CEO of OpenTable, advises that there are no "wrong turns" in a career, encouraging individuals to see all experiences as additive to their ultimate path. She shares her personal journey, including an initial disappointment with a Google Maps assignment that ultimately provided invaluable skills, and offers three tips: work hard in any position, remain open-minded, and don't over-stress career decisions.

title: If I’d invested £1,000 in Alphabet stock 5 years ago, here’s how much I’d have now
summary: This article analyzes the hypothetical returns of investing £1,000 in Alphabet stock (Class A and Class C) five years ago, comparing its performance to major US indexes. The author, John Choong, details the growth of such an investment and discusses future prospects for Alphabet, despite recent bear market conditions, citing its strong financial health and diverse growth avenues.

title: Privacy Lawsuit Exposes Google Employees Cracking Jokes About Chrome's Incognito Mode
summary: A lawsuit against Google in the Northern District of California reveals internal communications where Google employees joked about the misleading nature of Chrome's Incognito mode. The suit claims Google collected user data despite users believing Incognito mode offered true privacy. Emails and messages from Google Chrome engineers and marketing chief Lorraine Twohill indicate awareness within the company that Incognito mode was not fully private, with one internal proposal suggesting a disclaimer stating, "You are NOT protected from Google."

title: Cadence, Google Cloud team on system, semiconductor design ...
summary: Cadence has partnered with Google Cloud to accelerate system and semiconductor design using cloud-ready tools, resulting in up to a 10x improvement in design and verification throughput. Cadence tools, including the Cloud Passport, have been certified for Google Cloud, with Google silicon teams already utilizing them for developing Tensor SoCs and TPUs. This collaboration aims to provide engineers with enhanced scalability, access to state-of-the-art HPC infrastructure, and faster time to solution.

title: Solving customer problems in real-time with at NetApp Cloud Field Day Exclusive
summary: This article discusses the second part of NetApp's Cloud Field Day Exclusive, focusing on real-world solutions for hybrid and multicloud challenges. It highlights how NetApp addresses customer pain points like skill gaps, complexity, cost optimization, and consistency in diverse cloud environments. The day also included a Q&A session with Google Cloud executives, discussing the partnership and the future of cloud file storage.

title: Lionsgate Signs Worldwide TV Distribution Chief Jim Packer To Long-Term Deal
summary: Lionsgate's President of Worldwide Television Distribution, Jim Packer, has signed a long-term extension to his employment agreement. Under Packer's leadership, Lionsgate has nearly doubled revenues from its film and television library, reaching almost $800M last year. This extension comes as Lionsgate considers spinning off its studio and as its international streaming service, Starzplay, rebrands to Lionsgate+.

title: U.S. Speaker Pelosi's husband sold Nvidia, Micron options at a loss
summary: U.S. House Speaker Nancy Pelosi's husband, Paul Pelosi, sold call options in chipmakers Micron Technology and Nvidia, incurring losses close to $1 million. The transactions were disclosed in a transparency filing, which also showed he exercised call options for Alphabet shares and allowed Walt Disney call options to expire at a loss. This comes amid scrutiny over stock trading by lawmakers and their families, leading to legislative efforts to restrict such activities.

title: Fossil finally puts Wear OS 3 on a smartwatch
summary: Fossil has released its Gen 6 line of smartwatches, including the new Wellness Edition, featuring the updated Snapdragon Wear 4100+ chipset for improved performance and battery life. While initial Gen 6 models launched with Wear OS 2, they received an update to Wear OS 3 in October 2022, and the Wellness Edition launched directly with Wear OS 3, making it the first Fossil watch to do so. The new watches offer enhanced health tracking, including continuous heart rate monitoring, blood oxygen levels, and VO2 Max estimation.

title: More Global Organisations Commit to Ad Net Zero International Roll Out
summary: Global organizations, including PubMatic and Reckitt, are joining the Ad Net Zero Global Group to support the international expansion of this climate action initiative for the advertising industry. This push aims to decarbonize the industry and promote sustainable behaviors, with a key focus on developing a consistent framework for measuring and managing GHG emissions from media. John Osborn has been appointed as the first Ad Net Zero USA Director, emphasizing the critical role the US market will play in these efforts.

title: Battle with Bots Prompts Mass Purge of Amazon, Apple Employee Accounts on LinkedIn
summary: LinkedIn experienced a significant purge of fake employee accounts for companies like Amazon and Apple, with some firms seeing a 33% to 50% drop in listed employees overnight. The purges are believed to be LinkedIn's attempt to combat the proliferation of AI-generated profiles and fraudulent activity, including "pig butchering" scams and identity theft. This event highlights a larger problem of bot accounts and raises questions about the accuracy of LinkedIn's employee statistics and its broader impact on the platform's reliability for hiring and networking.

title: Report: Oracle slows hiring, especially in Bay Area and Seattle, raising fears of deeper cuts
summary: Oracle has reportedly slowed its hiring efforts, particularly in the Bay Area and Seattle, leading to concerns about potential deeper job cuts. Hiring managers have been instructed to delay start dates for new recruits as the company significantly scales back its recruitment activities. This move by Oracle follows similar actions by other tech giants like Microsoft and Google, signaling a potential shift in the tech job market.

title: Enhancing your world with Augmented Reality
summary: This article explores the concept and vast applications of Augmented Reality (AR), distinguishing it from Virtual Reality (VR). It highlights how AR overlays digital information onto the real world, transforming industries from entertainment and retail to healthcare and agriculture. The author emphasizes AR's potential to provide real-time information and enhance human perception, making everyday tasks and specialized fields more interactive and efficient.

title: RAGNAROK ARENA: THE NEWEST RPG MOBILE GAME BY GRAVITY GAME HUB IS COMING SOON
summary: Gravity Game Hub (GGH) PTE.LTD, a subsidiary of GRAVITY Co., Ltd., announced the upcoming release of their new mobile game, Ragnarok Arena, in late Q4 2022. This RPG, strategy, and idle game combines classic Ragnarok Online elements with new features like monster deployment and guild wars, and will be showcased at ESGS 2022. The game will be available across various regions on Apple and Google stores, and players can pre-register soon.

title: Wells Fargo’s New Virtual Assistant, Fargo, to Be Powered by Google Cloud AI
summary: Wells Fargo is launching a new virtual assistant called Fargo, powered by Google Cloud's AI, to enhance customer banking experiences. Fargo will offer personalized financial insights, account servicing, and eventually Spanish language capabilities, aiming to provide convenient and proactive financial guidance. This collaboration with Google Cloud is a key part of Wells Fargo's digital strategy to become digital-first and reinvent personal finance.

title: Wells Fargo taps Google AI on virtual assistant launch
summary: Wells Fargo announced its collaboration with Google Cloud to power its new virtual assistant, Fargo, which will assist customers with tasks like managing debit cards, checking credit limits, and searching transactions. This move is part of Wells Fargo's broader digital transformation strategy. Fargo will use Google's conversational AI platform, Dialogflow, and will eventually offer predictive analytics and support Spanish speakers, aiming to enhance customers' financial well-being.

title: Opinion: This one stock stands out when looking at key data from the FAANG+ group
summary: This article discusses the current landscape for tech-oriented stocks, noting that 2022 has been difficult for the sector. It suggests that smart long-term investors should look for bargains among quality merchandise, particularly within the FAANG group. The piece aims to identify one stock from this group that offers a good combination of low valuation and high growth, despite the challenging market conditions.

title: Spotify profit margins squeezed by slow ad growth; stock sinks
summary: Spotify reported that its third-quarter profit margins were negatively impacted by slow advertising growth, currency fluctuations, and retroactive royalty payments, leading to a 4% slide in its shares. Despite beating user growth forecasts, the company's ad-supported income growth was modest, and its gross margins dropped. Spotify's CEO attributed the issues to broader economic concerns affecting digital advertising, though he expressed long-term optimism.

title: Google Pixel Watch Review: A Break-Out First Effort
summary: The Google Pixel Watch is highlighted as a strong Wear OS smartwatch that integrates well within Google's ecosystem, providing a solid alternative to the Apple Watch for Android users. While praised for its design, screen, and responsive software, it faces challenges with sub-par battery life, a complex setup, and the necessity of a Fitbit Premium membership for some key features. Despite these drawbacks, the review considers it a promising first-generation product for Android users, especially if sleep tracking isn't a primary concern or if a Fitbit Inspire is used concurrently.

title: Google Cloud AI to power Wells Fargo’s new virtual assistant Fargo
summary: Wells Fargo is partnering with Google Cloud to power its new virtual assistant, Fargo, using artificial intelligence to provide a more personalized and intuitive banking experience. The virtual assistant will help customers with various banking tasks, offer proactive financial wellness guidance through predictive analytics, and will be available in Spanish in 2023. This collaboration aims to meet the growing demand for digital banking services and enhance customer financial support.

title: Predictions 2023: AI Will Become An Indispensable, Trusted Enterprise Coworker
summary: In 2023, AI technology is predicted to become an indispensable and trusted enterprise coworker, transforming global markets. Key predictions include TuringBots writing 10% of worldwide code, one in four tech executives reporting to their board on AI governance, and 10% of Fortune 500 enterprises generating content with AI tools. These advancements signify AI's foundational role in successful enterprises and continued investment.

title: Qualcomm Inc. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors
summary: Shares of Qualcomm Inc. (QCOM) fell 2.89% on Thursday, closing at $114.70, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose and the S&P 500 Index fell. This marked the stock's second consecutive day of losses and positioned it below its 52-week high. Qualcomm underperformed some competitors like NVIDIA Corp. and Cisco Systems Inc., though it fared slightly better than Alphabet Inc. Class A.

title: Egg appoints Julie Agnew as Chief Executive Officer
summary: Egg, the clean energy brand launched by Liberty Global Ventures, has appointed Julie Agnew as its new Chief Executive Officer, with Luke Milner taking the role of CFO. Agnew brings extensive experience from the TMT sector, previously as Executive Director of Construction & Delivery at Virgin Media. Egg aims to simplify clean energy solutions for consumers and businesses, offering services like EV charging, solar power, and battery storage.

title: 4 META ETFs With Buy the Dip Opportunity
summary: Following a significant drop in Meta Platforms Inc. (META) stock due to decreased quarterly profits and digital advertising numbers, this article identifies four ETFs presenting a "buy the dip" opportunity. Despite investor concerns over Mark Zuckerberg's metaverse investment, these ETFs hold META with a minimum 10% weight and could rebound. The ETFs highlighted are the Communication Services Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLC), Vanguard Communications Services ETF (VOX), Fidelity MSCI Communication Services Index ETF (FCOM), and Global X Social Media ETF (SOCL).

title: Top 15 Rulings from Tribunals and Commissions] GST; MakeMyTrip, Goibibo, OYO and Google penalized by CCI; Violation of Code of Ethics & Broadcasting Standards by News18; Bombay Dyeing and Wadia Group penalized by SEBI etc.
summary: This article compiles 15 significant rulings from various tribunals and commissions, covering diverse legal areas. Key decisions include GST rulings by the Authority for Advance Ruling, antitrust penalties against MakeMyTrip, Goibibo, OYO, and Google by the CCI, and action against News18 for violating broadcasting standards. SEBI also penalized Bombay Dyeing and the Wadia Group for financial misrepresentation, and a District Consumer Dispute Redressal Commission held Uber India liable for driver negligence.

title: Industry Voice: As Market Dynamics Pivot, Alpha Opportunities Are Set to Improve
summary: The US equity market has seen challenges for active managers due to low volatility, market concentration, and reliance on macro factors and momentum. Historically, however, such extreme market characteristics have often led to improved opportunities for active stock picking and outperformance against passive strategies as these conditions normalize. The article suggests that current market dynamics indicate a potential shift where active management could find more avenues for generating alpha.

title: OpenSSL vulnerability CVE-2022-3602 (Remote Code Execution) and CVE-2022-3786 (Denial of Service) Check Point Research Update
summary: Check Point Research released an update regarding two high-severity OpenSSL vulnerabilities, CVE-2022-3602 (remote code execution) and CVE-2022-3786 (Denial of Service), affecting OpenSSL versions 3.0.0 through 3.0.6. These vulnerabilities, now patched in version 3.0.7, relate to incorrect Punycode processing in X.509 certificates and can lead to stack corruption or remote code execution. Check Point products are not vulnerable, but other tech vendors need to upgrade their systems, and users are advised to stay alert and apply security best practices.

title: Opinion | Elon Musk’s Biggest Problem on Twitter May Be Its Advertisers (Published 2022)
summary: Elon Musk's acquisition of Twitter poses a significant threat to the platform's advertising revenue, which is its primary source of income. Musk's actions, such as amplifying misinformation, have created concern among advertisers about brand safety and reputation. If advertisers flee en masse, it could financially cripple Twitter and endanger its future.

title: Apple now valued at more than Amazon, Alphabet and Meta — combined
summary: Apple's market capitalization has surpassed the combined value of Alphabet, Amazon, and Meta Platforms, demonstrating its strong performance relative to other major technology companies this year. This achievement highlights Apple's dominant position in the tech industry, especially given that its stock has fared better than its peers.

title: Google, IBM, Wells Fargo Join Growing List of Companies Posting Pay for All US Jobs
summary: Google, IBM, and Wells Fargo have joined a growing list of companies that are voluntarily posting salary ranges for all US job listings, even in states where it is not legally mandated. This move comes as New York City and Colorado already require such disclosures, with California and Washington poised to follow suit. The trend reflects a broader shift towards greater pay transparency in the American job market.

title: Regeneron turns to IT to accelerate drug discovery
summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals is leveraging IT, particularly cloud computing and advanced data platforms, to significantly accelerate its drug discovery process. CIO Bob McCowan has spearheaded a major digital transformation, migrating the company's data to a multicloud environment (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and developing a MetaBio Data Discovery Platform, empowering scientists with tools to experiment and test hypotheses more efficiently. This strategic IT investment aims to reduce the drug development timeline from ten years to as few as four or five.

title: Matter is just the first step towards the ideal smart home
summary: The Matter smart home protocol officially launched, representing a significant collaborative effort by 300 technology companies to standardize smart home device interoperability. While it aims for a seamless user experience and a revolution in product-buying, initial adoption will involve a gradual transition focusing on specific device categories like lighting, HVAC, and door locks. Future stages will incorporate more device types, but the protocol still needs to address broader issues like user data security and privacy beyond just device interoperability.

title: EdgeDB raises $15M for open source graph-relational database
summary: EdgeDB, a startup founded in 2019, has secured $15 million in Series A funding for its open-source graph-relational database technology. The platform, which uses PostgreSQL as its foundation and features a query language called EdgeQL, aims to resolve the object-relational impedance mismatch by treating objects as primary entities rather than tables. EdgeDB plans to use the funding to further develop its open-source technology and launch a cloud service in 2023.

title: ‘Matter’ kicks off with a bang in Amsterdam – with Silicon Labs in starring role
summary: The new 'Matter' IoT standard launched in Amsterdam, featuring Silicon Labs in a prominent role and showcasing new smart home solutions. Silicon Labs, aiming to be "the Nvidia of IoT," predicts 40 billion IoT devices by 2030 and significant growth for smart home IoT due to Matter's interoperability. The standard, backed by nearly three hundred companies, aims to simplify smart home experiences for consumers.

title: The Quiet Invasion of 'Big Information'
summary: This article highlights how companies like RELX (Reed Elsevier LexisNexis) and Thomson Reuters operate largely unnoticed, despite collecting vast amounts of personal data and influencing critical life decisions. Unlike the Big Five tech companies, these "information giants" avoid public scrutiny while selling data to various institutions and privatizing public information. The author argues for robust data privacy laws, antitrust enforcement, and public funding for information access to curb their monopolistic power and prevent a digital dystopia.

title: STMicroelectronics and Thales power secure, contactless convenience in Google Pixel 7
summary: STMicroelectronics' ST54K single-chip NFC controller and secure element, combined with Thales' mobile secure operating system, is being used in the new Google Pixel 7 smartphone. This solution provides secure and reliable contactless communication for features like embedded SIM (eSIM), transit ticketing, and digital car keys, meeting high security and performance standards. The partnership allows for enhanced user experiences and robust protection for contactless transactions in Android devices.

title: AMD launches data center CPU 'Genoa', taps Google, Microsoft as customers (Nov. 10)
summary: AMD has launched its fourth-generation EPYC processor, codenamed "Genoa," for data centers, securing major customers like Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, and Oracle. This new chip offers significant performance and energy efficiency improvements over its predecessor, translating into lower operational costs for enterprises and cloud data centers. The launch comes as rival Intel faces delays with its own data center chip, and AMD continues to gain market share, boosting its stock by about 14%.

title: STMicroelectronics NFC chip and Thales OS selected for Google Pixel 7
summary: STMicroelectronics has announced that its ST54K chip, combined with a Thales mobile secure operating system, has been selected for the Google Pixel 7 smartphone. This integration provides control and security for contactless NFC communication. The device uniquely combines NFC control and a certified Android Ready SE in a single chip, optimizing space and enhancing security for contactless transactions.

title: C.H. Robinson Eliminates 650 Jobs
summary: C.H. Robinson, an Eden Prairie-based trucking company, has laid off approximately 650 employees, which is less than 4% of its workforce. The company attributed these job cuts to an anticipated period of slower economic growth and a cooling freight market, moving away from pandemic-induced peaks. The layoffs also come as the company implements new technology and automation to reduce its overall cost structure.

title: Got $5,000? These Are 2 of the Best Growth Stocks to Buy Right Now
summary: This article suggests two growth stocks, Duolingo (DUOL) and The Trade Desk (TTD), as strong long-term investments despite recent market trends favoring value stocks. Duolingo is highlighted for its impressive user growth and expansion beyond language learning, while The Trade Desk is praised for its ability to thrive in a challenging digital advertising market by providing efficient advertising solutions. Both companies are currently trading at discounted prices, presenting potential opportunities for significant long-term gains.

title: Primasun, backed by ResMed and Verily, Launches at HLTH 2022
summary: Primasun, a joint venture between ResMed and Verily, launched at HLTH 2022, offering a clinically-supported solution for complex sleep disorders. The platform aims to reduce the impact of untreated sleep disorders by connecting patients with certified sleep physicians for diagnosis and treatment. This initiative addresses the public health epidemic of insufficient sleep and the scarcity of sleep specialists in the U.S.

title: 3 Reasons Investors Should Look to Emerging Markets
summary: Emerging market stocks, despite a challenging year, are positioned for a potential rebound in 2023. This resurgence is anticipated due to China's likely shift towards pro-growth and stimulus-oriented policies, the expected weakening of the U.S. dollar, and evolving global trade relationships creating new opportunities for EM nations outside of China. Investors are encouraged to reassess their exposure to emerging markets, particularly focusing on China onshore companies, South Korea, Taiwan, and Brazil.

title: Salesforce Accessibility Execs Derek Featherstone, Catherine Nichols Talk Disability Inside And Out Of The Company In New Interview
summary: Salesforce accessibility executives Derek Featherstone and Catherine Nichols discuss the company's commitment to disability inclusion, both internally and externally. They highlight the collaborative efforts within the tech industry to promote accessibility, the role of Salesforce's Office of Accessibility and Abilityforce ERG, and the ethical component embedded in their product development. The executives emphasize that accessibility is a continuous journey and a core value for Salesforce, aiming to create a thriving environment for disabled employees and expanding their user base globally.

title: Google Owner Alphabet Has Too Many Employees, TCI Says
summary: TCI Fund Management Ltd. has urged Alphabet Inc. to aggressively cut expenses and reduce its employee headcount, citing an overgrown workforce and the need for hard choices amidst widespread tech industry job cuts. In a letter to CEO Sundar Pichai, TCI Managing Director Chris Hohn also recommended setting public profit margin targets, increasing share buybacks, and minimizing losses from the company's "Other Bets" portfolio. This comes as the investment firm pushes for greater financial discipline at the Google parent company.

title: Penguin Solutions Launches New Cloud-Native HPC/AI Control Plane and Announces Partnership with Google Cloud
summary: Penguin Solutions launched Scyld Cloud Central, a new cloud-native HPC/AI software offering, and announced a partnership with Google Cloud. This unified solution aims to simplify HPC/AI deployment, streamline user experience, and provide cost management for both on-premises and cloud-based environments. The platform offers rapid deployment, access to Penguin services, elastic scaling, and on-demand access to the latest processing technology, making HPC/AI more accessible and efficient for various users.

title: BlackRock Ups Stake in Penny Stock MULN; Trims Holdings in AAPL and MSFT
summary: BlackRock's latest 13F filing indicates an increased interest in penny stocks like Mullen Automotive (MULN) and Helius Medical Technologies (HSDT), alongside Alphabet (GOOGL/GOOG) and Tesla (TSLA). Concurrently, the firm reduced its holdings in major tech companies: Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), and Amazon (AMZN), though these still remain its largest positions. This reshuffle reflects BlackRock's strategy to balance investments in established tech giants with riskier, high-growth potential penny stocks.

title: Amazon lays off some devices unit staff as it targets 10,000 cuts
summary: Amazon has begun laying off employees in its devices group as part of a broader plan to cut approximately 10,000 jobs across the company, including in retail and human resources. This move comes as Amazon faces an "unusual and uncertain macroeconomic environment" and marks a significant shift for the tech giant, which has historically been known for its rapid job creation. The layoffs reflect a wider trend of dismissals within the technology sector, with other major companies like Meta Platforms and Twitter also announcing substantial job cuts.

title: Stock Split Watch: Is Adobe Next?
summary: This article discusses the possibility of Adobe undertaking a stock split, noting that it hasn't had one in 17 years. While splits don't change company fundamentals, they can increase retail investor interest. The author believes Adobe is a great long-term investment but doesn't anticipate a stock split happening soon, especially given current market conditions and the need for its stock price to rally above $500 to create pressure for a split.

title: Revealed: Just one in four Trump-endorsed candidates using Truth Social actively
summary: A Columbia Journalism Review investigation found that only 25% of candidates endorsed by Donald Trump for Senate, House, and gubernatorial elections actively use Truth Social. Despite this low engagement from even Trump-aligned politicians, the platform remains a significant hub for right-wing extremism and serves as Trump's primary communication channel after his ban from mainstream social media. The article highlights Truth Social's struggles with user attraction, advertising, and internal issues, alongside two federal investigations that jeopardize its future funding.

title: Key facts about Truth Social
summary: This article provides key facts about Truth Social and its users, based on an October 2022 Pew Research Center study. It highlights that while 27% of U.S. adults have heard of Truth Social, only 2% use it for news, and many prominent accounts are right-leaning or pro-Trump. The study also details the methodology used and topics frequently discussed on the platform, such as guns, the Capitol riot, and vaccines.

title: Is DigitalOcean Stock a Buy Now?
summary: DigitalOcean is presented as a cloud infrastructure company targeting small businesses, developers, and startups, distinguishing itself from giants like AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud with competitive pricing. The article highlights DigitalOcean's strong financial performance in Q3, including significant revenue growth and ambitious targets for 2024, despite considerations about stock-based compensation affecting free cash flow. The author concludes that DigitalOcean is a strong buy due to its rapid growth, reasonable stock valuation, and focused niche market.

title: Moody's closing its consulting business in China, laying off staff: Report
summary: Moody's Corp is reportedly shutting down its consulting business, Moody's Analytics, in China and laying off over 100 employees across its Beijing, Shanghai, and Shenzhen offices. This move comes as China's zero-Covid policy and subsequent lockdowns are causing many multinational firms to reconsider their operations in the country. However, Moody's credit rating business will continue to operate in China.

title: Opinion | How One of the Country’s Most Storied C.E.O.s Destroyed His Legacy (Published 2022)
summary: The article discusses Jack Welch's regret over his choice of successor at General Electric, highlighting his belief that he prioritized charm over competence. Author William D. Cohan explores Welch's complex legacy, acknowledging his leadership while also criticizing his succession process. The piece delves into the debate surrounding Welch's impact on GE, questioning whether his focus on short-term profits ultimately harmed the company.

title: A Guide to the Creator Economy
summary: The creator economy, where independent creators monetize their audiences through various channels, is experiencing a significant blurring of lines with traditional entrepreneurship. This guide explores its history, current landscape, and how creators are building platform-agnostic brands and diversifying income streams by focusing on owned channels and direct audience relationships. It also provides best practices for creators looking to transition into entrepreneurship, emphasizing building the right tech stack, developing meaningful partnerships, and prioritizing audiences over advertisers.

title: Tech forward: Beauty A.I. has inherent racial bias—but it doesn't have to
summary: Joy Buolamwini's experience at MIT revealed inherent racial bias in AI facial recognition, performing poorly on darker skin. Her research, echoed by Dr. Ellis Monk, links this to non-diverse training data and colorism in society. Google's partnership with Dr. Monk to implement the inclusive Monk Skin Tone Scale and Perfect Corp.'s proactive approach show how the industry is addressing and working to correct these biases for more inclusive AI.

title: Veritone buys Vision Semantics, gets Deloitte nod for growth
summary: Veritone has acquired Vision Semantics, a video recognition company, and released Tracker, a tool that uses AI algorithms to track objects without relying on personal biometric identification. The new software addresses privacy concerns by not using facial recognition. Additionally, Veritone was recognized on Deloitte's Fast 500 list for its rapid revenue growth, underscoring its success in providing AI services and solutions.

title: NASDAQ Trading - How to invest in NASDAQ (US100) index?
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide on investing in the NASDAQ (US100) index, detailing its structure, how it works, and reasons for its high volatility. It explains different trading positions (long and short) and identifies opportune times for trading, such as during periods of market panic or significant company announcements. The article also touches upon the use of CFDs and ETFs for investing in NASDAQ.

title: What does Google’s ₹2,274 crore fine mean for Big Tech?
summary: The article discusses the implications of the Competition Commission of India (CCI) fining Google ₹2,274 crore for anti-competitive practices, specifically regarding its Play Store and payment systems. It argues that such fines globally could signal the end of the unchecked dominance of major tech companies like Google, Apple, and Amazon, forcing them to re-evaluate their impact on innovation and society. The author suggests these regulations might ultimately save these companies from their own harmful habits.

title: Report: Chinese-made drones in DC skies, cause for alarm
summary: A POLITICO report has raised concerns about Chinese-made drones flying over Washington D.C., with over 100 incursions detected in a 45-day period. Lawmakers and experts fear these drones, even recreational ones, could be used by China for spying, given the potential for data access. The issue highlights broader concerns about Chinese technological threats, including apps like TikTok and land purchases near military installations.

title: Employers Rethink Need for College Degrees in Tight Labor Market
summary: A tight labor market is leading more employers, including Google, Delta Air Lines, and IBM, to reduce college degree requirements for certain positions, focusing instead on skills and experience. This trend has resulted in a decline in job postings requiring bachelor's degrees compared to pre-pandemic levels. The shift is influenced by high demand for workers, low unemployment, and efforts to address racial disparities in the workplace.

title: "Twelve Days of Christmas" - 12 companies for the Santa Claus Rally
summary: This article discusses the "Santa Claus Rally," a stock market phenomenon typically occurring in late December and early January, where the S&P 500 historically sees an average rise of 1.3%. It examines past rallies, their occurrence during bear markets, and potential reasons for the phenomenon, such as tax optimization and investor sentiment. The article then highlights 12 companies, including Amazon, Alphabet, and NVIDIA, that could benefit from this rally and provide investment opportunities based on their current valuations and market positions.

title: PR News | On the Move: McCulloch to Head Comms at PG&E - Mon., Nov. 28, 2022
summary: David McCulloch has been appointed vice president and chief marketing and communications officer at Pacific Gas and Electric Company (PG&E). McCulloch previously led communications for decarbonization efforts at X, the moonshot factory. The article also notes Debbie Serot's appointment as Chief Marketing and Revenue Officer at SWBC and Wendy Beswick's new role as Chief Marketing Officer at Elevations Credit Union.

title: Alphabet Shareholders | Who Owns The Most Shares of Alphabet?
summary: This article examines the ownership structure of Alphabet (GOOGL), detailing its largest shareholders among institutional investors and company insiders. It highlights that co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin control a significant portion of voting power through their Class B shares, while institutions like The Vanguard Group, The Capital Group, and State Street are major holders. The piece also lists top insider shareholders, excluding the founders.

title: Android users in 12 US states cleared to sue Google Play
summary: A California judge has approved a class-action lawsuit against Google regarding alleged anticompetitive practices in its Play Store. The suit, involving Android users in 12 US states and territories who made app or in-app purchases since August 2016, claims Google maintains a monopoly and charges a 30 percent commission, passing inflated costs onto consumers. This case echoes previous antitrust litigation against both Google and Apple, with the same law firm, Hagens Berman, involved, suggesting significant financial implications for Google.

title: No, you can’t buy a self-driving car yet, but a lot of people seem to think you can
summary: Despite widespread misconceptions, fully self-driving cars (Level 3-5 autonomy) are not yet available for purchase in Canada or the U.S. Many consumers mistakenly believe that current driver-assist technologies, like Tesla's Autopilot or GM's Super Cruise (which are Level 2), provide full self-driving capabilities, leading to dangerous misuse. Experts emphasize that drivers must remain attentive as these systems are only aids and not replacements for human operators, and more advanced systems are still in limited testing or decades away.

title: Google Sued in Louisiana for Allegedly Selling Biometric Data
summary: Google is being sued in Louisiana for allegedly capturing and selling residents' biometric data without consent, violating the state's consumer protection and privacy law. This class-action lawsuit is similar to a previous case in Illinois that Google settled for $100 million. The plaintiffs argue that Google's awareness of such issues in other states strengthens their claim in Louisiana.

title: 7 Quantum Computing Stocks to Buy for the Next 10 Years
summary: This article highlights seven quantum computing stocks poised for significant growth over the next decade, as quantum computing transitions from theory to reality and disrupts various industries. It details how major tech companies like Alphabet, IBM, and Microsoft are leading the charge, alongside smaller players like Quantum Computing and Chinese giants Alibaba and Baidu, as well as Intel in hardware development. The author emphasizes the transformative power of quantum computing and the potential for these companies to be big winners.

title: 40+ Spotify statistics 2026: SPOT stock, revenue and performance
summary: This article compiles over 40 statistics about Spotify (NYSE: SPOT) as of late 2022, detailing its market dominance, user growth, financial performance, and stock behavior. It highlights Spotify's leading market share in music streaming, its revenue model primarily driven by premium subscriptions, and its significant user base despite competition from tech giants. The report also touches upon Spotify's stock performance, including its all-time high and low, alongside insights into artist earnings and global user demographics.

title: MercadoLibre files complaints against Apple for 'anti-competitive practices'
summary: MercadoLibre has filed antitrust complaints against Apple in Brazil and Mexico, accusing the tech giant of anti-competitive practices related to its app store. The e-commerce company alleges that Apple abuses its monopoly by imposing restrictions on the distribution of digital goods and in-app purchases, including requiring the use of Apple's own payment system and preventing redirection to third-party websites. MercadoLibre attempted to negotiate with Apple but ultimately decided to pursue legal action, while also noting that Google's app store policies are less restrictive.

title: 2022 Year in Review: Music Industry
summary: The music industry experienced significant growth in 2022, primarily driven by a strong return to live music events and continued robust performance in music streaming. Online engagement with music content increased by 51% compared to 2019, while concert-related content saw a 27% rise in engagement from 2021. Artists from various global regions, particularly Korean and Spanish-language artists, made a significant impact on social media and streaming platforms, with Taylor Swift also breaking streaming records.

title: Pentagon Divides Big Cloud-Computing Deal Among 4 Firms (Published 2022)
summary: The Pentagon awarded lucrative cloud-computing contracts, potentially valued at up to $9 billion, to Amazon, Google, Microsoft, and Oracle. This decision marks a significant shift from an earlier attempt to award a sole contract to Microsoft, which was later scrapped after legal challenges and a recognition that the department's needs had evolved. The new Joint Warfighting Cloud Capability project aims to provide comprehensive cloud services across all security domains and classification levels for the Defense Department.

title: Google, Oracle, Amazon, Microsoft to share $9 billion ‘return of JEDI’ defense contract
summary: Google, Oracle, Amazon, and Microsoft will jointly share a $9 billion hybrid contract from the Pentagon to provide secure cloud services globally. The contract, known as Joint Warfighting Cloud Capability, is scheduled for completion by June 8, 2028, with work to be performed in Reston, Va. This agreement follows previous discussions and changes regarding the Department of Defense's cloud computing needs.

title: Pentagon splits $9 billion cloud contract among Google, Amazon, Oracle and Microsoft
summary: The Pentagon awarded $9 billion worth of cloud computing contracts to Google, Amazon Web Services, Microsoft, and Oracle. These Joint Warfighting Cloud Capability (JWCC) contracts will provide enterprise-wide cloud services across all security domains and classification levels until 2028. This move signifies the military's shift towards utilizing multiple vendors for its cloud computing needs, learning from past litigation that halted a single-vendor IT modernization project.

title: Pentagon awards multibillion-dollar cloud contract to Amazon, Google, Microsoft and Oracle
summary: The Pentagon has awarded a multibillion-dollar cloud computing contract, Joint Warfighting Cloud Capability (JWCC), to Amazon, Google, Microsoft, and Oracle. This move diverges from a previous single-vendor approach (JEDI contract) that faced legal challenges from Amazon alleging interference by former President Trump. The new multi-vendor contract, worth up to $9 billion, aims to provide resilient and cost-effective cloud services for the military, enabling access to sensitive data globally.

title: Best Company Culture 2022
summary: Comparably announced its 6th Annual Best Places to Work series, recognizing companies with the best cultures as rated by their own employees. The rankings are based on millions of anonymous employee ratings across nearly 20 workplace metrics, covering compensation, work-life balance, leadership, and career growth. The report differentiates between Top 100 Large companies (over 500 employees) and Top 100 Small/Mid-size companies (500 or fewer employees), with detailed methodology provided for the selection process.

title: UnitedHealth names Google exec Kirsten Gil to board of directors
summary: UnitedHealth Group has appointed Google executive Kirsten Gil to its Board of Directors. Gil, a long-time leader at Alphabet (Google’s parent company), brings extensive experience in business operations, strategy, and finance across various Google departments, including search, maps, AI, and sustainability. Her appointment is expected to strengthen UnitedHealth's board with diverse tech and business expertise.

title: AI-generated content helping redefine industry
summary: Chinese tech companies are significantly investing in the artificial intelligence-generated content (AIGC) sector, driven by the success of models like ChatGPT. Experts believe AIGC will revolutionize content creation by assisting humans and eventually generating content independently, with diverse applications in industries from finance to education. Companies like Baidu, ByteDance, and Kuaishou are already deploying AI-powered tools, signaling a major shift in content production and innovation.

title: 5 Charts on Big Tech Stocks’ Collapse
summary: In 2022, Big Tech stocks collectively lost nearly $4 trillion in market value, with the Morningstar US Technology Index down 28.7%. This collapse significantly impacted overall market returns, contributing 7 percentage points to the U.S. market index's 18.7% loss. Factors like recession fears, advertising slowdowns, and changes in consumer spending led to substantial declines for companies such as Meta Platforms, Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Nvidia.

title: The Robots Are Coming! 7 Stocks to Buy Before They Boom.
summary: This article identifies seven stocks to buy in the robotics sector, highlighting the significant growth potential of both industrial and medical robotics markets. It provides an overview of companies like Trimble, Alphabet, ABB, Rockwell Automation, Intuitive Surgical, Fanuc, and Knightscope, detailing their contributions to automation and robotics, along with their financial standing and market performance. The author suggests these stocks offer opportunities for investors to capitalize on the increasing integration of robots across various industries despite current market volatility.

title: Want to Attract Top Tech Talent? Prioritize Their Needs
summary: This article emphasizes that attracting and retaining top tech talent requires more than just competitive pay; companies must prioritize technologists' needs for growth, modern tools, and an inclusive work environment. It suggests strategies like clear communication during recruiting, investing in up-to-date technologies, and fostering a culture of acceptance and work-life quality. Ultimately, understanding and acting on these priorities will help businesses thrive in the competitive tech talent market.

title: TikTok data collection, ties to China spur bans
summary: Concerns about TikTok's data collection practices and its connections to China have led to bans of the app on government devices in the U.S. The Senate passed a bill prohibiting TikTok on federal devices, and several states and federal agencies have implemented similar bans. Lawmakers and agencies are worried about the Chinese government's potential access to U.S. user data, despite TikTok's efforts to store data on U.S. platforms.

title: The 7 Hottest Augmented Reality Stocks to Own for 2023 and Beyond
summary: This article highlights seven augmented reality stocks poised for growth in 2023 and beyond, given the AR market's projected expansion to nearly $600 billion by 2030. It covers established tech giants like Alphabet leveraging AR for practical applications, specialized component providers like Himax Technologies, and pure-play AR ventures, while also acknowledging the inherent risks and volatility in this developing sector. Each stock profile details its AR relevance, financial standing, and potential for investors.

title: 7 Long-Term Stocks to Buy to Bet on Nuclear Fusion
summary: This article highlights seven long-term stocks that could benefit from advancements in nuclear fusion technology following a major breakthrough announced by scientists. The selected companies, including tech giants, energy firms, and even a food manufacturer, are either directly investing in fusion startups or stand to gain indirectly from a future with abundant, clean energy. The author emphasizes that while the technology is still in its early stages, these companies offer interesting investment opportunities for patient investors.

title: Purpose pushes through with yield-focused single-stock ETFs
summary: Purpose Investments has launched its initial suite of Yield Shares ETFs, which are single-stock, yield-focused ETFs. These world-first ETFs allow Canadian investors to gain exposure to individual stocks like Tesla and Apple while receiving enhanced monthly distributions through a covered-call options strategy and moderate leverage. The ETFs also address an advisor pain point by simplifying the process of generating augmented income from individual stocks and offer Canadian dollar-denominated exposure to U.S. stocks with currency hedging.

title: Where Are Stocks Looking Cheap or Expensive?
summary: After a significant valuation reset in 2022, Morningstar analysts believe the stock market is finishing the year at its most undervalued level since March 2020. Small-cap and large-cap growth stocks, as well as sectors like communication services and consumer cyclicals, appear significantly undervalued, while utilities, oil and gas, and waste management remain relatively overvalued. This analysis is based on price/fair value ratios, with US stocks trading at an average 15% discount as of December 19, 2022.

title: Open-source hardware: a growing movement to democratize IC design
summary: The University of Michigan is a leader in a growing movement to democratize integrated circuit (IC) design through open-source hardware, making chip design more accessible and affordable. This initiative, championed by researchers like Dr. Mehdi Saligane and supported by Google and NIST, provides open-source design kits and tools, enabling cost-free fabrication for developers and fostering innovation in regions underrepresented in chip design. The movement aims to bring the collaborative and iterative mindset of open-source software to hardware design, exemplified by projects like openFASoC and the OpenROAD initiative, revolutionizing the semiconductor industry.

title: Indoor Location Application Platforms Market is Booming Worldwide | Microsoft, Juniper Networks, Google, Infsoft
summary: A new study by AMA Research evaluates the Global Indoor Location Application Platforms Market, forecasting its trends and market size up to 2027. Key players include Microsoft, Juniper Networks, and Google, among others. The market is driven by increasing applications across beacon and BLE tags, and the inefficiency of GPS indoors, with opportunities in RFID tags and demand for contact-tracing solutions.

title: Collaboration helps Hormel Foods leverage supply chain and sales data
summary: Hormel Foods has partnered with Crisp, an open-data retail platform, to improve its access to real-time in-store inventory and supply chain data. This collaboration allows Hormel to streamline data integration into Google's data cloud, providing faster insights for its retail partners and informing strategic decisions. The company aims to enhance customer experience, reduce food waste, and optimize marketing and new product development efforts through better data analytics.

title: Garmin Ltd. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
summary: Garmin Ltd. (GRMN) stock saw a 0.57% increase to $91.78, outperforming the mixed market on Tuesday. This marks its second consecutive day of gains, despite being significantly below its 52-week high. The company's stock also outperformed key competitors like Apple Inc. and Alphabet Inc.

title: Twitter Goes Down For Many After Upgrade, Elon Musk Says 'Works For Me'
summary: Many Twitter users experienced outages, particularly on desktop, after CEO Elon Musk announced "significant backend server architecture changes" to make the site faster. While many complained about not being able to tweet or encountering error messages, Musk responded to queries about the outage by stating, "works for me." Downdetector showed a spike in complaints, which have since largely subsided.

title: Why the Rich Have Become Richer
