summary: This article explores the reasons behind increasing wealth disparity within advanced economies, focusing on the U.S. It highlights that both earned income (due to higher wages for elite employees) and unearned income (from stock market gains) have contributed to the rich becoming richer, particularly noting the significant impact of stock ownership and dividend reinvestment for higher-income households. The author emphasizes that equity returns have substantially outpaced wage growth over the long term.

title: How the DeepMind Montreal Team is Contributing to Solving Intelligence
summary: Shibl Mourad, Research Engineering Director at DeepMind, highlights the Montreal team's significant contributions to AI research over its first five years. Established in October 2017 in partnership with Doina Precup, DeepMind Montreal has published over 45 scientific papers and attracted world-class talent, reversing a "brain drain" and strengthening Montreal's position as a global AI hub. The team remains committed to advancing science and developing AI safely and responsibly.

title: A Quantum Leap: 1st retail quantum computer now on sale
summary: The article announces that the first affordable retail quantum computer, manufactured by Shenzhen SpinQ Technology Co., Ltd., is now on sale. Priced around $5,000 and weighing 14 kg, these 2 and 3-qubit desktop NMR quantum computers (Gemini, Gemini Mini, Triangulum) are primarily for educational purposes and simple scientific research, not complex problem-solving. The author emphasizes the immense potential of quantum computing to revolutionize technology, communication, and human civilization, despite its current rudimentary stage, comparing it to the early days of classical computers.

title: What is TSMC?
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) is the world's largest semiconductor manufacturer, producing chips for major tech companies like Apple, Amazon, and Google. It focuses solely on manufacturing for third parties, unlike competitors, and boasts significant market dominance and advanced process technology. The article also discusses the potential impact of a Chinese invasion of Taiwan on TSMC and its global operations, as well as the company's expansion plans in the US.

title: Apple’s market value drops below $2 trillion
summary: Apple Inc.'s market capitalization fell below $2 trillion for the first time since March 2021, driven by concerns over high inflation and a slowing global economy impacting demand for its products. This significant drop follows a year after the company briefly reached a $3 trillion valuation. Analysts have downgraded Apple due to anticipated supply chain issues and reduced consumer spending on high-end phones, forecasting a potential revenue decline.

title: Korean webtoon ‘Lookism’ creator ‘The Grim Entertainment’ attracts $9.1 million investment
summary: The Grim Entertainment, known for popular webtoons like "Lookism," has secured $9.1 million in investment from entities including Korea Investment Partners and Naver Webtoon. The company, founded by writer Park Tae-joon, plans to expand its webtoon ecosystem through collaborations, nurturing new writers, and advancing into secondary creation markets such as animations, movies, and dramas using its intellectual property. Its enterprise value is estimated at over $81 million.

title: Sony Honda Mobility New Brand AFEELA Announced Prototype Unveiled at CES® 2023
summary: Sony Honda Mobility (SHM) announced its new brand "AFEELA" and unveiled a prototype as a new type of mobility at CES 2023. The company aims to become a Mobility Tech Company

title: The Power of 7: Tech that will disrupt 2023
summary: This article identifies seven key technology trends poised for significant disruption in 2023. These include advancements in Unsupervised Machine Learning, Robotic Process Automation, expanded applications of Virtual and Augmented Reality, the increasing sophistication of AI Chatbots, widespread adoption of 5G, the growth of the Internet of Things, and the emergence of AI-driven programming. The author highlights how these innovations will impact various industries, from healthcare and e-commerce to manufacturing, and discusses their potential effects on global GDP and job markets.

title: Baidu Research Unveils Top 10 Tech Trends for 2023
summary: Baidu Research has released its predictions for the top 10 technology trends of 2023, encompassing areas like big models, digital-real convergence, autonomous driving, and sustainability. These trends highlight the increasing integration of AI into various sectors and its role in addressing global challenges. Baidu will showcase innovations related to these trends at its annual AI developer conference, Baidu Create, on January 10, 2023.

title: PurpleUrchin Bypasses CAPTCHA and Steals Cloud Platform Resources
summary: Unit 42 researchers at Palo Alto Networks have identified a "freejacking" campaign called PurpleUrchin, orchestrated by the threat actor group Automated Libra. This group exploits free or limited-time cloud resources, leveraging DevOps automation and CAPTCHA bypass techniques to conduct cryptocurrency mining operations. They also employ a "Play and Run" tactic, creating fake accounts with fraudulent credit card information to incur unpaid balances on cloud service platforms, having created over 130,000 such accounts.

title: You might actually want a TiVo TV someday
summary: Xperi, the company that owns the TiVo brand, is launching its own TiVo OS smart TV platform in Europe in the first half of 2023, with U.S. availability planned for the future. The platform focuses on content discovery over apps, offering recommendations across various streaming services and an intuitive voice search. While it currently lacks a robust app store compared to competitors, its neutral approach and potential for ad revenue sharing aim to attract TV manufacturers.

title: 10 Data Center Companies You Need To Watch In 2023
summary: The data center market is experiencing significant growth driven by digital transformation, IoT, 5G, and streaming content, with hyperscalers accounting for a large portion of this expansion. This article highlights ten key data center companies—including major players like AWS and Equinix, and rapidly growing firms like Aligned Data Centers and Evocative—that are making significant investments in new construction, acquisitions, and technological advancements to meet the escalating global demand for capacity and enhance their service offerings. These companies are adapting to market changes, such as leadership transitions and strategic partnerships, to expand their footprints and provide advanced infrastructure solutions worldwide.

title: Baidu Create 2022 Outlines New Strategy for AI Development Based on Feedback-Driven Innovation
summary: Baidu's annual developer conference, Baidu Create 2022, outlined a new strategy for AI development focused on feedback-driven innovation, emphasizing deep learning as the cornerstone for technological revolution. The company introduced key advancements in AIGC technologies, intelligent transportation with UniBEV, and efficiency in metaverse space construction using XiRang MetaStack. Baidu also detailed its QIAN strategy for quantum computing and new computing architectures, alongside a commitment to train millions of AI talents to foster a stronger AI ecosystem.

title: BCI es la empresa con mejor reputación de Chile
summary: BCI has been recognized as the company with the best reputation in Chile for the eighth consecutive year, according to the Merco Chile 2022 ranking. Banco de Chile maintained its second position, while Mercado Libre rose to third place. Adicionalmente, Andrónico Luksic of Grupo Luksic was named the business leader with the best reputation for the fourth consecutive year.

title: Trump Facebook ban: Will the social media platform reinstate former president after two-year ban?
summary: Meta is deliberating whether to reinstate former President Donald Trump's access to Facebook and Instagram after a two-year ban following the January 6th Capitol attack. This decision is seen as highly consequential for online speech and could impact the 2024 US presidential election. While some argue for his return with strict rules, others believe his past actions warrant continued exclusion to prevent further harm.

title: Skagen Falster Gen 6's long-awaited Wear OS 3 update is here
summary: The Skagen Falster Gen 6 smartwatch is finally receiving its Wear OS 3 update, following Fossil's Gen 6 series which updated in October 2022. This update brings a stock Wear OS experience, pre-installed apps like Alexa and Spotify, but notably still lacks Google Assistant, which remains exclusive to Pixel Watch and Samsung Galaxy Watch models. Users should be aware that the update will factory reset their device, requiring a fresh setup via the Skagen Smartwatches app.

title: Google, Nvidia Express Concerns to FTC About Microsoft’s Activision Deal
summary: Google and Nvidia have joined Sony in expressing concerns to the Federal Trade Commission regarding Microsoft's proposed $69 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard. These concerns further support the FTC's lawsuit to block the deal, citing potential harm to competition in the video-game industry. The FTC has scheduled an in-house trial for August to address these issues.

title: Oracle jumps into payments for retailers
summary: Oracle has expanded its services for retailers to include in-store payment acceptance, offering tap-to-pay capabilities for various payment methods like debit, credit, Apple, Google, and Samsung digital wallets. This new feature, which became available in December, will be sold at a fixed fee without long-term contracts or minimum monthly payment volumes. Oracle aims to provide a more stable and cost-effective "payment to processing" solution, integrating it with their existing hardware and cloud-based software for inventory and staff management.

title: Families joined in lawsuit targeting social media as addictive, harmful to teens
summary: Over 100 families have joined a consolidated lawsuit against major social media companies like Instagram, TikTok, and Snapchat, alleging their platforms are addictively designed and have caused significant mental health harm to teenagers. The lawsuit, being handled in the Northern District of California, seeks to hold companies accountable for creating products that lead to anxiety, depression, and suicidal thoughts in young users. The legal action is unfolding amid increasing scrutiny on social media's impact on youth, with President Biden also calling for accountability for "Big Tech."

title: Getty Images is suing the creators of AI art tool Stable Diffusion for scraping its content
summary: Getty Images has initiated legal proceedings against Stability AI, creators of the popular AI art tool Stable Diffusion, alleging copyright infringement. The stock photo company claims Stability AI "unlawfully copied and processed millions of images protected by copyright" from its site to train its software without permission or compensation. This lawsuit marks a significant escalation in the ongoing legal battles concerning generative AI and intellectual property rights, as content creators seek clarity and compensation for their work used in AI training datasets.

title: Author: Bhagya Ayyavoo
summary: This page serves as an archive for articles written by Bhagya Ayyavoo for Sify.com. It highlights her role as an author and host for a podcast discussing "Toxic Leadership at workplace…" from January 18, 2023. The podcast features an all-women panel examining toxic management styles and whether women contribute to workplace toxicity.

title: Getty Images suing the makers of popular AI art tool for allegedly stealing photos
summary: Getty Images has filed a lawsuit against Stability AI, the creator of the AI art tool Stable Diffusion, alleging copyright infringement. The stock image company claims Stability AI copied and processed millions of its images without obtaining proper licenses. This action highlights the growing tension between traditional media suppliers and AI art generators, as artists become concerned about their work being used for AI training without credit.

title: AI Time: 10 Ways Artificial Intelligence is Getting Real
summary: A new report from Citi Global Insights highlights 10 areas of innovation in Artificial Intelligence, a market that has grown significantly at a 35% CAGR since 2015. The article discusses key AI technologies such as Large Language Models, AI Art, Quantum for AI, Edge AI, and Explainable AI, detailing their market potential and impact. It also features insights from leading AI experts on the future trajectory of AI development.

title: Best 12 Freelancing Sites in India
summary: The article lists and describes the top 12 freelancing websites available to workers in India, recognizing the significant increase in freelancers and the expanding gig economy. It highlights various platforms, noting whether they are national or internationally recognized, and details their unique features, typical job offerings, and how freelancers can engage with them. The platforms cater to a wide range of skills and experience levels, from beginners to specialized professionals.

title: Here are the layoff severance packages Google, Microsoft, Amazon and other tech giants have promised
summary: As major tech companies like Google, Microsoft, and Amazon announce significant layoffs, this article compares the severance packages offered by these and other tech giants like Meta, Salesforce, and Twitter. The packages vary widely, from Google's 16-week base severance plus additional weeks per year of service to Amazon's 60-day full pay period followed by several weeks of severance. The article highlights the differing approaches to severance, health benefits, and stock vesting amidst an uncertain job market for laid-off tech workers.

title: Amazon to Plow Extra $35 Billion Into Virginia Data Centers in Cloud Race
summary: Amazon's cloud unit, Amazon Web Services (AWS), plans to invest an additional $35 billion in new data centers in Virginia by 2040. This move aims to reinforce its leading position against competitors like Microsoft and Alphabet. The expansion is expected to create 1,000 jobs in the state, which is a crucial hub for AWS operations.

title: Stripe becomes a strategic payments partner for Amazon
summary: Amazon is significantly broadening its use of Stripe's payment processing platform, making Stripe a strategic payments partner across various Amazon business units in the US, Europe, and Canada. In return, Stripe will expand its utilization of Amazon Web Services (AWS), focusing on AWS Graviton chips and Nitro Enclaves for enhanced performance and security. This expanded partnership builds on an existing relationship, with Stripe set to handle a substantial portion of Amazon's transaction volume.

title: Spotify to slash 6% of workforce, adding to tech layoffs glut | Daily Sabah
summary: Spotify announced it will lay off 6% of its 10,000 employees, amounting to about 600 people, as the music streaming giant prepares for a possible recession and aligns costs with revenue growth. CEO Daniel Ek admitted he was "too ambitious in investing ahead of our revenue growth." This move follows similar layoffs across the tech sector, including Google, Meta, Amazon, and Microsoft, as companies face economic headwinds and a downturn in demand after pandemic-driven growth.

title: Oracle Corp. stock rises Tuesday, outperforms market
summary: Oracle Corp. (ORCL) shares rose 0.82% to $89.70 on Tuesday, outperforming the broader market which saw mixed trading with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising and the S&P 500 falling. The stock achieved a new 52-week high and outperformed competitors like Microsoft and Alphabet. Trading volume was slightly below its 50-day average.

title: Scripps Networks Get Carriage on Google, YouTube Platforms | Next TV | Broadcasting+Cable
summary: The E.W. Scripps Co. has secured a distribution deal with Google, bringing its national networks like Scripps News, Ion, and Bounce to YouTube TV. This agreement also includes the future launch of Scripps' FAST channels on YouTube FAST and broader distribution of its subscription service Brown Sugar on Google TV. This move is significant for Scripps' multiplatform strategy, expanding its reach into growing virtual MVPD and free ad-supported streaming markets.

title: WeRide, Baidu Lead More Self-Driving Breakthroughs in China
summary: China is rapidly advancing its autonomous transport capabilities, with significant developments announced across three major cities. WeRide has become the first company to receive a permit to test a Level 4 autonomous bus in Beijing, while Baidu's Apollo Go ride-hailing service is now authorized to charge fares in Shenzhen and is assisting Fuzhou in developing an autonomous vehicle testing area. These initiatives highlight China's commitment to becoming a leader in self-driving technology.

title: How Quantum Computing Will Transform Our World
summary: Quantum computing, while still developing, promises to revolutionize various fields by processing information at an exponentially faster rate than traditional computers. Companies like IBM are leading the charge, developing advanced quantum processors and exploring applications in areas like finance, materials science, and AI. However, this transformative technology also presents significant national security risks, particularly concerning the obsolescence of current cryptography, prompting a global race to develop quantum-safe security measures.

title: IBM, SAP latest to slash workforce
summary: IBM announced it would cut 1.5% of its global workforce, approximately 3,900 employees, despite beating revenue expectations, attributing the reductions to previous business unit spinoffs rather than current performance. This move follows similar workforce reductions by other major tech companies like Microsoft, Google, and Salesforce, as the sector navigates economic challenges. European software firm SAP also revealed plans to cut 3,000 jobs globally due to declining profits, aiming to refocus on strategic growth.

title: Is Augmented Reality safe?
summary: Augmented Reality (AR) blends digital information with the real world, raising significant safety concerns despite its benefits. The article highlights potential dangers in privacy, security, and mental health, including data collection, cyber-attacks, and the addictive nature of AR. It emphasizes the need for careful consideration and control as AR technology becomes more integrated into daily life.

title: Licking County becomes the 'bullseye' for development one year after Intel
summary: One year after Intel announced its plans for a $20 billion computer chip manufacturing facility in Licking County, Ohio, the area has become a hub for development. This growth has led to escalating property values and changes in the rural landscape, causing some residents to relocate. While the Intel project promises significant economic benefits and job creation, local officials are also grappling with challenges like funding disparities and utility expansion conflicts.

title: Google continued to ramp up federal lobbying spending before DOJ filed second antitrust lawsuit
summary: Google's parent company, Alphabet, significantly increased its federal lobbying expenditures, spending over $13 million in 2022, prior to the Department of Justice filing its second antitrust lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges Google monopolized the digital advertising technology sector by acquiring rivals and manipulating the auction process to stifle competition. Despite these lobbying efforts and substantial political contributions, particularly to Democratic candidates, Alphabet also announced mass layoffs of 12,000 employees in January 2023.

title: Big tech layoffs may ease software engineer shortage
summary: Recent layoffs in the big tech sector, totaling over 228,000 workers since early 2022, are creating a significant influx of software engineers into the job market. This situation presents an opportunity for smaller enterprises to recruit talented individuals, though challenges such as cultural fit and visa costs still exist. Experts suggest that smaller companies can attract talent by offering benefits beyond compensation, including flexibility and the chance for employees to make a greater impact.

title: AI - opportunity or threat?
summary: The AI revolution, particularly with the advent of DALL-E and ChatGPT, presents both significant opportunities and threats to tech giants and various industries. Companies like Microsoft are heavily investing in AI, while others like Google and social media platforms face challenges to their business models. The article details how AI impacts image design, web development platforms like Wix, the freelancing market through platforms like Fiverr, and even the gaming industry, forcing companies to adapt or risk disruption.

title: AV for startups and SMEs
summary: This article discusses the importance of effective audio-visual (AV) solutions for startups and SMEs in today's hybrid work environment. It highlights how accessible unified communications and conferencing (UCC) technologies have become, emphasizing simplicity, ubiquity, and flexibility through options like "bring your own device" (BYOD) and platform standardization. The piece also covers advanced AV applications such as meeting equity, digital signage, cloud telephony, and the future role of AR/VR and the Metaverse in business communication.

title: 10 Companies Who Inspire Creative Team Collaboration
summary: This article highlights ten companies that excel at fostering creative team collaboration. It explains how these businesses, ranging from tech giants like Google and Amazon to entertainment powerhouses like Disney and Blizzard Entertainment, prioritize open communication, diverse perspectives, and inclusive environments to drive innovation and achieve shared goals. The article emphasizes that creative collaboration is crucial for developing new ideas and products, leading to a sense of shared ownership and motivation among team members.

title: Social Media Lawsuits I Tech Giants Sued for Social Media Harm
summary: Tech giants like Meta, TikTok, and Snap are facing numerous lawsuits alleging their social media platforms cause mental health issues such as addiction, anxiety, self-harm, and eating disorders in children and teens. Parents and school districts claim these companies were aware of the harmful effects of their products but failed to warn users. The cases are consolidated in multidistrict litigation, with bellwether trials focusing on school districts and individual plaintiffs scheduled for summer 2026.

title: Samsung’s next frontier: XR in partnership with Qualcomm, Google
summary: Samsung Electronics is partnering with Qualcomm Technologies and Google LLC to enter the extended reality (XR) device market, which is projected to reach $1.39 trillion by 2030. This collaboration aims to challenge other Big Tech firms like Meta and Apple in the rapidly growing XR space. Although specific product details were not revealed, industry watchers expect Samsung to develop XR headsets utilizing Qualcomm's chipsets and Google's Android operating system, leveraging their existing smartphone collaboration.

title: ChatGPT sets fastest-growing user base record: study
summary: ChatGPT, OpenAI's popular chatbot, reportedly achieved 100 million monthly active users within two months of its launch, making it the fastest-growing consumer application in history, according to a UBS study. This rapid growth, evidenced by 13 million daily unique visitors in January, outpaced TikTok and Instagram. OpenAI has also introduced a US$20 monthly subscription to cover computing costs and offer enhanced services.

title: CEBA: Energy Customers Announce Record-High of Nearly 17 GW of Clean Energy in 2022, Despite Policy and Market Challenges
summary: In 2022, commercial and industrial energy customers procured a record-breaking 16.9 gigawatts of new clean energy deals, as announced by CEBA. Amazon led the list for the third consecutive year, with General Motors achieving its second-highest annual deal volume. The report highlights a significant commitment to decarbonization, with 54% of participating customers being new to the market, and CEBA's community having catalyzed 64.5 gigawatts of clean energy since 2014, surpassing their 2025 goal.

title: Calamos Investments Launches New Sustainable Equities ETF SROI
summary: Calamos Investments has launched the Calamos Antetokounmpo Global Sustainable Equities ETF (SROI) on NYSE ARCA, in collaboration with NBA star Giannis Antetokounmpo. This actively managed ETF aims for long-term capital appreciation by investing in quality growth companies that integrate ESG principles, utilizing both traditional financial and nonfinancial indicators. The fund will also donate a portion of its profits to charity, reflecting a commitment to community impact.

title: Google, Amex roll out anti-fraud tool
summary: Google and American Express have expanded their partnership to introduce a new security feature for Amex virtual card users in the U.S. This anti-fraud tool, initially launched last year with Capital One, replaces actual card numbers with unique virtual numbers during online purchases made via Chrome and Android apps, eliminating manual CVV entry and providing encrypted connections. Google plans to extend this feature to Visa and Mastercard virtual cards later this year.

title: Microsoft unveils plans to revamp Bing using AI
summary: Microsoft is revamping its Bing search engine and Edge Web browser with artificial intelligence, aiming to challenge Google's dominance in consumer technology markets. This move is part of Microsoft's strategy to invest billions in AI, leveraging its partnership with OpenAI and its chatbot ChatGPT to offer more powerful and user-friendly search capabilities and integrate AI across its product suite. The company believes this technology will reshape software categories and generate significant revenue increases for Bing.

title: Former Salesforce co-CEO Bret Taylor to launch new AI startup with Google veteran Clay Bavor
summary: Former Salesforce co-CEO Bret Taylor is partnering with outgoing Google VP Clay Bavor to establish a new artificial intelligence startup. The venture, set to launch in March, aims to apply AI to critical business problems. Bavor’s departure from Google will shift his current responsibilities within Google's Labs unit, distributing emerging technology and augmented reality projects to other internal teams.

title: Google shares plummet after chatbot 'Bard' gives bad answer
summary: Google's parent company, Alphabet Inc., saw its shares plummet by nearly 9% after its new AI chatbot, "Bard," provided inaccurate information in a promotional video. This incident led to a loss of $100 billion in market value for Google and fueled concerns that the company is falling behind its rival, Microsoft Corp., which has invested significantly in OpenAI's ChatGPT. Google acknowledged the error, emphasizing the need for a rigorous testing process for Bard.

title: Wanted: A Data-Based Method for Retaining Talent
summary: In an era of large-scale layoffs and economic uncertainty, companies face increased pressure to retain top talent. This article argues that traditional HR methods are no longer sufficient and advocates for the use of data-driven strategies and predictive analytics to understand employee motivations and refine employee value propositions. By identifying key factors influencing retention and tailoring incentives, businesses can better engage their workforce and mitigate the negative impacts on productivity and morale.

title: Here’s Some Wisdom: Expect the Unexpected
summary: The article discusses tough investing lessons from 2022, a year marked by high inflation, aggressive monetary policy, and geopolitical conflict, which challenged conventional investment wisdom. It covers five key lessons: the failure of traditional diversification, the unpredictable impact of rising rates on stocks, the vulnerability of sustainable investments to market cycles, the risks of thematic investing, and the fact that private markets do not always go up. The author emphasizes the importance of accepting uncertainty in investing, preparing for the unexpected, and focusing on fundamental factors over macro timing.

title: Wall St dips as Treasury yields rise after auction
summary: U.S. stock indexes closed lower on Thursday, reversing earlier gains as Treasury yields increased after a poorly received auction of 30-year bonds. This overshadowed strong earnings reports from companies like Disney and PepsiCo. Weak demand for the $21 billion sale of 30-year Treasury bonds led to a rise in yields, making bonds a more attractive alternative to equities for investors.

title: Uber picks Oracle and Google for 7-year cloud contracts, closing its own data centers
summary: Uber has signed two seven-year cloud contracts with Google Cloud and Oracle, signaling its intention to close its own data center infrastructure and move over 95% of its IT operations to the cloud. This strategic shift aims to reduce risks by utilizing multiple providers and leveraging the specific advantages of each cloud service for different aspects of its business, from mapping and advertising with Google to enterprise resource planning with Oracle. The company, which previously operated a "tripod strategy" involving its own colocation sites, now joins a growing list of enterprises migrating fully to the cloud.

title: Uber goes multicloud, announcing big deals with Oracle and Google
summary: Uber has announced a multicloud strategy, signing a seven-year partnership with Oracle to move critical workloads to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, a deal estimated to be worth over a billion dollars. Concurrently, Uber is expanding its existing partnership with Google Cloud to modernize its infrastructure and enhance the customer experience. This move aims to accelerate Uber's path to profitability by closing its on-premises data centers and focusing resources on core strengths, while leveraging the price, performance, and flexibility offered by Oracle and Google.

title: Post-quantum cryptography startup Sandbox AQ raises $500M
summary: Sandbox AQ, a quantum computing software startup that spun off from Alphabet Inc., has successfully raised $500 million in funding. The company specializes in post-quantum cryptography software designed to safeguard data from future quantum computer attacks, as well as quantum simulation software for drug development and quantum sensors. Sandbox AQ plans to target telecommunications, financial services, government, healthcare, and cybersecurity sectors, citing existing customers like Mount Sinai Health System and SoftBank.

title: How enterprises can use ChatGPT and GPT-3
summary: This article explores how enterprises can leverage chatbot platforms like ChatGPT and GPT-3 for various functions, from automating tasks and generating creative ideas to aiding in code development and customer support. It highlights the differences between ChatGPT and GPT-3, emphasizing GPT-3's broader capabilities and customizability for specific business needs through prompt engineering. The piece also addresses the critical risks associated with using these technologies, such as data security concerns and the potential for inaccurate or biased outputs, urging caution and the implementation of clear governance guidelines.

title: Akamai Stock Tumbles on New Cloud Computing Push
summary: Akamai Technologies (AKAM) stock tumbled after its fourth-quarter earnings report, not primarily due to the results themselves, but because of a major shift in strategy towards aggressive expansion in the highly competitive cloud computing market. The company plans substantial capital spending to build out its new Akamai Connected Cloud, aiming for cloud computing to eventually account for most of its revenue. Analysts expressed concern over the increased risk and competition with hyperscale cloud providers, leading to ratings downgrades and a significant drop in stock price.

title: Sandbox AQ Announces $500 Million Fundraise
summary: Sandbox AQ has announced a significant $500 million fundraising round with participation from investors including Breyer Capital, T. Rowe Price funds, TIME Ventures, and Eric Schmidt. Spun off from Alphabet Inc., the company focuses on developing post-quantum cryptography software and offers quantum sensing and simulation solutions. These solutions aim to serve various sectors like life sciences, security, finance, and manufacturing, providing resilience against potential threats from quantum computing.

title: In Pics | Indian-origin CEOs leading top companies
summary: This article highlights several Indian-origin CEOs who are leading major global companies, following Neal Mohan's appointment as YouTube's CEO. It lists prominent figures such as Sundar Pichai (Alphabet/Google), Laxman Narasimhan (Starbucks), Leena Nair (Chanel), Arvind Krishna (IBM), Vasant Narasimhan (Novartis), Anand Eswaran (Veeam Software), and Shantanu Narayen (Adobe). The piece acknowledges their notable achievements in the corporate world.

title: Dissecting the Chamber’s stance on climate policies
summary: A report by InfluenceMap indicates that the U.S. Chamber of Commerce’s climate policy positions largely align with its fossil fuel members, earning it a low E- score for 25 out of 39 negative policy stances last year. Despite claims of advocating for climate solutions, the Chamber opposed key legislation like the Inflation Reduction Act, frequently adopting high-level positive statements that contradict its specific opposition to regulations. The Chamber, however, asserts that its opposition to the IRA was tax-related and that it actively supports other climate-advancing initiatives.

title: Indian-origin exec Neal Mohan now YouTube's CEO
summary: Neal Mohan, an Indian-American executive with a long tenure at YouTube and Google, has been appointed as the new CEO of the video-sharing platform. This promotion places him among a distinguished group of Indian-origin individuals leading major global tech companies. Mohan's past contributions include focusing on developing YouTube Shorts, Music, and subscription services, and he previously held senior advertising and product roles at Google and DoubleClick.

title: Who is Neal Mohan - new YouTube CEO
summary: Neal Mohan, an Indian-American, has been appointed as the new CEO of YouTube, succeeding Susan Wojcicki. Mohan, who previously served as YouTube's Chief Product Officer, joined Google in 2008 and has been instrumental in the growth of the platform, as well as the development of Google's AdSense and DoubleClick. He holds degrees from Stanford University, where he specialized in electrical engineering and later earned an MBA.

title: Kroger workers who quit are getting texts and emails from the company asking them to come back
summary: Kroger, the largest supermarket operator in the U.S. by sales, is actively reaching out to former employees via texts and emails, encouraging them to return. This unusual recruitment strategy is a response to the current low unemployment rate and the competitive job market, with Kroger's chief people officer stating that "alumni are also a talent source." The company has found that returning workers are often more productive than new hires and has seen success in re-recruiting individuals, sometimes offering higher salaries.

title: PJM to offer time-matched renewable energy certificates as demand for 24/7 coverage grows
summary: PJM Environmental Information Services will begin trading renewable energy certificates with hourly time stamps in March, responding to increased customer demand for more granular, 24/7 clean energy data. This initiative aims to provide a transparent and auditable way for energy buyers, including large corporations and the federal government, to verify hourly carbon-free energy use, building on a concept popularized by Google for achieving round-the-clock clean energy coverage. While currently primarily of interest to voluntary energy buyers, PJM anticipates demand will grow as decarbonization efforts progress and the percentage of clean energy on the grid increases.

title: Microsoft teams up with Nvidia and Nintendo in 10-year deal to stream Xbox games
summary: Microsoft has secured 10-year agreements with Nvidia and Nintendo to stream Xbox games as part of its strategy to address antitrust concerns regarding its proposed acquisition of Activision Blizzard. This partnership aims to make Xbox and potential Activision titles available on Nvidia's GeForce Now cloud gaming service and Nintendo platforms, covering various devices. The move is intended to placate regulators scrutinizing the $69 billion deal.

title: Nvidia adds to AI hype with new cloud-based service, stock jumps on forecast
summary: Nvidia's shares surged after the company announced strong fiscal fourth-quarter results and plans to make its AI software available on major cloud platforms like Oracle, Microsoft, and Alphabet. CEO Jensen Huang highlighted the potential of their new browser-based DGX Cloud AI supercomputer service to accelerate adoption of higher-margin data-center software, capitalizing on the booming interest in artificial intelligence. This move is expected to expand the chip maker's revenue streams beyond hardware.

title: Baidu's ChatGPT-like app will revolutionise its search engine, says CEO
summary: Baidu's CEO Robin Li announced that its upcoming ChatGPT-like app, Ernie Bot, will revolutionize its search engine by enhancing user experience and engagement, significantly expanding the market size of search engines. The company plans to build an AI ecosystem around Ernie Bot, leveraging its advanced generative AI technology to boost online advertising and serve as a productivity tool for other businesses. Baidu aims to regain market share and capitalize on its "first mover" advantage in China's AI market with Ernie Bot, which is trained on years of search data and is "state of the art" in understanding Chinese language and culture.

title: Meet the $10,000 Nvidia chip powering the race for A.I.
summary: The Nvidia A100 chip, costing around $10,000, has become a critical tool in the artificial intelligence industry, powering applications like ChatGPT and Stable Diffusion. Nvidia dominates the market for GPUs used in machine learning, with the A100 serving as a "workhorse" for AI professionals and requiring substantial investment for companies to train and deploy AI models. Despite the high costs and emerging competition, Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang asserts that their GPUs offer a much more affordable solution for developing sophisticated AI models compared to traditional CPU-based data centers.

title: As chatbots boom, Nvidia sales outlook beats Wall Street expectations
summary: Nvidia Corp (NVDA.O) surpassed Wall Street's first-quarter revenue expectations, driven by a surge in demand for its chips used in AI services like chatbots. The company announced a new cloud computing service to help businesses develop generative AI, positioning itself to significantly benefit from the growing AI sector despite a broader downturn in the PC market. Analysts project this boom in generative AI could add an additional $14 billion to Nvidia's revenue by 2027.

title: Mercedes enlists Google’s help in hunt for billion-dollar software windfall
summary: Mercedes-Benz is partnering with Google to develop a new operating system for its vehicles, aiming to generate approximately 10 billion euros (US$10.6 billion) in additional software-based revenue by the end of the decade. The luxury automaker plans to debut this system in 2025 across its entire lineup, focusing on retaining control over the technology while leveraging partnerships with companies like Alphabet, Nvidia, and Luminar. Mercedes also expects 1 billion euros in earnings before interest and taxes from software sales by 2025 and is significantly increasing its software R&D budget and hiring software engineers.

title: The Decoding of the Digital Age!
summary: Uma Iyer discusses the evolution of coding from complex languages to the rise of "no-code" and "low-code" platforms, democratizing technology for a wider audience. She explains how the digital age is moving towards intuitive, object-based interaction, making programming accessible to those without traditional coding knowledge, and highlights the role of AI and gaming in shaping the future of software development.

title: Step-By-Step Guide To Opening A moomoo Account
summary: This article provides a step-by-step guide to opening a moomoo securities account, highlighting the ease and speed of the process. It covers downloading the app, filling in details, waiting for confirmation, and funding the account, while also mentioning moomoo's value-added services like paper trading and a worldwide investment community. The guide aims to encourage both new and experienced Singaporean investors to try the moomoo platform due to its low trading fees and useful features.

title: Westport schools joined a new lawsuit against YouTube, TikTok and other apps. Here's why.
summary: Westport Community Schools have joined a national class-action lawsuit against social media giants like TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube, alleging their platforms contribute to a youth mental health crisis. The lawsuit, filed by Frantz Law Group, claims these companies intentionally design addictive platforms despite knowing the severe impact on youth mental health, leading to increased rates of depression, anxiety, and other issues. The district will incur no cost by joining the suit, and the school superintendent emphasizes the unique challenges posed by social media and the need for new digital literacy programs.

title: Google employees are voicing their frustrations over layoffs at BARD
summary: Google employees are expressing significant dissatisfaction with the company's recent layoffs and CEO Sundar Pichai's request for them to spend time improving the AI chatbot, Bard. They are using Bard itself, as well as internal meme generators, to voice their criticisms and ridicule the perceived mishandling of both the job cuts and Bard's rushed launch. This situation highlights the complexities of integrating AI in the workplace, especially when juxtaposed with major organizational changes.

title: SKYY Top Holdings List & Exposure (First Trust Cloud Computing ETF)
summary: This article details the top holdings and exposure of the First Trust Cloud Computing ETF (SKYY). It lists the top 25 stocks by weight, comprising 72.95% of the fund, including CoreWeave, Arista Networks, and Alphabet. The ETF's primary geographic exposure is the United States, and its largest sector exposure is Technology, with Software being the leading industry.

title: The 10 most innovative companies in augmented and virtual reality of 2023
summary: Fast Company has released its list of the 10 most innovative companies in augmented and virtual reality for 2023, highlighting AR and VR advancements despite a decrease in consumer headset sales. The article discusses how companies like Unity, Meta, Google, Niantic, and Varjo are pushing the boundaries of spatial computing for both consumer and enterprise applications. It also notes Apple's secretive efforts in mixed reality and the overall steady improvement in hardware and software for spatial computing experiences.

title: C3 AI Announces Fiscal Third Quarter 2023 Financial Results
summary: C3 AI (NYSE: AI) announced strong financial results for its fiscal third quarter ended January 31, 2023, exceeding revenue guidance with $66.7 million. The company highlighted successful partnerships, expansion into new markets, and a confident outlook towards profitability in FY24, driven by its consumption-based pricing model and innovations in enterprise AI and Generative AI.

title: Generative AI, Explained
summary: This article explains generative AI, a rapidly evolving field using deep learning algorithms to create new and original content. It highlights the technology's history, starting with Google's Transformer model, and explores its wide range of current and future use cases across various industries such as content creation, healthcare, finance, and gaming. The piece concludes that generative AI is still in its infancy with vast potential to revolutionize many aspects of life and work.

title: The Age of AI and Other Trends in Tech and Beyond
summary: This article from Morgan Stanley Research discusses several key trends in the technology, media, and telecom (TMT) sector, with a significant focus on artificial intelligence. It highlights AI's potential to disrupt various industries despite current cost and accuracy challenges, the promising development of non-invasive blood monitoring technology, and the current downturn and potential recovery of the personal computer market. The report also touches on the macroeconomic impact on internet companies and provides an outlook for various TMT sub-sectors.

title: 10 Undervalued, High-Quality Stocks With Competitive Advantages
summary: This article identifies 10 undervalued, high-quality stocks with wide economic moats, according to Morningstar analysts. These companies, including Adobe, Microsoft, and Alphabet, have strong balance sheets, high profitability, and competitive advantages expected to last over 20 years. The stocks listed were trading below their fair value estimates as of March 2, 2023.

title: Google’s Champion of Holistic Insurance: Karna’s Journey Continues
summary: This article highlights Dr. Henna Karna, Google's general manager of Global Industry Solutions in Insurance, and her two-decade mission to improve the efficiency and customer-centricity of the insurance industry. Journalist Russ Banham recounts Karna's journey to pragmatically evolve the entire operation of an insurance company using cloud computing, automation, and AI. Her vision involves sharing accurate risk exposure data across all aspects of an insurance company, from underwriting to claims management.

title: Mercedes Adds Biometric Payments to Five Models, Turning the Car into a Payment Device
summary: Mercedes-Benz is launching Mercedes pay+, an in-car payment service that uses biometric two-factor authentication via a fingerprint sensor in the vehicle. This marks the first time Visa's Delegated Authentication and Cloud Token Framework are being integrated natively into a car. Initially available in Germany for five models (EQS, EQE, S-Class, C-Class, GLC), the service will expand to other European markets and eventually the U.S., allowing drivers to pay for digital services, hardware upgrades, and eventually fuel.

title: Sonos CEO Patrick Spence on right to repair, spatial audio and those never-ending lawsuits
summary: Sonos CEO Patrick Spence discusses the company's new Era 100 and Era 300 speakers, focusing on the emergence of spatial audio and its mainstream adoption challenges. He also addresses Sonos' commitment to right-to-repair principles, its premium pricing strategy, the future of the smart home market, and ongoing lawsuits with Google.

title: Big Tech Has Layoffs All Wrong
summary: This article argues that the recent wave of layoffs in Big Tech companies is a misguided and conventional approach, contrary to their self-proclaimed innovative management styles. It suggests that these staff cuts, often driven by investor expectations and a desire to reassert power over workers, are detrimental to long-term financial performance and employee morale. The author proposes alternative strategies like "secondment" programs and furloughs, highlighting that many profitable tech giants have suitable alternatives to mass dismissals.

title: Zero trust illustrated: Secure cloud migration
summary: This article focuses on securing cloud migrations using a zero-trust architecture (ZTA), emphasizing posture control and secure workload communications. It highlights the importance of protecting against misconfigurations, vulnerabilities, and threats in cloud-native applications, advocating for early security integration in the software development lifecycle. The piece stresses that robust zero-trust protection must extend beyond user access to encompass the security of applications and workloads themselves.

title: Physical-Digital Integration Market May Set New Growth Story : Google, Infosys, Oracle, Microsoft
summary: A new research publication by Advance Market Analytics highlights the significant growth in the Physical-Digital Integration Market, driven by increasing R&D spending and technology adoption. Key players like Microsoft, SAP, IBM, Infosys, Oracle, Google, and Toshiba are profiled within the 232-page report. The integration of physical infrastructure with digital technologies, especially through IoT, is identified as a major trend, with the retail sector showing maximum adoption for enhanced consumer interaction.

title: Experts call for AI regulation during Senate hearing
summary: During a recent Senate hearing, experts advocated for the immediate implementation of AI regulations by Congress to protect consumers from the potential harms of the technology, such as algorithmic bias and the misuse of deepfakes. Concerns were raised about AI's impact on safety, privacy, economic, and national security, with comparisons drawn to the European Union's regulatory efforts and the White House's Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights. The discussion emphasized the need for transparency, accountability, and safeguards in AI development and deployment,

title: Federal Circuit Allows Apple’s Fintiv Challenge To Proceed
summary: The Federal Circuit Court of Appeals has revived Apple's challenge to the PTAB's Fintiv practice of discretionary denials, allowing it to proceed to the district court. This decision questions the USPTO's ability to issue rules through precedential decisions rather than traditional notice-and-comment rulemaking. The resolution of this case could significantly impact how the USPTO operates regarding Inter Partes Review practices.

title: Smart move: Artificial intelligence and 3D laser scanning for MEP applications
summary: This article explores how artificial intelligence (AI) and 3D laser scanning can significantly benefit Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing (MEP) applications. It highlights how 3D laser scanning provides crucial accurate data for new construction or retrofitting projects, while AI can then process this data for pattern recognition, identifying optimal and energy-efficient solutions, and enhancing project management. The combination of these technologies, along with human expertise, is presented as a powerful tool for improving efficiency and decision-making in MEP design and implementation.

title: Netgear Nighthawk RS700 Router Brings Wi-Fi 7 and a Hefty Price Tag
summary: Netgear has announced the Nighthawk RS700, a new Wi-Fi 7 router priced at $699, which will be available in Q2 2023. This tri-band router supports Wi-Fi 7 features such as multi-link operation and preamble puncturing for reduced latency and increased data rates, alongside 10 Gbps WAN and LAN ports to accommodate emerging high-speed internet services. It also supports mesh operation and includes a year of Netgear Armor security.

title: Driverless trucks will deliver food to 3 Kroger stores in DFW area
summary: Kroger has partnered with Gatik to use driverless trucks for food deliveries to three stores in the Dallas-Fort Worth area. These 20-foot autonomous trucks will operate seven days a week from Kroger's Dallas distribution center, with the goal of improving online order fulfillment speed and efficiency. Initially, the trucks will include a safety driver for data collection, a practice that could last 19-25 months to ensure road safety.

title: Artificial Intelligence in China: Implications and Opportunities
summary: The rapid adoption of generative AI platforms like ChatGPT has highlighted China's strong position to benefit from the AI trend. Driven by massive data, a skilled workforce, supportive government policies, and an advanced manufacturing base, Chinese companies like Baidu, ByteDance, Alibaba, and Tencent are poised to become leaders in generative AI. The article details the foundational strengths of China's AI ecosystem and the potential beneficiaries within its tech and semiconductor industries.

title: China’s Answer to ChatGPT Gets an Artificial Debut and Disappoints (Published 2023)
summary: Baidu's debut of its ChatGPT rival, Ernie, disappointed investors after its "live" demonstration was revealed to be prerecorded, causing Baidu's shares to tumble. This event highlights China's ongoing efforts to catch up with the United States in AI development, particularly given restrictions on high-end computing chips. The botched rollout also points to the intensive global race in large language model technology, dominated by a few major players.

title: Here's how Chinese cyber spies exploited a critical Fortinet bug
summary: Mandiant security researchers have revealed that suspected Chinese spies exploited a critical Fortinet vulnerability (CVE-2022-41328) to steal credentials and maintain network access using custom malware. The group, UNC3886, also targeted VMware ESXi hypervisors in similar attacks last year. The sophisticated methods involved novel malware families like CASTLETAP and THINCRUST, and exploited the Fortinet bug via path traversal by uploading malicious files through a specific command.

title: eToro has launched a new investment portfolio
summary: eToro has expanded its smart investment portfolio offerings by launching the AI-Revolution portfolio, focusing on the artificial intelligence industry. This new portfolio divides companies into two groups: those involved in AI technology infrastructure and those across various sectors utilizing AI to enhance their products and services. A minimum investment of $500 is required for this portfolio.

title: Scientists at DeepMind and Meta Press Fusion of AI, Biology
summary: Scientists from DeepMind and Meta are pushing for a closer integration of AI and biology, citing recent breakthroughs in computational biology as evidence of its potential. Meta Platforms' new tool for predicting protein structures is presented as a continuation of advancements initiated by Alphabet's DeepMind, with experts anticipating that these AI systems will significantly accelerate progress in life sciences, especially in drug development.

title: Poly Studio X Family of Android-Based Video Bars are Now Certified for Google Meet
summary: HP announced that its Poly Studio X family of Android-based video bars are now certified for Google Meet, enabling organizations to leverage Poly's innovative room solutions. This certification guarantees compatibility and provides a seamless native experience with the Google Meet app built-in, simplifying setup and fostering efficient collaboration with advanced audio and video features like Poly DirectorAI and NoiseBlockAI. The Poly Studio X portfolio, including the X30, X50, and X70, offers solutions for various meeting space sizes, ensuring investment protection and continuous growth within the Google ecosystem.

title: Rackspace Lays Off 275 Employees As Stock Hits Bottom
summary: Rackspace Technology announced it is laying off 275 employees, approximately 4% of its global workforce, due to an uncertain economic climate and the need to align its cost structure with business demands. This decision comes as the company's stock has plummeted by 84% over the past year, trading at a near-all-time low of $1.76 per share, and after reporting an $805 million net loss in 2022 and experiencing a ransomware attack in December. Rackspace joins a growing list of tech companies implementing significant layoffs in 2023 amid a broader industry downturn.

title: Rackspace cutting workforce in response to downturn. Amid string of losses, its stock has plummeted.
summary: Rackspace Technology Inc. is laying off nearly 4 percent of its global workforce, approximately 275 employees, in response to a broad economic downturn. This decision comes as the company's stock has plummeted more than 80 percent in the last year, including a significant drop after a December ransomware attack. Rackspace is also struggling with a string of quarterly losses despite record revenues, and its CEO is implementing a new business strategy.

title: Understanding ChatGPT: The Promise and Nuances of Large Language Models
summary: The article explores ChatGPT, a Large Language Model (LLM) that has rapidly gained popularity, detailing its capabilities, underlying technology, and key limitations such as its inability to search the web or guarantee factual accuracy. It discusses potential business applications across various sectors while also highlighting associated risks and the importance of prompt engineering for optimal use.

title: Enterprise Connect 2023 Expo Hall: RingCentral, VMware, Five9, Cisco, More
summary: The Enterprise Connect 2023 conference showcased over 170 organizations at the Gaylord Palms Resort in Orlando, Florida. A major highlight was the significant advancement of artificial intelligence in UCaaS and CCaaS, with tech giants like Google Cloud, Microsoft, and Zoom unveiling new AI offerings. The article includes a slideshow featuring photos from the expo floor, highlighting the innovation and booth creativity of participating companies.

title: Is Google ending?
summary: This article explores whether Google's dominance in search and AI is coming to an end, citing challenges like failed product launches (Duplex), internal scandals, and a talent drain. The rise of competitors like OpenAI's ChatGPT and increased competition from Amazon and Apple in online search are highlighted as significant threats. Despite these setbacks, the article concludes that Google's vast resources, default search engine status, and strategic investments in AI mean it is still too early to write the company off.

title: McDonald's temporarily shuts US offices, prepares layoff notices: report
summary: McDonald's has temporarily closed its U.S. offices and instructed corporate staff to work from home as it prepares to announce layoffs. This move is part of a broader restructuring initiative, "Accelerating the Arches 2.0," aimed at improving efficiency and cutting costs. CEO Chris Kempczinski previously warned employees of "difficult discussions and decisions" regarding roles and staffing levels.

title: China AI: What Lies Ahead?
summary: A Citi Research report by Alicia Yap examines the AI landscape in China, forecasting significant growth in its generative AI application market from Rmb66.3bn in 2022 to Rmb207bn by 2025. The report also highlights the rapid expansion of China's cybersecurity market, projected to reach US$31.9bn by 2026, driven by digital economy demands and government support. It discusses how generative AI could revolutionize the SaaS industry and considers the impact of data privacy and censorship on China's AI progress.

title: Google Cloud brings new blood into its go-to-market organization
summary: Google Cloud is making significant changes to its go-to-market organization, bringing in new executive hires and rebranding its consultancy services to enhance sales efficiency. These changes follow the elevation of Adaire Fox-Martin as the top sales executive, indicating a strategic effort to strengthen Google Cloud's market position amidst slowing industry growth and increasing pressure for profitability. Industry analysts view these moves as a concerted effort to improve coverage, relevance, and access in the enterprise market, leveraging the new executives' deep experience.

title: Sirius XM Holdings Inc. stock rises Wednesday, outperforms market
summary: Sirius XM Holdings Inc. (SIRI) saw its stock rise by 3.13% on Wednesday, closing at $3.96. This performance outpaced the broader market, as the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 0.24% while the NASDAQ Composite Index fell 1.07%. The rise ended a two-day losing streak for the company, though its shares remain significantly below their 52-week high.

title: Flaherty & Crumrine Total Return Fund Inc. (FLC) Stock Live Price, Chart & News
summary: This article provides live stock price, chart, and news for Flaherty & Crumrine Total Return Fund Inc. (FLC). It details current trading information, performance statistics, and fundamental data for FLC, which is trading on the NYSE. The article also includes a list of recent news headlines related to FLC from Seeking Alpha, highlighting past analysis and outlooks on the fund.

title: General Motors hates your iPhone
summary: General Motors has decided to remove CarPlay and Android Auto support from its future electric vehicles, opting instead for its own Android Automotive-based infotainment system. This decision, driven by GM's desire for increased control over data and subscription revenues, is criticized for prioritizing corporate interests over user convenience and potentially degrading the in-car experience. The article raises concerns about app compatibility, data syncing, and the long-term software quality in GM vehicles, questioning whether other automakers will follow suit or leverage CarPlay as a competitive advantage.

title: Qualcomm, Nvidia spar for top spot in AI chip efficiency tests
summary: Qualcomm's AI 100 chips outperformed Nvidia's H100 chips in two out of three power efficiency categories for AI inference tasks, specifically image classification and object detection. Nvidia, however, took the lead in natural language processing performance and power efficiency. This highlights the competitive landscape in the growing market for data center inference chips, where power consumption is a key cost factor.

title: Expedia CEO Looks to AI to ‘Rebalance’ Dependence on Google
summary: Expedia Group CEO Peter Kern believes that generative AI and other emerging technologies could help rebalance the travel advertising market, reducing reliance on Google. Kern and Chairman Barry Diller have long criticized Google's dominance in the sector, and they see AI as a potential way to diversify dependencies among big tech players. This shift could lead to a less centralized online travel ecosystem.

title: Billionaire Barry Diller says the media should 'absolutely' sue AI makers over ingesting text from articles
summary: Billionaire Barry Diller, chairman of IAC, urged media publishers to sue AI makers for using their content without compensation. He warned that generative AI tools like ChatGPT could "cannibalize" published works, drawing parallels to the early days of the internet when content was freely used. Diller emphasized that companies have legal grounds to sue under copyright law and should actively pursue litigation to ensure payment for the use of their intellectual property by AI.

title: Undervalued Communication Services Stocks
summary: This article identifies undervalued communication services stocks, highlighting names like Comcast, Disney, and Alphabet, which are trading at discounts after a challenging 2022. It screens for companies within the Morningstar US Communication Services Index that have a 4 or 5-star Morningstar Rating and a wide economic moat, providing analyst insights for the top five. The communication services sector saw a significant rebound in early 2023 after substantial losses in the previous year.

title: Amazon Launches AI Platform Aimed At Corporate Customers—Joining Google And Microsoft In AI Race
summary: Amazon has launched a new generative AI service called Bedrock, targeting corporate customers through its Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud platform. This move distinguishes Amazon from competitors like Microsoft and Google, which have focused more on consumer-facing AI products. Bedrock offers tools for businesses to build and scale their own AI applications, including Amazon's Titan large language models and integrations with other AI startups, aiming to democratize AI development securely and efficiently for enterprises.

title: 13 Top Earth Day Campaigns This Decade
summary: This article showcases 13 impactful Earth Day campaigns from the past decade that successfully blend brand messaging with environmental awareness. It highlights how companies like Lundberg Family Farms, Sony BBC Earth, Impossible Foods, and Mastercard have utilized creative strategies, from catchy slogans and video content to gamified sustainability and strategic partnerships, to engage consumers and promote eco-friendly initiatives. The piece emphasizes that sustainability is not only good for the planet but also a powerful tool for business, resonating with a growing number of environmentally conscious consumers.

title: Gartner Magic Quadrant for Analytics and Business Intelligence Platforms 2023
summary: This article summarizes the 2023 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Analytics and Business Intelligence Platforms, categorizing vendors into Leaders, Challengers, Visionaries, and Niche Players. It details the strengths and cautions for each vendor based on Gartner's evaluation of their ABI platforms. The report highlights Microsoft, Salesforce, and Qlik as leaders, and notes movements of several companies since the 2022 report, particularly Pyramid Analytics' jump to visionary status.

title: Tap collaboration devices for employee experience data
summary: This article explores how collaboration devices can significantly enhance employee experience. It details four key strategies: leveraging device data to understand employee behavior and engagement, simplifying the process of starting in-room meetings with one-click-to-join systems, ensuring equity between in-office and remote participants through advanced camera setups and touchscreens, and encouraging the use of wellness features embedded in communication software. The piece emphasizes the importance of analyzing collected data and taking action to improve productivity and engagement based on these insights.

title: Google DeepMind: Bringing together two world-class AI teams
summary: Google and Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai announced the formation of Google DeepMind, merging Google Research's Brain team and DeepMind. This new unit aims to accelerate progress in AI development, focusing on building more capable, safe, and responsible general AI systems. Demis Hassabis will lead Google DeepMind as CEO, while Jeff Dean will take on the elevated role of Google's Chief Scientist, focusing on future AI research and multimodal AI models.

title: Better Bear-Market Buy: Unity Technologies Stock or Digital Turbine Stock?
summary: This article compares Unity Technologies (U) and Digital Turbine (APPS) as potential bear-market buys, noting both have seen significant stock drops. It highlights their direct competition in the mobile app discovery market, particularly after Unity's merger with ironSource, and ultimately suggests Unity may be the better buy due to its "end-to-end platform" approach.

title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai receives third $200 million-plus stock award
summary: Google CEO Sundar Pichai has received his third stock award exceeding $200 million, as disclosed by Alphabet Inc. The $218 million award, made public in an SEC filing, follows a similar pattern of triennial bonuses that align his compensation with long-term shareholder value and Alphabet's stock performance. This award vests over three years and includes both time-based and performance-based components.

title: B+C Hall of Fame 2023: Matt Bond
summary: Matt Bond, Chairman of Content Distribution at NBCUniversal, is honored by the B+C Hall of Fame for his influential career in cable programming and distribution. Known as a tough negotiator from his time at TCI and Comcast to his current role at NBCU, Bond has been involved in numerous significant transactions. The article also highlights his remarkable recovery from a severe heart attack and his philosophical view of his career journey from the "Model T era" to the "Tesla era" of television.

title: Google Workspace Extends Enterprise-Grade Security and Device Management for Hybrid Work with Okta and VMware
summary: Google Cloud announced new security alliances with Okta and VMware to enhance enterprise-grade security and device management for Google Workspace, supporting hybrid work environments. These partnerships extend Google Workspace's built-in capabilities, offering large organizations more choice and flexibility in identity, access, and device management. Additionally, JumpCloud integrates with Google Workspace to provide small and midsize organizations with modern, cloud-based directory services as an alternative to legacy solutions.

title: Google (GOOGL) announces plan for additional $70 billion share buyback
summary: Alphabet, Google's parent company, has announced authorization for an additional $70 billion share buyback, nearly a year after a similar $70 billion buyback. This decision follows its Q1 2023 earnings report, where the company beat EPS and revenue expectations. The buyback aims to return capital to shareholders, with shares expected to be repurchased through various market transactions.

title: 3 reasons why the Vanguard MSCI Index International Shares ETF (VGS) could be an excellent investment for beginners
summary: The Vanguard MSCI Index International Shares ETF (VGS) is presented as an excellent investment option for beginners due to its significant diversification across major developed markets and sectors, its strong long-term performance, and its low management fees. The ETF offers exposure to over 1,400 businesses globally, including tech giants, and has historically delivered solid returns while keeping costs down.

title: Elon Musk Threatens to Sue Microsoft Over Alleged Illegal Data Usage
summary: Elon Musk is threatening to sue Microsoft, alleging unauthorized use of Twitter's data for training its AI models. This comes after Microsoft stopped supporting Twitter on one of its advertising platforms due to Musk's high asking price for API access. Musk claims Microsoft "trained illegally using Twitter data" and sees this as an opportune moment for legal action, especially given Microsoft's $10 billion investment in OpenAI and the integration of GPT-4 into Bing.

title: Google Search vs. ChatGPT AI: Which Should P/C Insurers Use?
summary: Travelers' technology and data science leaders suggest that P/C insurers should use both Google Search and ChatGPT for optimal results. While Google is better for current information and fact-checking, ChatGPT can also be a valuable tool. The article highlights that using a combination of both AI tools is the best approach for gaining comprehensive insights.

title: U.S. government has 'serious concerns' that some H-1B visa applicants are trying to game the system
summary: The U.S. government has expressed "serious concerns" that some H-1B visa applicants are manipulating the lottery system, as application numbers soared by 61% this year. Authorities suspect some applicants are submitting multiple bids through different company names without genuine job offers to gain an unfair advantage. U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services is conducting fraud investigations and referring some cases to federal prosecutors, noting a significant increase in registrations tied to multiple applications for the same individual.

title: Monument class action alleges telehealth platform shared personal health info with third parties
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Monument Inc., an online alcohol counseling and treatment company, alleging it shared patients' private health and identifiable information with third parties like Meta and Google without consent. The lawsuit claims Monument used a tracking pixel to transmit data including names, birthdates, and IP addresses, and that the company acknowledged disclosing information of over 108,000 individuals. The plaintiff seeks to represent a nationwide class and a Florida subclass, demanding a jury trial, injunctive relief, and damages.

title: Survey Signals Pause-and-Pivot Year for CEOs
summary: The 2023 Gartner CEO and Senior Business Executive Survey indicates a shift from short-term reactivity to cautious long-term planning, with CEOs focusing on business productivity and new three- to seven-year growth strategies. While growth remains a top priority, profitability is investors' primary concern, leading to an emphasis on multiyear productivity engineering and the creative application of new technologies like AI and automation. Talent and workforce issues, including shortages and compensation demands, continue to be significant challenges for executives.

title: Nextdoor Is Integrating Generative AI to Drive Engaging and Kind Conversations in the Neighborhood
summary: Nextdoor announced the integration of generative AI technologies, developed with OpenAI and leveraging the ChatGPT API, to foster more positive and engaging community interactions in the U.S. These new features will assist neighbors in writing posts that drive positive engagement and will suggest kinder revisions for potentially unkind comments. Nextdoor aims to personalize content and strengthen communities through these AI applications, led by Head of AI Qi He.

title: 5 Amazing Ways Meta (Facebook) Is Using Generative AI
summary: Meta, the parent company of Facebook, is heavily investing in generative AI despite its initial focus on the metaverse. The company is implementing generative AI across various platforms to enhance advertising, power AI-driven chat functionalities, revolutionize image and natural language generation, and ultimately, simplify content creation within its metaverse platform, Horizons. These initiatives aim to make content creation more accessible and potentially increase user engagement with Meta's virtual world.

title: Msci Kld 400 Social Index: Meaning, Example, FAQs
summary: The MSCI KLD 400 Social Index is a market capitalization-weighted stock index that focuses on large, mid, and small-cap U.S. companies with high environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards, excluding certain industries. Founded as the Domini 400 Social Index, it was a pioneer in socially responsible investing. The index is reviewed quarterly and tends to be dominated by large American technology companies like Microsoft, NVIDIA, Alphabet, Tesla, and Visa.

title: CII Opposes Admitting Dual-Class Stock to S&P Indexes
summary: The Council of Institutional Investors (CII) has expressed "surprise and disappointment" over S&P Dow Jones Indices' decision to reverse its 2017 ban on admitting new dual-class stock companies to its indexes. This policy change, which allows companies like Blackstone into the S&P 500 despite having unequal voting rights, is criticized by CII for its opaque rationale and lack of investor consultation. S&P defends the change by citing the proliferation of dual-class structures, especially in tech, and the inconsistency of its previous grandfathering policy.

title: Is the Market Reacting Too Negatively to Pinterest Earnings?
summary: Pinterest's stock tumbled 25% despite beating Q1 2023 top and bottom-line estimates and growing monthly active users (MAUs). The sell-off was primarily driven by concerns over slowing growth, rising operating expenses (opex), and a GAAP net loss of $209 million due to restructuring charges. While analysts remain cautiously optimistic with reiterations, several lowered price targets, reflecting market uncertainty regarding the company's profitability amidst its strategic restructuring efforts.

title: Google’s Quantum Computing Technology in 2024 Review
summary: This article reviews Google's quantum computing technology in 2024, tracing its journey from the Quantum AI Lab's launch in 2013 to its achievement of "quantum supremacy" in 2019. It details Google's core quantum technologies, offerings like QVM and Cirq, and its efforts in post-quantum cryptography. The piece also outlines Google's main competitors, funding sources, significant customers, and strategic partnerships, while acknowledging challenges such as the refined classical algorithms that have narrowed the 'quantum supremacy' gap.

title: WSJ News Exclusive | Wendy’s, Google Train Next-Generation Order Taker: an AI Chatbot
summary: Wendy's is partnering with Google to automate its drive-through service using an AI chatbot. This chatbot is powered by Google's natural-language software and has been trained to understand various menu items and ordering styles, including specific Wendy's terminology like 'JBC' and 'biggie bags'. The initiative positions Wendy's among companies adopting generative AI for business growth.

title: Google AI pioneer says he quit to speak freely about technology's 'dangers'
summary: Geoffrey Hinton, an artificial intelligence pioneer, resigned from Google to openly discuss the potential "dangers" of AI, which he now believes could become smarter than humans much sooner than initially anticipated. He expressed concerns about AI's ability to create convincing fake content, displace workers, and be misused by malicious actors, urging policymakers to regulate the technology. Hinton clarified that Google acted responsibly and his departure was not to criticize his former employer.

title: Barry Diller joins News Corp and Axel Springer to fight ‘destructive’ AI
summary: Billionaire Barry Diller, chairman of IAC and Expedia, is collaborating with News Corp and Axel Springer to combat the perceived threat of generative AI to news publishers. Diller warns that AI, particularly platforms like ChatGPT, poses a "destructive" risk similar to the early internet's impact on journalism, advocating for redefined copyright laws and direct compensation for publishers whose content is used to train AI models. He also commented on Rupert Murdoch's legacy, stating the Dominion case stained his reputation.

title: Here's how AI is driving the rise of GPU cloud specialists
summary: The rise of AI is fueling a new class of cloud providers specializing in GPU compute, offering lower prices than generalized cloud competitors. Companies like CoreWeave, Lambda Labs, RunPod, and Arc Compute are catering to AI workloads that require substantial GPU resources. These niche providers differentiate themselves through competitive pricing, strategic locations, and ready access to scarce compute resources amidst supply chain challenges.

title: Launch of first TiVo-based TVs delayed to second half of 2023
summary: The launch of the first TiVo-based connected TVs, manufactured by Vestel, has been delayed from spring to the second half of 2023, aligning with the holiday buying season. Despite this delay, Xperi, TiVo's parent company, maintains its revenue guidance for the year and announced a second "top 10" connected TV OEM partner for the European market, with product shipments expected in 2024. Xperi aims to expand this into a global business, emphasizing an independent, unbiased media platform in the competitive smart TV market.

title: Comparing Google’s Bard with ChatGPT: What’s wrong, what’s bright
summary: This article provides a critical comparison between Google's Bard and OpenAI's ChatGPT, highlighting Bard's shortcomings despite Google's vast data resources. The author, Satyen K.
