Bordoloi, describes issues with Bard's memory, translation capabilities, and tendency to "hallucinate" incorrect information, contrasting it with ChatGPT's more coherent, though sometimes less creative, outputs. The piece argues that while Google has the potential to create a superior AI, Bard still requires significant development to live up to expectations.

title: Roundhill Launches Generative AI & Technology ETF With CHAT
summary: Roundhill Investments has launched the Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT), an actively managed ETF focusing on global companies leading in generative AI technology. The fund aims to offer precise exposure to generative AI, distinguishing itself from other AI-themed ETFs that may lack such specific focus. Top holdings include Nvidia, Microsoft, and Alphabet, with expectations for the basket to expand as more companies genuinely contribute to the generative AI space.

title: Roundhill Investments Launches Generative AI & Technology ETF (NYSE Arca: CHAT)
summary: Roundhill Investments has launched the Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (NYSE Arca: CHAT), providing investors with exposure to companies at the forefront of generative AI technology. The ETF aims to capitalize on the rapidly growing generative AI market, which Roundhill estimates to have a total addressable market of over $120 billion for enterprise software. CHAT is an actively-managed ETF with top holdings including Nvidia, Microsoft, Alphabet, and Salesforce, offering investors a way to participate in this innovative and transformative technology.

title: What you need to know about Temu, the online shopping app dominating download charts
summary: Online shopping app Temu, owned by Chinese company PDD Holdings Inc., has quickly risen to popularity in Canada with its aggressive marketing, low prices, and gamified shopping experience. While it offers bargain deals, experts caution consumers about product quality, potential environmental impact from shipping and returns, and data privacy concerns given its sister app's issues. The platform's "growth-at-all-costs" approach and referral schemes are disrupting the e-commerce market, leading to a "race to the bottom" that could hurt local businesses.

title: University of Chicago Gets Quantum Tech Funding From IBM, Google
summary: The University of Chicago has secured up to $150 million in funding from IBM and Google to accelerate the development of quantum computing. IBM plans to collaborate with the university and the University of Tokyo over ten years to build a quantum-centric supercomputer, a project valued at $100 million. This partnership aims to advance the field of quantum technology, which promises faster computing and enhanced online security.

title: ChatGPT plugins' potential impact on the travel booking experience
summary: The article discusses the potential of ChatGPT plugins to revolutionize the travel booking experience by providing real-time data for transactional purposes, a significant advancement over previous AI chatbot limitations. It highlights how these plugins can transform travel inspiration, planning, and search, moving towards more personalized and conversational interactions. The author suggests that travel companies should embrace this AI wave early to adapt to the evolving digital landscape, comparing the potential impact to that of the iOS App Store.

title: Fidelity Metaverse ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:FMET)
summary: This article provides an overview of the Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET), including its price, holdings, and recent news. FMET tracks a market cap-weighted index of equities related to the metaverse, launched on April 19, 2022, and is managed by Fidelity. The ETF has seen a significant increase in share price since the beginning of 2025 and lists its top institutional shareholders and key holdings.

title: Burney U.S. Factor Rotation ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:BRNY) $BRNY
summary: This article provides details on the Burney U.S. Factor Rotation ETF (BRNY), including its current price, key statistics, holdings, and recent news. As of March 13, 2026, BRNY is trading at $48.24, with assets under management of $476.48 million and a net expense ratio of 0.79%. The ETF is an actively managed fund that dynamically shifts US equity exposure based on market phases and includes major holdings like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft.

title: Wahed FTSE USA Shariah ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:HLAL) $HLAL
summary: The Wahed FTSE USA Shariah ETF (HLAL) tracks the FTSE USA Shariah index, focusing on Sharia-compliant US equities. The ETF, launched in 2019 and managed by Wahed, had a market capitalization of $736.85 million and assets under management of $758.04 million at the time of the article. Its top holdings include major tech companies like Apple, Microsoft, and Alphabet, and it recently declared quarterly distributions.

title: 강남 부자들이 줄섰다는데…年19% 배당에 차익 노리는 이 상품은
summary: 서학개미들이 최근 미국 증시에서 장기채 ETF, 특히 ‘아이셰어스 만기 20년 이상 국채 바이라이트(TLTW)’를 집중 매집하고 있다. 이는 채권 금리 횡보 전망에 따라 매매 차익보다는 연 19%에 달하는 고배당 수익을 노린 투자 전략으로 분석된다. TLTW는 월 지급 배당 상품으로, 현재 금리 변동성이 큰 시기에 투자자들에게 매력적인 상품으로 부상하고 있다.

title: ETF of the Week: Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP)
summary: VettaFi’s Tom Lydon discusses the Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP) which offers exposure to top holdings favored by hedge funds without an overweight toward any single stock. GVIP provides diversification across various companies, including some mid-cap and international stocks, making it a less volatile option for investors seeking innovative companies. It provides a consensus of top companies chosen by leading hedge fund managers, appealing to investors looking for strong, well-vetted companies.

title: ETF of the Week: Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP)
summary: The Goldman Sachs Hedge Industry VIP ETF (GVIP) is highlighted as the ETF of the Week. GVIP provides exposure to top stock picks from hedge funds, focusing on a consensus of leading managers rather than an overweighting of individual stocks. The ETF diversifies holdings across large-cap, mid-cap, and international companies, offering a less volatile way for investors to participate in innovative companies.

title: Google announces US-Taiwan-Philippines cable with Chunghwa, Globe, and AT&T
summary: Google has announced plans for a new subsea cable system, TPU (Taiwan-Philippines-US), linking the US, Taiwan, and the Philippines, partnering with Chunghwa Telecom, Innove (Globe Group), and AT&T. This new cable, expected to be ready for service in 2025 with NEC as the supplier, will provide 260Tbps of capacity and offer an alternative route to Google's existing Apricot cable, enhancing resilience and capacity in the APAC region. Additionally, the article notes another subsea cable development, the EMC West system in the Middle East, being built by Center3 and ASN.

title: NVIDIA Announces DGX GH200 AI Supercomputer
summary: NVIDIA has unveiled the DGX GH200 AI supercomputer, designed to handle massive models for generative AI, recommender systems, and data analytics. This new system connects 256 Grace Hopper Superchips to function as a single 1-exaflop, 144TB GPU, offering significantly more memory than its predecessor. Companies like Google Cloud, Meta, and Microsoft are among the first to explore its capabilities, and NVIDIA is also building its own Helios supercomputer based on this design.

title: Google Pay
summary: This article from Barclays Private Bank details how to use Google Pay with Barclays debit cards, highlighting its security, convenience, and functionality for in-store and online payments. It provides step-by-step instructions for adding cards, answers frequently asked questions about eligibility, device compatibility, transaction limits, and what to do if a device is lost or stolen. Contact information for various client types is also included for support.

title: U.S. Supreme Court declines to hear bid to sue Reddit over child porn
summary: The U.S. Supreme Court has declined to hear a lawsuit attempting to hold Reddit Inc. liable for child pornography posted on its platform, upholding a lower court's decision that Reddit is shielded by Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act. The case explored the scope of a 2018 amendment, the Fight Online Sex Trafficking Act (FOSTA), which allows lawsuits if the claim involves child sex trafficking, but the court found Reddit did not "knowingly benefit" from such activity. This decision follows a similar sidestepping of Section 230 immunity in a separate case involving Google and YouTube.

title: Everything you need to know about Salesforce’s Q1 2024 financial results
summary: Salesforce, Inc. reported increased first-quarter adjusted earnings in Q1 2024 ($1.69 per share) and revenue growth of 11% year-over-year to $8.25 billion. All key operating segments showed growth, driven by its generative AI ecosystem including Einstein GPT, Slack GPT, and Tableau GPT. CEO Marc Benioff highlighted the role of the Salesforce GPT Trust Layer in securing customer data and enhancing enterprise automation.

title: Google head of communications and advisor to CEO Sundar Pichai to exit
summary: Corey duBrowa, Google's head of communications and a key advisor to CEO Sundar Pichai, is leaving the company after five years. He will be taking on the role of chief executive officer at BCW Global, a public relations firm based in New York.

title: Apple Bets On The Emerging Markets Growth Story
summary: Apple is increasingly focusing on emerging markets, particularly India, to drive growth amidst a challenging global smartphone market. The company achieved marginal growth in iPhone sales despite overall industry decline, with India seeing significant retail and manufacturing expansion plans. While services and financial technology offer long-term opportunities, the emerging markets strategy is a key component of Apple's immediate growth narrative, alongside ongoing share repurchases and dividend increases.

title: Vimeo is tuning out its streaming TV apps
summary: Vimeo is discontinuing its dedicated streaming TV apps for Apple TV, Android TV, Amazon Fire TV, and Roku starting June 27th. Users can still watch Vimeo content on their TVs by casting from the Vimeo mobile app via Apple AirPlay 2 or Google Chromecast. This move aligns with Vimeo's strategic shift in recent years from a consumer-facing streaming destination to focusing on video-streaming services for businesses.

title: Microsoft Inks Deal With NVIDIA-Backed CoreWeave
summary: Microsoft has signed a deal with NVIDIA-backed cloud-computing startup CoreWeave, planning to invest billions to bolster its cloud computing infrastructure for AI operations. This partnership will provide Microsoft easier access to NVIDIA’s GPUs, which are crucial for AI-based applications like ChatGPT, and aims to give Microsoft a competitive edge against rivals like Alphabet Inc. and Amazon Web Services. CoreWeave’s access to over 45,000 GPUs and its status as an elite CSP in the NVIDIA Partner Network positions it as a key enabler for Microsoft's AI endeavors.

title: Introducing Apple Vision Pro: Apple’s first spatial computer
summary: Apple has unveiled Apple Vision Pro, its first spatial computer, which blends digital content with the physical world using a three-dimensional user interface controlled by eyes, hands, and voice. Running on the new visionOS, Vision Pro features an ultra-high-resolution display system, custom Apple silicon, and offers new ways to interact with apps, enjoy entertainment, relive memories, and connect via FaceTime. The device is designed with advanced materials, focuses on privacy, and will be available early next year starting at $3,499 (U.S.).

title: Rep. Zoe Lofgren weighs in on quantum plans
summary: Rep. Zoe Lofgren, ranking member of the House Science Committee, discussed her objectives for reauthorizing the National Quantum Initiative Act (NQIA). Her goals include incorporating NASA, increasing fundamental scientific investment, and focusing on commercialization, while emphasizing bipartisanship. Lofgren also commented on the funding of the CHIPS and Science Act and the feasibility of a new package given the current political climate.

title: Sonos Prevails Over Google in $32.5 Million Lawsuit Win
summary: A San Francisco federal jury has ordered Google to pay $32.5 million to Santa Barbara-based Sonos for infringing on one of its wireless audio device patents. This decision, made on May 26, is part of ongoing legal battles between the two companies, stemming from a 2020 lawsuit by Sonos alleging patent infringement by Google in its Home and Chromecast Audio products. While Sonos had initially sought up to $3 billion in damages, the final settlement was reduced to $32.5 million.

title: IT and Networking | Large Enterprise Solutions
summary: Lumen offers large enterprise IT and networking solutions designed to prepare businesses for the AI revolution, addressing the concerns of 86% of CIOs about network readiness. The platform provides secure, scalable, and high-performance connectivity, cybersecurity, and collaboration tools, leveraging a globally peered network and a programmable network fabric for AI workloads. Lumen also provides various infrastructure, connectivity, security, and communication services, featuring customer success stories and extensive resources for intelligent enterprise solutions.

title: TCL Takes Step Toward Its Own TVOS With Cineverse Deal For FAST Content and Streaming Tech
summary: TCL is moving to develop its own streaming operating system by partnering with Cineverse. This deal involves TCL licensing Cineverse's FAST channels and using Cineverse's Matchpoint SaaS streaming distribution platform for its phones and smart TVs in North America, marking a shift away from its previous reliance on Roku and Google TV. The Cineverse content and tech will first appear as a new streaming app on TCL TVs powered by Google TV, and later on TCL's Roku TVs.

title: Traders Could Use Google as an Alternate Play on AI
summary: Google is presented as an alternative investment for traders looking to capitalize on the growing artificial intelligence (AI) trend, given its increasing focus on AI capabilities beyond its search engine. The article discusses Direxion Investments' ETFs (GGLL and GGLS) for both bullish and bearish plays on Google, as well as the Direxion Daily Robotics, Artificial Intelligence & Automation Index Bull 2X ETF (UBOT) for a broader, leveraged AI investment.

title: A breakdown of AI ETFs as tech names burn bright
summary: This article breaks down various AI-focused ETFs available in Europe, examining their methodologies, holdings, and performance. It highlights the differences in how these ETFs define and capture the AI theme, from market-cap weighted exposure to companies involved in big data and NLP to equally-weighted exposure based on AI-related revenue or IP. The piece also compares their costs, liquidity, and correlation of returns, noting that while some are dominated by large tech names, others offer differentiated exposure to companies leveraging machine learning.

title: Additions to Fortinet FortiFlex deliver more flexible licensing options to customers
summary: Fortinet Inc. has introduced new product and service additions to its FortiFlex program, offering customers more flexible licensing options, including support for physical FortiGate next-generation firewall appliances. The program, which uses a points system for usage-based pricing, is now available via private offer in the Amazon Web Services Inc. Marketplace. FortiFlex aims to address the evolving needs of highly distributed networks by allowing organizations to pay by usage and manage licenses more efficiently across various deployment types.

title: Accenture looks to power AI efforts with $3 billion investment
summary: Accenture announced a significant $3 billion investment over three years into its data and AI practice, aiming to double the number of employees working with AI to 80,000. This move highlights a broader industry trend of companies scaling up generative AI offerings, following similar initiatives by Microsoft, Alphabet, and Salesforce. The investment also comes after recent layoffs at Accenture and includes the launch of an "AI Navigator for Enterprise" tool to guide businesses in AI adoption.

title: AMD's AI chip challenge to Nvidia remains uphill fight with no big customers named
summary: AMD unveiled new details about its MI300X artificial intelligence chip, designed to challenge Nvidia's market dominance, but did not announce any major customers for the product. While the chip offers significant memory capacity aimed at cost reduction for AI services, the lack of customer commitments disappointed Wall Street, causing AMD's stock to fall. Analysts noted that Nvidia's strong market position is reinforced by its software ecosystem, which AMD is attempting to counter with its Rocm platform.

title: Someone is posing as a fake security company to create malicious GitHub repositories
summary: An unidentified actor is creating malicious GitHub repositories under the guise of a fake security company, High Sierra Cyber Security, offering exploits for popular products but delivering malware. These threat actors went to great lengths to establish fake personas and accounts, even using real security researchers' headshots. VulnCheck researchers, who uncovered this activity, warn that security researchers are valuable targets and advise caution when downloading code from GitHub, recommending vetting code thoroughly or running unknown exploits in isolated virtual machines.

title: Fidelity Metaverse ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (FMET)
summary: The Fidelity Metaverse ETF (FMET) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" with an average price target equal to its current share price of $36.66. The rating is based on 613 analyst ratings covering 20 companies (69.5% of the portfolio) over the past year. The ETF's top holdings include major tech companies like AMD, Alphabet, Apple, and NVIDIA.

title: EPAM Announces Strategic Global Partnership with Google Cloud
summary: EPAM Systems, Inc. announced a strategic global partnership with Google Cloud to develop and deploy AI-first solutions for enterprise digital transformation. This enhanced collaboration will leverage Google Cloud's AI and ML capabilities with EPAM's engineering expertise to deliver innovative cloud-enabled AI, data modernization, and analytics solutions across various industry verticals. The partnership aims to help clients accelerate their transformations into AI-enabled businesses and drive growth.

title: Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (METV)
summary: This article provides an aggregate rating and price target for the Roundhill Ball Metaverse ETF (METV) based on Wall Street analyst ratings of its holdings. METV has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" and a price target of $16.79, derived from 595 analyst ratings covering 18 companies within its portfolio. The top holdings include tech giants like Apple, Roblox, NVIDIA, Alphabet, and Meta Platforms, along with their individual analyst ratings and potential upsides.

title: Alger 35 ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (ATFV)
summary: The Alger 35 ETF (ATFV) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" based on 624 analyst ratings covering 24 companies, representing 89.7% of its portfolio. The ETF's aggregate price target is $33.33, with a range between $33.33 and $33.33. This rating is derived from a detailed assessment of its holdings by Wall Street analysts over the past year.

title: Sensex, Nifty Decline As RIL, ICICI Bank Drag: Market Wrap
summary: India's benchmark stock indices, Sensex and Nifty, declined on Monday despite opening higher, primarily dragged down by heavyweight stocks like ICICI Bank Ltd. and Reliance Industries Ltd. The S&P BSE Sensex fell by 216 points, and the NSE Nifty 50 closed 71 points lower. This decline mirrored a cautious global market sentiment following a strong second-quarter rally in Wall Street that lost momentum.

title: Comparing the Expense Ratios of ETFs Investing in AI: Which One Is Right for You?
summary: This article compares the expense ratios and year-to-date returns of four ETFs that invest in artificial intelligence, focusing on options with lower fees. It discusses broad-based tech ETFs like FTEC, IETC, and IYW, and a new economies composite ETF, KOMP, highlighting their investment strategies and top holdings. The piece advises investors to consider expense ratios, YTD returns, investment goals, risk tolerance, and portfolio diversification when choosing an AI-focused ETF.

title: HP Enterprise Offers Access to Cray Supercomputers for Building AI Models
summary: HP Enterprise is positioning itself as a key player in the AI revolution by offering access to its Cray supercomputers for building AI models. This move follows the trend of generative AI boosting other tech giants. HP Enterprise hopes to join the list of companies benefiting from the growing demand for AI development.

title: Intel pairs its hardware with HPE solutions to drive enterprise AI success
summary: Intel Corp. and Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. have partnered for 30 years and are now collaborating to deliver AI-focused solutions for enterprises. Intel's strategy involves powerful hardware like its GPU Max Series with 100 billion transistors and a comprehensive software suite called OpenVINO for training video-based AI use cases. This partnership aims to make advanced AI technology accessible to various sectors, including government, cloud companies, and general enterprises.

title: Some ESG Issues Take Center Stage at Big Tech Investor Meetings
summary: ESG principles are increasingly important at big tech companies, with shareholder demands evolving beyond traditional environmental concerns to focus on social and governance issues. Recent investor meetings at Alphabet and Meta, both holdings in the Invesco ESG Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQMG), saw a rise in shareholder resolutions concerning online safety, gender/racial equity, human rights, and executive compensation. This trend highlights the growing emphasis on the "S" and "G" in ESG, potentially positioning QQMG as a relevant investment for those seeking exposure to mega-cap growth with an ESG focus.

title: Amazon Is Spending $100 Million to Teach Cloud Customers About AI
summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is investing $100 million in a new Generative AI Innovation Center. This initiative aims to help its cloud customers develop and deploy AI products, connecting them with AI and machine learning experts. The move positions Amazon to compete with Microsoft and Google in the rapidly expanding generative AI market, with early users including Highspot, Twilio, Ryanair, and Lonely Planet.

title: Baidu: Why It’s One Of The Best Chinese Stocks To Own
summary: Baidu is presented as a strong opportunity among Chinese stocks despite current market underperformance. The article highlights its strengthening financials, particularly in ads and cloud, and its bullish innovation in AI with Ernie Bot and Apollo Go. Analyst upgrades and a significant price target increase by Morgan Stanley reinforce its potential for substantial upside.

title: Zoox headcount grows as Amazon's self-driving unit expands testing in Vegas
summary: Amazon's self-driving vehicle unit, Zoox, has increased its headcount by 16% to about 2,200 employees despite a challenging environment for autonomous driving companies. The unit is expanding testing of its driverless robotaxis on public roads in Las Vegas, preparing for a commercial launch. This expansion comes as other major players like Ford and Volkswagen have scaled back their autonomous driving ambitions.

title: AI Startup Typeface Raises $100 Million From Salesforce, GV
summary: Typeface Inc., an AI startup specializing in content creation services for businesses, has successfully raised $100 million in funding from investors including Salesforce Ventures and Alphabet’s GV. This investment values Typeface at $1 billion and increases its total fundraising to $165 million, reflecting growing investor interest in generative AI technologies.

title: Typeface lands $100M for its generative AI content creation engine
summary: Typeface Inc., a generative AI content creation platform, announced a $100 million Series B funding round, bringing its total raised to $165 million and valuing the company at $1 billion. The funding, led by Salesforce Ventures and other major VCs, will be used for sales and product development. Typeface's platform helps businesses automate and personalize content creation across various functions, including text and image generation, and has already secured partnerships with Salesforce and Google Cloud.

title: Oakmark Equity and Income Fund: Second Calendar Quarter 2023
summary: The Oakmark Equity and Income Fund gained 5.3% in Q2 2023, outperforming the Lipper Balanced Fund Index, despite market concentration in large-cap tech stocks. The fund adjusted its portfolio by reducing technology exposure and initiating new positions, primarily in the healthcare sector, based on a value-driven investment philosophy. The article also reflects on the foundational principles of diversification in portfolio construction, influenced by economist Harry Markowitz, and the fund's approach to asset allocation.

title: Q3 Stock Market Outlook: Time to Batten Down the Hatches or Raise the Sail?
summary: Morningstar's Q3 2023 stock market outlook suggests that while the U.S. market is still undervalued, the margin of safety has decreased, and the rally in the first half of the year has been disproportionately driven by a few "Magnificent Seven" stocks. Investors are advised to take profits from overextended growth and technology stocks and reallocate to undervalued sectors like communications and real estate, and to value, mid-cap, and small-cap stocks. The outlook anticipates slower market gains due to economic slowdown, tight monetary policy, and reduced credit availability, but expects any future pullbacks to be relatively shallow.

title: Apple makes history as first $3 trillion company amid tech stock surge
summary: Apple Inc. achieved a historic milestone, becoming the first company to reach a $3 trillion market value, driven by investor optimism about its expansion into new markets and a projected easing of interest rate hikes. This accomplishment is set against a backdrop of a broader tech stock surge, with other companies like Tesla and Nvidia also seeing significant gains due to advancements in AI and strategic partnerships. The article highlights Apple's strong financial performance and its reputation as a safe investment, alongside the success of other tech giants.

title: Why India Could Be the Next Big Destination for Global Investors
summary: India is rapidly emerging as a significant global economic player and an attractive destination for foreign investors, building stronger strategic and commercial ties with the U.S. Major global corporations are increasing their investments in the country, recognizing its flourishing economy driven by a growing middle class, favorable demographics, and policy reforms. Projections suggest India could become the world's second-largest economy by 2075, offering substantial opportunities despite existing challenges.

title: Communication Services Stock Outlook: As Meta and Alphabet Soar, Traditional Telecom Comes Under Renewed Pressure
summary: This article discusses the contrasting performances within the communication services sector, highlighting the surge in Meta and Alphabet shares due to renewed online advertising demand and AI excitement. Conversely, traditional telecom companies like Verizon and AT&T face renewed pressure, though recent price increases and cost-cutting efforts are expected to improve their cash flow. The report also provides specific picks and fair value estimates for Disney, Verizon Communications, and Pinterest.

title: Kopin Corp is the Technology Seen Behind Smart AR Glasses
summary: Kopin Corp (NASDAQ: KOPN), a high-resolution microdisplay maker, is highlighted as a key technology provider for smart AR glasses, including supplying displays for Vuzix and Alphabet. Despite an 80% drop from 2021 highs, the company has made significant advancements, such as achieving 3 million nits brightness for its 2K x 2K MicroLED displays, essential for AR in bright conditions. Kopin's Q1 2023 results showed increased product revenues, especially in defense, and the new CEO emphasized renewed focus on quality and efficiency to improve margins and achieve cash flow breakdown by year-end.

title: Worldwide Public Cloud Services Revenues Surpass $500 Billion in 2022 -
summary: Worldwide public cloud services revenue reached $545.8 billion in 2022, a 22.9% increase from the previous year, with SaaS – Applications being the largest revenue source. Microsoft, Amazon Web Services, Salesforce Inc., Google, and Oracle remain the dominant providers, collectively capturing over 41% of the global revenue. IDC predicts continued growth in foundational cloud services, especially IaaS and PaaS, driven by digital transformation and AI integration.

title: First-of-its-Kind App, TheStreet Powered by Tornado, Launches One-Stop Investing Experience
summary: TheStreet and Tornado have launched a new app specifically designed to provide a comprehensive investing experience for all levels of investors. The app integrates TheStreet's news and investing ideas with Tornado's technology and community, offering tools like expert Q&A, tailored notifications, S&P Capital IQ data, and portfolio optimization. This collaboration aims to empower investors with knowledge and tools, expanding reach to a new audience.

title: Trading Analysis Tools - Analytics and Chart Tools
summary: FOREX.com offers a suite of trading analysis tools, including Performance Analytics powered by Chasing Returns to help traders refine their skills through behavioral science insights. The platform also provides advanced charts integrated with TradingView, offering extensive indicators, drawing tools, and customization options for technical traders. Additionally, users can access real-time market news from Reuters, expert market analysis, an economic calendar, and various alert features to keep track of market movements.

title: Indian-origin Indra Nooyi and Jayshree Ullal among America’s richest
summary: Four Indian-origin women, including Jayshree Ullal and Indra Nooyi, have been featured in Forbes' list of 'America’s Richest Self-Made Women,' with a combined net worth of USD 4.06 billion. Jayshree Ullal, President and CEO of Arista Networks, is ranked 15th with $2.4 billion, while Indra Nooyi, former PepsiCo CEO, is 77th with $350 million. Neerja Sethi and Neha Narkhede also made the list, reflecting significant achievements in technology and business.

title: SEC acknowledges bitcoin ETF filings from BlackRock, Bitwise and others
summary: The SEC has formally acknowledged spot bitcoin ETF applications from major asset managers including BlackRock and Bitwise. This move follows a surge of institutional filings, notably spurred by BlackRock's application in June, indicating a significant step forward in the regulatory review process for these financial products. The applications are now officially under review, with various firms looking to leverage this new investment opportunity.

title: Chinese e-commerce giant JD.com launches own large language model, ChatRhino
summary: Chinese e-commerce giant JD.com has launched its own large language model (LLM) called ChatRhino, aiming to integrate AI into various industrial applications. The company emphasizes that the value of LLMs will be realized through industrial use cases, with ChatRhino designed to offer targeted solutions across sectors like retail, logistics, finance, and healthcare by combining general data with intelligent supply chain data. This move reflects the broader Chinese tech industry's efforts to catch up with Western advancements in AI and aligns with the government's push to regulate LLMs.

title: Why the Nasdaq 100 may not capture the full growth potential of AI
summary: This article argues that while the Nasdaq-100 has seen significant gains from AI-driven tech stocks like Nvidia, it may not offer investors the most comprehensive or effective exposure to the long-term growth potential of artificial intelligence. The authors suggest that dedicated AI thematic strategies can provide broader diversification into pure-play AI companies, capture the growth of future mega-caps earlier, and improve risk-adjusted returns compared to a core allocation in the Nasdaq-100. They emphasize that while the Nasdaq-100 taps into AI through a few large companies, a targeted AI strategy offers more comprehensive exposure across the evolving AI landscape.

title: Caribbean phone companies suffering from “market failure” due to Over-The Top services, Social Media
summary: Caribbean telecommunication companies are experiencing "market failure" due to the prevalent use of Over-The-Top (OTT) services and social media, which consume the majority of data and international call traffic, leading to significant financial losses for local providers. Despite annual investments exceeding US$500 million in network upgrades since 2017, these companies face declining voice revenues and a lack of significant contribution from major tech companies that utilize their infrastructure. Industry leaders are advocating for government intervention and a unified CARICOM approach to address this imbalance and ensure a fair share from big tech, warning of dire consequences for regional connectivity and economic development if the issue remains unresolved.

title: Unstructured raises $25M for large language model data processing expansion
summary: Unstructured Technologies Inc. has secured $25 million in new funding to expand its operations for processing data for large language models. Founded by a former CIA analyst, the company provides a platform that converts unstructured internal data into LLM-compatible formats, addressing the challenge of scattered data for organizations. The funding round was led by Bain Capital Venture Associates LLC with participation from several other investors.

title: Nasdaq Is Reducing the Influence of Giant Tech Firms in a Popular Stock Market Index
summary: Nasdaq is implementing a "special rebalance" of its Nasdaq 100 index to reduce the dominance of giant tech firms like Apple and Microsoft. This move addresses the historic rally of a few mega-cap tech stocks, which have caused overconcentration in the index and potentially increased diversification risk for investors. The rebalance, effective July 24, will prompt funds tracking the index to adjust their portfolios, potentially leading to trading costs and minor market volatility.

title: TD Cowen: Record year expected as US data center leases hit 2.1GW in 90 days due to AI boom
summary: TD Cowen reports that the US data center market is poised for a record year, with 2.1GW of leases signed in the last 90 days, primarily driven by artificial intelligence demand. Hyperscalers like Google and Microsoft have secured massive deals, leading to a significant tightening of the leasing market and a shift towards pre-leasing capacity further in advance. This boom is expected to benefit companies like Equinix and Digital Realty, though supply chain issues with long lead times for equipment persist.

title: Barbenheimer and Redefining Movie Marketing Strategies
summary: The "Barbenheimer" phenomenon, involving the simultaneous release and unexpected mutual bolstering of "Barbie" and "Oppenheimer," significantly boosted the theatrical movie business. Both films achieved impressive box office numbers domestically, with "Barbie" leveraging social media, especially TikTok, to drive engagement and redefine marketing strategies. This success highlights the importance of tailored social media approaches and presents new opportunities for long-term growth and audience connection for brands.

title: Apple, Meta, Google argue they're not 'bookies' in casino app appeal
summary: Apple, Meta, and Google are appealing a 2022 ruling that found them potentially liable for facilitating purchases of virtual chips in casino game apps, arguing that this interpretation of Section 230 could jeopardize the entire internet economy. They contend that holding platforms liable for payment processing related to third-party content would force extensive monitoring and is a misinterpretation of existing legal precedents. The companies emphasize they act as publishers offering payment services, not creators of the allegedly illegal apps or chips, and that their conduct in these cases is distinct from prior rulings.

title: Google’s Co-Founder, Larry Page, gains $5 billion to his net worth
summary: Larry Page, co-founder of Alphabet Inc., has seen his net worth increase by $5.4 billion, reaching an impressive $109 billion and placing him 12th on Forbes' list of richest billionaires. This surge is primarily due to a 5.94% rise in Alphabet's share price following a strong second-quarter earnings report that surpassed analyst expectations. The company demonstrated exceptional performance in ad revenues for both Google and YouTube, and its Google Cloud business reported a profit for the second consecutive quarter.

title: YieldMax GOOGL Option Income Strategy ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:GOOY)
summary: This article provides detailed information on the YieldMax GOOGL Option Income Strategy ETF (GOOY), including its current price, dividend yield, expense ratio, and holdings. The ETF, launched on July 27, 2023, aims to generate income and capped gains from Alphabet Inc. stock through a synthetic covered call strategy. It also includes recent news headlines and frequently asked questions about the ETF's performance and investor details.

title: YieldMax Launches Synthetic Covered Call ETFs for Meta, Google
summary: YieldMax has introduced two new actively managed ETFs, the YieldMax META Option Income Strategy ETF (FBY) and the YieldMax GOOGL Option Income Strategy ETF (GOOY), designed to generate monthly income through synthetic covered call strategies on Meta and Alphabet stock, respectively. These ETFs, which do not directly invest in the underlying stocks, follow the recent launch of the YieldMax AMZN Option Income Strategy ETF (AMZY) and cater to increasing advisor interest in options-based income strategies. Both new funds carry an expense ratio of 0.99% and are advised by Tidal Financial Group and subadvised by ZEGA Financial.

title: After Earnings, Is Alphabet Stock a Buy, Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar analyzes Alphabet's Q2 earnings, highlighting accelerated search growth, YouTube ad revenue recovery, and cloud service stability. The article maintains its Fair Value Estimate of $161 per share, rating Alphabet's stock as undervalued with a wide economic moat due to intangible assets and network effects, despite high uncertainty related to advertising reliance.

title: AI and Data Centers: It’s About Keeping Cool
summary: A report from Citi Research highlights the critical role data centers play in the AI revolution, emphasizing that keeping servers cool will be vital. Despite historical predictions, data center energy use has been largely stable due to efficiency gains, but AI's computational demands are accelerating, making cooling and renewable energy increasingly important for sustainability. The article notes that while AI compute has outpaced Moore's Law, increased scrutiny of carbon emissions will drive higher corporate renewable PPAs.

title: Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 07/30/2023
summary: This article details the top-performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week ending July 30, 2023, highlighting that these funds are high-risk due to leverage. Key performers include China-focused ETFs like Direxion Daily FTSE China Bull 3X Shares (YINN) and Direxion Daily CSI China Internet Index Bull 2x Shares (CWEB), along with those tracking electric vehicles (EVAV) and specific tech giants like Meta (FBL) and Google (GGLL). The performance was driven by factors such as China's policy actions, growth in the EV sector, and strong tech earnings.

title: Best Augmented Reality & Virtual Reality Stocks In 2023: December List
summary: This article identifies top Augmented Reality (AR) and Virtual Reality (VR) stocks for 2023, acknowledging the market's slow but potential growth, similar to the early days of smartphones. It emphasizes investing in stable, well-capitalized companies that are involved in AR/VR but do not solely depend on it for revenue, such as Alphabet, Apple, Autodesk, Meta, and Microsoft. The article also highlights the importance of risk management, portfolio diversification, and continuous monitoring of emerging trends in this evolving technological landscape.

title: White Castle to roll out voice AI to over 100 drive-thrus
summary: White Castle is partnering with SoundHound to implement voice AI in over 100 drive-thrus by the end of 2024, continuing its early adoption of restaurant automation. This technology aims to automate order taking, process orders within 60 seconds with a 90% completion rate, and potentially boost revenue through consistent upsells. This move follows an earlier trial in 2020 and places White Castle among other QSRs testing drive-thru AI solutions to enhance efficiency and address labor savings.

title: T‑Mobile Launches First‑Ever 5G Network Slicing Beta for Developers
summary: T-Mobile has launched its first-ever 5G network slicing beta program for developers, initially focusing on enhancing video calling applications. This initiative leverages T-Mobile's nationwide 5G Standalone (5G SA) network to offer customized network slices that provide optimized performance for applications requiring consistent speeds, lower latency, and increased reliability. Major companies like Dialpad, Google, Webex by Cisco, and Zoom are already participating in the beta to develop next-generation communication tools.

title: phoenixNAP Powers Expanded AI and Cloud Services with Energy-saving, Ampere-Based Servers from Hewlett Packard Enterprise
summary: phoenixNAP, a global IT services provider, is expanding its Bare Metal Cloud platform by deploying cloud-native HPE ProLiant RL300 Gen11 servers across its 18 data centers. These servers utilize energy-efficient Ampere® Computing processors, enhancing performance and energy efficiency for AI inferencing, cloud gaming, and cloud-native workloads. This expansion addresses the growing demand for data-intensive workloads while mitigating the environmental impact of increasing energy consumption in data centers.

title: Gen Digital earnings beat, revenue was in line with estimates
summary: Gen Digital (NASDAQ: GEN) reported its first-quarter earnings, with an EPS of $0.47, surpassing analyst estimates of $0.46. The company's revenue for the quarter was $946 million, matching the consensus estimate. Gen Digital's stock has seen an 8.57% increase in the last three months, though it is down 30.00% over the last 12 months.

title: Tinder’s new AI feature builds the perfect profile by choosing your photos
summary: Tinder is introducing a new AI feature that selects the best photos for users' dating profiles to enhance matchmaking and reduce the "awkwardness" of online dating. The feature is currently in testing and aims to help users create more appealing profiles by leveraging artificial intelligence. This initiative comes as Tinder's parent company, Match Group, explores broader AI applications, including AI-generated bios, to combat user "swipe burnout" and dating fatigue, aligning with findings that Gen Z is open to using AI for dating profiles.

title: Top 20 Atlassian Competitors and Alternatives
summary: This article identifies and details 20 major competitors and alternatives to Atlassian, an Australian software company known for products like Jira and Confluence. The list includes technology giants such as Microsoft, IBM, Google Workspace, and ServiceNow, as well as specialized platforms like GitLab, Asana, and Slack. Each competitor's founding year, headquarters, market focus, and key offerings are highlighted, demonstrating the diverse landscape of tools available for project management, collaboration, DevSecOps, and enterprise software.

title: Google Meet vs Zoom: Which Video Platform Is Best?
summary: This article compares Google Meet and Zoom, two leading video conferencing platforms, to determine which is best for various business needs. It concludes that Google Meet offers better value, especially for businesses integrated with Google Workspace, due to its affordability and comprehensive features. Zoom, however, excels in user experience and boasts more advanced host controls for larger webinars, despite a past security settlement.

title: Verizon to ‘sunset’ Blue Jeans vidconf platform
summary: Verizon is discontinuing its BlueJeans video collaboration platform, which it acquired in April 2020. The company cited "changing market conditions in a post-pandemic landscape" as the reason, shifting its focus to other priorities like 5G and mobile edge computing. The move highlights the intense competition in the video conferencing market and the impact of evolving work trends.

title: Unlocking the potential of AI for the energy industry
summary: Artificial intelligence is transforming the energy industry by enhancing safety, accelerating subsurface characterization, and promoting sustainability through autonomous operations. To fully realize its potential, the industry must adopt open and secure data platforms, embrace rapid experimentation, integrate new technologies, and develop an AI-trained workforce. SLB is addressing these needs through open data initiatives, accessible AI tools, specialized innovation centers, and a "Domain Data Scientist" training program.

title: YieldMax Launches Option Income Strategy ETF on Coinbase
summary: YieldMax has launched the YieldMax COIN Option Income Strategy ETF (CONY) on NYSE Arca, aiming to generate monthly income through a synthetic covered call strategy on Coinbase Global (COIN). This ETF, actively managed by ZEGA Financial, is part of a growing suite of YieldMax option income strategy ETFs focusing on growth companies that may not pay dividends. It provides investors with an opportunity to earn income and potentially protect against downside while gaining exposure to a growth company like Coinbase.

title: Dropbox review: Still the best at syncing and sharing
summary: This PCWorld review reaffirms Dropbox's position as a top choice for fast, reliable file synchronization and sharing, despite its limited free storage and lack of intermediate subscription tiers. While it excels in its core functionality and offers business-oriented features, its pricing model lacks options between the free 2GB and the paid 2TB. The article highlights its strengths for collaborative file access and its backup capabilities.

title: The Next Big Things From SAP
summary: SAP recently unveiled significant innovations at its Sapphire event in Orlando, focusing on responsible AI integrated into business solutions, ledger-based accounting for carbon tracking (green ledger initiative), and industry-specific networks to enhance supply-chain resilience. These advancements aim to help customers transform their business models in the cloud, prioritize sustainability, and boost agility in an evolving future. SAP is also expanding partnerships, like with Google Cloud for open data, and committing to upskill two million people globally to meet the demand for SAP experts.

title: Congress Large Cap Growth ETF Holdings
summary: The Congress Large Cap Growth ETF (CAML), established on August 22, 2023, is an actively managed fund investing in large-cap companies for long-term capital growth. With $333.49 million in assets under management and 41 holdings, its top investments include NVIDIA, Apple, and Alphabet. The ETF focuses on companies anticipated to experience earnings growth.

title: Arm, the Chip Designer, Files for an I.P.O. Expected to Be Among the Largest (Published 2023)
summary: Arm, the chip design company owned by SoftBank, has filed for an initial public offering (IPO) on the Nasdaq exchange, which is anticipated to be one of the largest in recent years. This move follows Nvidia's abandoned bid to acquire Arm for $40 billion 18 months prior. The IPO will provide SoftBank with additional capital for investments, and SoftBank will retain its position as Arm's controlling shareholder.

title: Will India be able to deal with Google’s adtech monopoly?
summary: This article examines Google's monopolization of the adtech sector, detailing how the company's acquisitions and anti-competitive practices have harmed media and newspaper publishers globally, including in India. It discusses actions taken by regulatory bodies in the EU, UK, and US, and suggests that India's current competition regime needs significant amendments to effectively address Google's dominant position and ensure fair market practices. The piece highlights the dire financial consequences for publishers and the need for greater transparency and regulation in the digital advertising landscape.

title: Hedge fund exposure to 7 biggest tech stocks at record high, Goldman Sachs says
summary: Hedge funds have reached record exposure to the seven largest tech stocks by market capitalization, according to Goldman Sachs data, driven by strong performance and the artificial intelligence theme. These seven stocks, including Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft, now constitute about 20% of the total net market value held by hedge funds tracked by Goldman Sachs. Despite the concentration risk, investment managers rationalize these allocations due to the significant returns generated by these companies this year.

title: Avis, Google, Hibbett, other settlement checks in the mail
summary: This article reports on several class action settlement checks being mailed to consumers from companies like Avis, Google, and Hibbett. These settlements address various issues including hidden e-toll fees, FACTA violations, illegal ATM fees, disposable contact lens price-fixing, data breaches, and privacy violations related to Google Photos. Readers have reported receiving payments ranging from tens to over a thousand dollars from these lawsuits.

title: GM, Google exploring ways to use AI across automaker’s business
summary: General Motors and Google are expanding their partnership to explore the integration of AI technologies across GM's business operations. This collaboration builds on their previous work on GM's OnStar Interactive Virtual Assistant, aiming to revolutionize the automotive experience through generative AI. The companies anticipate AI will enhance vehicle validation, software, and in-car assistance, with potential future applications including customer interaction and new features.

title: Get Ready for Google Cloud Firewall Plus, Network Integration Ease
summary: Palo Alto Networks and Google Cloud are introducing two new offerings to enhance cloud security: Cloud Firewall Plus and participation in the Google Cloud Network Service Integration Manager (NSIM). Cloud Firewall Plus provides a scalable, cloud-native firewall with Intrusion Prevention capabilities using Palo Alto Networks Threat Prevention technologies. The NSIM will simplify deployment and integration of VM-Series virtual Next-Generation Firewall (NGFWs), allowing for consistent security management across diverse environments.

title: Worker confidence in AI may be growing despite unease about the tech
summary: A Robert Half survey indicates growing worker confidence in generative AI, with 4 in 10 U.S. workers believing it will positively impact their careers, despite past unease regarding job displacement. The survey, conducted in May 2023, shows HR and tech professionals, along with younger generations, are particularly optimistic about AI's ability to automate tasks and increase efficiency. Companies are increasingly adopting AI for various functions, leading to a surge in demand for AI-related skills.

title: SAP nabs new global AI head from Microsoft, Walter Sun
summary: SAP has appointed Walter Sun, formerly VP of Copilot Applied Artificial Intelligence at Microsoft, as its new global head of AI. This strategic hire underscores SAP's commitment to prioritizing AI, following recent collaborations with Microsoft and Google Cloud to integrate generative AI into its enterprise offerings. Sun, with his extensive background in AI and machine learning, is tasked with leveraging SAP's broad applications and customer base to transform business operations through innovative AI solutions.

title: Bandwidth Partners with Google and Cognigy to Launch AIBridge, Integrating Artificial Intelligence with Contact Centers Using Maestro
summary: Bandwidth has announced a partnership with Google and Cognigy to launch AIBridge, a new capability within its Maestro platform. AIBridge enables enterprises to deploy voice-based AI tools to their contact centers, allowing for faster and more efficient call resolution in the communications cloud without direct agent intervention. This integration is designed to improve customer experience, increase agent satisfaction, and significantly reduce contact center operating costs by leveraging conversational AI from partners like Cognigy.AI and Google Cloud's Dialogflow.

title: Andretti special purpose acquisition company to merge with Boston-based AI startup
summary: The blank-check company started by former IndyCar driver Michael Andretti, Andretti Acquisition Corp., is merging with Boston-based artificial intelligence startup Zapata AI. This merger values Zapata at approximately $200 million, with the combined company's equity value projected to be between $281 million and $365 million depending on share redemptions. The transaction is anticipated to close in the first quarter of next year, bringing together Andretti's SPAC with a "generative AI software company" that has roots in quantum computing and focuses on industrial AI solutions.

title: Snapchat announces new features to protect teenagers from online risks
summary: Snapchat is introducing new features to enhance teen safety by implementing a strike system for inappropriate content and pop-up warnings when strangers attempt to contact them. These measures aim to prevent contact from unknown individuals and expose teens to only age-appropriate content, alongside educational resources for parents. The updates also include requirements for more mutual friends before teens can add new contacts.

title: Why Goldman Sachs believes that Spotify will remain the world’s dominant music streaming service in 2030
summary: Goldman Sachs predicts that Spotify will remain the dominant music streaming service globally through 2030, maintaining its market share around the mid-30% level outside of China. Despite facing competition from larger tech conglomerates and new services like TikTok Music, Spotify's market share outside of China has remained broadly stable since 2017. Goldman Sachs also anticipates YouTube Music will become the second-largest DSP by 2030, surpassing Apple Music, and foresees consolidation among smaller streaming platforms.

title: More writers sue OpenAI for copyright infringement over AI training
summary: A group of U.S. authors, including Pulitzer Prize winner Michael Chabon, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI in federal court in San Francisco. They allege that OpenAI misused their copyrighted writings to train its AI-powered chatbot, ChatGPT, without permission. This is at least the third such class-action lawsuit by authors against OpenAI, with the plaintiffs seeking monetary damages and an injunction against OpenAI's business practices.

title: US, Vietnam firms talk business during Biden visit; AI and Boeing deals unveiled
summary: During U.S. President Joe Biden's visit to Hanoi, executives from top American and Vietnamese firms in technology, semiconductors, and aviation met to establish new business partnerships. The meeting led to significant deals, including Vietnam Airlines purchasing 50 Boeing 737 Max jets and plans for Microsoft and Nvidia to advance AI solutions and chip design centers in Vietnam. These collaborations aim to deepen cooperation in key technological sectors and reinforce Vietnam's role in global supply chains, especially in chipmaking.

title: OpenAI’s Sam Altman tells Salesforce’s Marc Benioff that AI ‘hallucinations’ are more feature than bug
summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman suggested to Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff that AI "hallucinations" should be seen as a feature, not a bug, emphasizing their creative potential. This discussion took place at the Dreamforce conference where Salesforce rolled out new AI products, integrating with platforms like Google Workspace. Altman's perspective highlights a focus on generative AI's creative capabilities over its strict adherence to factual accuracy.

title: Pulitzer winner Chabon, other authors sue Meta over AI program
summary: A group of writers, including Pulitzer Prize winner Michael Chabon, Tony-winning playwright David Henry Hwang, and authors Matthew Klam, Rachel Louise Snyder, and Ayelet Waldman, have sued Meta Platforms in San Francisco federal court. They accuse Meta of misusing their copyrighted works to train its Llama artificial-intelligence software, alleging that pirated versions of their writings were used in the training datasets. This lawsuit follows a similar proposed class-action suit against OpenAI by the same authors, highlighting a growing legal challenge for AI companies regarding intellectual property.

title: In Silico Part - IV : AI and its impact on labor, productivity, and tech-driven deflation
summary: This article examines the uncertain macroeconomic impacts of Artificial Intelligence, focusing on its potential effects on labor, productivity, and deflation. It discusses AI's role in creative destruction, its likelihood of augmenting rather than fully replacing human labor in the near term, and its historical and future contributions to productivity growth and deflationary pressures. The authors conclude that AI is anticipated to boost productivity and exert deflationary forces, while acknowledging that long-term labor displacement is possible, leading to the creation of new job categories.

title: Publishers’ Lawsuit Accuses Libgen of “Staggering” Copyright Infringement
summary: Major publishers including Cengage, Macmillan Learning, McGraw Hill, and Pearson Education have filed a copyright infringement lawsuit against Library Genesis (Libgen), a prominent "shadow library," in the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York. The lawsuit claims "staggering" levels of infringement, with over six million files on Libgen infringing copyrights, and seeks damages, an injunction, and control over Libgen's domains. Publishers argue that Libgen's operations cause significant financial and creative harm, undermining the publishing industry and authors' income, despite the site's claim of providing free access to educational literature.

title: Manyika appointed to Airbnb board
summary: Google's Research, Technology and Society senior vice-president, James Manyika, a Zimbabwean-born businessman, has been appointed to the board of American online homestay firm, Airbnb Inc., effective immediately. Manyika, an expert in AI and technology's impact on society, will help Airbnb leverage artificial intelligence and joins eight other board members at the US$91.96 billion valued company. His extensive background includes roles at McKinsey & Company, advising the White House, and serving on various influential boards, bringing significant expertise in technology, economy, and global trends to Airbnb.

title: Morgan Stanley’s AI Assistant Marks New Era For Finance Sector
summary: Morgan Stanley has launched an internal AI assistant, 'AI @ Morgan Stanley Assistant', built on OpenAI's GPT-4 software. This move positions Morgan Stanley as a pioneer among major banks in utilizing generative AI for financial advisors and support staff. While the bank also faces a significant lawsuit, its stock saw a slight increase following the AI announcement, signaling a new era for AI in the finance sector.

title: Communications ETF XLC Offers Loud & Clear Exposure to the Space
summary: The Communication Services Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLC) provides investors with exposure to the growing digital side of the U.S. economy, encompassing communications, media, and technology companies. Launched following a 2018 GICS reclassification, XLC tracks a market-cap-weighted index of U.S. telecom, media, and entertainment components of the S&P 500, with top holdings including Meta Platforms and Alphabet. This ETF offers an accessible and low-cost way to invest in leading digital economy companies.

title: Google to Pay $93 Million for Tricking Users
summary: Google has agreed to pay $93 million to the state of California for violating consumer protection laws by deceptively tracking users' locations. The settlement stems from a multi-year investigation revealing that Google continued to collect location data for advertising even after users attempted to opt out. As part of the agreement, Google must increase transparency regarding its location tracking practices and provide users with more control and information about how their data is used.

title: 3 Quantum Computing Stocks That Should Be on Every Investor’s Radar This Fall
summary: This article highlights three leading quantum computing stocks: IBM, Alphabet, and Microsoft, emphasizing their significant advancements in the field. IBM is recognized for its quantum breakthrough with over 100 qubits. Alphabet's quantum computer can solve complex problems far faster than traditional supercomputers, while Microsoft is advancing quantum computing to revolutionize chemistry and materials science.

title: Expedia Group builds generative AI into multiple products in its “Fall Release”
summary: Expedia Group is unveiling its "Fall Release 2023," introducing numerous features and products for travelers and supplier partners, heavily integrating generative AI. Key updates include AI-powered conversational capabilities within its apps, dynamic travel guides, and "EG Labs" for consumer feedback on new tech. The release also brings influencer content, group trip planning tools, and AI-driven recommendations for hotel partners to enhance performance and guest insights.

title: What Market Pros Saw Last Earnings Season That Spurred Investments in Tech and AI Stocks
summary: Market experts, managing over $67 billion, analyzed second-quarter earnings reports and concluded that the bull market has room to run, a U.S. recession is unlikely, consumer spending remains strong, and AI is not in a bubble but rather a significant driver of corporate profits. They identified key sectors and companies benefiting from current growth trends, including broad-market strength beyond AI and Big Tech, and a re-emerging strong momentum in European economies.

title: SK Telecom to triple investment in AI
summary: SK Telecom, Korea's largest mobile carrier, plans to triple its investment in AI, shifting its focus from its traditional telecommunications business. The company aims for 33% of its total investment over the next five years to go into AI, targeting 25 trillion won ($18.5 billion) in annual revenue by 2028. Its AI strategy centers on its Korean large language model A dot, urban air mobility (UAM), and AI health care solutions.

title: The fraud of the Writers Guild contract: Reject and mobilize against this rotten deal!
summary: This article criticizes the tentative agreement reached by the Writers Guild of America (WGA) with studios, networks, and streaming services, urging writers to reject it as a "fraud." The author argues the WGA leadership does not represent its members' interests, highlighting issues like the isolation of the strike, the anti-democratic process of ending the strike before ratification, and the perceived inadequacies of the contract regarding residuals and AI. The article claims the deal benefits corporations and will not prevent job losses or the decline in writers' incomes, calling for writers to mobilize through rank-and-file committees for a more radical struggle.

title: What Is a CTA and Why It Matters In Marketing (Plus Cool CTA Examples)
summary: This article explains what a Call-to-Action (CTA) is and why it's crucial for digital marketing conversions. It highlights how strong CTAs transform visitors into customers, providing statistics on their effectiveness and offering best practices for creating and optimizing them, including clarity, urgency, and personalization. The article also covers how to measure CTA performance using metrics like Click-Through Rate, Cost Per Click, and Conversion Rate, and provides examples from Spotify, CrazyEgg, and ClickUp.

title: 10 Best Live TV Streaming Services for Cord Cutters in 2023
summary: This article lists the 10 best live TV streaming services for cord-cutters in 2023, based primarily on subscriber numbers. It details the evolving landscape of television consumption, from traditional cable to streaming, highlighting the recent challenges faced by live TV streaming services and YouTube TV's growth due to its NFL Sunday Ticket acquisition. The piece provides an overview of each of the top 10 services, including their features, pricing, and channel lineups.

title: Okta Introduces Okta AI – Securely Connecting People to Technology in the AI Era
summary: Okta, Inc. has announced Okta AI, a new suite of AI-powered capabilities designed to enhance security, user experience, and app development within its Workforce Identity Cloud and Customer Identity Cloud. This initiative leverages Okta's unique threat intelligence and Identity data, in collaboration with Google Cloud's Vertex AI, to accelerate AI innovation for Identity. Okta AI aims to provide real-time Identity actions while maintaining strict privacy and compliance standards for customer data.

title: PayPal Makes Good on Its Apple Pay Card Vow
summary: PayPal has fulfilled its promise to integrate its PayPal and Venmo credit and debit cards with Apple Wallet, nearly a year after the initial vow. This integration allows users to add their cards to Apple Pay for in-app and Safari browser transactions. Former CEO Dan Schulman stated that this move aims to offer more choice to PayPal's merchants and increase branded transactions, citing a similar success with Google Pay in Germany.

title: This bond market is providing double-digit returns
summary: Catastrophe bonds, or "cat bonds," are offering investors double-digit returns, with some seeing as high as 16% this year, despite broader bond market turmoil. These bonds protect insurers and companies from large disaster losses, paying investors attractive coupons if no such events occur. The market dynamically adjusts to rising interest rates and increased risk premiums due to climate change, attracting more asset managers and issuers while requiring investors to accept a unique risk profile.

title: Who owns the news? Mail titles, News UK and Reach dominate, report finds
summary: A new report, "Who Owns the UK Media? 2023," reveals that three companies—DMG Media, News UK, and Reach—control 90% of the UK's national newspaper market and over 40% of the top 50 online news brands. This concentration, also prevalent in local news, raises concerns about media diversity and the influence these conglomerates exert on the national agenda. The report calls for urgent reform to address this concentrated ownership and regulate the dominant tech platforms that act as gatekeepers to news.

title: PacBio Announces Complete Computational Workflow for Human Whole Genome Sequencing Data Analysis
summary: PacBio has announced the availability of its WGS Variant Pipeline, a complete and standardized computational method for analyzing HiFi whole genome sequencing (WGS) data. This new software integrates PacBio and third-party tools into a single workflow, allowing customers to detect various variant types, along with methylation and phasing data, more efficiently than before. The pipeline aims to streamline data analysis, enable deeper biological discovery, and is accessible through several bioinformatics analysis platforms.

title: Citi Maintains Alphabet-A(GOOGL.US) With Buy Rating, Maintains Target Price $280
summary: Citi analyst Ronald Josey has maintained a Buy rating on Alphabet-A (GOOGL.US) and kept the target price at $280. According to TipRanks, the analyst boasts a 60.5% success rate and an average return of 24.6% over the past year. The information is provided for informational purposes and is not investment advice.

title: Google joins Adobe, Microsoft in promising to protect its AI users from copyright claims
summary: Google has announced it will legally protect users of its generative AI products against copyright infringement claims. This move follows similar commitments from other tech giants like Adobe and Microsoft, aiming to alleviate concerns about intellectual property violations in AI-generated content. The commitment means Google will shoulder legal responsibility for its users in such cases.

title: Malicious “RedAlert - Rocket Alerts” application targets Israeli phone calls, SMS, and user information
summary: Cloudflare's Cloudforce One Threat Operations Team discovered a malicious Android application impersonating the legitimate "RedAlert - Rocket Alerts" app, widely used in Israel for airstrike alerts. This malicious version, distributed via a deceptive website, collects sensitive user data including call logs, SMS, contacts, and account information, while mimicking the original app's functionality. Users who installed the Android version from `redalerts[.]me` are advised to delete it immediately and reinstall the authentic app from the Google Play Store.

title: Gartner Identifies the Top 10 Strategic Technology Trends for 2024
summary: Gartner has announced its top 10 strategic technology trends for 2024, emphasizing the need for organizations to act boldly amidst technological disruptions and socioeconomic uncertainties. The trends, presented at Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo 2023, highlight the increasing role of AI, sustainability, and intelligent automation in driving business value and resilience. Key themes include the democratization of Generative AI, AI Trust, Risk and Security Management, AI-Augmented Development, and the emergence of Machine Customers and Industry Cloud Platforms.

title: Will AI Take Your Job? Answer: Yes, No & Awesomely So
summary: This article explores the impact of AI on jobs, arguing against the doomsday predictions of massive layoffs. While acknowledging that job displacement will occur, the author, Satyen K. Bordoloi, emphasizes that AI will also create new opportunities and redefine the job market, leading to increased productivity and a higher standard of living in the long run. The piece encourages individuals to adapt and leverage AI for personal and professional growth, viewing it as an opportunity rather than a threat.

title: DaVita Collaborates with Google Cloud to Develop Customized Clinical Operating System Designed to Transform Kidney Care
summary: DaVita Inc. and Google Cloud have partnered to develop a new clinical operating system called Center Without Walls (CWOW) to transform kidney care for over 200,000 DaVita patients. This system centralizes clinical documentation and leverages AI and analytics from over 30 million dialysis treatments annually to improve clinical outcomes and streamline workflows. Built on Google Cloud's infrastructure, CWOW aims to enhance data sharing between care teams, physician partners, and hospitals, ultimately improving patient quality of life and lowering care costs.

title: Stanford’s AI transparency index shows foundational models are shrouded in secrecy
summary: A Stanford University report reveals that most popular foundational AI models from companies like OpenAI, Google, and Meta lack transparency regarding their development and societal impact. Meta's Llama 2 topped the transparency index, while OpenAI's GPT-4 scored lower despite extensive public information from partners, indicating that open-source models generally lead in transparency. The researchers highlight the need for greater disclosure to inform policymakers and foster trust in AI, especially as regulations like the EU's Artificial Intelligence Act emerge.

title: Google Pay Enters Lending Business In India
summary: Google Pay is expanding its services in India by entering the lending business, partnering with regulated lenders to offer various credit initiatives. This move includes providing personal loans, merchant financing, and sachet loans, leveraging the UPI network and Rupay credit cards. The Reserve Bank of India is closely monitoring the rapid growth in unsecured loans, advising banks and NBFCs to strengthen their risk management and underwriting standards.

title: Ask AI Guru: Genpact turns to generative AI to help employees learn
summary: Genpact, a NYSE-listed professional services company, has rolled out an internal generative AI chatbot called "AI Guru" to enhance employee learning and development. This tool, powered by Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service, helps employees with tasks such as IT migration, test case generation, and management advice, and provides 24/7 access to collective expertise from internal "master gurus." The initiative aims to improve employee retention and efficiency by offering personalized and constantly updated learning content, addressing a bottleneck in their traditional learning methods.

title: Enphase Energy (NASDAQ: ENPH) stock down 22% - higher interest rates kill installations
summary: Enphase Energy (NASDAQ: ENPH) stock dropped 22% due to slowing installations, a trend exacerbated by higher interest rates impacting the attractiveness of high-capital-cost solar energy solutions. The slowdown, following similar results from SolarEdge Technologies, highlights an underlying problem where increased financing costs make solar installations less appealing to buyers, affecting sales and revenues across the sector. This situation is seen as damaging to Enphase's stock price as it undermines previous expectations of swift growth.

title: Cathie Wood explains how the artificial intelligence boom could propel Ark's Innovation ETF
summary: Cathie Wood believes the artificial intelligence boom could significantly boost Ark Invest's flagship Ark Innovation ETF (ARKK). While the fund has gained 17% this year, Wood emphasized that AI represents a reality, not just a dream, and that its disruptive nature will create new industry leaders. She cautioned investors to be discerning with their AI bets, highlighting Tesla as a key holding while expressing reservations about some other "Magnificent Seven" stocks.

title: Apple to spend $1 billion on AI in catch-up move: report
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly investing $1 billion to accelerate its efforts in generative artificial intelligence. This move comes after the company was reportedly "caught off guard" by the rapid rise of AI tools like ChatGPT last year. The investment aims to integrate generative AI technology across all Apple products, with the initiative being led by senior vice presidents John Giannandrea and Craig Federighi.

title: Google, Nvidia Back AI Startup That Helps Combat Chip Shortage
summary: CentML, an AI startup focused on improving the efficiency of machine learning systems, has raised $27 million in a seed funding round. The investment, backed by Google's Gradient Ventures, Nvidia Corp., Radical Ventures, Deloitte Ventures, and Thomson Reuters Ventures, aims to help developers optimize computing power for AI training, which could alleviate chip shortages.

title: Top 10 Cloud Security Companies
summary: The cloud-managed services market is projected to reach US$164 billion by 2027, driven by increasing enterprise demand and a lack of IT expertise, while cloud security is poised for substantial growth to US$170.51 billion by 2032 due to rising cyber threats. This article lists the top 10 cloud security companies in 2023, based on market capitalization, including industry giants like Microsoft, Alphabet, and Amazon, highlighting their key offerings and recent developments in cloud security. The companies presented offer diverse solutions, from comprehensive cloud platforms and cybersecurity infrastructure to data analytics and application security.

title: Nasdaq 100 outlook: How will Amazon earnings impact AMZN stock?
summary: Amazon is set to release its Q3 earnings, with analysts expecting significant revenue and EPS growth driven by easier year-over-year comparisons and improved margins. The focus will be on Amazon Web Services (AWS) and its growth stability, especially in light of AI advancements, as well as the recovery of e-commerce operations and the performance of Prime Day sales. Although Amazon shares are currently at five-month lows, the company's advertising revenue continues to show strong growth, and the overall outlook for Big Tech earnings remains critical amidst economic uncertainty.

title: Best Graphic Design Tools for Creating Digital Ads
summary: This article lists and describes 12 of the best graphic design tools available for creating digital ads, categorizing them by their strengths, pricing, and free trial options. It highlights how these tools empower marketers to craft compelling visuals for various ad types even without professional design skills. The selection includes industry standards like Adobe Illustrator and Canva, as well as AI-powered options like MidJourney, catering to different needs and budgets.

title: Insight 2023: NetApp Enhanced Data Pipeline for Hybrid Multi-Cloud AI and Advancements on Secure Storage Vs. Ransomware
summary: NetApp announced significant portfolio updates at Insight 2023, focusing on enhancements to its data pipeline for hybrid multi-cloud AI and advancements in secure storage against ransomware. The company is simplifying AI data pipeline deployment across hybrid multi-cloud environments, while also strengthening data protection with features like autonomous ransomware detection and an extended Ransomware Recovery Guarantee. These innovations aim to address the dual challenges of leveraging AI opportunities and mitigating ransomware threats for customers.

title: Microsoft Edged Out Its Cloud Computing Competitors in Q3
summary: Microsoft's cloud computing service, Azure, reported 29% growth in Q3, outperforming competitors Google Cloud and Amazon Web Services, largely due to strong demand for its AI capabilities. The article suggests inverse ETFs like MSFU, CLDL, and UBOT for traders looking to capitalize on Microsoft's continued competitiveness in cloud computing and the growth potential of AI. Microsoft's significant investment in OpenAI contributed to a 13% revenue jump, highlighting its strategic focus on integrating AI across its technology stack.

title: Shoot, Don’t Kill
summary: Byrna Technologies, a less-lethal weapon manufacturer, is attempting to create a market for "less lethal" guns for self-defense amidst a polarized American gun culture.
