Their devices use compressed gas to launch non-lethal projectiles, offering an alternative to traditional firearms. Despite challenges in marketing and operational scaling, Byrna aims to appeal to a broad consumer base by highlighting their products as safe and unregulated alternatives for self-defense.

title: Sonos v. Google : A Decision Based on Ignorance of Patent Law That Must Be Overturned
summary: This article argues that a district court's decision in the Sonos v. Google patent litigation, which found Sonos's patents unenforceable due to laches because Sonos filed a continuation, demonstrates a fundamental misunderstanding of patent law. The author asserts that filing a continuation is a right and that the ruling, which also controversially suggested "fraud on the court" and "new matter," is legally unsound and must be overturned by the Federal Circuit to prevent significant disruption to patent practice. The piece also highlights the perceived double standard where patent owner delays are sanctioned, but alleged "efficient infringement" strategies by well-funded defendants are not.

title: GoodRx class action claims company shares personal info with Meta, Google, others
summary: GoodRx is facing a class action lawsuit alleging that it shared customers' personal and sensitive health information with third parties like Meta, Google, and Criteo without consent. The lawsuit claims GoodRx violated federal wiretapping statutes and consumer protection laws by allowing tracking pixels to intercept user communications, including mouse movements, clicks, and entered information. Plaintiffs argue that releasing sensitive physical and mental health data to advertisers negatively impacts them.

title: Here’s How Much $10,000 Invested in Verizon (VZ) Stock Would Be Worth Today
summary: This article analyzes the financial performance of Verizon (VZ) stock over the past decade, evaluating how a $10,000 investment made in 2014 would perform today. It details Verizon's strategic shifts, including attempts to become a media company and subsequent divestment to focus on its core telecommunications business and 5G expansion. The analysis concludes that despite a 7.4% dividend, the stock has significantly underperformed the S&P 500, with the initial $10,000 investment now worth around $7,300, plus an additional $4,300 from dividends.

title: Diamond Hill Capital Reduces Stake in Alphabet Inc.
summary: Diamond Hill Capital Management Inc. reduced its stake in Alphabet Inc. Class A (GOOGL) by 8,431 shares. title: Diamond Hill Capital Reduces Stake in Alphabet Inc.

title: Generative AI’s Impact and Opportunity in Tech and Beyond
summary: Generative AI is poised to disrupt various sectors, presenting significant opportunities and challenges. title: 10월 하락장 맞춘 AI ETF…엔비디아보다 '이것' 담았다 [글로벌마켓나우 플러스]
summary: LG AI연구원과 인공지능 전문 자산운용사 크래프트 테크놀로지스가 협력하여 'LG 크래프트 AI 파워드 US 라지캡 코어 상장지수펀드'(LQAI ETF)를 뉴욕증권거래소에 상장했습니다. 이 ETF는 엔비디아보다 구글과 마이크로소프트 비중을 높이고, 크래프트 테크놀로지스의 AI 위험 예측 지표를 통해 10월 하락장과 11월 초 반등을 예측하는 정확도를 보였습니다. 내년 시장 전망에서는 넷플릭스를 최선호 종목으로 꼽고, 기술주와 헬스케어 비중을 높이며 부동산 섹터는 편입하지 않고 있습니다.

title: FAANG Gives Way to the ‘Magnificent Seven’
summary: The "FAANG" stock group has evolved into the "Magnificent Seven," with Netflix replaced by Nvidia, and Microsoft and Tesla added to the mix of top U.S. market names. ETF issuers are beginning to launch products specifically targeting these seven stocks, while existing ETFs like the Vanguard Mega Cap Growth ETF (MGK) already hold significant concentrations of them. Investors have various options, from highly concentrated ETFs to diversified funds, to gain exposure to these influential companies.

title: Tepper’s Appaloosa dumps Apple stake and loads up on Amazon’s stock
summary: David Tepper’s Appaloosa hedge fund significantly increased its stake in Amazon.com Inc. and completely divested its position in Apple Inc. during the last quarter. The fund also raised its holdings in Google parent Alphabet Inc. and Facebook parent Meta Platforms Inc., as detailed in a recent SEC filing. This move indicates a shift in Tepper's portfolio allocation towards these tech giants.

title: How Microsoft’s Legal Legacy Shapes the Antitrust Case Against Google (Published 2023)
summary: The ongoing Google antitrust trial frequently invokes the landmark Microsoft antitrust case from the 1990s as a legal precedent. Both the Justice Department and Google draw parallels and distinctions, with the judge acknowledging the Microsoft case as a guiding framework. The core of the comparison lies in alleged monopolistic practices, the impact of "network effects" in digital markets, and the potential remedies for perceived anti-competitive behavior.

title: NBA's Phoenix Suns Set to Get Pulled Off YouTube TV Amid vMVPD's Dispute With Gray Television
summary: The NBA's Phoenix Suns' local TV home, KTVK Arizona's Family, is set to be removed from YouTube TV starting November 18, 2023, due to a carriage dispute between YouTube TV and KTVK owner Gray Television. This means YouTube TV subscribers will lose access to Suns games, local news, and other programming on the channel. However, other Gray-owned stations like KPHO-TV will remain on YouTube TV as their carriage is negotiated directly with Paramount Global.

title: First Pacific Advisors LLC Reduces Stake in Alphabet
summary: First Pacific Advisors LLC, managed by Richard Atwood, reduced its stake in Alphabet Inc. Class C (GOOG) by 95,179 shares. summary: Despite cloud computing being fundamental to AI programs, some cloud funds, like WisdomTree’s Cloud Computing Fund, have not fully capitalized on the AI rally. This article explores why emerging cloud stocks have lagged, highlighting that the primary beneficiaries are the major cloud infrastructure providers such as Amazon, Microsoft, and Google, which are part of the "Magnificent Seven" and have seen significant gains. It also discusses how these cloud leaders are preparing for future demands from generative AI and quantum computing through increased capital expenditure and strategic partnerships.

title: Which Cloud Stocks are Benefitting from the AI Boom?
summary: While cloud computing is fundamental to AI, some cloud-focused funds have underperformed despite the AI rally, suggesting a potential future bounce. The global cloud infrastructure market is dominated by Amazon, Microsoft, and Google, who are heavily investing in capacity and partnerships for generative AI. These "Magnificent Seven" companies have shown significant gains driven by the AI boom, indicating that the benefits of the AI-cloud synergy are largely accruing to the major infrastructure providers.

title: Nova, MACOM, KLA Corporation, Lam Research, and Monolithic Power Systems Stocks Trade Up, What You Need To Know
summary: Several semiconductor and analog semiconductor stocks, including Nova, MACOM, KLA Corporation, Lam Research, and Monolithic Power Systems, experienced significant jumps in their stock prices. This rally was fueled by renewed enthusiasm for artificial intelligence, particularly after Alphabet announced its upgraded Gemini 3 AI model, and positive sentiment from the New York Fed regarding a potential December interest rate cut. The article also provides a specific focus on Nova's recent stock performance and market context.

title: Sonos is reportedly working on a streaming video TV set top box, along with headphones
summary: Sonos is reportedly expanding its product line beyond speakers and soundbars. The company isSaid to be working on over-the-ear headphones for a March 2024 launch, possibly priced between $400 and $500, and high-end earbuds. Additionally, Sonos is reportedly developing an Android-based streaming video TV set-top box, codenamed "Pinewood," which could launch in late 2024 or early 2025.

title: 3 Compelling Cloud Storage Stocks to Send Your Portfolio Higher
summary: This article identifies three compelling cloud storage stocks: Alphabet (GOOGL), Dropbox (DBX), and Shopify (SHOP). It highlights Alphabet's strong Google Cloud growth and its dominant Google Services. Dropbox is discussed for its collaboration with Nvidia on generative AI and its potential to convert a large base of free users to paying subscribers. Shopify is included not as a pure-play cloud storage company, but because its successful e-commerce platform relies heavily on Google Cloud infrastructure and its recent financial performance has been strong.

title: Is Nvidia Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued After Earnings?
summary: Nvidia's recent earnings exceeded expectations, driven by strong demand for its GPUs in the AI sector, though the company anticipates a decline in China revenue due to U.S. export restrictions. Morningstar maintains a "fairly valued" assessment for Nvidia stock with a fair value estimate of $480 per share, acknowledging its wide economic moat from intangible assets and switching costs. Despite a Very High Uncertainty Rating due to competition and potential reliance reduction by hyperscale vendors, Nvidia is expected to remain a market leader in AI semiconductors for years.

title: Gartner Hype Cycle Shows AI Practices and Platform Engineering Will Reach Mainstream Adoption in Software Engineering in Two to Five Years
summary: Gartner's Hype Cycle for Software Engineering 2023 indicates that AI-augmented software engineering, AI coding assistants, and platform engineering will achieve mainstream adoption within two to five years. By 2027, 50% of enterprise software engineers are expected to utilize machine learning-powered coding tools, significantly boosting productivity and allowing focus on higher-value tasks. Platform engineering is also highlighted as crucial for managing technological complexity, with 80% of software engineering organizations projected to establish platform teams by 2026.

title: AI ETFs see huge performance dispersion in 2023
summary: Artificial Intelligence ETFs have shown significant performance dispersion in 2023, with top strategies more than doubling the returns of the lowest performers. This disparity is largely attributed to the heavy weighting of certain ETFs towards US equities and the "magnificent seven" tech stocks. While these mega-cap companies have driven much of the AI theme's returns, there is still potential for pure-play AI companies to join the rally.

title: Extended warranty Market to Reach $284.7 billion, Globally, by 2032 at 8.4% CAGR: Allied Market Research
summary: The global extended warranty market is projected to reach $284.7 billion by 2032, growing at an 8.4% CAGR from $129.7 billion in 2022, according to Allied Market Research. Key growth drivers include increased awareness, higher penetration of mobile devices, and the growing adoption of AI analytics in customer support. The report also highlights that the standard protection plan, manufacturers, automobiles, and individual segments are currently leading the market.

title: Which Stocks Capture the AI Healthcare Revolution?
summary: The healthcare industry is poised for significant growth driven by AI, despite recent underperformance in health tech stocks. Experts like Vinay Thapar of Alliance Bernstein and Bessemer Venture Partners foresee AI transforming R&D, clinical trials, commercial development, and diagnostics, with market forecasts predicting a CAGR of over 35% for AI in healthcare. Investors can gain exposure through individual stocks like Veeva Systems, ICON, and Alphabet, or diversified ETFs like the Goldman Sachs Future Health Care Equity ETF.

title: The Ad Council’s 69th Annual Public Service Award Dinner Celebrates Extraordinary Progress of Social Impact Efforts and Inspires the Media and Marketing Industries to Come Together for Tomorrow
summary: The Ad Council hosted its 69th Annual Public Service Award Dinner, honoring JPMorgan Chase Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon. It celebrated the progress of social impact efforts and aimed to inspire unity within the media and marketing industries for future collaborations.

title: Celestica stock rides another tech wave – but can it avoid a crash this time?
summary: Celestica Inc., a Toronto-based manufacturer, is experiencing a resurgence due to the AI boom, supplying equipment to data centers for major tech companies like Amazon, Google, and Meta. Despite a significant increase in stock price and revenue, questions remain about the sustainability of this growth compared to the company's past crash during the dot-com era. CEO Rob Mionis argues that Celestica is a more diversified and insulated company now, focusing on higher-margin, complex equipment and seeing sustained demand in the early stages of AI development.

title: 美股科技股“疯牛”行情未完待续? AI仍是引爆明年行情的关键
summary: Wedbush機構預計美股科技股在2024年將上漲至少20%，主要得益於人工智能（AI）和雲計算支出的大幅增長。分析師將AI比作互聯網的“1995時刻”，預計未來十年AI支出規模將達到1萬億美元，並點名英偉達和微軟將引領這一趨勢。儘管宏觀經濟不確定性存在，Wedbush仍堅定看好科技行業，並預計納斯達克100指數明年在高科技巨頭帶領下將大漲20%以上。

title: Five9 Breaks General Policy to Quash Acquisition Rumors

The article suggests that a Zoom acquisition is unlikely given Zoom's development of its own contact center platform and recent acquisitions, and speculates on other potential enterprise tech juggernauts if Five9 were considering an offer.

title: Microsoft Annual Shareholders Meeting 2023
summary: The 2023 Microsoft Annual Shareholders Meeting featured discussions on record financial results for FY23, strategic investments in AI, and responsible technology development. Key executives, including Satya Nadella, Amy Hood, and Brad Smith, addressed topics ranging from AI integration across product lines and revenue generation plans to safeguards against AI misuse, partnerships, and commitments to privacy and social responsibility. The meeting also covered the election of directors and various shareholder proposals.

title: TIDAL cuts 10% of staff as parent company Block enforces cost-saving measures
summary: Music streaming service TIDAL has laid off 10% of its workforce, impacting about 40 employees, as part of parent company Block Inc.'s cost-saving measures. This move aligns with Block CEO Jack Dorsey's November letter to investors, stating the company's intent to cap payroll at 12,000 employees. The layoffs at TIDAL reflect a broader trend of staff reductions across the tech and music industries, following similar cuts by companies like Spotify, Amazon, Google, and Meta.

title: Just 3 of the "Magnificent Seven" Have Outperformed the S&P 500 Since 2022. Here's the One I'd Buy for 2024 and 2025.
summary: This article analyzes the performance of the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks against the S&P 500 since 2022. It identifies Nvidia, Microsoft, and Apple as the only three that have outperformed the S&P 500. The author argues that Alphabet, despite underperforming recently, is the best buy for 2024 and 2025 due to its valuation, potential in digital advertising, and cloud segment growth.

title: BCE Inc. stock falls Monday, underperforms market
summary: BCE Inc. (BCE) stock experienced a drop of 0.65% on Monday, closing at C$54.84, thereby underperforming the broader Canadian market. The S&P/TSX Composite Index fell by only 0.06%. The company's stock is C$10.82 short of its 52-week high, and trading volume for the day significantly exceeded its 50-day average.

title: How Google’s Epic Legal Defeat Threatens the App-Store Industry’s $200 Billion Business Model
summary: Google's recent legal defeat against Epic Games threatens the $200 billion app store industry, which includes Apple. A jury found Google's app store practices anticompetitive, which could lead to significant changes in commission rates and developer options. This verdict could also impact Apple's similar business model, despite its previous victory against Epic, as regulators and lawmakers globally scrutinize app store rules and consumer sentiment leans against the power of tech giants.

title: GenAI’s World-Changing Power Is Putting Knowledge To Work
summary: Generative AI (GenAI) is poised to fundamentally disrupt industries by driving the cost of knowledge activation towards zero and closing the knowledge cycle, creating a virtuous loop of expanding knowledge. This disruption will impact firms differently based on their use of knowledge: high-expertise firms will see their human know-how amplified, while data-rich firms with repeatable processes will gain efficiency, potentially leading to growth. New competitors will also emerge, capitalizing on knowledge capture and private model delivery.

title: Google, AES, others to create marketplace for 24/7 hourly renewable energy certificates
summary: Google, AES, Microsoft, and Constellation have partnered with LevelTen Energy to create a marketplace for "granular certificates," a new type of energy attribute certificate that tracks renewable energy generation on an hourly, location-specific basis. This initiative aims to provide more accurate signals for renewable energy development and help corporate and utility buyers meet 24/7 clean energy goals. The platform is expected to launch next year, offering both forward and spot markets for these certificates.

title: What are the most visited social media platforms among Gen Z?
summary: This article analyzes the most visited and fastest-growing social media platforms among Gen Z (18-24 year olds) based on mobile app usage. It highlights YouTube as having the highest reach, while BeReal and LinkedIn are the fastest growing platforms for this demographic. The analysis also compares Gen Z's social media usage to the general population and other age groups, noting their higher engagement on most platforms except for Facebook and LinkedIn in terms of reach.

title: Technology – Checking Their Way Out?
summary: The article discusses the evolving role of self-checkout systems in retail, highlighting their popularity among customers for convenience despite significant challenges like increased shoplifting and staffing issues. It explores how technology, including AI, is being used to mitigate these risks and improve efficiency, but also notes a pushback from some retailers who are reverting to staffed tills due to customer preference and "shrink" losses. The author suggests AI as a potential middle ground to balance convenience and security in the future of retail checkouts.

title: The Best Artificial Intelligence ETFs of 2023
summary: The article highlights the top-performing Artificial Intelligence (AI) ETFs of 2023, with returns as high as 71%. It details five specific AI ETFs, including thematic funds that invest in companies benefiting from AI growth and AI-powered funds that use AI to select stocks, identifying key holdings and year-to-date performance for each.

title: Meta: Sell it? Buy it?
summary: Meta's stock price has experienced significant volatility in recent years, oscillating from a high of $384 in August 2021 to below $100 in October 2022, before rebounding to around $340. This article explores the company's journey from being considered a "sell" to a "buy," focusing on challenges such as Apple's privacy policy changes and competition from TikTok, and how Meta has responded by investing heavily in AI and re-evaluating its metaverse strategy. The future success of Meta is tied to its AI applications across its businesses, especially in advertising and new ventures.

title: Intellexa and Cytrox: From fixer-upper to Intel Agency-grade spyware
summary: Cisco Talos has conducted an in-depth analysis of Intellexa, a spyware vendor previously known as Cytrox, detailing its operational paradigms, procedures, and the evolution of its Predator spyware. The analysis reveals that Intellexa's Predator spyware continues to evade detection, offers persistence even after device reboots, and demonstrates a sophisticated business model with high-priced solutions targeting state-sponsored agencies. Despite public disclosures and the US adding Intellexa to its Entity List, the company effectively continues its operations by adapting to patched exploits and maintaining plausible deniability through its sales and delivery methods.

title: PLDT warns public on AI-generated scams
summary: PLDT and Smart Communications are warning the public about the rise of AI-generated scams, which are becoming increasingly sophisticated and convincing. Criminals are leveraging AI to create polished messages and visuals that mimic official communications, making it harder for individuals to distinguish genuine messages from fraudulent ones. PLDT advises checking the source of emails and texts and adhering to the basic rule of not communicating with strangers to avoid falling victim to these scams.

title: Top 6 Portfolio Moves for 2024
summary: This article outlines six key portfolio adjustments investors should consider for 2024, focusing on balancing risk and maximizing returns. Recommendations include rebalancing portfolios, increasing bond allocations due to improved yields, optimizing cash holdings for competitive yields without over-allocating, maintaining inflation protection, and considering exposure to small-cap stocks. These moves aim to address market shifts, inflation concerns, and current valuation disparities.

title: Alphabet Inc: Report on Assessing Systemic Climate Risk from Retirement Plan Options
summary: This report from As You Sow details a shareholder resolution requesting Alphabet Inc. to disclose how it protects retirement plan beneficiaries, particularly those with long investment horizons, from increased future portfolio risk due to current investments in high-carbon companies. The resolution highlights the significant economic consequences of unmitigated climate change and the material risk it poses to retirement savings. It points out that Alphabet's default retirement plan option, the Vanguard Target Retirement fund series, invests heavily in high-carbon companies, contributing to systemic climate risk for its employees' savings.

title: T. Rowe Price Capital Appreciation Equity ETF (TCAF): A Promising Active Fund
summary: The T. Rowe Price Capital Appreciation Equity ETF (TCAF) has shown significant growth since its launch in June 2023, accumulating over $600 million in assets. The fund focuses on high-quality U.S. large-cap companies, employing a bottom-up stock selection approach with top holdings like Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon. TCAF's investment philosophy emphasizes strong performance in areas like technology, healthcare, and consumer discretionary.

title: Cathie Wood Goes Big Into ProShares Bitcoin, Dumps Grayscale
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Next Generation Internet ETF has significantly altered its Bitcoin-related holdings by selling all 2.25 million shares of the Grayscale Bitcoin Trust and purchasing 4.32 million shares of the ProShares Bitcoin Strategy ETF. This move was made "out of an abundance of caution" regarding the Grayscale fund's conversion to a spot-Bitcoin ETF and reflects Wood's continued optimism about Bitcoin. The ARK ETF also bought shares of the ARK 21Shares Active Bitcoin Futures Strategy ETF and sold Coinbase Global Inc. shares.

title: Sada Systems, Under New Owner, to Stay in NoHo
summary: Cloud services provider Sada Systems Inc. has been acquired by Insight Enterprises Inc. for $410 million. Sada, a major Google Cloud Platform partner with 850 employees, will remain headquartered in North Hollywood and operate as a separate entity under Insight, doing business as Sada, An Insight Company. This acquisition is expected to boost Insight's cloud and digital services profits and expand its service-delivery capabilities, particularly in areas like generative AI.

title: Mindlessly scroll on bike? ‘Tiktok fitness powered by Peloton’, stock soars
summary: Peloton has announced a partnership with TikTok to create a new fitness hub called #TikTokFitness, powered by Peloton. This collaboration aims to bring short-form fitness videos, live classes, instructor series, and celebrity collaborations to TikTok's vast audience. Following the announcement, Peloton's stock shares rose by 7%, signaling a positive market reaction to their efforts to recover from recent financial downturns, including another recent partnership with YouTube TV.

title: Google and Samsung Set To Use Qualcomm's New VR Chip, Could Compete With Apple and Meta
summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) recently unveiled its new Snapdragon XR2+ Gen 2 Platform chip for virtual and augmented reality (VR/AR). Google (GOOGL) and Samsung are slated to integrate this chip into their upcoming VR/AR experiences, aiming to challenge market leaders like Apple (AAPL) and Meta Platforms (META). This development could intensify competition in the growing VR headset market, especially following Apple's Vision Pro and Meta's dominance in 2023.

title: Scripps Selects Its Second Journalism Journey Initiative Class
summary: The E.W. Scripps Company, in partnership with Google, has announced its second class of 13 veteran print journalists for the Journalism Journey Initiative. This program aims to retrain mid-career journalists for video-driven reporting platforms in Scripps' local TV stations and national news network. This expansion follows a successful first year and builds on Google's multiyear financial commitment to the initiative.

title: Using Modern APIs to Deliver Network-aware Applications
summary: This article discusses how modern APIs are crucial for telco transformation and 5G network monetization by enabling third-party developers to create network-aware applications. It highlights initiatives like GSMA Open Gateway and CAMARA which standardize API access and address security concerns. Microsoft's Azure Programmable Connectivity (APC) platform is presented as a key tool facilitating developers' connection to these APIs, ultimately supporting the development of advanced applications for consumers and enterprises.

title: Google's AI Spin-Off Isomorphic Labs Strikes Big Pharma Deals, Paving the Way for Future of Drug Discovery
summary: Isomorphic Labs, an AI spin-off of Alphabet's DeepMind, has secured partnerships with Eli Lilly And Co and Novartis AG, totaling approximately $3 billion. These collaborations aim to leverage AI, specifically AlphaFold 2 technology, for new drug discovery and development. The financial agreements include significant upfront payments and performance-based milestones, highlighting the growing role of AI in the pharmaceutical industry.

title: FNGU ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:FNGU
summary: This article provides an analysis of the MicroSectors FANG+ 3X Leveraged ETNs (FNGU), detailing its key statistics such as Assets Under Management ($1.47 billion), expense ratio (2.60%), and the fact that it does not pay dividends. It highlights FNGU's structure as a geared product with daily resets, making it suitable for short-term trading rather than long-term investment due to compounding effects. The ETF offers 3x exposure to an index of FANG companies and other similar businesses, with constituents being equally weighted.

title: Fortinet expands its global SASE POPs with Google Cloud
summary: Fortinet has partnered with Google Cloud to expand its SASE Points-of-Presence (POPs) to new locations, enhancing its Universal SASE solution's global reach. This collaboration aims to provide secure access to applications for hybrid workforces, leveraging Google Cloud's infrastructure for improved performance and cost efficiency. The expanded partnership will accelerate customer adoption of Fortinet's integrated cloud-delivered security.

title: Naysayers Were Wrong About the 60/40 Portfolio. Here’s Why.
summary: The article discusses how the 60/40 portfolio, despite predictions of its demise after a challenging 2022, rebounded significantly in 2023 with an 18% return. It explains that while legitimate concerns existed, strong equity performance from "Magnificent Seven" stocks and improved bond returns contributed to its unexpected success. The piece also delves into the future prospects and potential challenges for the 60/40 portfolio, including inflation and moderate equity returns, while emphasizing its long-term resilience.

title: SKYX’s All-in-One Smart Platform is Everything from a Smart Speaker to a Smoke Detector
summary: SKYX Platforms Corp. debuted its patented All-in-One Smart Platform at CES 2024, aiming to make existing homes technologically advanced. This platform integrates multiple smart home features like a smoke/CO detector, smart speaker, Wi-Fi repeater, and various lighting controls, all controllable via app and voice commands. The product installs easily into a ceiling and supports major smart home ecosystems, with SKYX also renewing a global licensing agreement with GE.

title: Dow Theory Letters - REX Shares and Tuttle Capital Management Expand T-REX Single-Stock ETF Suite with Apple, Alphabet, and Microsoft Offerings
summary: REX Shares, in collaboration with Tuttle Capital Management, has expanded its T-REX Single-Stock ETF suite by adding three new products: 2X Long Apple Daily Target ETF (AAPX), 2X Long Alphabet Daily Target ETF (GOOX), and 2X Long Microsoft Daily Target ETF (MSFX). These ETFs offer seasoned traders 200% leveraged exposure to the daily price movements of these tech giants. The T-REX suite, which debuted in October 2023 with NVIDIA and Tesla, now provides diverse sector coverage for focused, leveraged trading strategies.

title: AI Reaches Space with Telesat Lightspeed and Aalyria
summary: Telesat has partnered with Aalyria to deploy its Spacetime next-generation networking software for the Telesat Lightspeed Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite constellation. Spacetime leverages machine learning and AI to optimize network orchestration, ensuring efficient data flow and high-quality service by analyzing millions of paths and dynamically routing traffic. This technology, proven through its use at Google and Alphabet, will also enable the constellation to learn and make faster, more efficient decisions over time.

title: Victoria's Secret & Co. gets personal as it taps Google Cloud AI and generative AI technologies
summary: Victoria's Secret & Co. (VS&Co) has partnered with Google Cloud to integrate AI and generative AI technologies, aiming to create more personalized online shopping experiences and improve operational efficiencies. The collaboration will leverage Vertex AI for a conversational assistant offering tailored product recommendations and enhance search capabilities on their website and mobile app. This initiative builds on VS&Co's acquisition of Adore Me, which already uses Google Cloud's AI, and seeks to optimize internal operations, marketing, supply chain, and HR.

title: Lawyers for Google app developers get $26 mln in antitrust settlement
summary: A U.S. federal judge approved a $26 million payout to lawyers representing Android software developers in an antitrust settlement with Google. This payout is part of a larger $112 million settlement, which includes $90 million in cash for developers and $22 million in savings from Google agreeing to maintain a reduced 15% service fee until May 2025 for developers' first $1 million in annual revenue. The ruling comes amidst other antitrust challenges against Google, including a recent jury verdict loss to Epic Games and a $700 million settlement with states and consumers.

title: Meta to default teen Facebook, Instagram users to most restrictive settings
summary: Meta Platforms announced it will set the default content settings for teenage users on Facebook and Instagram to the most restrictive to enhance their safety. This decision follows a lawsuit by 42 attorneys general alleging harm to teenage users by social media platforms. The update will hide more sensitive content, such as discussions of self-harm and eating disorders, and apply to both new and existing teen users.

title: How Stantec and WSP became Canada’s unlikely stock market stars — and made engineering sexy
summary: Stantec and WSP Global, two Canadian engineering firms, have become unexpected stock market stars with their shares soaring 242% and 197% respectively over five years, outperforming even major U.S. tech companies. Their success is attributed to shifting focus from fixed-price construction contracts to per-hour consulting fees, diversifying through acquisitions, expanding into areas like water infrastructure, and disciplined growth strategies focused on profitability rather than just revenue. Both companies prioritize strategic acquisitions, debt management, and maximizing workforce utilization, making engineering an attractive investment sector.

title: NetApp Hires AWS Executive To Manage Global Cloud Sales
summary: NetApp has hired Ashish Dhawan, formerly AWS's managing director and worldwide head of enterprise workload sales, as its new senior vice president of global cloud sales. This new role aims to strengthen NetApp's commitment to cloud sales and strategy, with Dhawan responsible for developing and executing the company's cloud sales strategy across all major cloud providers. This is NetApp's second high-profile hire this year, following Riccardo Di Blasio as SVP of North America sales.

title: Nuclear Fusion / OpenAI Boss Sam Altman ‘Motivated To Invest More’
summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman expressed his motivation to further invest in nuclear fusion, emphasizing that a breakthrough in energy is crucial to meet the immense future demands of artificial intelligence. Altman has already invested in Helion Energy and chairs the fusion company Oklo, highlighting his ongoing commitment to the sector. He believes that without significant advancements in clean energy, the growing energy needs of AI cannot be sustainably met.

title: AST SpaceMobile Secures Strategic Investment From AT&T, Google and Vodafone
summary: AST SpaceMobile has secured a strategic investment of up to $206.5 million from AT&T, Google, and Vodafone. This financing, which includes convertible notes and commercial payments, aims to accelerate AST SpaceMobile's mission to provide 5G cellular broadband directly from space to unmodified smartphones globally. The investment and expanded partnerships underscore confidence in the company's technology to bridge global connectivity gaps.

title: AES positioned to provide tech sector with growing renewable energy solutions
summary: AES Corp. is strategically positioned to be a leading provider of renewable energy solutions to the technology sector, especially given the rising power demands from data centers and generative AI. The company focuses on long-term contracted revenues with credit-worthy partners and offers customized solutions to meet the unique sustainability goals of major tech firms like Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta. With significant growth projected in key markets and a strong track record of project execution, AES aims to capitalize on the increasing demand for clean energy in the tech industry.

title: Wayfair and Macy’s are cutting thousands of jobs as retail layoffs deepen
summary: Retail companies Wayfair and Macy's have announced significant job cuts as layoffs deepen across the sector. Wayfair is cutting 1,650 jobs, or 13% of its global workforce, citing over-hiring during a strong economic period. Macy's is laying off approximately 2,350 employees, about 3.5% of its total headcount, and plans to close five stores as part of a new strategy to adapt to evolving consumer and market needs.

title: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Stock Rocketed Over 200% in 2023
summary: Polen Capital's "Polen Focus Growth Strategy" fund saw significant returns in 2023, with NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) being a key contributor as its stock rocketed over 200%. The article highlights NVIDIA's crucial role in the generative AI boom, providing the processing power for large language models and AI factories. NVIDIA was held by 180 hedge fund portfolios at the end of Q3 2023, up from 175 in the previous quarter.

title: WATCH: Our CEO Mike Fries on CNBC at the World Economic Forum
summary: Liberty Global CEO Mike Fries discussed the impact of AI on customers, network efficiency, and employee productivity during an interview with CNBC at the World Economic Forum. He emphasized the importance of being "fast" in adopting new AI technology and highlighted the value of Liberty Global's network investments in fibre and 5G. Fries also mentioned an upcoming strategic update during the company's Q4 earnings call in February.

title: Online retailer eBay is cutting 1,000 jobs. It’s the latest tech company to reduce its workforce
summary: Online retailer eBay is cutting approximately 1,000 jobs, or 9% of its full-time workforce, citing that its employee count and costs have outpaced its business growth in a slowing economy. This move follows a trend of layoffs across the tech industry, where companies that rapidly expanded during the COVID-19 pandemic are now trimming expenses. CEO Jamie Iannone informed employees that the company will also reduce its alternate workforce contracts and highlighted the need for better organization and nimbleness.

title: eBay Stock Surges After Saying It Will Cut 9% of Its Workforce—Monitor These Key Levels
summary: eBay announced plans to cut 9% of its workforce, or 1,000 jobs, which led to a nearly 3% surge in its stock price. CEO Jamie Iannone stated that the company's headcount and expenses had outpaced business growth. The article also provides key technical levels for investors to monitor eBay's stock performance within a descending channel pattern.

title: IonQ Achieves Technical Milestone One Year Ahead of Schedule
summary: IonQ announced it achieved its 35 algorithmic qubits (#AQ) technical milestone a full year ahead of schedule on its IonQ Forte system. This innovation enables larger and more sophisticated quantum circuits, making the systems more valuable for applications like quantum machine learning and chemistry. The company's CEO, Peter Chapman, highlighted this achievement as a testament to IonQ's technical leadership and aggressive roadmap in quantum computing.

title: Salesforce slashes 700 jobs, adding to brutal string of 2024 tech layoffs
summary: Salesforce Inc. is reportedly laying off approximately 700 workers, continuing a trend of recent job cuts in the tech industry at the beginning of 2024. These layoffs affect less than 1% of its workforce and follow a 10% reduction a year prior, as the company focuses on cost-cutting and expanding profit margins under shareholder pressure. Other major tech firms like Microsoft, Google, and Amazon have also announced layoffs this month.

title: Scrip Asks…What Does 2024 Hold For Biopharma? Part 4: Transformative Technology
summary: This article is part four of a series from Scrip exploring what 2024 holds for the biopharma industry, focusing specifically on transformative technology. The headline highlights the rise of Artificial Intelligence, suggesting it will be a key area of development and impact. The article lists related content and companies engaged in various innovative technologies within the biopharmaceutical space.

title: ESG Investing in “Democratic” Companies
summary: The article discusses the Democratic Large-Cap Core Fund (DEMZ), an ETF that invests in S&P 500 companies contributing over 75% of their political donations to the Democratic Party. It examines the fund's portfolio, investment criteria, and the broader implications of politically-driven ESG investing, distinguishing between ESG activism and investing.

title: Community Health Systems Completes Data Migration and Implements New Generative AI Innovations with Google Cloud
summary: Community Health Systems (CHS) has completed its migration to a FHIR-based clinical data platform leveraging Google Cloud, enhancing data centralization and interoperability. This move enables CHS to implement new generative AI innovations using Google Cloud technologies to improve administrative efficiencies, streamline clinical workflows, and ultimately enhance patient experiences across its 71 affiliated hospitals. The partnership aims to provide real-time insights and automate tasks, allowing healthcare professionals to focus more on patient interaction.

title: Zuckerberg Gives Impromptu Apology at Child Safety Senate Hearing
summary: During a tense Senate hearing on child safety, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg offered an unscripted apology to families of children who were victims of sexual exploitation on social media platforms. He expressed sorrow for their suffering, stating that no one should have to endure such experiences. The apology came as social media executives were being grilled by Congress over their platforms' impact on children.

title: Markets Brief: Will the ‘Magnificent Seven’ Stocks Keep Carrying the Market After Earnings?
summary: The article analyzes the performance of the "Magnificent Seven" stocks after their latest earnings reports, noting that while most beat forecasts, market reactions were mixed due to high expectations. It discusses whether these tech giants can continue to drive market gains in 2024, given their current valuations and the potential for a broader market rally. The article also provides a detailed recap of individual company performances and analyst commentary.

title: Google Store financing now requires a purchase of $149 or more
summary: The Google Store has recently imposed a new minimum purchase requirement of $149 for customers to utilize its financing option, which is offered in partnership with Synchrony. This change means that purchases under $149 can no longer be financed and must be paid for via credit card or PayPal. While the exact implementation date is unclear, it likely occurred between late 2022 and mid-2023, primarily affecting smaller accessory purchases, as financing is generally intended for larger items like smartphones.

title: Security startup Wiz adds former Zscaler COO, plans hiring spree
summary: Cybersecurity startup Wiz has hired Dali Rajic, former COO and president of Zscaler, as its first-ever president and COO, signaling its intent for rapid growth and an eventual public offering. The company announced it reached $350 million in Annual Recurring Revenue in 2023 and plans to add 400 new employees globally in 2024. Wiz, valued at $10 billion, is also actively seeking acquisition targets in the security sector.

title: FNDX ETF: Fundamentals Are Attractive, Performance Is Not (NYSEARCA:FNDX)
summary: The Schwab Fundamental U.S. Large Company Index ETF (FNDX) tracks the Russell RAFI US Large Company Index and holds 730 companies, offering attractive valuation metrics and sector diversification. However, since its inception in 2013, FNDX has underperformed its Russell 1000 benchmark and several factor-based ETFs, despite its strong fundamental attributes. The article suggests that while the ETF's holdings show promise, its overall market performance has been lacking.

title: DocuSign Stock Falls Amid Third Round of Layoffs in Two Years
summary: Shares of DocuSign fell after the e-signature software maker announced its third round of layoffs in two years, cutting 6% of its workforce primarily in sales and marketing. This restructuring comes after growth slowed post-pandemic and recent takeover talks with private equity firms stalled, leading to an estimated $28 million to $32 million in one-time restructuring charges. The company previously laid off employees in September 2022 and February 2023 in efforts to right-size after a pandemic-driven boom.

title: EA SPORTS Madden NFL 24 Predicts Kansas City Chiefs to Win Second Consecutive Super Bowl
summary: EA SPORTS Madden NFL 24 has predicted the Kansas City Chiefs to defeat the San Francisco 49ers 30-28 in Super Bowl LVIII, with Patrick Mahomes earning his second consecutive Super Bowl MVP. The prediction is part of EA SPORTS' extensive presence around Super Bowl LVIII, including community events like "Field the Future" to empower young women in football, in-game content, and the Ultimate Madden Bowl tournament. The brand is connecting sports, culture, and entertainment with various activations and celebrity performances during Super Bowl week.

title: Motorola Solutions and Google partner on safety and security
summary: Motorola Solutions and Google Cloud have announced a multi-year collaboration focused on enhancing cloud solutions within Motorola Solutions’ safety and security technologies. This partnership will leverage Google Cloud's infrastructure for developing assistive intelligence, video content delivery, mapping, and AI capabilities to tackle real-world safety challenges. The initiative aims to improve public safety and enterprise security outcomes through advanced cloud security software like Avigilon Alta.

title: Google joins coalition to label AI-generated content
summary: Google has joined the Coalition for Content Provenance and Authenticity (C2PA), a standard for labeling digital content to indicate its origin and creation methods, alongside Microsoft, Meta, and Adobe. This move is significant given Google's market share with Android and YouTube, aiming to combat misinformation by making AI-generated content more transparent. While the C2PA aims for widespread adoption to make unlabeled content stand out, critics question its tamper-proof nature, suggesting that bad actors could strip or swap metadata, and it may not fully address the issue of malicious misinformation.

title: ADT welcomes Silicon Valley veteran Gilles Drieu as new CTO
summary: ADT has appointed Gilles Drieu, a veteran of Silicon Valley tech giants like Google, Apple, and Adobe, as its new Chief Technology Officer. Drieu will work alongside Chief Product Officer Naveen Chhangani and Chief Information Officer Sam Jaddi to accelerate ADT's innovation in smart home security offerings. His experience includes leading the development of HTML5-enabled web browsers at Apple and pivotal work on Chromecast, Home, and Nest products at Google.

title: Exclusive: Cisco to cut thousands of jobs as it seeks to focus on high growth areas
summary: Cisco is reportedly planning to restructure its business, which will involve laying off thousands of employees to focus on high-growth areas. An announcement could come as early as next week, ahead of its earnings call on Feb. 14. This move follows a previous restructuring in late 2022 and comes as many other tech companies are also cutting jobs.

title: Silicon Valley Has a Harvard Problem
summary: The article argues that Silicon Valley, much like elite universities, suffers from a lack of authentic belief and moral courage among its leadership, leading to a managerial class primarily focused on self-preservation and avoidance of controversy. Authors Alexander C. Karp and Nicholas W. Zamiska connect this issue to the recent congressional testimony of university presidents, suggesting a broader cultural problem where a fear of offending stifles critical thinking and engagement with significant moral questions. They contend that this vacuum leaves major tech companies without a clear sense of purpose beyond self-interest, making them vulnerable to external agendas.

title: AI frenzy puts Nvidia briefly ahead of Amazon in market value
summary: Nvidia briefly surpassed Amazon.com in market capitalization on Monday, driven by the ongoing euphoria around artificial intelligence. The chipmaker reached a record high of $734.96, giving it a market value of $1.82 trillion, slightly ahead of Amazon's $1.81 trillion. This event marks Nvidia's highest valuation relative to Amazon since 2002, with investors keenly awaiting its upcoming quarterly results on February 21st.

title: Akamai extends its edge-computing platform as it looks to challenge AWS, Azure and GCP
summary: Akamai has launched its Gecko "Generalized Edge Compute" platform, expanding its cloud-computing network with 10 new regions in Q1 and another 75 throughout the year. This initiative aims to bring compute capacity closer to users, reducing latency and enabling new use cases, directly challenging major cloud providers like AWS, Azure, and GCP. The company plans to open hundreds of small regions in the coming years and integrate its container service and automated workload platform.

title: Going Into Earnings, Is Nvidia a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar assesses Nvidia (NVDA) ahead of its Q4 earnings report, highlighting the dominance of its data center segment and the impact of AI growth. The company maintains a "Wide" economic moat due to intangible assets and switching costs, but its stock is considered overvalued with a "Very High" uncertainty rating and a fair value estimate of $480. Key factors to watch include supply constraints, U.S. restrictions on sales to China, and competition from hyperscalers developing in-house AI chips.

title: Nvidia replaces Alphabet as Wall St's third most valuable company
summary: Nvidia has surpassed Google-parent Alphabet to become the third most valuable U.S. company, with a market capitalization of $1.825 trillion. This milestone follows its recent overtaking of Amazon, driven by strong investor interest in the dominant AI chipmaker ahead of its upcoming quarterly report. Nvidia's stock has surged due to its control of about 80% of the high-end AI chip market and strong demand for its products.

title: February 2024 The AI race makes first meaningful impact on the suppliers of optics
summary: The AI race significantly impacted optics suppliers in Q4 2023, with Coherent and Innolight reporting substantial revenue growth driven by Nvidia's demand for 800G transceivers. While Nvidia initially handles much of the optics in-house, it allocates larger volumes to these suppliers later. Major tech companies show mixed spending on infrastructure, although overall market momentum is positive with AI leading the recovery.

title: Could This Be The Key to Rural Telehealth Scalability?
summary: The University of Rochester Medical Center (URMC) has partnered with Five Star Bank, Verizon, Higi Health, and Dexcare to place telehealth kiosks in bank branches across rural New York. This initiative aims to address care access issues for rural residents, especially Medicare and Medicaid recipients, by overcoming geographical and technological barriers. The model offers a scalable solution compared to individual community centers and also addresses social determinants of health by connecting patients with financial planning resources.

title: Stock market concentration is on the rise. Will this continue?
summary: Stock market concentration in the U.S. has reached multi-decade highs, largely driven by the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks. While these mega-caps have benefited from market rotation and AI excitement, historical trends suggest that steep rises in concentration tend to reverse, and this could be influenced by future liquidity and the economic cycle. Investors should consider the implications of this concentration on future performance, especially in different economic scenarios like a soft landing.

title: Rackspace Technology Aims to Double Revenue in India with Growing AI Adoption
summary: Rackspace Technology plans to double its revenue in India within two years, driven by the increasing adoption of AI and demand for private cloud solutions. The company, which already has 40% of its global workforce in India, will establish a data center in the country. Rackspace aims to leverage India's growing startup ecosystem and high cloud consumption to expand its presence and brand recognition, focusing on digital transformation and AI-enabled cloud services.

title: Here's How Much Money You'd Have if You Invested $7,000 in the "Magnificent Seven" in 2017
summary: This article examines the investment returns if one had invested $7,000 across the "Magnificent Seven" stocks (Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta Platforms, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla) in 2017. It highlights the significant growth these companies experienced, with Nvidia and Tesla showing the highest returns, and compares this performance to an investment in the S&P 500 over the same period. The author concludes that these established growth stocks can still offer good long-term returns despite their current size.

title: Nvidia CEO Wears Fuchsia Leather Jacket At Insight Sales Event
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang wore a fuchsia leather jacket, Insight's signature color, at the company's Amplify sales event, signaling a strong partnership in generative AI. An Insight executive stated that Huang's virtual presence and choice of attire underscored the significance of their collaboration, especially as Nvidia expands its AI solutions. The event also featured Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger and focused on generative AI use cases, infrastructure upgrades, and the growing demand for AI PCs amidst a broader PC refresh cycle.

title: NVIDIA Announces Financial Results for Fourth Quarter and Fiscal 2024
summary: NVIDIA reported record-breaking financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2024, driven by surging demand for accelerated computing and generative AI. The company achieved record quarterly revenue of $22.1 billion and full-year revenue of $60.9 billion, with significant growth in its Data Center segment. NVIDIA also announced a quarterly cash dividend and provided an optimistic outlook for the first quarter of fiscal 2025.

title: General Mills rolls out MillsChat, an internal generative AI tool
summary: General Mills has launched MillsChat, a private generative AI tool built on Google's PaLM 2 model, for its employees in the U.S., Canada, U.K., and India. The tool is designed to assist with writing, summarizing, and brainstorming in a secure environment. This initiative reflects General Mills' increasing investment in AI, having grown its data science headcount fortyfold since 2018, and aligns with other food brands like Kraft Heinz, Coca-Cola, and Procter & Gamble that are also integrating generative AI into their operations to improve business outcomes and operational efficiencies despite a challenging consumer landscape.

title: Disturbing AI Images of Children Found for Sale on Shutterstock
summary: PetaPixel discovered disturbing, sexually suggestive AI-generated images of children, including some with offensive text and others depicting inappropriate interactions, for sale on Shutterstock. These images were created using Shutterstock's internal AI image generator. Following PetaPixel's report, Shutterstock removed the content, identified and banned the user, and implemented new safety blocklists, acknowledging challenges in moderating AI-generated content.

title: Vice Media to stop publishing on its website, lay off 'several' hundreds
summary: Vice Media has announced it will cease publishing content on its website, vice.com, and lay off several hundred of its employees. CEO Bruce Dixon stated that distributing digital content is no longer cost-effective, and the company will focus on other distribution channels. This move reflects the ongoing financial struggles in the media industry, with many legacy and digital media brands facing job cuts or shutdowns.

title: All things AI with Melissa Waters
summary: Melissa Waters, CMO of Upwork, discusses her company's approach to AI, emphasizing a human-centered policy that prioritizes safety and responsible data handling. She details how Upwork's marketing teams are using AI tools like ChatGPT and Regie.ai to boost productivity, customize content, and expand their online presence. Waters also highlights the potential of AI for hyper-personalization in marketing and advises brands to encourage experimentation among employees while addressing concerns about job redundancy.

title: What's Cathie Wood been selling?
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Investment Management has sold shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Corp. and Nvidia Corp., diversifying holdings amidst the AI frenzy. This marks Wood's first sale of TSMC stock in over two years and follows previous sales of Nvidia. The move comes as both TSMC and Nvidia have seen significant gains, with Wood's funds betting on growth potential in less-talked-about software companies like UiPath and Twilio, despite earlier predictions of AI's transformative impact.

title: After Earnings and a Massive Rally, Is Nvidia Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Following a strong fiscal fourth-quarter earnings report and a significant stock rally, Morningstar assesses Nvidia's valuation. While the company's AI dominance and strong demand are sustainable, its stock is currently rated as fairly valued, with a 3-star Morningstar Rating and a fair value estimate of $730 per share. The "Very High" Uncertainty Rating reflects potential competition and the volatile nature of some of its markets.

title: Fidelity Fundamental Large Cap Core ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (BATS:FFLC) $FFLC
summary: This article provides detailed information on the Fidelity Fundamental Large Cap Core ETF (FFLC), including its current price, performance, key statistics, top holdings, and expense ratios. FFLC is an actively managed ETF investing in large-cap stocks based on fundamental factors, with major holdings in tech giants like NVIDIA, Alphabet, and Amazon. The ETF has seen a 1.9% increase in stock value since the beginning of the year.

title: EU lawmakers back draft rules on patents for connected cars, telecom equipment
summary: EU lawmakers have approved draft rules concerning patents for technologies in connected cars and telecom equipment, despite criticism from patent holders like Nokia and Ericsson. The proposed rules aim to resolve expensive and prolonged patent disputes, while industry groups like IP Europe argue they could stifle innovation, whereas the Fair Standards Alliance supports them for promoting fair licensing terms. The rules will now proceed to negotiations between the European Parliament and EU member countries.

title: TiVo OS to Generate $190 Million in Annual Revenue by 2026, Xperi Predicts
summary: Xperi Corp. has significantly increased its revenue projections for its TiVo-branded smart TV operating system, now forecasting $190 million in annual revenue by 2026, up from an earlier estimate of $140 million. This prediction comes as the TiVo OS slowly expands its presence in Europe, with new OEM agreements including China's SkyWorth and retail partnerships for Sharp TVs under the Bush brand. Xperi also expects its video-over-broadband business for pay-TV operators to reach $100 million in the same timeframe.

title: 3 Stocks Under $10 With Triple-Digit Upside Potential
summary: This article identifies three stocks under $10 – Rocket Lab USA (RKLB), AST SpaceMobile (ASTS), and Lithium Americas (LAAC) – that are poised for significant growth, potentially reaching triple-digit upside. Each company is positioned to capitalize on major market trends: RKLB in the booming space industry, ASTS in global satellite-based cell service, and LAAC in the high-demand lithium market, all benefiting from strong backlogs, strategic funding, or favorable market shifts.

title: Alphabet Faces ‘Clear and Present Danger’ of Falling Short in AI
summary: Alphabet's shares tumbled after its flagship AI product, Google Gemini, experienced misfires, erasing $80 billion in market value in a single day. This event highlighted the critical importance investors place on the company's success in artificial intelligence, with errors in AI responses contributing to stock weakness. The CEO has stated that the AI failures are unacceptable, underscoring the urgent need for Alphabet to succeed in the AI race.

title: Microsoft spends big on data center land near Gainesville
summary: Microsoft Corp. has acquired 124 acres of land in Gainesville, Virginia, for over $465 million, intending to build data centers. The property, located near Conway Robinson State Forest and Manassas National Battlefield Park, is already zoned for heavy industrial use, accommodating data center development. A rezoning application has been submitted to allow for nearly 4 million square feet of data centers and a new electric substation.

title: Unlocking The Advertising Power of YouTube
summary: This article discusses the growing importance of YouTube Shorts in the advertising landscape, driven by the increasing consumer preference for short-form video content. It highlights how Comscore's enhanced Campaign Ratings, integrated with YouTube, provides advertisers with comprehensive cross-platform insights to measure campaign impact effectively across various YouTube inventories, including Shorts. The piece emphasizes the shift in retail media towards e-commerce driving content and the significant role AI plays in democratizing access to innovation in this space.

title: Magnificent Seven vs. the ‘Granolas’: How does Europe’s version stack up?
summary: This article compares the U.S. "Magnificent Seven" stocks with Europe's "Granolas," highlighting their differences in market value, sector diversity, and profitability. While the Magnificent Seven are largely tech-driven and exceptionally profitable, the Granolas offer broader sector representation and solid returns, often trading at a discount despite their strong performance. The piece examines historical returns, valuation ratios, and individual stock assessments for both groups.

title: Greater Miami & The Keys Chapter of the American Red Cross honors Ryder System, Inc. at 2024 Red Cross Ball
summary: The Greater Miami & The Keys Chapter of the American Red Cross honored Ryder System, Inc. with the Corporate Humanitarian Award at its 39th Annual American Red Cross Ball and 4th Annual Party in Red. Ryder System, Inc. was recognized for decades of support in disaster relief. The event, held at the JW Marriott Marquis in Miami, raised significant funds for Red Cross preparedness, response, and recovery programs in South Florida.

title: Why Mega-Cap Tech Stocks’ Dominance Is a Risk
summary: The "Magnificent 7" tech stocks now comprise a significant 30% of the S&P 500 Index’s market capitalization, creating concentration risks for investors. This historical top-heaviness makes portfolios vulnerable to significant drops if these stocks, highly correlated and sensitive to interest rates, decline. The article suggests strategies like investing in U.S. bonds, non-U.S. equities, equal-weighted S&P 500 strategies, and alternative assets to mitigate this risk.

title: The Optimists Were Right About FANG Stocks
summary: The article discusses how the FANG stocks (Facebook, Amazon, Netflix, Google) have significantly outperformed other investments since their popularization in 2013, contrary to skeptics' predictions. It highlights that the strong stock market performance was backed by dominant business fundamentals, not just speculative enthusiasm. The author questions whether similar skepticism regarding the current "Magnificent Seven" stocks will also prove incorrect.

title: No infringement of Trademark: Supreme Court dismisses MMT's case against Booking.com, Google
summary: The Supreme Court of India dismissed Make My Trip's (MMT) trademark infringement case against Booking.com and Google. The court found no deception, stating that using trademarks as keywords in Google Ads by competitors does not inherently constitute infringement, supporting the Delhi High Court's previous stance on the matter.

title: Microsoft Says Russian Gov Hackers Stole Source Code After Spying on Executive Emails
summary: Microsoft has stated that the Russian government-backed hacking group, Midnight Blizzard, not only spied on senior executive emails but also stole source code and may still be active within its internal systems. The company disclosed that the hackers are leveraging exfiltrated email information to gain unauthorized access, including to source code repositories, and have significantly increased related attack volumes. This ongoing incident comes after an initial breach detected in January 2024, linked to a password spray attack on a legacy test account.

title: Megacap tech stocks dominate many ESG funds. Here’s why
summary: Megacap tech stocks frequently dominate ESG funds because these funds still aim for top performers within economic groups, and the tech sector is considered "cleaner" regarding environmental impact. While ESG funds saw net outflows in Q4 2023 due to advisors pulling back, individual investor interest remains strong, viewing ESG as a long-term allocation strategy rather than a momentum play. Despite misconceptions, ESG funds can and do invest in energy companies, as explained by experts from DWS Group and former VettaFi.

title: Alphabet is the bargain stock among the ‘Magnificent Seven’
summary: Alphabet, Google's holding company, is highlighted as a compelling investment opportunity among the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks due to its valuation. Its stock trades at a lower price/earnings valuation than the S&P 500, positioning it as a bargain compared to its peers and the broader market. This situation is likened to the opportunity presented by Meta's stock after its 2022 decline.

title: Alphabet is the bargain stock among the ‘Magnificent Seven’
summary: Alphabet (GOOGL, GOOG) is identified as a compelling investment opportunity among the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks due to its valuation. The article highlights that Alphabet is the only member of this group trading at a lower price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio than the S&P 500, suggesting it's a bargain. This situation is compared to Meta's stock in 2022, offering a similar investment chance.

title: Equinix Announces Leadership Succession Plans: CEO to Move to Executive Chair and Google Cloud Exec Selected as Next CEO
summary: Equinix announced a leadership transition plan where current President and CEO Charles Meyers will become Executive Chairman in late Q2 2024. Adaire Fox-Martin, Google Cloud's Go-to-Market President, will take over as the new President and CEO. Peter Van Camp, the current Executive Chairman, will move to an advisory role to the Board.

title: Equinix hires Google Cloud executive as CEO
summary: Equinix (EQIX.O) has named Adaire Fox-Martin, a Google Cloud executive, as its new CEO, effective late next quarter. Fox-Martin, who has been an Equinix board member since 2020, will succeed current CEO Charles Meyers, who will transition to executive chair. The data center operator recently forecast optimistic adjusted core earnings, driven by anticipation of steady client spending on its data centers due to increased artificial intelligence technology integration.

title: Equinix's CEO to Transition to Executive Chair, Adaire Fox-Martin Named Successor
summary: Equinix, Inc. has announced a significant leadership transition where current President and CEO Charles Meyers will become Executive Chairman in Q2 2024. Adaire Fox-Martin, currently President of Google Cloud Go-to-Market, will take over as Equinix's new President and CEO. This change aims to support Equinix's global expansion, building on recent financial growth and leadership appointments like Merrie Williamson as Chief Customer and Revenue Officer.

title: Google reaffirms commitment to back ‘Digital Philippines’
summary: Google has reaffirmed its commitment to building a "Digital Philippines" through initiatives focused on digital skilling and infrastructure development. This includes expanding Google Career Certificates through DTI virtual campuses and progressing with the TPU subsea cable system. These investments have already contributed significantly to the Philippines' GDP and job creation, with further collaboration anticipated.

title: Markets Showing Signs of Bubble on Big Tech, Crypto Surge, BofA’s Hartnett Says
summary: Bank of America’s Chief Investment Strategist Michael Hartnett believes that the current market surge in "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks and cryptocurrencies exhibits characteristics of a bubble. He points to re-accelerating inflation, soft growth, and the narrowness of gaining assets as signs of a "bubble mentality."

title: Data management trends: GenAI, governance and lakehouses
summary: The article discusses the top data management trends for 2024, highlighting generative AI, data governance, observability, and the shift towards data lakehouses. It explains how generative AI is being integrated into data platforms to simplify tasks and how data lakehouses are gaining momentum for analytics. The piece also emphasizes the increasing importance of data governance and observability for security, privacy, and accuracy, even as investment funding for emerging vendors slows down.

title: Federal Court of Appeal rejects Google’s appeal on smart-speaker technology patent
summary: The Federal Court of Appeal has dismissed Google's appeal regarding its patent for adaptive echo and noise control in electronic devices. The court upheld a previous finding that Sonos Inc.'s smart speaker technology does not infringe on Google's patent. This decision relates to the case Google LLC v. Sonos, Inc.

title: Gaia Launches Gaia+, the World’s Most Extensive Streaming Platform for Transformation
summary: Gaia, Inc. has launched Gaia+, an enhanced streaming platform offering over 8,000 documentaries and series focused on transformation, spiritual growth, and consciousness expansion. This new platform provides access to immersive workshops, live events, and guided programs led by expert teachers. Gaia+ aims to empower its community with resources for personal development, stress alleviation, and abundance manifestation, available across various mobile and connected TV devices.

title: Options Bulls Resume Blitz on Alphabet Stock
summary: Alphabet (GOOGL) stock is seeing renewed bullish options activity, with shares up 6.1% following reports of Apple potentially licensing Google's Gemini AI. Despite a recent uptick in bearish bets, call volume is again dominating today's options trading as GOOGL eyes its Jan. 29 record high.

title: 5 Stocks to Buy From the Best Money Managers
summary: This article identifies five undervalued stocks—Alphabet, PepsiCo, Comcast, RTX Corp, and Medtronic—that are popular among top-rated, concentrated fund managers. It highlights that the market is "fully valued" and emphasizes the importance of focusing on undervalued stocks for long-term gains. Each stock is discussed with an analyst's commentary on its competitive advantages and growth prospects, reinforcing their "wide economic moat" ratings.

title: Meta Bows Down to EU Regulations After Apple And Google, Cut Subscription Prices for Ad-Free Facebook and Instagram
summary: Meta Platforms has significantly reduced the subscription fee for its ad-free Facebook and Instagram services in Europe, lowering the monthly cost to 5.99 euros from 9.99 euros. This move addresses concerns from privacy and antitrust regulators and comes as a response to the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA). The company aims to find a middle ground amidst EU privacy laws and the DMA's stipulations, following similar concessions by Google and Apple.

title: Lisa Larson-Kelley
summary: Lisa Larson-Kelley is the founder and CEO of Quantious, an Inc5000 honoree and a trailblazer for women in the tech sector for nearly two decades. She specializes in bridging the gap between technology and marketing strategies, having worked with major companies like Meta, Google, Shopify, and Salesforce. The article also lists various articles she has authored for Inc., covering topics from AI implementation for executives to successful agency partnerships and avoiding burnout.

title: Google, Microsoft, and Nucor partner for new energy tech PPAs
summary: Google, Microsoft, and Nucor Corporation are collaborating to develop and fund early-stage clean energy projects, including advanced nuclear, geothermal, and long-duration energy storage. They aim to reduce risk for developers by aggregating demand and designing new commercial models to bring these technologies online by the early 2030s. The companies have issued an RFI for potential projects in several US regions, with an initial focus on the US to prove the demand aggregation and procurement model for these advanced technologies.

title: Microsoft, Google, Nucor partner on initiative to spur emerging 24/7 clean power technologies
summary: Microsoft, Google, and Nucor have launched the Advanced Clean Electricity initiative to accelerate the development and deployment of reliable, low- or zero-carbon electricity generation technologies like advanced nuclear, next-generation geothermal, and long-duration energy storage. The initiative aims for widespread commercial deployment by the early 2030s and aggregates the three companies' demand for clean electricity to spur first-of-a-kind and early commercial projects. Projects must be located in the U.S., with a preference for the PJM region, and meet specific evaluation criteria, including a potential capacity factor above 50% and project capacity above 50 MW.

title: Clean Electricity: Microsoft, Google and Nucor Join Forces
summary: Microsoft, Google, and Nucor are collaborating to overcome barriers in developing clean electricity technologies. They plan to establish new business models and aggregate their demand to accelerate first-of-a-kind projects such as advanced nuclear, next-generation geothermal, clean hydrogen, and long-duration energy storage. The companies are issuing an RFI for potential projects in the US, aiming to bring clear customer voice to policymakers and develop new tariff structures.

title: Under Pressure: Antitrust and Competition Authorities are Now Focusing on Apple
summary: Antitrust and competition authorities in both the EU and the US are increasingly scrutinizing Apple's market practices, a significant shift for a company that previously avoided intense regulatory attention. Recent actions include a substantial fine from the European Commission related to App Store policies and a lawsuit from the US Department of Justice alleging anticompetitive practices designed to maintain iPhone user lock-in. These developments suggest a global movement towards stricter regulation of big tech, challenging Apple's long-held justifications of privacy and security for its closed ecosystem.

title: Apple’s stock has faltered, but a Steve Jobs moment in AI may be on the horizon
summary: Apple's stock performance has been underwhelming, leading to concerns about its AI capabilities. However, an analyst suggests that Apple CEO Tim Cook might be preparing a "Steve Jobs moment" by announcing an AI app store, similar to how Wall Street initially underestimated the iTunes Store. This could represent a significant play in the artificial intelligence sector, despite previous doubts fueled by reports of Apple discussing AI engines with other tech giants.

title: Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 03/24/2024
summary: This article details the top-performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week of March 24, 2024, highlighting their weekly returns and the underlying factors contributing to their performance. It covers various sectors, including cannabis, homebuilders, technology (Nvidia, META, FANG+), travel, banking, auto industry, and semiconductors, explaining the market drivers behind each ETF's gains. The article emphasizes the rapid movement characteristic of leveraged funds and the importance of due diligence.

title: London Stock Exchange to start trading crypto ETNs on May 28
summary: The London Stock Exchange (LSE) is set to begin trading Bitcoin and Ether Exchange Traded Notes (ETNs) on May 28, following approval from the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA). These crypto ETNs will be exclusively available to institutional investors. The move is fueled by the significant success of spot Bitcoin ETFs in the United States, which have seen massive uptake and substantial capital appreciation.

title: Zoom spreads AI across collaboration services
summary: Zoom has launched Workplace, an AI-powered collaboration hub that integrates chat, phone, video, and email services, including its AI Companion at no extra cost. This move positions Zoom to compete with rivals like Microsoft and Google, which charge for similar AI services, and addresses enterprise concerns about data control and privacy in AI applications. The company also announced improvements to its video conferencing platform and a partnership with Avaya to expand its reach.

title: A new product allows 5x leveraged bets on the Magnificent Seven. Traders should be wary.
summary: A new exchange-traded product allows investors to make 5x leveraged bets on the Magnificent Seven group of stocks, raising concerns among analysts. While levered exchange-traded products are gaining popularity, traders are warned about the significant risks associated with such highly leveraged instruments. ETF analyst Todd Sohn from Strategas Securities notes that this product appears to be the most heavily levered available in any developed market.

title: Gene-editing expert Gaudelli joins GV (Google Ventures)
summary: Nicole Gaudelli, a gene-editing specialist previously with Beam Therapeutics and a postdoctoral researcher at Harvard and Broad Institute, has joined GV (Google Ventures) as an entrepreneur in residence. Gaudelli is known for her work on CRISPR technology and base editing, which can address rare diseases. Her expertise is particularly relevant to GV, as her base editor technology is already utilized by two of the firm's portfolio companies, Beam Therapeutics and Verve Therapeutics.

title: 3 Great Value Funds for 2024
summary: Russel Kinnel suggests three value funds for investors looking to balance growth-heavy portfolios. He recommends Vanguard High Dividend Yield Index Fund (VHYAX) for steady value exposure, Dodge & Cox Stock (DODGX) for strong stock-picking, and Oakmark Select Investor (OAKLX) for a focused fund straddling the value/blend border with a mix of value and growth stocks. These funds offer diversification and potential resilience if growth stocks decline.

title: Google Agrees to Delete ‘Incognito’ Browsing Data to Settle Class-Action Lawsuit
summary: Google has agreed to delete millions of records of users' browsing activities as part of a class-action lawsuit settlement. The lawsuit alleged that Google tracked users without their knowledge even when they used Incognito mode. This settlement includes no payment from Google but allows individuals to pursue damages independently.

title: Communication Services Stocks: Advertising Continues to Rule
summary: Advertising continues to be the dominant force in the communication services sector, with Alphabet and Meta Platforms leading the market. While traditional TV ad revenue is declining, online digital advertising is growing significantly, and streaming platforms are attracting more ad spending. The article highlights Warner Bros. Discovery, Crown Castle, and Comcast as top picks in the sector.

title: Meta Joins Hand With Mukesh Ambani’s Reliance To Set Up Firm’s First Data Center In India
summary: Meta Platforms is partnering with Mukesh Ambani's Reliance Industries Limited to establish its first data center in India, which will be hosted on RIL’s Chennai campus. This move aims to localize data processing for Meta's platforms, including Facebook, Instagram, and Whatsapp, which have a vast user base in India, currently having their data managed from Singapore. The collaboration strengthens the existing relationship between the two companies, as Meta previously invested in Reliance's Jio Platforms.

title: The Rise of FinOps and GreenOps
summary: The article introduces FinOps and GreenOps as crucial methodologies for optimizing cloud usage in terms of both cost and environmental sustainability.
