This new system aims to help users contextualize their glucose levels and improve metabolic health.

title: YieldMax Dorsey Wright Hybrid 5 Income ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:FIVY)
summary: This article provides detailed information on the YieldMax Dorsey Wright Hybrid 5 Income ETF (FIVY), including its current price, and key statistics like market capitalization, assets under management, and dividend yield. It outlines the ETF's investment strategy, which involves tracking an index of five stocks and five affiliated ETFs using synthetic covered call strategies. The article also lists recent dividend declarations and its top holdings, such as YieldMax options income ETFs for GOOGL, AMD, PLTR, MRNA, and RDDT.

title: AI’s Next Big Winners: 3 Small-Cap Stocks to Watch in 2025
summary: This article highlights three small-cap AI stocks—Airship AI (AISP), BigBear.ai (BBAI), and Rezolve AI (RZLV)—to watch in 2025, each targeting significant revenue opportunities in diverse sectors like military, law enforcement, and e-commerce. It details their current performance, analyst ratings, and future growth prospects. The companies are presented as

title: Jabil’s Winning Streak Begins: Market Bottom Set, Future in Focus
summary: Jabil's fiscal Q1 2025 report indicates that the company has hit its market bottom, with a strong outlook for future growth despite a top-line contraction. The company outperformed expectations for both revenue and earnings, providing optimistic guidance and benefiting from strength in cloud, data center, and digital commerce segments. Analyst and institutional investor trends are positive, supporting a potential new all-time high for the stock.

title: Does the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV) pay passive income?
summary: The iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV) can provide passive income through dividends, although its 12-month trailing dividend yield was close to 1% as of December 2024, which is lower than current term deposit rates. This low yield is due to its holdings being primarily growth-oriented technology companies with low dividend payout ratios and high valuations. While not ideal for income-focused investors, the IVV ETF has historically delivered strong capital growth, making it a robust wealth-builder.

title: Apple looks to make a splash in 2025 with AI-infused smart home devices
summary: Apple is set to launch an AI-powered smart home hub in 2025 with a 6-inch display and new operating system, alongside upgrades to existing Home devices. The company is also developing an in-home security camera and a smart doorbell with advanced facial recognition, leveraging its commitment to privacy to gain consumer trust and expand revenue in the smart home market. These new offerings signal a significant push by Apple into the connected home ecosystem.

title: Quantum computing boom: Innovations, investments, cyber security risks
summary: The article discusses the rapid advancements in quantum computing, highlighted by Google's new Willow Chip, and the urgent need for post-quantum cryptography to mitigate severe cyber security threats. It details significant investments and innovations from companies like Scope Technologies Corp., SEALSQ Corp., Arqit Quantum Inc., Quantum Computing Inc., and D-Wave Quantum Inc., in both problem-solving and cyber security applications. The cloud-based quantum computing market is projected to reach $55.22 billion by 2031, with the cloud encryption market expected to hit $42.5 billion by 2032.

title: Netflix files suit against Broadcom over VMware virtual machine patents
summary: Netflix Inc. is suing Broadcom Inc. over five patents related to virtual machines, which Netflix claims are being infringed upon by Broadcom's subsidiary, VMware Inc. The lawsuit, filed in a California federal court, alleges willful infringement by VMware since 2012 and seeks unspecified damages. This legal action adds to an existing patent dispute between Netflix and Broadcom concerning video streaming technology.

title: Is Tesla set to Launch Self-Driving Taxis in 2025?
summary: Elon Musk has announced plans to launch driverless ride-hailing services in California and Texas by 2025, but Tesla faces significant technical and regulatory challenges, especially in California. Texas offers a more favorable regulatory environment, yet extensive testing is still required. The company's Full Self-Driving system, despite previous setbacks and regulatory scrutiny, forms the basis for its robotaxi program, with the recent unveiling of the Cybercab causing a surge in Tesla's stock and impacting competitors.

title: Jim Cramer Cisco Systems, Inc. (CSCO): ‘I Wouldn’t Recommend Buying Aggressively Over $50’
summary: Jim Cramer has advised investors against aggressively buying Cisco Systems, Inc. (CSCO) stock if it's trading above $50, despite recent positive earnings and a 30% gain since its Q4 earnings report. Cramer also expressed skepticism about the recent surge in quantum computing stocks, drawing parallels to non-fungible tokens, and criticized the Federal Reserve's recent interest rate cut given the underlying economic data. He suggests that 2025 might be a year to focus on "cult stocks" that defy conventional valuation methods.

title: Jim Cramer on Micron Technology Inc. (MU): ‘I Think It Could Drop To Around $98 or $99. At That Point, I Would Consider Buying More’
summary: Jim Cramer discussed Micron Technology Inc. (MU), suggesting that a drop to around $98 or $99 would be a buying opportunity. He also commented on the broader market, criticizing the Federal Reserve's recent interest rate cut and expressing skepticism about speculative areas like quantum computing and Bitcoin. Cramer advises investors to focus on "cult stocks" that defy conventional valuation in 2025.

title: The concept of Quantum Computing has gained traction again, as WiMi Hologram Cloud officially announces the development of QRAM, with the stock price soaring nearly 80% in a day.
summary: WiMi Hologram Cloud's stock price surged by nearly 80% after announcing the development of a Quantum Random Access Memory (QRAM) architecture. This development, aimed at improving data processing in quantum systems, has reignited interest in quantum computing. The market's enthusiasm for quantum computing is further evidenced by similar stock surges for other quantum technology companies following Google's new quantum chip announcement.

title: Gaxos.ai Inc. (GXAI) Launches Meshy 4 Update to Revolutionize 3D Modeling Workflows for Game Developers
summary: Gaxos.ai Inc. (GXAI) has released Meshy 4, an update to its Gaxos Labs platform, designed to enhance 3D modeling for game developers using AI-driven generative geometry. This update allows for dynamic mesh topology, polycount management, and improved geometry quality, aiming to accelerate asset creation. The generative AI market is projected to reach $1.3 trillion by 2032, driven by demand for AI products and infrastructure, with significant impact on various industries including gaming.

title: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) Poised for Strong 2025 Growth with Custom AI Chips and Strategic Partnerships, Says William Blair
summary: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) is anticipated to experience strong growth in 2025, driven by its custom AI chip development and strategic partnerships with major cloud customers, according to William Blair. The company recently surpassed a $1 trillion market cap and its CEO announced the development of custom AI chips with three large cloud clients. William Blair maintains an Outperform rating, expecting steady growth from existing partnerships and potential new clients like ByteDance, OpenAI, and Apple.

title: How AI is being misused in creating deep fakes and 3 countries which top this list of viewers
summary: Websites offering AI tools to create non-consensual explicit images are raising significant ethical concerns, attracting millions of visits annually, primarily in the U.S., India, and Japan. A majority of these sites were created in 2024, exacerbating the spread of deepfake content, 98% of which was sexually explicit in 2023. Countries worldwide are now working to counter this misuse of AI and deepfake technology.

title: Apple class action allege App Store offers malicious crypto apps
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Apple Inc., claiming the company authorized and maintained malicious crypto applications on its App Store, leading to the theft of consumers' personal financial assets. Plaintiffs allege Apple misrepresented the safety and legality of these apps, including Digicoins, SolLuna, and Forex5, which they assert were "spoofing" programs designed to steal account information. The lawsuit seeks to represent a nationwide class of consumers for alleged violations of California's Consumers Legal Remedies Act and Unfair Competition Law.

title: Samsung Display's investment the biggest FDI project licensed in Vietnam in 2024
summary: Samsung Display Vietnam's (SDV) $1.8 billion project in Bac Ninh province was the largest foreign-invested project licensed in Vietnam in 2024, focusing on manufacturing OLED products for IT equipment and vehicles. This article details significant FDI projects in Vietnam for 2024, including major investments from Amkor, LG Display, Chinese automaker Chery, and South Korea’s Hyosung, highlighting the country's growing appeal for global technology and manufacturing giants. It also mentions future pledges from companies like Nvidia, SpaceX, and Siam Cement Group.

title: Synaptics partners with Google for multi-modal edge AI for IoT
summary: Synaptics and Google are collaborating to integrate Google's machine learning technology with Synaptics' Astra AI hardware and software. This partnership aims to develop multi-modal, context-aware devices capable of performing edge AI for IoT applications, supporting various modalities like vision, voice, and sound. The initiative seeks to bring transformative edge AI experiences across consumer, automotive, enterprise, and industrial systems.

title: Synaptics Gains on Google Collaboration to Advance Edge AI in IoT Applications
summary: Synaptics' shares jumped 8% following the announcement of a partnership with Google to enhance Edge AI for Internet of Things (IoT) applications. This collaboration combines Google's machine learning core with Synaptics' Astra AI-Native processing platform to enable context-aware computing and maximize multimodal processing in various consumer, business, and industrial devices. The initiative aims to accelerate AI research for IoT devices to seamlessly handle diverse input challenges like vision, speech, and sound, leveraging Synaptics' low-power silicon and Google's MLIR-compliant ML core.

title: QTUM: Investing In Quantum The Right Way (NASDAQ:QTUM)
summary: The article suggests that while quantum computing stocks are highly volatile, the Defiance Quantum ETF (QTUM) offers a safer and more diversified investment approach. It highlights Alphabet's advancements in quantum computing and McKinsey's projection of a significant market by 2040. Despite some bearish technical signals, QTUM is recommended for long-term portfolios due to its outperformance, reasonable expense ratio, and yield, although better entry points might appear soon.

title: Omnicom Media Group to partner with Google on search, Digiday reports
summary: Omnicom Media Group (OMG) is partnering with Google on a two-pronged search initiative. This collaboration, announced during CES, focuses on developing a planning tool and using AI for investment and creative decisions in search-driven efforts. This is the first of four major platform partnerships OMG plans to unveil this year.

title: Omnicom Media Group hits CES with a blitz of search-related partnerships, starting with Google
summary: Omnicom Media Group (OMG) announced a series of search-related partnerships at CES 2025, spearheaded by a significant collaboration with Google. This two-pronged partnership introduces a "Share of Voice" planning tool utilizing Google's Gemini AI for campaign optimization, and a "Next Generation Search Agent" to provide AI-driven investment and creative guidance within Omnicom's Omni platform. The initiatives aim to help clients navigate the evolving landscape of search marketing, particularly with the advancements of generative AI.

title: Can Kwai and Bigo Live replace TikTok? alternatives for 2025
summary: With a potential TikTok ban looming in early 2025, marketers and users are seeking alternative short-form video platforms. This article explores Kwai and Bigo Live as strong contenders, alongside established alternatives like YouTube Shorts and Instagram Reels. It details their strategies, target demographics, and features, assessing their potential to fill the void left by TikTok.

title: Delta Just Announced Its Plan to Use AI to Solve the Worst Thing About Traveling
summary: Delta Air Lines, celebrating its 100th birthday, announced several initiatives at CES, including Delta Concierge, an AI-powered personal assistant within its Fly Delta app. This new tool aims to personalize the travel experience, offering suggestions and eventually enabling complex commands like finding alternative flights using natural language, which could mitigate travel uncertainty. Other announcements included partnerships with Uber and YouTube, and a plan for free Wi-Fi across its global fleet.

title: What is the Google Gemini AI model (formerly Bard)?
summary: Google Gemini, previously known as Bard, is an AI chatbot developed by Google that uses NLP and machine learning to mimic human conversations. It is a multimodal AI large language model (LLM) capable of understanding language, audio, code, and video, and is integrated into various Google services and available for developers. The article details its functionality, evolution from Bard, various model sizes (Ultra, Pro, Nano), and its competitive landscape against other AI models like OpenAI's GPT-3 and GPT-4, while also addressing its limitations and concerns regarding bias and accuracy.

title: Lumen’s Finke Charts Network, AI Intersections
summary: Josh Finke, Lumen's Senior Vice President, Public Sector, discusses how Lumen Technologies is supporting Federal government agencies with network modernization and AI integration. The company is focusing on simplifying technology consumption, enhancing network security and reliability, and building an "AI fabric" to support data-intensive AI workloads. Finke also shared Lumen's perspective on Federal AI policy, emphasizing a multi-phase approach to AI adoption for measurable outcomes.

title: Apple, Google And Meta Can't Escape Gambling Lawsuits, Judge Rejects Section 230 Shield In Landmark Ruling On Casino-Style Apps
summary: A federal judge has ruled that Apple, Google, and Meta must face lawsuits alleging they profited from illegal casino-style apps, rejecting their Section 230 defense. The companies are accused of hosting and promoting addictive gambling apps and taking 30% commissions on in-app purchases, with plaintiffs seeking compensatory damages for alleged addiction and its consequences. This ruling adds to the growing legal scrutiny faced by tech giants.

title: ESPN, Fox, Warner Bros. Discovery Drop Venu Sports Joint Venture
summary: ESPN, Fox, and Warner Bros. Discovery have decided to shut down their joint venture, Venu Sports, before its public launch. This decision comes shortly after Disney, ESPN's parent company, settled an antitrust lawsuit with Fubo Inc. by acquiring a majority stake in Fubo and forming a new joint venture with them. The companies stated that they opted to focus on existing products and distribution channels, citing an "ever-changing marketplace."

title: Bentley Systems Welcomes James Lee as Chief Operating Officer
summary: Bentley Systems has appointed James Lee, formerly of Google and SAP, as its new Chief Operating Officer to drive operational excellence and growth initiatives. The company also streamlined its organizational structure by consolidating product development under Chief Technology Officer Julien Moutte, making the Chief Product Officer role redundant and leading to Mike Campbell's departure. These changes aim to accelerate innovation and capitalize on growth opportunities related to infrastructure AI.

title: What’s new with Matter: how Matter 1.4 is reshaping interoperability and energy management
summary: Matter 1.4 is the latest update to the smart home interoperability standard, introducing support for new device types like solar panels, heat pumps, and water heaters, alongside enhanced energy management features. This update also streamlines the multi-admin experience, allowing users to connect devices to several smart home systems more efficiently. While adoption is gradual, Matter 1.4 further solidifies the protocol's goal of creating a unified and user-friendly smart home ecosystem.

title: Liberty All-Star Equity Fund Reports December Decline Despite Strong 20.67% YTD Gains
summary: The Liberty All-Star Equity Fund (NYSE: USA) released its December 2024 monthly update, reporting a decline of -5.31% in NAV and -4.79% in market price for the month. Despite this, the fund maintained strong year-to-date returns of 14.06% (NAV) and 20.67% (market price). The fund, which categorizes as large-cap core, had net assets totaling $2,007.0 million, with 97.8% invested in equities, primarily in Information Technology, Financials, and Health Care.

title: EPAM Expands Collaboration with Google Cloud to Deliver Scalable AI Solutions for Industry Transformation
summary: EPAM Systems, Inc. announced an expanded strategic partnership with Google Cloud to deliver scalable AI solutions for industry transformation. The collaboration leverages Google Cloud’s Vertex AI platform to provide innovative solutions across media and entertainment, energy, and retail verticals, aiming to drive measurable business outcomes through generative AI, legacy modernization, and data analytics. This partnership will introduce four new solutions, including Video Search and Indexing, JenAii™ for Retail, Geospatial Data Visualization for Energy, and Talk to Your Data, enhancing customer satisfaction and operational efficiency.

title: At NRF, companies like Dick’s Sporting Goods and Sweetgreen talk up expanded uses of AI
summary: At the 2025 NRF Big Show, retailers demonstrated tangible applications of AI beyond basic customer service, focusing on operational efficiency. Dick's Sporting Goods showcased an AI-powered pricing model for inventory optimization, while Sweetgreen highlighted its "Infinite Kitchen" robotics for faster food preparation. Despite the benefits, experts like Centric Software's Joe Groves noted the overwhelming number of AI solutions on the market and the continued need for human oversight and contextual data in AI models, as emphasized by Dick's Sporting Goods.

title: AI revolution: Impact Series
summary: The article discusses the significant infrastructure demands of the AI revolution, highlighting the strain on resources like power, land, water, and materials due to increasing AI adoption and model training. It emphasizes the need for responsible and sustainable development of AI, examining energy consumption by data centers and the growing demand for "firm" and "dispatchable" power sources like nuclear and geothermal energy. Authors Christian Keller, William Thompson, and Akash Utsav contributed to this Impact Series report from Barclays Research, stressing the global challenge of balancing economic opportunities with environmental sustainability in AI development.

title: Comscore Announces Unified Content Measurement
summary: Comscore has launched Comscore Content Measurement (CCM), a new solution within The Comscore Platform that unifies content measurement across various channels and platforms. This advanced self-service tool provides content owners and creators with a holistic view of consumer behavior, enabling them to understand viewing habits and commercialize their offerings more effectively. CCM integrates measurement across linear TV, CTV/Streaming, PC, Mobile, and Social, offering deduplicated audience reach insights and enhancing content and ad monetization strategies for brands like Google, NBCUniversal, and Paramount.

title: Biden Administration Releases Executive Order on AI Infrastructure
summary: The Biden Administration issued an Executive Order on AI Infrastructure on January 14, 2025, aimed at advancing U.S. leadership in AI, preventing reliance on foreign infrastructure, and promoting clean energy for AI operations. The EO directs federal agencies to lease federal land for private-sector construction of "frontier AI infrastructure," including data centers and clean energy facilities, by specified timelines. It also outlines requirements for expedited permitting and upgrades to electricity grid infrastructure to support these AI developments.

title: CHAT: Generative AI Stocks Still Look Relatively Attractive
summary: The Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT) has seen a significant rise of +13.60% since October 2024, outperforming the S&P 500. The ETF maintains a diversified portfolio with key holdings in NVIDIA, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Meta Platforms. While previously rated a "Strong Buy," the rating has been revised to "Buy" due to macroeconomic uncertainties, even as it appears between fair value and "slightly undervalued."

title: Oracle Named a Leader in the 2024 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud Database Management Systems
summary: Oracle has been recognized as a Leader in the 2024 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cloud Database Management Systems, highlighting its converged database architecture and continuous innovation. The company's database services, including Oracle Autonomous Database and Oracle Database 23ai, offer flexible intercloud and multicloud deployment choices, enabling customers to leverage various data and application development models for enterprise data and AI workloads. Oracle Database is lauded for its rapid pace of innovation and industry-leading multicloud capabilities, now available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.

title: Sonos takeover speculation grows, with Spotify or Amazon tipped as potential suitors (report)
summary: Speculation is mounting that audio technology company Sonos could be acquired following a problematic app rollout in 2024 and recent leadership changes, including the departure of CEO Patrick Spence. Bloomberg's Mark Gurman identifies Spotify and Amazon as the most likely suitors. While Amazon could integrate Sonos' premium audio into its Echo devices, Spotify could gain immediate entry into the hardware market, despite its past struggles with products like the "Car Thing."

title: Google Cloud Solution Provider SADA Confirms Layoffs
summary: SADA, a Google Cloud solution provider, confirmed layoffs across various North American departments as part of resource alignment with strategic priorities. The company declined to disclose the percentage of employees affected but stated it has between 500 and 1,000 employees. SADA also mentioned providing support to employees impacted by Southern California wildfires, in addition to those affected by the layoffs.

title: Unveiling the Future of Agency Media Platforms: Omnicom’s Omni vs. the Competition - White Paper
summary: This white paper by The Myers Report examines the evolving landscape of agency media platforms, focusing on Omnicom Media Group's Omni. It analyzes Omni's advanced AI capabilities, cross-channel measurement tools, and personalization features, comparing them against rival platforms such as Publicis’ Epsilon PeopleCloud, WPP’s mPlatform, Dentsu’s Merkle M1, and IPG’s Acxiom and MAGNA. The report provides a detailed analysis of each platform's strengths, weaknesses, and future outlooks in the battle for dominance in data-driven media planning and buying.

title: 7 Best Content Distribution Platforms to Grow Your Reach
summary: This article identifies and compares seven top content distribution platforms for 2026, including Buffer, Bynder, Distribution.ai, Hootsuite, Mailchimp, RELAYTO, and StoryChief. It emphasizes the critical role of content distribution in ensuring content is seen by a wide audience, especially with evolving search engine algorithms and AI. The article also provides guidance on how to choose a suitable platform, highlighting factors like UI, usability, integrations, and cost, alongside advice for building an effective content distribution strategy.

title: Meta at ATH after Announcing Massive $65B CapEx Plan for AI Push 📈
summary: Meta Platforms has announced a massive $60-65 billion capital expenditure plan for 2025, a significant increase aimed at bolstering its AI infrastructure and becoming a leader in the AI landscape. This aggressive investment includes building a new data center and acquiring 1.3 million GPUs, with the goal of developing Meta AI into a leading assistant serving over a billion users. Despite the substantial spending, market reaction has been largely positive, pushing Meta's stock to an all-time high as investors support the company's strategic AI focus.

title: Tech’s TikTok Dilemma: Follow the Law or President Trump? (Published 2025)
summary: A federal law banning TikTok in the U.S. has created a schism among American tech companies, forcing them to choose between complying with the law or adhering to President-elect Trump's executive order to pause its enforcement. Companies like Amazon, Apple, and Google have moved to comply with the ban, while others like Oracle, Akamai, and Fastly continue to support TikTok. This situation highlights the legal and political complexities surrounding the TikTok ban and the varying approaches taken by major tech firms.

title: Webull Thailand Named Best Online Trading Platform for US Market Access by The Global Economics
summary: Webull Thailand has been recognized as the "Best Online Trading Platform for US Market Access" by The Global Economics, highlighting its rapid growth and commitment to innovative investment solutions. Since its launch, Webull Thailand has attracted over 300,000 registered users and was the most downloaded brokerage app in Thailand during November and December 2024. The platform offers unique features such as 24-hour trading of US stocks and ETFs and US options trading, aiming to connect Thai investors to global opportunities.

title: AI Stock Rout: Monday’s Selloff in 5 Charts
summary: A new AI model from Chinese startup DeepSeek, which performs advanced AI tasks without relying on the most advanced semiconductor chips, triggered a significant selloff in AI-related stocks on Monday. Nvidia plunged 17%, and other chipmakers and AI-beneficiary sectors like utilities also saw steep declines. However, some large-cap tech names like Apple and Meta Platforms posted gains, and Morningstar analysts maintain their fair value estimates for key tech companies, anticipating long-term benefits from reduced AI model pricing.

title: Aurora Mobile Partners With China's DeepSeek To Boost AI Capabilities
summary: Aurora Mobile's enterprise AI platform, GPTBots.ai, has integrated China's DeepSeek R1 large language model (LLM), enhancing its capabilities alongside existing models like OpenAI, Meta Llama, and Google Gemini. This integration aims to provide businesses with more efficient, adaptable, and cost-effective advanced AI solutions, leveraging DeepSeek R1's strong reasoning tasks performance and affordability. The partnership positions GPTBots.ai as a comprehensive platform for diverse LLMs, allowing businesses to select models catering to specific operational, customer service, or marketing needs.

title: AI stock rout: The US selloff in 4 charts
summary: US stocks experienced a selloff, particularly in large-cap growth, following news that Chinese AI startup DeepSeek developed an advanced AI model that requires less sophisticated semiconductor chips. This development triggered concerns about the necessity of significant capital expenditures by large US tech companies in generative AI, leading to a 17% plunge in Nvidia stock and declines in other chipmakers and utility stocks. However, some tech giants like Apple and Meta Platforms saw gains, and Morningstar analysts maintain their fair value estimates for Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet, expecting long-term benefits from reduced AI model pricing.

title: RBC’s plans to marry large language and transaction models
summary: RBC is integrating its in-house AI models, trained on vast financial data, with large language models developed by a local rival to Microsoft and Google. This initiative aims to address a paradox in banking where generative AI excels more in language tasks than complex numerical calculations, despite the technology's potential to revolutionize financial operations. The bank is exploring how these combined AI capabilities can be applied to both internal processes and customer-facing services.

title: Meta Posts Q4 Earnings Beat, But Reality Labs Losses Mount
summary: Meta Platforms reported strong Q4 2024 earnings, exceeding analysts' expectations in revenue and EPS, driven by its Family of Apps (Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp). However, the Reality Labs division continued to incur substantial operating losses, raising concerns about the metaverse's future and prompting significant AI infrastructure investments. Despite these losses, Meta's shares performed well, indicating investor confidence in Zuckerberg's overall strategy.

title: Wix partners with YouTube to support social shopping experience -
summary: Wix.com has announced a new integration with YouTube Shopping, allowing its merchants to sell products directly through YouTube. This partnership expands Wix's social shopping capabilities, enabling merchants to display products on their YouTube profiles, tag items in videos and live streams, and track performance using YouTube's analytics. The collaboration simplifies the selling process for businesses and enhances the shopping experience for consumers directly within YouTube.

title: Yext CEO awarded $500k bonus, director resigns
summary: Yext, Inc. has awarded CEO Michael Walrath a one-time cash bonus of $500,000 for his contributions to operational efficiency. Simultaneously, Julie Richardson is stepping down from her role as a board member after nearly a decade, with her departure not being due to disagreements. These changes occur as Yext approaches its fiscal year-end, prompting investor attention to the company's future strategic direction.

title: Alphabet director Frances Arnold sells $22,000 in class C stock
summary: Alphabet director Frances Arnold recently sold 110 shares of the company's Class C Capital Stock for $22,000. This transaction was conducted under a Rule 10b5-1 Trading Plan and leaves Arnold with 16,819 shares. The sale occurred while Alphabet's stock is near its 52-week high, amidst other positive developments for the company.

title: Going Into Earnings, Is Alphabet Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar assesses Alphabet (GOOGL/GOOG) ahead of its Q4 earnings report, maintaining a 4-star "undervalued" rating with a fair value estimate of $220 per share. The report highlights key areas to watch including GenAI monetization, YouTube's growth, and the overall ad business performance, while noting the company's strong financial position and wide economic moat despite antitrust concerns.

title: Outbrain-Teads $1 Billion Merger Deal Cleared by U.K. Antitrust Regulator
summary: The U.K.'s antitrust watchdog has approved the $1 billion merger between U.S. online recommendation platform Outbrain and media company Teads. The companies announced their merger plans in August 2024, aiming to strengthen their position in the digital advertising market against major players like Google and Meta. The Competition and Markets Authority will publish its full decision on the deal shortly, having initiated its probe last December.

title: Technology in 2025: The Cycle Rolls On
summary: Goldman Sachs Asset Management foresees a robust technology sector in 2025, driven by AI momentum and a broadening of opportunities beyond the "Magnificent 7." The article highlights investment potential in hyperscalers, software companies benefiting from AI, and ASIC semiconductor manufacturers, emphasizing the importance of active management to identify growth areas.

title: Intel 2024 revenue dips amid corporate overhaul
summary: Intel's annual revenue for 2024 fell by 2% year over year to $53.1 billion, with its internal foundry business incurring a $13 billion loss, though the company aims for it to break even by late 2027. Amidst these financial challenges, Intel is undergoing a corporate overhaul, including a search for a new CEO after Pat Gelsinger stepped down. The company is also implementing cost-cutting measures, such as a 15% workforce reduction, and navigating market volatility caused by new tariffs while striving to compete in the AI chip sector.

title: AI to drive 165% increase in data center power demand by 2030
summary: Goldman Sachs Research anticipates a significant surge in global data center power demand, projecting a 50% increase by 2027 and up to 165% by 2030 compared to 2023, primarily driven by the expansion of artificial intelligence. This growth will strain infrastructure, necessitating substantial investment in the electric grid and potentially affecting supply-demand balance for data centers, with occupancy rates expected to peak around 95% in late 2026 before moderating. The market expansion presents diverse investment opportunities across hyperscalers, asset managers, utilities, and data center operators.

title: February 2025 Android Updates by Google; Severe Flaws in VMware & Microsoft’s Azure AI
summary: Google has released its February 2025 Android security updates, patching 46 vulnerabilities, including the actively exploited Kernel flaw CVE-2024-53104 and a critical Qualcomm WLAN vulnerability. Concurrently, VMware addressed multiple vulnerabilities in its Aria Operations products, with CVE-2025-22218 being the most severe information disclosure flaw. Microsoft also issued patches for critical vulnerabilities in Azure AI Face Service (CVE-2025-21415, an authentication bypass) and Microsoft Account (CVE-2025-21396, a privilege escalation flaw), urging users and organizations to apply updates promptly to mitigate risks.

title: Ford CFO swap: Sherry House, GM engineer turned investment banker, rises
summary: Ford Motor Co. has announced Sherry House will replace John Lawler as Chief Financial Officer, effective February 6, 2025. House brings a unique background from product engineering at General Motors to investment banking and CFO roles at Lucid Motors and Waymo, highlighting her passion for innovation and its funding. Lawler will transition to vice chair, focusing on strategic development and global partnerships.

title: Redfin video privacy lawsuit voluntarily dismissed by plaintiff
summary: A video privacy lawsuit against Redfin, alleging violations of the Video Privacy Protection Act and California Invasion of Privacy Act, has been voluntarily dismissed with prejudice by the plaintiff, Guillermo Mata. Mata had accused Redfin of sharing consumers' personally identifiable information from video home tour views with third-party companies. The dismissal occurred after Mata agreed to individual binding arbitration for his claims.

title: News | Cousins Properties buys into office rebound with $1 billion investment spree
summary: Cousins Properties invested nearly $1 billion in office properties during the fourth quarter of 2024, acquiring three trophy assets across the Sun Belt, including the Sail Tower in Austin and Vantage South End in Charlotte. This investment spree signals the company's belief in an office market rebound, positioning itself to meet growing demand for high-quality office space. The company's strategy focuses on well-located, amenitized spaces, which has contributed to an 89.2% occupancy rate and significant leasing activity in 2024.

title: Top Public Sector Leaders to Watch in 2025: NetApp’s Michelle Rudnicki
summary: Michelle Rudnicki, President of NetApp U.S. Public Sector, is recognized as a top public sector leader to watch in 2025. Under her guidance, NetApp is expanding its offerings and certifications to help government agencies modernize their data infrastructures, focusing on AI and cybersecurity. NetApp aims to be a strategic partner for the U.S. Public Sector by providing solutions that simplify infrastructure, adopt cloud technologies, and drive scalability while reducing operational costs.

title: 3 More Stocks to Buy Before They Rebound
summary: This article from Morningstar discusses the current market environment, including tariff impacts and upcoming economic reports, and features an interview with Morningstar's Chief US Market Strategist, Dave Sekera. Sekera provides updates on several companies, including Alphabet, Amazon, Advanced Micro Devices, and Palantir, after their recent earnings reports, and recommends three stocks to buy: Alphabet (GOOGL), SLB Ltd (SLB), and United Parcel Service Inc Class B (UPS). He also touches on Gilead Sciences, Kraft Heinz, Papa John's, Eli Lilly, Novo Nordisk, Estée Lauder, and FMC.

title: Tech Moves: Google Cloud hires Microsoft exec Hayete Gallot in new president role; Impinj CRO retires; WSU names president
summary: Google Cloud has hired former Microsoft executive Hayete Gallot as its new president of customer experience, aiming to streamline AI solution implementation for customers. This article also details other significant personnel changes within the Pacific Northwest tech community, including the retirement of Impinj CRO Jeff Dossett, Washington State University naming Elizabeth Cantwell as its first female president, and various new hires at generative AI startup Docugami. Additionally, it covers leadership changes at Fortive and its new spin-off Ralliant, and updates from United Planet, Holland America Line, and the Technology Association of Oregon.

title: Alphabet (Google) Stock Price Dropped by 10%: Should You Buy?
summary: Alphabet Inc.'s stock price dropped by 10% after its Q4 FY 2024 earnings report, despite strong growth across all segments. Concerns revolve around slightly lower-than-expected Google Cloud revenue growth and anticipated increases in depreciation and capital expenditure. The article analyzes these concerns, suggesting the dip might present an investment opportunity given the company's strong financial position and the rapidly expanding AI market.

title: Is Alphabet a No-Brainer Bargain Buy Right Now?
summary: Alphabet's shares recently dropped after its quarterly revenue missed analysts' estimates and capital spending plans exceeded predictions, leading to investor disappointment. Despite this, the company's core Google Services and Google Cloud segments showed double-digit revenue growth and significant AI advancements. With a strong track record and market leadership, the current valuation at 20x forward earnings makes Alphabet a potential bargain buy for long-term investors.

title: DeepSeek Shows There Is No Longer One “AI Trade,” but Many
summary: Recent developments from Chinese company DeepSeek, particularly its R1 model which uses fewer expensive chips, are signaling a new phase in AI investing. Fund managers now anticipate greater divergence among AI players, with potential benefits for companies like Broadcom and Marvell due to a shift towards more efficient chips. While efficiency gains could impact demand for some hardware, the overall growth in AI applications is expected to compensate, benefiting key players like Nvidia and Taiwan Semiconductor.

title: DeepSeek引发美股剧烈动荡 “科技七巨头”今年盈利增速预计放缓
summary: DeepSeek在全球市场的出现引发了投资者对AI基础设施投资和科技巨头资本支出回报的担忧，导致美股剧烈动荡。“科技七巨头”今年的总盈利增幅预计将大幅放缓至18%，远低于2024年的34%，预示着其在美股市场的核心推动作用可能面临挑战。此外，云业务增速放缓、巨额资本支出合理性存疑以及地缘政治风险等多重因素，都将对科技股的表现构成不确定性。

title: Home Wi-Fi Router Market to Grow by USD 3.34 Billion (2025-2029), Driven by E-Learning Growth, with AI Driving Market Transformation - Technavio
summary: The global home Wi-Fi router market is projected to grow by USD 3.34 billion between 2025 and 2029, with a CAGR of 12.5%. This growth is primarily fueled by the expansion of e-learning and strategic partnerships between telecom providers and router manufacturers. However, challenges such as inadequate network infrastructure in developing countries, particularly the lack of 5G technology, could hinder market expansion. AI is also expected to play a key role in transforming this market.

title: ZK International stock hits 52-week low at $2.01 By Investing.com
summary: ZK International (ZKIN) stock has fallen to a new 52-week low of $2.01, reflecting a significant year-to-date decline of -48.58%. Despite the underperformance, InvestingPro's Fair Value analysis suggests the stock may be undervalued. The company has recently reported a 405% increase in gross profits, announced a 1-for-7 reverse stock split, and detailed plans for its subsidiary xSigma Corp. to launch a crypto banking wallet and integrate cryptocurrency payments into its operations.

title: Google, SoftBank invest in QuEra's neutral atom quantum computer project - CHOSUNBIZ
summary: Google and SoftBank have made investments in QuEra, a company focused on developing neutral atom quantum computers. This collaboration signifies major backing for QuEra's quantum computing initiatives.

title: Western Digital Corporation (WDC): Among the Cheap Technology Stocks to Buy According to Analysts
summary: Western Digital Corporation (WDC) is highlighted as a cheap technology stock to buy, driven by analyst expectations of significant revenue growth fueled by increasing data storage demand, AI, and autonomous driving advancements. Wells Fargo reiterated a Buy rating with an $85 price target, projecting a total addressable market growth from $65 billion in 2024 to $100 billion by 2030. The company's HDD business is thriving, with record shipments and gross margins, and some hedge funds believe its earnings potential is underestimated due to AI growth and attractive valuation.

title: David Abrams – Top 10 Holdings – Latest 13F
summary: This article details the top 10 holdings of superinvestor David Abrams as of December 31, 2024, based on his latest 13F-HR filing. His portfolio has a market value of over $6.2 billion, with a significant 98.70% concentration in these top 10 positions. The list provides individual stock names, their symbols, values, and percentage of the portfolio.

title: Payments players mull new approaches to fraud
summary: Companies in the payments industry, including Google, Nacha, Early Warning Services, and Truist, are actively seeking new strategies to combat push-payment fraud, which cost the Americas $102.6 billion in 2023. These organizations are advocating for increased data sharing, greater involvement from law enforcement, and improved digital identity tracking to better protect consumers and corporate customers from sophisticated scam operations. The challenge lies in balancing fraud prevention with maintaining frictionless payment experiences and managing the vast scale of transactions under constant attack from criminal groups.

title: Arista Networks’ growth accelerates, driven by big AI investments
summary: Arista Networks Inc. reported a significant increase in profit and revenue for its fourth quarter, driven by strong demand for advanced networking infrastructure supporting AI workloads. The company beat Wall Street estimates for both profit and revenue, with CEO Jayshree Ullal highlighting fiscal 2024 as a "remarkable year." Despite a conservative forecast for the current quarter which caused a temporary stock dip, analysts remain optimistic about Arista's long-term prospects due to continued AI investments from major hyperscale customers and effective cost management.

title: VIG, VOX: 2 Funds to Consider as Vanguard Becomes the King of ETFs
summary: Vanguard’s VOO ETF has surpassed State Street’s SPY ETF to become the world’s largest ETF, driven by its lower expense ratio. This shift highlights Vanguard's growing dominance in the ETF market. Investors are advised to consider two Vanguard ETFs, VIG and VOX, both offering over 10% upside potential in the next year, with VIG focused on dividend appreciation and VOX on communication services.

title: Google and AutoNation partner on self-driving car program
summary: Google's Waymo, its automated vehicle technology arm, has partnered with AutoNation, the largest auto dealership chain in the U.S., for maintenance and repair services for its self-driving car fleet. The multi-year deal will initially cover Chrysler Pacifica vehicles and extend to future models. This partnership highlights the increasing collaborations between tech giants and automotive companies in the race to develop widely available self-driving technology.

title: Spotify partners with ElevenLabs to expand its library of AI-narrated audiobooks
summary: Spotify has partnered with ElevenLabs to significantly expand its collection of AI-narrated audiobooks. This collaboration allows authors to use ElevenLabs' advanced AI voice technology to narrate their books in 29 languages and then upload them to Spotify via Findaway Voices, after a review process. While this move is expected to boost the number of AI-narrated titles, it also anticipates debate within the publishing community regarding the potential impact on audiobook quality.

title: PepsiCo joins major US companies in tweaking DEI policies
summary: PepsiCo is joining other major U.S. companies in revising its Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) policies, influenced by former President Donald Trump's push to dismantle such practices. The company will end DEI workforce representation goals, transition its chief DEI officer to a broader role, and introduce a new "Inclusion for Growth" strategy. These changes come as PepsiCo's five-year DEI strategy concludes, with other corporations like Target and Google also re-evaluating their DEI programs.

title: Ooma vs Google Voice: Which VoIP System is Superior?
summary: This article compares Ooma and Google Voice, concluding that Ooma is generally superior due to its features, customer support, and integrations, although Google Voice is more affordable for small teams and offers better security. The comparison details pricing, features, integrations, security, customer support, and international calling rates to help businesses choose the best VoIP system for their needs.

title: AI boom could inflate Michigan power bills. Consumers Energy is proposing safeguards
summary: Michigan's Consumers Energy is proposing safeguards to prevent higher power bills for average residents due to the potential influx of "hyperscale" data centers from tech giants. The utility wants to implement measures like 15-year power contracts, exit fees, and minimum capacity payments for large data centers to protect existing customers from stranded costs if these facilities consume less power than expected or leave the state. Watchdogs and state regulators are reviewing the proposal to ensure it adequately protects ratepayers, while the Data Center Coalition views the terms as discriminatory.

title: Paul Harris' Top Picks: Alphabet, Novo Nordisk & MSCI Inc
summary: Paul Harris, a portfolio manager at Harris Douglas Asset Management, has identified Alphabet, Novo Nordisk, and MSCI Inc. as his top stock picks to watch in the market. The article highlights his selections, providing insights from a financial expert for potential investors.

title: Chegg sues Google for hurting traffic with AI as it considers strategic alternatives
summary: Chegg has filed a lawsuit against Google, alleging that Google's AI summaries in search results have negatively impacted Chegg's traffic and revenue. This legal action comes as Chegg, facing a significant decline in revenue and subscribers, is exploring strategic alternatives including acquisition or going private, and has engaged Goldman Sachs for assistance. Chegg claims Google leverages its content without compensation, despite Chegg also pursuing its own AI strategy and partnering with OpenAI.

title: SpaceX launches Intuitive Machines’ 2nd Moon lander, 3 rideshare spacecraft
summary: SpaceX successfully launched Intuitive Machines' second Nova-C lunar lander, Athena, along with three rideshare spacecraft, marking a return to the Moon for the Houston-based company. The mission, part of NASA's CLPS Program, aims for a lunar landing on March 6 at Mons Mouton near the Moon's South Pole, carrying several scientific and commercial payloads, including the Micro Nova Hopper named Grace. This flight builds on lessons learned from its predecessor, Odysseus, focusing on improved operational readiness and a robust global communications network.

title: Nvidia to report earnings amid infrastructure spending, DeepSeek concerns
summary: Nvidia is set to release its fourth-quarter earnings, capping off a remarkable year of growth driven by demand for its data center GPUs essential for AI. Despite its success, investors are cautious due to concerns about major customers potentially tightening capital expenditure and the emergence of more efficient AI models from startups like DeepSeek, which could reduce the need for extensive GPU infrastructure. CEO Jensen Huang is expected to address these concerns, emphasizing the continued need for GPUs in AI development and deployment, particularly for the inference phase.

title: Apptronik: Humanoid robots to build humanoid robots
summary: Apptronik has partnered with Jabil to scale up production of its humanoid Apollo robots, with a long-term goal for Apollo robots to manufacture their own kind. Initial steps involve Jabil using Apollo robots for simple logistics and repetitive tasks, similar to a pilot project with Mercedes-Benz. Apptronik recently secured $350 million in Series A funding and is collaborating with Google DeepMind on AI development for the robot.

title: Amdocs and Google Cloud Announce Partnership Expansion to Optimize 5G Network Operations with AI
summary: Amdocs and Google Cloud are expanding their partnership to optimize 5G network operations using AI. Amdocs will launch its Network AIOps solution, built on Google Cloud's Vertex AI and BigQuery, to help telecommunications companies automate operations, enhance reliability, and improve customer experiences. This collaboration aims to address the complexities of 5G networks by providing AI-driven insights, automated workflows, closed-loop automation, and predictive maintenance.

title: Nokia gets EU nod for $2.3 bn Infinera buy, challenging Ciena and Huawei
summary: Nokia has received unconditional EU approval for its $2.3 billion acquisition of Infinera, a move expected to significantly bolster its position in the optical networking market. This acquisition will make Nokia the second-largest vendor globally, enhancing its ability to supply major tech companies and compete more effectively with Huawei and Ciena. Nokia anticipates achieving €200 million in operating profit synergies by 2027 and positive earnings per share contribution in the first year.

title: Earnings call transcript: Eventbrite Q4 2024 misses EPS forecast, stock drops
summary: Eventbrite Inc. (NYSE: EB) reported a wider-than-expected loss in Q4 2024 with an EPS of -$0.09, missing the -$0.05 forecast, despite revenue slightly surpassing expectations at $76.5 million. The stock subsequently dropped nearly 12% in after-hours trading. The company outlined 2025 as a "year of transition," focusing on expanding product offerings and market presence, including reintroducing free ticket listings and investing in Eventbrite Ads, while anticipating a revenue range of $295 million to $310 million for the full year.

title: Digital Camera Market to Reach USD 12.45 Billion by 2032 |
summary: The Digital Camera Market is projected to reach USD 12.45 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 5.06% from 2024 to 2032, driven by rising demand for high-quality imaging, AI advancements, and increasing content creation. Key segments include built-in lens dominating revenue but interchangeable lenses growing fastest, and mirrorless cameras leading by product while compact digital cameras show the fastest growth. Asia Pacific currently leads the market, with Europe expected to see the fastest growth due to high demand for premium imaging devices and social media influencer trends.

title: Alphabet director Frances Arnold sells shares worth $20,606
summary: Alphabet Inc. director Frances Arnold sold 121 shares of the company's Class C Capital Stock for a total of $20,606 on February 28, 2025, as part of a pre-arranged trading plan. Following the sale, Arnold still holds 16,938 shares of Class C stock and various Class C Google Stock Units. The transaction occurred while Alphabet's stock declined 11% year-to-date, though InvestingPro analysis suggests it may be slightly undervalued.

title: Milberg Lawsuit Accuses Meta of Tracking Users Without Consent
summary: Milberg has filed a class action lawsuit against Meta, alleging that the social media giant bypasses user privacy settings by directing them to Meta's in-app browser to track their online activity. The lawsuit claims Meta uses JavaScript injection to monitor user interactions on third-party websites without consent, despite users' privacy preferences. This legal action seeks compensatory damages and injunctive relief for a national class, a California subclass, and an iOS subclass of Meta users.

title: Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (FYEE)
summary: The Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF (FYEE) holds an aggregate "Moderate Buy" rating with a consensus price target of $29.02. This rating is based on 758 analyst ratings for 24 companies, which constitute 52.7% of the ETF's portfolio. Top holdings include NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft, with NVIDIA showing a potential upside of 53.4%.

title: 8 Top Performance Marketing Trends for SMBs To Know in 2026
summary: This article outlines eight key performance marketing trends for SMBs in 2026, focusing on how businesses can adapt to evolving technology and consumer behavior. Key trends include AI-driven optimization, personalized marketing, signal-based targeting in a post-cookie world, and the growing importance of various forms of video content, niche partnerships, outcome-based marketing, and creative as a primary performance lever. The article emphasizes that successful performance marketing in 2026 will blend technology with human oversight, prioritizing measurable results and creative agility in a privacy-conscious landscape.

title: 3 AI Stocks to Buy Before They Steal Nvidia’s Crown
summary: This article identifies three "AI Applier" stocks — Global X Uranium ETF (URA), Coupang (CPNG), and GE HealthCare (GEHC) — that could offer significant investment gains as the AI revolution matures. The author suggests these companies are poised to capitalize on foundational AI technologies, focusing on energy demands, e-commerce optimization, and AI-enabled medical devices, respectively, even as dominant players like Nvidia face increasing competition and market volatility.

title: China bans imports of Illumina's gene sequencers right after Trump tariff action
summary: China announced a ban on imports of genetic sequencers from U.S. medical equipment maker Illumina, effective immediately after the U.S. imposed additional tariffs on Chinese goods. This ban is part of China's retaliatory measures and follows Illumina's placement on Beijing's "unreliable entity" list. The move has led to a significant jump in shares for Illumina's Chinese rivals, MGI and BGI.

title: Alphabet's Google urges US government to avoid breaking up firm, source says
summary: Alphabet's Google reportedly met with the Trump administration, urging them to halt efforts to break up the company. The U.S. Department of Justice is pursuing two antitrust cases against Google, one over search and another over advertising technology, with a trial on remedies expected in April. Industry experts suggest President Trump may ease some antitrust policies from the previous administration, potentially impacting the push to dismantle Google.

title: Parents are blaming Snapchat for their teens' fentanyl deaths. Will an L.A. lawsuit shape the future of social media?
summary: Over 60 families are suing Snap Inc., the company behind Snapchat, alleging that the app's design facilitates drug sales to teenagers, leading to fentanyl overdose deaths. The lawsuits, filed in Los Angeles County Superior Court, challenge Snap's protection under Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act, with a judge ruling that the case can proceed based on allegations of the app's destructive design. This legal battle could have significant implications for how social media companies are held accountable for content and user safety.

title: Malaysia to pay Arm Holdings $250 million for chip design blueprints
summary: Malaysia will pay Arm Holdings $250 million over 10 years for chip design blueprints to enable local manufacturers to produce their own chips within the next decade, driven by the AI boom. Arm will also establish its first Southeast Asian office in Kuala Lumpur, and the deal includes training 10,000 engineers in Malaysia. This initiative aims to build a complete supply chain in advanced industries like AI data servers, autonomous vehicles, and robotics, and to foster 10 local chip companies with significant yearly revenue.

title: Carrier, Google Partner for Energy Management Initiative
summary: Carrier Global Corp. and Google Cloud are collaborating to enhance grid flexibility and smart energy management. This partnership will integrate Carrier Energy's battery-enabled HVAC technology with Google Cloud's AI-powered analytics and WeatherNext AI models to create intelligent, connected solutions for residential HVAC energy customers. The initiative aims to help balance electricity supply and demand, enable homeowners to store and use energy during peak demand, and support both companies' goals for carbon-free energy solutions and greenhouse gas emissions reduction.

title: Are Google Ads Worth It? When and Why They Don’t Work
summary: This article examines the effectiveness of Google Ads in 2025, outlining when they work best and when they prove to be ineffective. It highlights that Google Ads are most suitable for companies with substantial budgets, strong online presences, and clearly defined target audiences. Conversely, they are less effective for small businesses, those in highly competitive fields, or brands needing immediate results, often leading to budget overruns and diminishing returns due to audience fatigue.

title: From AI to cybercriminal notoriety: Three prevailing cybersecurity trends shaping the sector
summary: This article identifies three key cybersecurity trends for 2025: the blurring lines between SecOps and ITOps, leading to security prioritizing system isolation during attacks; the evolution of cybersecurity teams into AI-augmented operators rather than mere responders; and the need to depersonalize cybercriminal notoriety to prevent empowering them. It advocates for a more proactive, intelligence-driven approach, emphasizing collaboration, AI integration, and strategic narrative reshaping to build resilient security postures.

title: Coinbase to Debut Combined Equities & Crypto Index Futures
summary: Coinbase Derivatives is set to launch Mag7 + Crypto Equity Index Futures on September 22, offering the first US-listed derivative that provides combined exposure to traditional equities and cryptocurrency ETFs. This innovative product aims to provide thematic exposure to innovation and growth assets, diversification, and strategic risk management. The index will include the "Magnificent 7" stocks (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA, META, TSLA), Coinbase Global Inc. (COIN), and cryptocurrency ETFs (IBIT, ETHA), with an even-weighting methodology and quarterly rebalancing.

title: Exclusive: How NetApp is navigating the fourth wave of technology with AI-driven data infrastructure
summary: NetApp's Chief Technology Evangelist, Matt Watts, discusses how the company is adapting to the "fourth wave" of technology driven by intelligent data infrastructure and AI, learning from past technological shifts like virtualization and cloud adoption. The article highlights NetApp's unified storage and intelligent services as key solutions for AI integration, addressing challenges such as data silos and security, while also predicting quantum computing as the potential "fifth wave." Watts emphasizes the importance of letting go of outdated systems and embracing change to stay innovative.

title: US OPEN: Selloff on Monday as Uncertainty Continues
summary: US markets experienced a selloff on Monday, with major indices showing negative performance and the VIX fear gauge climbing, indicating rising market anxiety. European markets also saw significant declines, while specific company news included Google Chrome divestiture pressures, plummeting Tesla China sales, Rocket Companies acquiring Redfin, and Robinhood settling with FINRA for $30 million.

title: Is It Time to Buy the Magnificent Seven?
summary: After a recent selloff in the US stock market, the valuations of the "Magnificent Seven" mega-cap tech stocks have decreased. Morningstar analysts now consider Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet to be undervalued, while Nvidia and Tesla are fairly valued, and Apple remains overvalued. Despite these changes, Morningstar strategists continue to recommend underweighting growth stocks, favoring a rotation into value stocks.

title: Tech Moves: Longtime Google leader Umesh Shankar joins Microsoft as new corporate VP
summary: Umesh Shankar, a veteran leader from Google, has joined Microsoft AI as a corporate vice president of engineering, focusing on privacy and security for the Copilot AI assistant. This move is part of a trend of Google executives moving to Microsoft AI. The article also details other significant personnel changes at Remitly, Crelate, Bloomberg, Pioneer Square Labs, Seattle Aquarium, Built Oregon, Fred Hutch Cancer Center, and Trove.

title: Is it time to buy the Magnificent Seven?
summary: The "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks have experienced a significant selloff, making some of them appear undervalued according to Morningstar's fair value metrics. While Meta, Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet are now rated as buys, Nvidia and Tesla are fairly valued, and Apple remains overvalued. Morningstar strategists still recommend underweighting growth stocks overall, favoring value stocks due to market volatility and slowing economic growth.

title: CrowdStrike & Google Cloud: AI-driven cloud security
summary: CrowdStrike Holdings Inc. and Google LLC are collaborating to deliver AI-powered cloud security solutions at scale, addressing critical enterprise challenges in cloud adoption. This partnership leverages CrowdStrike's cloud-native security platform and AI-driven threat intelligence with Google Cloud's infrastructure and Marketplace to enhance protection, streamline procurement, and accelerate security deployment for businesses. The integration aims to simplify security, allowing enterprises to confidently build and operate in the cloud while effectively stopping breaches.

title: Alphabet’s John Hennessy sells shares worth over $33,000
summary: John L. Hennessy, a director at Alphabet Inc., recently sold 200 shares of the company's Class C Capital Stock for approximately $33,106. The transaction was conducted under a pre-arranged 10b5-1 trading plan. Despite the sale, InvestingPro analysis suggests Alphabet is currently undervalued, with strong financial health indicators.

title: Stock Market Today: Dow Sinks 537 Points On Tariff Threats As S&P 500 Corrects; Gold Miners Jump, Meta Triggers Sell Rule (Live Coverage)
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 537 points, and the S&P 500 corrected amid new tariff threats from the Trump administration. Gold miners rallied while Home Depot and Meta Platforms experienced sell-offs, with Meta triggering a sell rule. Despite a late attempt by traders to cover short positions, major indexes ended the trading day lower.

title: Live Nation Loses Bid to Narrow DOJ Ticketmaster Case
summary: A federal judge in New York denied Live Nation Entertainment Inc.'s attempt to narrow the Justice Department’s antitrust case against it, which seeks to break off Ticketmaster. The DOJ and some 30 states allege Live Nation has illegally monopolized the live events industry, controlling a significant portion of concert venues and ticketing. A trial is scheduled for March 2026.

title: News Corp sued by Brave Software, a Google search engine rival
summary: Brave Software, a rival to Google's search engine, has sued News Corp to pre-empt a lawsuit from Rupert Murdoch's company regarding its indexing of copyrighted articles from the Wall Street Journal and New York Post. News Corp had sent Brave a cease-and-desist letter, claiming misappropriation of copyrighted content through "scraping" and indexing. Brave argues that indexing content is "fair use" for search engines and accuses News Corp of trying to stifle advances in generative AI, which relies on search engine responses.

title: Is Wesco International Inc. (WCC) the Best Stock to Buy According to Seth Klarman?
summary: The article discusses Wesco International Inc. (WCC) as a stock held by Seth Klarman's Baupost Group, which has refocused its strategy after a period of underperformance. Klarman's firm generated a 10% return in 2024 and increased its credit investments. Wesco International (WCC) is highlighted as an industrial company benefiting from growth trends, especially in data centers, and delivered strong Q4 2024 results.

title: Billionaire Hedge Fund Manager Pours $33,720,000 Into Soaring Asset, Adds New Stake in Tech Giant Google
summary: Billionaire hedge fund manager John Paulson has invested $33.72 million into International Tower Hill Mines Ltd. (THM) by increasing his firm's stake by over six million shares, bringing his total ownership to more than 70 million shares or 37% of the gold mining company. Additionally, Paulson & Co. established a new $23.73 million position in Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL), acquiring 126,000 shares, reflecting a diversified strategy balancing gold's safe-haven appeal with technology growth. These moves highlight Paulson's long-standing strategy of identifying value amidst economic uncertainty.

title: F5 CEO: AI Is Sparking Cloud Repatriation Movement
summary: F5 CEO François Locoh-Donou states that enterprises are increasingly repatriating application workloads from the cloud due to rising costs and data sovereignty concerns, with AI accelerating this trend. An IDC report suggests 80% of organizations will see some level of repatriation within the next year, indicating a shift towards hybrid and multi-cloud strategies rather than full cloud adoption. This movement creates significant opportunities for partners to help businesses manage and secure applications across diverse environments efficiently.

title: When Facebook Ads Won’t Help Performance Advertisers
summary: This article discusses when Facebook ads are beneficial for performance advertisers and when they may not be. While Facebook offers extensive targeting and can drive traffic, marketers should be aware of diminishing returns due to factors like inappropriate budgets, ad frequency fatigue, rising CPA, and audience demographics. The piece emphasizes the importance of a diversified media mix and exploring alternative advertising channels for sustainable growth.

title: NVIDIA, Alphabet and Google Collaborate on the Future of Agentic and Physical AI
summary: NVIDIA, Alphabet, and Google are expanding their collaboration to advance AI, focusing on agentic AI, robotics, and broader accessibility of AI tools. Their joint efforts include optimizing open models like Gemma, developing advanced robotics capabilities through Intrinsic, and applying AI to drug discovery with Isomorphic Labs and grid optimization with X's Tapestry. Google Cloud will also adopt NVIDIA's latest Blackwell GPUs to power these research and production initiatives.

title: Apple loses German antitrust fight, faces greater scrutiny
summary: Apple lost its challenge in Germany's top civil court against its classification as a significant market power, a designation that grants antitrust regulators more authority to scrutinize its business practices. The Federal Court of Justice upheld the German cartel office's 2023 ruling, placing Apple alongside Google (Alphabet) and Meta as tech giants subject to measures curbing dominance. This decision strengthens the German antitrust watchdog's position, especially concerning Apple's App Store and tracking regulations, though the EU's Digital Markets Act remains the primary standard for Big Tech.

title: INOD Unveils Nvidia-Powered AI Safety Platform: Buy or Hold the Stock?
summary: Innodata (INOD) has launched a new Generative AI Test & Evaluation Platform, powered by Nvidia's inferencing technology, to address AI risk mitigation. While the company has shown impressive financial performance with significant revenue growth and outperforming the market, its stock valuation appears stretched. Investors are advised to exercise caution due to the premium pricing, suggesting a "Hold" recommendation for current shareholders and waiting for a better entry point for new investors.

title: Google to set sail in 35-story downtown Austin tower near Lady Bird Lake this year
summary: Google has confirmed it will finally move into its 35-story sail-shaped tower in downtown Austin this year, after several delays due to the shift to remote and hybrid work environments. The company signed a lease for the entire building overlooking Lady Bird Lake six years ago, and real estate investment trust Cousins Property recently acquired the tower for over $520 million. This move is significant for the Austin office market, which has faced rising vacancy rates, indicating a bullish long-term outlook despite ongoing challenges.

title: Quest, Google team on gen AI; GE Healthcare plans autonomous imaging
summary: Medtech companies showcased their latest AI advancements at Nvidia’s GTC 2025 conference. Quest Diagnostics announced a partnership with Google Cloud to leverage generative AI for data streamlining and personalized customer experiences. GE Healthcare expanded its collaboration with Nvidia to develop autonomous X-ray and ultrasound systems aimed at alleviating staff shortages and improving imaging workflows.

title: Alphabet COA Amie Thuener O’Toole Increases Stock Holdings
summary: Amie Thuener O’Toole, Alphabet Inc.'s Chief Accounting Officer, has increased her stock holdings through a recent dividend distribution. She received 53 Class C Google Stock Units (GSUs) as dividend-equivalent units (DEUs), which will vest gradually based on continued employment. Following this transaction, O’Toole directly holds a significant number of Class C GSUs and both Class A and Class C shares.

title: Quest Diagnostics Strikes Generative AI Partnership with Google Cloud
summary: Quest Diagnostics has partnered with Google Cloud to integrate generative AI capabilities into its services, aiming to enhance predictive population health analytics, customize physician reporting, and improve patient engagement. This collaboration will leverage Google Cloud's AI technologies, such as Google Agentspace, to generate personalized health insights, streamline access to comprehensive laboratory data for clinicians, and boost reporting efficiency through natural-language prompts. The initiative also seeks to uncover subtle patterns in data for faster diagnoses and personalized medicine.

title: Instacart Rolls Out AI-Powered Universal Campaigns for Advertisers
summary: Instacart has launched new AI-powered automation tools, including Universal Campaigns, to help advertisers optimize their ad campaign performance and achieve business goals. Universal Campaigns allow brands to set a single budget and objective, with Instacart's AI automatically managing budget allocation and ad format optimization across various placements like sponsored products and shoppable display ads. These tools aim to simplify campaigning for brands, as demonstrated by early adopters like Rescue Dog Wines and Celsius, who have reported increased customer acquisition and sales.

title: Equities in 2025: Embracing a Broader Landscape
summary: Goldman Sachs Asset Management foresees a broadening landscape of equity market opportunities for active investors in 2025, moving beyond the dominance of US mega-cap tech. The report highlights potential in small-cap companies, developed markets like Japan and Europe, and emerging markets such as India and specific sectors in China. Investors are encouraged to pursue diversified, active strategies and explore long-term themes like reshoring and increased energy demand.

title: Google’s landlord lists tech firm’s Venice campus for sale
summary: Net Lease Office Properties, a spin-off of W.P. Carey, is listing Google's Venice campus, known as the Binoculars Building, for sale through CBRE. The 78,578-square-foot property's triple-net-lease with Google is up for renewal in October 2030. This sale comes after W.P. Carey's 2023 decision to spin off 59 office properties, and follows Google's reversal on its L.A. office expansion last year.

title: Google defeats part of US shareholder class action over digital ads
summary: A federal judge in San Francisco dismissed part of a shareholder class-action lawsuit against Alphabet's Google. The lawsuit accused Google of misleading investors about its digital advertising practices and user privacy. While the judge ruled that claims based on website statements lacked adequate support, she allowed a claim concerning a 2020 statement by CEO Sundar Pichai to Congress to proceed.

title: Can Nuclear Energy Help Big Tech Meet Data Center Needs?
summary: The growing electricity demands of data centers, particularly due to AI, are leading Big Tech companies to consider nuclear energy, including small modular reactors (SMRs). While wind and solar are carbon-free, they don't provide the consistent power required, and natural gas, though quick and cheap, has carbon emissions. However, SMRs are expensive and take a long time to come online, posing challenges for utilities trying to meet this new demand while managing costs and timelines.

title: Gravity Officially Launches Mobile Version of Roguelike Deck-Building Adventure Game 'Shambles: Sons of the Apocalypse' Globally
summary: Gravity has officially launched the mobile version of its roguelike deck-building adventure game, 'Shambles: Sons of the Apocalypse,' globally. The game, which combines text adventure, card deck-building, and roguelike genres, is set in a post-apocalyptic world where players' choices significantly impact the storyline and gameplay. Available for USD 6.99 on Google Play and Apple App Store, it also offers seven DLC packs for expanded content.

title: Kyndryl Stock Sinks After Short-Seller Alleges IBM Deal Masked Billions In Hidden Costs – But Retail Traders Aren’t Buying It
summary: Kyndryl Holdings shares plunged after short-seller Gotham City Research accused the company of accounting manipulation, claiming the stock is significantly overvalued due to misrepresented financial health and undisclosed IBM-related costs. Gotham alleges Kyndryl inflates adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow and benefits from favorable, expiring terms with IBM that mask true operational expenses. Despite these accusations, retail traders on Stocktwits expressed bullish sentiment, with many planning to hold their shares.

title: Lloyds Bank, one of the largest banks in the UK and Europe, is sending top management to Cambridge University and enrolling employees on courses with Google
summary: Lloyds Banking Group is investing heavily in AI education and infrastructure, partnering with Cambridge University to train 300 senior staff in artificial intelligence and enrolling 3,000 employees in Google-led AI courses. This initiative is part of the bank's technology transformation, aiming for broad AI integration to streamline processes, cut costs, and improve services. The bank is also actively recruiting for technology and data roles, particularly in the UK and India.

title: AI²: How radical innovations are revolutionizing tech and health care
summary: This article discusses the transformative impact of "AI²" — artificial intelligence (AI) and artificial incretins (GLP-1s) — on the technology and healthcare sectors. It highlights AI's role as a major productivity enhancer, driven by large tech investments and the rise of LLMs, and positions GLP-1s as ushering in a new era of healthcare. The authors emphasize that both innovations are multiyear developments with vast, evolving investment potential beyond their initial applications, making them crucial for active managers to monitor.

title: A few minutes with Jeff Bush, GM executive director of digital vehicle experience
summary: Jeff Bush, GM's executive director of digital vehicle experience, shares insights from his career journey from Silicon Valley startups like Apple and Google to the automotive world. He discusses GM's commitment to electrification, his vision for over-the-air updates improving user experience, and the company's efforts to modernize its software and hardware stack. Bush emphasizes that GM offers engineers exciting challenges in shaping the future of mobility through safe, reliable, and enjoyable products.

title: HLAL ETF Holdings List — NASDAQ:HLAL
summary: The article provides a detailed list of the top holdings for the Wahed FTSE USA Shariah ETF (HLAL) as of February 26, 2026. It includes company names, stock symbols, weight percentages, share counts, and market values for each holding. Key holdings include Apple Inc., Microsoft Corporation, and Alphabet Inc. (Class A and C).

title: XLC ETF Holdings List — AMEX:XLC
summary: This article provides a detailed list of the holdings for the State Street Communication Services Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLC) as of February 25, 2026. The ETF's top holdings include major tech and telecommunication companies like Meta Platforms, Alphabet (Class A and C), Verizon Communications, and AT&T. Each holding is listed with its symbol, weight percentage, number of shares, and market value in USD.

title: XAIX ETF Holdings List — FWB:XAIX
summary: The XAIX ETF, Xtrackers Artificial Intelligence & Big Data UCITS ETF, holds a diversified portfolio of companies involved in artificial intelligence and big data. As of February 27, 2026, its top holdings include major tech companies like Samsung Electronics, Micron Technology, SK hynix, Alphabet, and Alibaba. The ETF aims to provide exposure to the performance of some of the largest and most liquid companies globally that are engaged in the AI and big data sectors.

title: AOTG ETF Holdings List — NASDAQ:AOTG
summary: This article lists the top holdings for the AOT Growth and Innovation ETF (AOTG) as of March 3, 2026. The ETF's major holdings include NVIDIA, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, Advanced Micro Devices, Alphabet, and Toast. The content provides details on the weight, shares, and market value for each of the listed companies.

title: Papa Johns expands Google AI partnership
