summary: Papa Johns is significantly expanding its partnership with Google Cloud, leveraging AI, data analytics, and machine learning to enhance its ordering and delivery experience. The company has formed a new innovation team, PJX, to implement Google Cloud's BigQuery, Vertex AI, and Gemini platforms for hyper-personalization, predictive ordering, AI-powered customer interactions, and optimized restaurant operations. This collaboration aims to increase order frequency, improve customer satisfaction, and reduce service costs by offering tailored recommendations and a more seamless omnichannel experience.

title: GDE ETF Holdings List — CBOE:GDE
summary: This article provides the holdings list for the WisdomTree Efficient Gold Plus Equity Strategy Fund (GDE), traded on CBOE. As of March 2, 2026, the fund's top holdings include a mutual fund, NVIDIA, Apple, Alphabet, and Microsoft, along with other major tech and diversified companies. The list details the symbol, weight percentage, number of shares, and market value for each holding.

title: The VanEck Wide Moat ETF is down more than 15% from its peak. Is it time to load up?
summary: The VanEck Morningstar Wide Moat ETF (ASX: MOAT) has fallen over 15.5% from its late January peak due to market volatility stemming from new US tariffs. The article questions whether this significant drop makes MOAT an attractive buying opportunity for investors. It highlights the ETF's investment strategy, which focuses on companies with strong economic "moats" like Disney and Alphabet, and its historical average annual return of 15.72% since 2015.

title: Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai sells $5.15 million in company stock
summary: Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai recently sold approximately $5.15 million in company stock through a pre-established trading plan. The sales occurred while Alphabet's stock traded near its 52-week low, though Wall Street maintains a bullish outlook. Pichai's remaining direct holdings in Alphabet amount to over 2.7 million shares.

title: Mark Zuckerberg says Meta’s latest Llama models put open-source AI in the driving seat
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. has unveiled new additions to its Llama family of large language models, including Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick, which Zuckerberg claims will put open-source AI in the driving seat. These models are available through Meta AI assistant and are designed for enhanced reasoning skills and various tasks. Meta is also working on a larger model, Llama 4 Behemoth, intended to be the "highest performing base model in the world."

title: Alphabet: Broad-Based Selling Pushes Stock Into 5-Star Territory
summary: Despite recent stock weakness, Morningstar maintains its $237 fair value estimate for Alphabet, classifying it as materially undervalued with a 5-star rating. The company's high-return-on-ad-spending search business is expected to show resilience during economic uncertainty, and its cloud business is projected to accelerate sales in 2025 as capacity constraints ease. The firm also anticipates monetization of developer interest in its leading-edge LLM, Gemini 2.5, through cloud sales.

title: Ambarella, Inc. (AMBA) Ships 30M Edge AI Chips and Pushes Forward in Generative AI Processing
summary: Ambarella, Inc. (AMBA) has shipped 30 million edge AI chips and is making significant advancements in generative AI processing. The company's focus on edge generative AI technology and its third-generation CVflow AI accelerator highlights its leading position as a supplier of edge AI systems-on-chip, supporting GenAI models from 0.5 to 34 billion parameters. This news comes amidst China's efforts to foster open-source AI models, challenging US dominance in AI innovation and expanding access to AI in developing markets.

title: Palo Alto Networks Surpasses $1.5 Billion in Sales on Google Cloud Marketplace
summary: Palo Alto Networks announced it has surpassed $1.5 billion in cumulative sales on Google Cloud Marketplace, highlighting the demand for its AI-powered security solutions. The company also received five 2025 Google Cloud Partner of the Year awards, including for Artificial Intelligence and Security. This milestone underscores their commitment to helping organizations securely adopt cloud technologies and AI, offering 31 listings and jointly engineered solutions on the marketplace.

title: Fortune 100 Company Plans $1B Data Center in Conway
summary: The Conway City Council has approved a memorandum of understanding with an unnamed Fortune 100 company to build a $1 billion, 300,000-SF data center in the city. This project, which will create about 50 jobs, represents the largest capital investment in Conway's history, driven by the area's power availability and real estate. The agreement includes a property tax abatement and extensive infrastructure upgrades to support the facility.

title: Lloyds Banking Group accelerates AI innovation with Google Cloud
summary: Lloyds Banking Group is significantly enhancing its AI capabilities by partnering with Google Cloud, leveraging Vertex AI for its machine learning and generative AI platform. This collaboration has already enabled the migration of 15 modeling systems, saving 27 Co2 tonnes of operational emissions, and facilitated the deployment of over 80 new machine learning use cases and 18 GenAI systems, with more planned. The initiative aims to transform customer and colleague experience by providing cutting-edge, personalized financial services and driving innovation in the financial sector.

title: NetApp Partners With Google Cloud to Simplify Scaling High-Performance Workloads in the Cloud
summary: NetApp and Google Cloud announced new capabilities for Google Cloud NetApp Volumes, a fully managed file storage service, to enhance performance and simplify scaling for high-performance cloud storage workloads, including those with petabyte-scale datasets. These new features integrate with Google Cloud’s Vertex AI Platform, offer performance improvements for Premium and Extreme service levels, allow independent scaling of capacity and performance for the Flex service level, and will support Google Cloud Assured Workloads. The collaboration aims to help businesses leverage AI and improve data management across hybrid-cloud environments.

title: Is It Time To Consider Buying Yelp Inc. (NYSE:YELP)?
summary: Yelp Inc. (NYSE:YELP) has seen significant share price movement and its current trading price of US$35.73 is considered relatively reasonable according to a price multiple model comparing its P/E ratio to the industry average. The company's earnings are expected to grow by 43% over the next few years, indicating an optimistic future. While the market seems to have priced in this positive outlook, potential investors are encouraged to further examine other factors like the balance sheet for future buying opportunities amidst its volatile stock price.

title: Hugo Barra is back in consumer tech with Sonos board seat
summary: Hugo Barra is re-entering the consumer tech space by joining the Sonos board of directors, replacing long-time director Mike Volpi. Barra brings extensive experience from his roles at Google, Xiaomi, and Meta, particularly in Android andculus VR development. This move could signal a strategic shift for Sonos, which has faced recent challenges, toward refocusing on its core strength as an audio manufacturer and potentially integrating AI into future products.

title: Simple, scalable, and global: Containers are coming to Cloudflare Workers in June 2025
summary: Cloudflare announced that Containers will be released in open beta in late June 2025, deeply integrating with Workers and built on Durable Objects. This new offering aims to provide a simple, scalable, and global solution for running various application types, including stateful AI code execution, stateless FFMpeg processing, and complex orchestrations. The article highlights how Workers can act as an API Gateway, Service Mesh, and Orchestrator for Containers, offering fine-grained control and cost efficiency by paying only for active compute time.

title: Türkiye: TikTok content moderators sue Telus over allegations of poor conditions & union retaliation; incl. co. response
summary: A group of 15 TikTok content moderators in Türkiye are suing Telus, alleging they were dismissed in retaliation for unionizing. The workers, who were reportedly low-paid and exposed to disturbing content, claim severe mental health issues and poor working conditions. Telus has provided a statement in response to these allegations, which are said to be part of a broader issue in outsourced content moderation.

title: Li Lu: Top 5 Positions Make Up 93.27% Of His Portfolio (Analysis)
summary: The article analyzes Li Lu's highly concentrated investment portfolio, revealing that his top five positions account for 93.27% of his holdings. His largest investment is in Bank of America (BAC), representing 29.31%, followed by significant stakes in Alphabet (GOOG and GOOGL), Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B), and East West Bancorp (EWBC). The analysis provides insight into his long-term bets on the financial and technology sectors and his value-oriented approach.

title: Rezolve Ai Secures $9.8 Million Annual Contract with Liverpool Mexico
summary: Rezolve Ai (NASDAQ: RZLV) has secured a $9.8 million annual contract with Liverpool Mexico, the country's leading department store chain. This agreement highlights Liverpool's confidence in Rezolve Ai's AI-powered commerce technology, braincommerce, and its SEO Studio, which integrates deeply with Google's ecosystem. The partnership reaffirms Rezolve Ai's strategic alignment with Google and its ability to drive significant results in digital commerce for major retailers.

title: Internet Services Stocks to Buy: Baidu, Dropbox, Crexendo
summary: This article highlights three Internet Services stocks to consider buying: Baidu (BIDU), Dropbox (DBX), and Crexendo (CXDO). The selections are based on macroeconomic factors affecting the industry, the increasing importance of AI, and individual company performance and analyst ratings. The report analyzes the sector's valuation and volatility, noting that these companies are well-positioned despite industry-wide challenges.

title: Salesforce draws a sharper edge on agentic AI with Agentforce and Google Cloud
summary: Salesforce is heavily investing in agentic AI by expanding its Agentforce offering and strengthening its partnership with Google Cloud, according to Susan Emerson, SVP for AI product at Salesforce. Agentforce provides a flexible AI architecture with guardrails and instructions to prevent hallucinations, enabling broader enterprise AI adoption. The integration with Google Gemini offers multimodal capabilities and open LLM support, while a new agent-to-agent framework aims to enhance interoperability across different systems and break down process silos.

title: Disney: How the Fubo Sports Deal Became a Game Changer
summary: The Walt Disney Co. has made a significant move in the streaming sector by merging its Hulu + Live TV business with FuboTV, creating a new vMVPD entity that Disney will mostly own. This deal turns a former adversary into a partner, settles an antitrust lawsuit, and positions Disney to boost its live sports offerings and advertising revenue. The combined platform will boast 6.3 million subscribers, placing it as the second-largest vMVPD behind YouTube TV.

title: Alphabet: Ad Tech Antitrust Decision Carries Weight but Is Not a Major Risk
summary: A US District Court judge found Alphabet (GOOG) to have an illegal monopoly in the sell-side ad market but rejected claims regarding the advertiser network market. While the decision carries weight, Morningstar believes the market had largely priced in an adverse outcome and maintains its fair value estimate of $237 for Alphabet, viewing its shares as materially undervalued given the Google Network business's declining sales and prior offers to spin it off. Alphabet plans to appeal the decision, likely delaying remedies until 2026.

title: Rubrik Inc. sees $832,154 stock sale by Lightspeed Management
summary: Lightspeed Management Company, a significant shareholder in Rubrik, Inc. (NASDAQ:RBRK), sold 13,213 shares of the company's Class A Common Stock for approximately $832,154 on April 15, 2025. This transaction occurred amidst Rubrik’s strong market performance and continued strategic developments in cybersecurity, including collaborations with Mandiant and new initiatives for Google Cloud customers. Despite the sale, managing members Barry Eggers and Peter Nieh maintain substantial indirect holdings, while analysts largely maintain positive ratings and upwardly revised price targets for the stock.

title: 10 cloud programming languages developers need to know
summary: This article identifies ten essential cloud programming languages for developers, detailing their use cases, advantages, and limitations. It emphasizes the importance of choosing the right language based on factors like cloud client and service integration, targeted stack layer, team familiarity, and risk aversion. The languages covered include JavaScript, Node.js, Python, C, Go, .NET, Java, Kotlin, Swift, and Rust.

title: Carousel Ads: Best Uses and Formats Explained
summary: This article explains carousel ads, their benefits, and best practices for their use in digital marketing. It covers how carousel ads can improve engagement and conversion rates by showcasing multiple products or telling a deeper story, and provides strategies for both B2C and B2B companies, including A/B testing and retargeting methods.

title: Lumen’s love affair with cloud hyperscalers could reshape the internet
summary: Lumen Technologies is partnering with Google Cloud to rebuild the internet's U.S. national architecture, creating new 400G cloud on-ramps. This initiative aims to replace outdated infrastructure, provide multi-cloud access through a single Lumen port, and support the growing demands of AI applications. Lumen's CTO Dave Ward emphasizes the "co-opetition" strategy, where Lumen offers its access network to hyperscalers who lack it, while gaining a global-scale partner in return.

title: Wearable Technology Market Size, Share & Growth, 2033
summary: The global wearable technology market was valued at USD 33.25 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 132.56 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 16.61%. Key drivers include increasing health consciousness, technological advancements in sensors and connectivity, and the integration of AI. The market faces challenges related to limited functionality of budget devices, security, and privacy concerns regarding sensitive health data.

title: MediaTek and HP bring educational Chromebooks to EDIX Tokyo 2025
summary: MediaTek and HP are showcasing the new HP Fortis Flip G1m 11 Chromebook, powered by the MediaTek Kompanio 520 processor, at EDIX Tokyo 2025. This collaboration aims to provide K-12 students in Japan with powerful, efficient, and versatile educational tools, supporting the country's GIGA program. The Chromebook offers enhanced performance, extended battery life, and multitasking capabilities designed to improve digital learning experiences.

title: Tencent Music lets musicians generate AI tracks and send them directly to streaming app QQ Music
summary: Tencent Music Entertainment (TME) is expanding its AI capabilities, allowing musicians to generate AI tracks with lyrics and vocals using DeepSeekR1 and AI songwriting tools, then publish them directly to QQ Music. The company's ESG report details these advancements, which include features like AI Tone Magician and Music Separation, alongside a significant increase in independent artists on its platform. However, the report also acknowledges risks associated with evolving AI regulations, potential copyright infringement, and the need for clear ownership and attribution of AI-generated content.

title: CMB.TECH to merge with Golden Ocean, expanding global fleet
summary: CMB.TECH plans to merge with Golden Ocean Group, creating one of the largest diversified listed maritime groups with over 250 vessels and a total value exceeding $11 billion. CMB.TECH had previously acquired a 40.8% stake in Golden Ocean for nearly $1.2 billion. The merger is expected to finalize by the end of the current quarter and complete in the third quarter, pending regulatory approvals.

title: Google Parent Alphabet's Stock Climbs as AI-Fueled Search Growth Drives Revenue Beat
summary: Alphabet (GOOGL) reported first-quarter revenue and profit that surpassed analyst expectations, driven by strong AI-fueled search growth and cloud revenue. The company announced a 5% increase in its quarterly dividend and an additional $70 billion in stock buybacks, leading to a nearly 5% rise in its Class A shares during after-hours trading. Alphabet also reiterated plans for significant spending on AI infrastructure, highlighting its strategic partnership with Nvidia for chip technology.

title: Alphabet – what to expect from Q1 2025 earnings❓
summary: Alphabet (GOOGL.US) is set to release its Q1 2025 earnings report, which will be a crucial test for the company amidst widespread weakness in the Big Tech sector and market uncertainty. Investors will focus on financial data, management's outlook, the performance of Google Cloud, and the impact of tariffs and AI development on revenue. Despite a year-to-date stock decline, analyst sentiment remains optimistic, with expectations of increased revenue and improved operating income.

title: Alphabet Earnings: Solid Results as AI Monetization Continues to Improve
summary: Alphabet reported solid first-quarter 2025 results with 12% sales growth and 230 basis points operating margin improvement year-over-year, driven significantly by Google Cloud's 28% growth. The company demonstrates clear progress in AI monetization across various platforms, leading Morningstar to maintain its $237 fair value estimate and view the stock as materially undervalued. Despite antitrust concerns and a turbulent macro environment, Alphabet's continued execution and strategic AI investments are highlighted as key strengths.

title: Live S&P VOOG: Tariff Impact Hits Corporate America, GOOGL (Nasdaq: GOOGL) Up, ODFL Falls
summary: The article discusses a mixed market day where the S&P 500 experiences fractional losses, while the VOOG ETF sees modest gains. Key movers include Alphabet (GOOGL) rising due to strong Q1 earnings and an analyst upgrade, and Old Dominion Freight (ODFL) falling significantly after its earnings report. The piece highlights the ongoing impact of tariffs on corporate America and consumer sentiment.

title: Dow Jones Index Today: DIA Slips After a Strong Rally This Week
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) slipped on Friday, despite a strong week, influenced by China's promise to support its economy amid trade tensions. However, positive news regarding a potential U.S.-Japan trade deal and strong Q1 results from Alphabet (GOOGL) helped mitigate larger losses. The article also discusses the performance of the SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF Trust (DIA) and its "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts.

title: Microsoft Corporation (MSFT): Among Billionaire Steve Cohen’s Large-Cap Stock Picks With Huge Upside Potential
summary: Billionaire investor Steve Cohen's Point72 Asset Management has identified Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) as a top large-cap stock pick with significant upside potential. Despite Cohen's warnings about a potential economic slowdown due to tariffs and slower immigration, he remains optimistic about the artificial intelligence sector, where Microsoft is a key player. Analysts at Mizuho project impressive long-term growth for Microsoft, particularly from its Azure cloud unit and AI investments.

title: Behavioral Targeting: What It Is, How It Boosts Conversions
summary: Behavioral targeting is a marketing technique that leverages a consumer's online activities like website visits, product views, and search queries to deliver personalized advertisements. This method is crucial for increasing ad relevance, boosting conversion rates, and reducing wasted ad spend by focusing on high-intent audiences. The article explains how behavioral targeting works through data collection, profiling, audience segmentation, and AI-powered ad delivery, contrasting it with contextual and demographic targeting.

title: Alphabet Sets Positive Tone Ahead of Tech Earnings
summary: This article discusses the Q1 earnings season with a focus on big tech companies. While Tesla reported a decline in earnings and revenue, Alphabet surpassed analyst estimates due to strong performance in its search and advertising units, driven by AI investments. Intel also beat estimates but provided weak forward-looking guidance and announced workforce reductions.

title: Another Tech-Adjacent Sector Could Lift QQQ
summary: The communication services sector, which emerged in 2018 from previously tech-classified components, holds significant weight in ETFs like Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM). Despite recent struggles by some "Magnificent Seven" companies within this sector, opportunities abound for investors as several communication services stocks, including Alphabet, Meta Platforms, and Charter Communications, are seen as undervalued or having potential for long-term returns. This sector's strong presence and the attractive valuations of some of its constituents could contribute to the uplift of QQQ and QQQM.

title: Going Into Earnings, Is Meta Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar assesses Meta Platforms (META) ahead of its Q1 2025 earnings report, focusing on AI monetization, Reels profitability, and Reality Labs spending. The stock is currently rated as undervalued with a 4-star rating and a fair value estimate of $770 per share, supported by a wide economic moat and strong financial health despite investments in AI and Reality Labs. Key areas to watch include ad monetization, capex plans, genAI monetization, and operational efficiencies, with antitrust concerns noted but considered less impactful than market speculation suggests.

title: PayPal Brings Together Developers, AI Leaders to Power Agentic Commerce at Dev Days
summary: PayPal is leading the agentic commerce revolution, providing developers and merchants with innovative AI tools to create next-generation shopping experiences. The company is hosting Dev Days, collaborating with AI leaders like Amazon Web Services, Anthropic, Google Cloud, and Microsoft, to demonstrate the power of agentic experiences. PayPal's goal is to enable AI agents to transact on behalf of customers and businesses, providing secure and efficient commerce operations.

title: REWE Group Chooses the Cloud to Transform Digitally with RISE with SAP
summary: REWE Group, with 16,000 food retail stores and travel agencies, has selected RISE with SAP to migrate its IT infrastructure to Google Cloud. This move aims to modernize IT processes across finance, accounting, and human resources, leveraging SAP S/4HANA Cloud for greater scalability, efficiency, and real-time adaptability. The digital transformation will harmonize processes, standardize technical operating models, and introduce innovations like artificial intelligence, significantly reducing hardware and operating costs.

title: Meta Faces Copyright Reckoning in Authors’ Generative AI Case
summary: A federal court in San Francisco is set to hear a crucial summary judgment in a copyright lawsuit against Meta Platforms Inc., brought by authors including Sarah Silverman, Ta-Nehisi Coates, and Andrew Sean Greer. The case will examine whether Meta's use of pirated books to train its Llama AI model constitutes fair use, with the outcome potentially having significant implications for copyright law and the generative AI industry. The authors allege direct copyright infringement by Meta through the use of "shadow libraries," while Meta argues its AI model is a transformative product falling under fair use, regardless of how the training data was acquired.

title: After Earnings, Is Alphabet Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Alphabet released solid first-quarter earnings with strong growth in sales and Google Cloud, despite macroeconomic uncertainties and antitrust concerns. Morningstar maintains a $237 per share fair value estimate, considering the stock significantly undervalued with a 5-star rating and a wide economic moat due to strong intangible assets and network effects. The company's financial strength is deemed unassailable, and continued investments in AI are expected to be value-accretive, positioning it well for future growth despite potential regulatory pressures.

title: Ansys provides brief updates on $35B proposed acquisition by Synopsys, still requires some regulatory approval
summary: Ansys has provided an update on its proposed $35 billion acquisition by Synopsys, noting that it has received approval in the U.K. while other regulatory reviews are ongoing. The company still expects the deal to finalize in the first half of 2025. This acquisition represents a significant transaction in the technology sector.

title: Apple clears Spotify update under new rules allowing purchases within apps
summary: Apple has approved Spotify's U.S. app update, allowing users to access pricing information and make purchases directly within the app, following a U.S. federal judge's order against Apple's in-app commission policy. This change enables Spotify users to see pricing, purchase subscriptions, and use alternative payment methods. Apple, despite disagreeing and planning to appeal, stated it will comply with the court's decision, marking a significant development for developers seeking fairer competition.

title: Coursera and Canva registered as VAT payers in Uzbekistan
summary: Online education platform Coursera and graphic design service Canva have officially registered as VAT payers in Uzbekistan. This brings the total number of foreign legal entities registered for VAT to 69, with 11 companies registering this year alone, doubling the registrations from all of 2024. Tax revenue from foreign big tech companies in Q1 2025 increased by 50% compared to Q1 2024, reaching 36.2 billion UZS, primarily from Apple, Meta, Google, OpenAI, and TikTok.

title: Jim Cramer Surprised Alphabet (GOOGL) AI “Didn’t Cannibalize Google” — Still “Doesn’t Care for the Stock”
summary: Jim Cramer expressed surprise that Alphabet's (GOOGL) AI product, Gemini, did not negatively impact its core Google search revenues, despite his personal disinterest in the stock. He lauded the resilience of large technology companies, highlighting their scale, financial strength, and ability to adapt to new challenges like AI. Cramer also emphasized the importance of supporting these "super stocks" against legal challenges.

title: AI Will Send 1 in 4 Workers to a New Job, Salesforce Predicts
summary: Salesforce predicts that one in four workers will be shifted to new roles due to the widespread adoption of AI agents in the workplace. These AI tools can perform tasks autonomously, leading to significant efficiency gains and changes in workforce composition. HR leaders anticipate reskilling initiatives, a greater emphasis on soft skills, and a future where human and AI workers collaborate.

title: Billionaire John Paulson’s 10 Stocks with Huge Upside Potential
summary: This article highlights Thryv Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:THRY) as one of billionaire John Paulson’s top stock picks with significant upside potential. It details Thryv's strong Q1 2025 performance, including a 50% year-over-year increase in SaaS revenue, and outlines the company's strategic shift toward its highly profitable software business. Laughing Water Capital also expresses optimism, noting Thryv's growing SaaS customer base, improved net dollar retention, and share repurchase program.

title: Earnings call transcript: Viant Technology Q1 2025 earnings beat expectations
summary: Viant Technology Inc. (DSP) reported a significant beat in its Q1 2025 earnings, with an EPS of $0.03 against a forecasted loss of $0.07 and revenue of $70.6 million, far exceeding expectations. The company saw a 32% year-over-year revenue increase and a 76% increase in adjusted EBITDA, driven by strong performance in connected TV (CTV) advertising and AI solutions. Viant's stock rose 4.96% in aftermarket trading, reflecting investor confidence and an outlook for continued growth, despite minor macroeconomic uncertainties causing some Q2 campaign activations to shift to the second half of 2025.

title: Nutanix reveals cloud cluster solution alongside Google Cloud
summary: Nutanix is partnering with Google Cloud to release Nutanix Cloud Clusters (NC2), a solution designed to simplify hybrid multicloud infrastructure. This offering helps companies modernize legacy systems and migrate applications across clouds, particularly addressing the growing demand for Google Cloud adoption and AI workload management. The collaboration leverages Google's bare metal service to provide customers with more choice and agility in hybrid cloud environments.

title: Google's parent company Alphabet shares plunge over 8% amid AI search competition, malware threats
summary: Alphabet Inc. shares dropped over 8% following revelations from an antitrust trial, citing increased AI search competition and new malware threats. Apple executive Eddy Cue testified about declining Google search traffic due to AI alternatives and announced future AI choices in Safari. Google also disclosed a new malware strain, LOSTKEYS, linked to a Russian hacking group, and partnered with Elementl Power to build nuclear plants to power AI data centers, addressing the anticipated surge in electricity usage.

title: Alphabet: Testimony From Apple Search Executive Rattles Investors
summary: Apple's Eddy Cue testified in the DOJ lawsuit against Alphabet, indicating Apple may end its long-term Google Search partnership for Safari. This news caused investor concern, given Google's significant revenue-sharing agreement with Apple. Despite potential changes and AI's impact on search, Morningstar maintains its fair value estimate for Alphabet, believing the market's reaction is overblown and that Google will adapt with AI-infused offerings.

title: Google's Internet Traffic Share in Korea Hits Record 31%, Fees Unpaid
summary: Google's internet traffic share in Korea has reached a record 31.17%, the highest for two consecutive years, yet the company continues to avoid paying network usage fees amounting to hundreds of billions of won. This places a significant burden on Korean telecom companies, which are struggling to meet the rising demand for network investment due to increased traffic from streaming services. Despite growing calls for government and industry intervention to address Google's "network free-riding," factors like potential trade friction, a political vacuum, and an SK Telecom hacking incident have stalled efforts.

title: LDRX Top Holdings List & Exposure (SGI Enhanced Market Leaders ETF)
summary: The SGI Enhanced Market Leaders ETF (LDRX) is an equity fund focused on large-cap strategies within North America, with its top holdings including NVIDIA, Apple, and Alphabet, which make up a significant portion of the fund's total assets. The ETF exhibits major exposure to the technology sector, particularly semiconductors and media, and is exclusively exposed to the USD currency and primarily the United States geographically. The article details LDRX's top 25 stock holdings by weight, alongside a comprehensive breakdown of its geographic, currency, sector, and industry exposures.

title: monday.com Integrations for 2026: Improve Your Workflow
summary: This article details how to effectively use monday.com integrations to enhance workflow and project management. It outlines the process of adding integrations, categorizes beneficial integrations such as CRM, email, team management, and communication tools, and highlights the expanded possibilities through Zapier for connecting a wider range of applications. The piece emphasizes that leveraging these integrations can significantly improve team efficiency and communication within monday.com.

title: Morgan Stanley says Google Cloud could grow 50%+ in 2026
summary: Morgan Stanley analysts project that Google Cloud's revenue could expand by over 50% in 2026, driven by an updated backlog model. Analyst Brian Nowak notes that Google Cloud is seen as a key driver for GOOGL's multiple expansion and AI-driven outperformance. This growth is supported by a strong backlog and consistent growth in on-demand workloads.

title: Perion Acquires an Advanced AI Company to Accelerate Market Share Expansion and Growth
summary: Perion Network Ltd. has acquired Greenbids, an advanced AI platform specializing in custom algorithms for campaign optimization across major advertising channels. This acquisition aims to enhance Perion's Perion One platform, drive client retention, and significantly increase market share by integrating Greenbids' AI-driven capabilities for performance-based advertising, including into walled garden platforms like YouTube and Facebook. The deal, valued at $27.5 million cash upfront plus earnouts and retention a total of $65 million, is expected to be accretive to Perion's adjusted EBITDA and revenue, accelerating its growth from 2026 onwards.

title: Chegg to lay off 22% of its workforce
summary: Chegg announced it will lay off 22% of its workforce, totaling 248 employees, primarily affecting Chegg Study and corporate services in the US and Canada. This decision is in response to macroeconomic pressures and the impact of AI developments, particularly Google's AI Overviews and free educational AI offerings from companies like OpenAI and Anthropic. The restructuring aims to achieve $45-$55 million in savings in 2025 and $100-$110 million in 2026.

title: If You Invested $10K In Walt Disney Stock 10 Years Ago, How Much Would You Have Now?
summary: An investor who put $10,000 into Walt Disney stock ten years ago would now have $10,941, representing a 9.41% total return from stock appreciation and dividends. This return significantly lags the S&P 500's 231% total return over the same period. Despite past performance, analysts project a "Buy" rating for Disney with a potential upside of over 13%, driven by strong Q2 2025 earnings and performance in its Entertainment and Experiences segments.

title: Embracing the Digital Shift: Why Brands Must Adapt to Gen Z’s Social Media Search Habits
summary: This article highlights how Gen Z's preference for social media as a primary search tool for products and services necessitates a strategic shift for brands. Chris Brownlee from Yext emphasizes the importance of maintaining an accurate, consistent, and multi-platform digital presence, utilizing tools like knowledge graphs, to effectively engage with this demographic and ensure discoverability in an evolving digital landscape. Inaccurate information can damage reputation and negatively impact search rankings.

title: Google Cloud and Equinix power up Jakarta's AI Infrastructure with major data center expansion
summary: Google Cloud and Equinix are expanding their data center operations in Jakarta, significantly boosting Indonesia's AI computing power and infrastructure. Google Cloud has expanded its Jakarta cloud region's capacity to meet customer demand and support local organizations, while Equinix has launched its first International Business Exchange (IBX) data center in the city. These investments are crucial for Indonesia's ambition to become a leading AI powerhouse in Southeast Asia.

title: Alphabet’s stock is now almost as cheap as eBay’s by this metric — but should you buy it?
summary: Alphabet's stock has become inexpensive based on its price-to-earnings multiple, making it appear nearly as cheap as eBay's, despite Alphabet being a tech juggernaut and investors having concerns about its slowing search-engine growth. The article questions whether this perceived cheapness is worth the risks, particularly given the strong bullish ratings from Wall Street analysts who have an average price target of $200.

title: Netflix Is Turning Into an Advertising Company
summary: Netflix, once a pioneer of ad-free streaming, is rapidly transforming into an advertising company. Its ad-supported tier now boasts 94 million global monthly active users and is its fastest-growing segment. The company is actively developing new ad formats, including AI-powered contextual ads, to compete directly with major digital advertising players like Google, Meta, and Amazon, signaling a significant shift in its business model.

title: Amesite Inc’s Innovative Leap: Unveiling the Next Chapter
summary: Amesite Inc. (NASDAQ: AMST) saw its stock rise by 50.41% following the launch of NurseMagic, a HIPAA-compliant B2B service that expands its enterprise strategy in healthcare AI. Despite current negative EBIT and EBITDA, the company shows a strong gross margin and aggressive investment in product development, signaling potential future growth in the healthcare SaaS market. This strategic pivot aligns with broader industry trends in AI-driven healthcare solutions.

title: David Abrams: Top 5 Positions Make Up 79.95% Of His Portfolio (Analysis)
summary: David Abrams's investment portfolio is highly concentrated, with his top five holdings comprising 79.95% of his total assets. His largest position is Loar Holdings Inc (LOAR), making up nearly half of his capital. The article details his rationale behind significant stakes in aerospace, automotive retail, sleep and wellness, and a strategic tech holding in Alphabet.

title: With 67% ownership of the shares, Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE:VZ) is heavily dominated by institutional owners
summary: Institutional investors hold a significant 67% ownership in Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE:VZ), indicating their strong influence on the stock's performance. The Vanguard Group, Inc. is highlighted as the largest single shareholder. While individual investors constitute 33% ownership, the analysis suggests that insider holdings are less than 1%, prompting a recommendation to monitor insider buying activity.

title: Mystery project revealed: Google plans $10B campus in Memphis metro
summary: Google is planning to develop a $10 billion data center campus in West Memphis, Arkansas, which is part of the Memphis metropolitan area. This project marks the unveiling of the tech giant's previously secretive plans for a massive investment in the region. The development is expected to have a significant economic impact on the Memphis metro.

title: Alphabet Earnings Preview: Trading Q1 Results With Single Stock Daily Leveraged & Inverse ETFs
summary: This article previews Alphabet Inc.'s Q1 2025 earnings report, focusing on AI spending, cloud revenue, and YouTube advertising. It discusses analyst expectations for EPS and revenue, recent stock underperformance, and Wall Street sentiment. The article also highlights how traders can use Direxion's Single Stock Daily Leveraged & Inverse ETFs (GGLL and GGLS) to capitalize on GOOGL's earnings-driven volatility for short-term trading strategies.

title: Dynamic markets may favor a hybrid of active and passive investing
summary: This article argues that dynamic market conditions favor a hybrid investment approach combining active and passive strategies. A well-designed active-enhanced index strategy can add value by seeking market-plus returns with market-like risk, utilizing strict risk controls and fundamental stock selection. The strategy's advantages are particularly pronounced during uncertainty, relying on experienced analysts to identify opportunities and manage risk across various sectors and industries.

title: Period Tracking App Users Win Class Status in Google, Meta Suit
summary: Users of the period tracking app Flo have been granted class-action status in a lawsuit alleging the app unlawfully shared sensitive health data with Google LLC and Meta Platforms Inc. A US District Court judge ruled that the millions of potential users share common legal and factual questions regarding privacy disclosures and the app's use of software development kits from the tech giants. This ruling follows a previous rejection of Google's summary judgment motion, setting the case for a jury trial in July.

title: Arkansas getting a new Google data center
summary: Google is planning to build a new $10 billion data center in West Memphis, Arkansas, code-named Project Pyramid. This will be the third large data center in Arkansas, following earlier announcements for Conway and Little Rock. The facility will be powered by Entergy Arkansas and is expected to employ around 300 workers.

title: AES, Meta Sign PPAs for 650 MW of Solar Power in Kansas and Texas
summary: AES Corporation has signed two long-term power purchase agreements (PPAs) with Meta for 650 MW of solar power to support Meta's data centers in Kansas and Texas. These projects will contribute to Meta's goal of using 100% clean and renewable energy, create jobs, and provide significant tax revenue to local communities. AES currently has 32.7 GW in operation and a substantial backlog of signed PPAs, including 10.1 GW with major hyperscalers.

title: Sergey Brin's $700 Million Alphabet Stock Transfer: A Philanthropic Gesture? - News and Statistics
summary: Sergey Brin, co-founder of Google, has transferred over 4 million Alphabet shares worth nearly $700 million, though the recipient remains undisclosed. This transaction, detailed in an SEC filing, sparks speculation about a possible philanthropic donation, aligning with Brin's well-known history of charitable contributions. The stock transfer occurred as Alphabet's stock saw an increase following new feature announcements at Google's I/O developer conference.

title: The IT wingspan of JPMorgan Chase & Co.
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPMC), the largest US bank, employs a complex hybrid IT infrastructure strategy balancing enterprise data centers with multi-cloud solutions (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) for resilience and leading-edge capabilities. Infrastructure CIO Darrin Alves highlights JPMC's commitment to technology, significant investments, and plans to consolidate its global data center estate from 32 to 17 facilities while rigorously applying legal, security, and resiliency criteria before cost optimization. The bank is also investing in AI and quantum computing research, leveraging cloud for generative AI experimentation, and utilizing mainframes for critical systems.

title: Market Volatility: The Stock Strategies That Are Paying Off in 2025
summary: In 2025, during a period of significant market volatility, Morningstar identifies low-volatility stocks as the leading investment factor, outperforming the broader market. While low-volatility stocks like Berkshire Hathaway and Coca-Cola provided capital preservation, quality and momentum factors, including companies like Nvidia and Alphabet, underperformed due to high valuations and shifting market leadership. The article emphasizes that factor investing requires long-term patience and diversification, as factor leadership constantly changes.

title: Gannett’s SWOT analysis: digital growth amid print decline shapes stock outlook
summary: Gannett, the largest U.S. newspaper publisher, is navigating a challenging media landscape by transitioning from print to digital, as detailed in an InvestingPro SWOT analysis. While facing overall revenue decline and difficulties in its Digital Marketing Solutions (DMS) segment, the company shows strength in its growing digital audience, subscriptions, cost discipline, and strategic AI partnerships. Future success hinges on accelerating digital revenue growth and managing debt as asset sale opportunities diminish.

title: Doubt the Market? 3 Stocks to Rideout Fear, Uncertainty and Doubt
summary: In a volatile market characterized by fear, uncertainty, and doubt, investors are advised to seek stability in blue-chip stocks. This article highlights Philip Morris International (PM), Cardinal Health (CAH), and Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) as three companies well-positioned to offer such stability. These stocks are chosen based on their strong fundamental factors like valuation, earnings potential, dividend strength, and low volatility to help investors navigate market fluctuations effectively.

title: The Trade Desk vs. Criteo: Which Ad Tech Stock is the Better Buy Now?
summary: This article compares two ad tech companies, The Trade Desk (TTD) and Criteo (CRTO), to determine which is a better investment. While TTD is gaining from strong revenue growth in the first quarter of 2025, it faces challenges from macroeconomic uncertainties, intense competition, and regulatory scrutiny. Criteo, on the other hand, is transitioning towards high-growth areas like Retail Media with its AI-driven platform and direct retailer access, and is considered the smarter pick due to its stronger valuation and focus on partnerships.

title: Alphabet Inc: in 10 years, we'll laugh at these valuations, unless
summary: The article discusses Alphabet's (GOOG) valuation, suggesting its current forward P/E of 16x is low given its strong financials and potential for growth, especially with AI advancements. It highlights that major investors like Bill Ackman and value investors see AI as a strengthening factor for Alphabet's business model, contrasting with market fears of disruption. The author argues that Alphabet's substantial cash flow, R&D investments, and growing segments like Google Cloud indicate a potential for significant reinvention and growth, making its current valuation appear irrational with a long-term perspective.

title: CoStar Group breaks ground with Azure AI Foundry and GPT-4o Realtime API audio models
summary: CoStar Group is revolutionizing the online home search experience by developing a conversational AI search solution with Azure OpenAI and the Mistral model in Azure AI Foundry Models. This innovative approach aims to replicate the nuanced conversations buyers have with human real estate agents, leveraging CoStar's extensive proprietary data. The solution utilizes the GPT-4o Realtime API audio models for low-latency performance, offering a secure, scalable, and intuitive way for millions of users to find homes globally.

title: Nelnet Hit With Privacy Suit Over Info Disclosures to Google
summary: Nelnet is facing a proposed class-action lawsuit alleging that it allows Google to track users on its student loan servicer website, potentially revealing sensitive financial information to the search giant. The plaintiff argues that student loan debt information is highly personal and that this tracking constitutes an invasion of privacy. The suit was filed in the US District Court for the Northern District of California.

title: Trump Orders Nuclear Overhaul: These 3 Stocks Stand to Benefit
summary: President Donald Trump's recent executive order to expand U.S. nuclear output has invigorated the nuclear energy sector. The order aims to streamline the licensing process and permit new construction on federal land. This move, coupled with increasing demand from tech companies for clean energy and advancements in small modular reactor (SMR) technology, signals a bullish outlook for nuclear stocks in 2025, with NuScale Power (SMR), Cameco Corp (CCJ), and BWX Technologies (BWXT) identified as key beneficiaries.

title: A envergadura de TI do JPMorgan Chase & Co.
summary: Darrin Alves, CIO de plataformas de infraestrutura do JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPMC), detalha a estratégia de TI do banco, que combina data centers corporativos robustos com uma abordagem multinuvem e híbrida. A empresa, um gigante bancário, visa resiliência e eficiência, tendo superado a meta de mover uma grande porcentagem de seus dados e aplicações para a nuvem. Apesar dos investimentos significativos em tecnologia, o JPMC mantém e otimiza seus data centers on-premise, priorizando segurança e conformidade regulatória.

title: Business Brief: BlackBerry is back
summary: BlackBerry Ltd., under new CEO John Giamatteo, has transformed from its smartphone-era identity to focus on becoming a dominant player in the software-defined vehicle market through its QNX division. The company has undertaken significant cost-cutting measures, including shedding its cybersecurity business, refinancing debt, and reducing headcount, putting it on stable financial footing. This strategic shift aims to re-establish BlackBerry as a technology leader, though it faces the challenge of maintaining investor confidence and possibly severing ties with its Waterloo origins.

title: Rubrik CFO Choudary Kiran Kumar sells shares worth $332,010
summary: Rubrik's CFO, Choudary Kiran Kumar, sold 3,500 shares of Class A common stock for $94.86 per share, totaling $332,010, through a pre-arranged trading plan. This transaction occurred while Rubrik's stock is near its 52-week high, having delivered a significant return over the past year. The company has also recently received positive analyst coverage, higher price targets, and made strategic announcements to enhance its cyber resilience offerings.

title: Li Lu: Top 5 Positions Make Up 94.08% Of His Portfolio (Analysis)
summary: Li Lu's investment portfolio is highly concentrated, with his top 5 positions accounting for 94.08% of its total value. His largest holding is Bank of America, despite a recent trim, followed by Berkshire Hathaway, Alphabet Class A, Alphabet Class C, and East West Bancorp. This reflects his conviction in long-term value and specific market segments.

title: Chrome to Distrust Chunghwa Telecom and Netlock Certificates
summary: Google announced that its Chrome Root Store will no longer trust digital certificates issued by Chunghwa Telecom and Netlock due to "patterns of concerning behavior observed over the past year." This change will take effect in Chrome 139 for certificates issued after July 31, 2025, and website operators are advised to replace affected certificates to avoid security warnings for users. Google emphasizes that this decision is to preserve the integrity of the Chrome Root Store and ensure user safety.

title: Google Chrome to distrust Chunghwa Telecom, Netlock certificates in August
summary: Google Chrome will distrust root CA certificates from Chunghwa Telecom and Netlock starting August 1, 2025, due to persistent compliance failures. This change will cause "Your connection is not private" warnings for websites using their certificates, prompting web administrators to switch to trusted Certificate Authorities. This action follows Google's increased scrutiny of CAs and similar decisions against other providers.

title: Google Chrome to distrust newer Chunghwa Telecom digital certificates
summary: Google Chrome will stop automatically trusting new digital certificates issued by Chunghwa Telecom after July 31, 2025, due to "compliance failures." Chunghwa Telecom stated that this decision is not linked to any cybersecurity issues. The full story is available in the CNA English news archive for subscribers.

title: Google Chrome to Distrust Two Certificate Authorities Over Compliance and Conduct Issues
summary: Google Chrome announced it will no longer trust digital certificates issued by Chunghwa Telecom and Netlock, effective with Chrome 139 in August 2025. This decision stems from a pattern of compliance failures and unfulfilled commitments by both Certificate Authorities. Website operators using these CAs after July 31, 2025, will need to transition to a new publicly-trusted CA to avoid security warnings for Chrome users.

title: June 2025 US stock market outlook: Has the storm passed?
summary: The US stock market is currently trading at a 3% discount to fair value, but Morningstar warns of impending volatility due to trade tariffs, slowing economic growth, and rising Treasury yields. Investors are advised to be cautious, overweight value and small-cap stocks, and prepare for potential market downturns despite the current calm. Several sectors and specific stocks are highlighted for their current valuations and performance.

title: Google cuts trust in Chunghwa Telecom's certificates: What's at stake for internet security chain?
summary: Google has decided to remove default trust for digital certificates issued by Chunghwa Telecom starting July 31, 2025, due to "longstanding patterns of compliance failures." This move, following Mozilla Firefox's earlier consideration of delisting, has significant implications for Chunghwa Telecom, which dominates Taiwan's market, and highlights broader issues in internet security and certificate authority governance. The article explores the function of digital certificates, why browsers can unilaterally delist CAs, and the potential fallout for businesses and users.

title: Plus, an AI-Based Virtual Driver Software Company Powering Factory-Built Autonomous Trucks, to Go Public via Merger with Churchill Capital Corp IX
summary: Plus, an AI-based virtual driver software company for autonomous trucks, announced its plan to go public via a merger with Churchill Capital Corp IX. The transaction is expected to provide up to $300 million in gross proceeds to fund Plus through the commercial launch of its SuperDrive-enabled, factory-built autonomous trucks in 2027, valuing Plus at $1.2 billion pre-money equity. This strategic move aims to address the $2 trillion trucking freight market by leveraging Physical AI and OEM partnerships with companies like TRATON GROUP, Hyundai, and IVECO.

title: Rubrik Earnings Reveal Massive Growth: Revenue Jumps 49% with $1.18B Subscription ARR
summary: Rubrik Inc. reported strong first-quarter fiscal year 2026 results, exceeding all guided metrics with a 49% year-over-year revenue increase to $278.5 million and subscription Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) growing 38% to $1.18 billion. The cyber resilience company also saw a 28% increase in customers with $100K or more in Subscription ARR, reaching 2,381. Rubrik provided optimistic guidance for Q2 and the full fiscal year 2026, projecting continued revenue and Subscription ARR growth.

title: GenAI and agentic AI are business revolutions, not technology transformations
summary: Amdocs Chief Marketing Officer Gil Rosen discusses how GenAI and agentic AI represent a business revolution for telcos, not just a technological upgrade. He details Amdocs' work in integrating GenAI into existing systems to significantly improve customer service and operational efficiency. Rosen also highlights the concept of "personality engineering" for agentic AI and its potential impact across various telco domains, emphasizing the shift towards highly personalized experiences and proactive problem-solving.

title: The Closing Bell: Robinhood Support Flunks My Responsible Gaming Test
summary: This article examines issues with Robinhood integrating sports betting alongside traditional investments, highlighting concerns about responsible gaming support. The author details interactions with Robinhood's chatbot that failed to address problem gambling concerns adequately. It also covers legal battles prediction market Kalshi faces in several states and the upcoming Senate hearing for Brian Quintenz, President Trump's nominee for the CFTC chairmanship, which could impact the future of prediction markets.

title: Exclusive / Netflix buys Daily Beast TV pilot as it races against YouTube
summary: Netflix has acquired a pilot television series from The Daily Beast, produced with Bright North Studios, a move signaling its interest in quickly produced, topical documentary-style content. This initiative suggests Netflix is looking to compete more directly with YouTube for audience eyeballs, despite its CEO Ted Sarandos previously downplaying YouTube as a talent competitor. The potential success of The Daily Beast series could be a significant win for the publication's new leadership, which has focused on cost reduction and an editorial overhaul, leading to its recent profitability.

title: Open Internet: Key Aspects and Importance for Advertisers
summary: This article defines the "open internet" in contrast to "walled gardens" like Google and Meta, emphasizing its importance for advertisers seeking transparency, control, and diverse audience reach, especially with the phasing out of third-party cookies. It highlights how AI-driven tools can be leveraged for programmatic advertising, contextual targeting, native ads, and creative optimization within the open web. The piece concludes by recommending a balanced advertising strategy, allocating budget to both open web platforms and walled gardens for optimal reach and data building.

title: Walmart Just Made a Game-Changing Announcement, and Everyone Else Will Have to Play Catch-Up
summary: Walmart has announced a significant expansion of its drone delivery service, making it the first retailer to scale this technology across five states: Arkansas, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, and Texas. This move will allow millions of customers in major cities like Atlanta, Charlotte, Houston, Orlando, and Tampa to receive orders, including items like ice cream and eggs, within minutes via drone. The company is partnering with firms like Zipline and Wing (an Alphabet subsidiary) to fulfill deliveries from Walmart parking lots, with drones flying up to 6 miles and lowering packages via tether, returning at speeds up to 65 mph.

title: Cineverse Announces Commercial Availability of cineSearch for Business - a Revolutionary, AI-Powered Tool that Solves Search & Discovery for Digital Platforms and Streaming Services
summary: Cineverse has announced the commercial availability of cineSearch for Business, an AI-powered content search and discovery tool for digital platforms and streaming services. This tool, developed using Google Cloud's Vertex AI and Gemini 2.0 Pro, aims to improve viewer search experiences, user retention, and time spent on platforms by offering personalized and superior content recommendations. CineSearch for Business is now available for licensing to OEMs and streaming platforms, addressing industry challenges such as poor discovery leading to subscriber cancellations and long search times.

title: Snap Plans to Launch New Consumer ‘Specs’ AR Glasses Next Year
summary: Snap, the company behind Snapchat, announced plans to release a new consumer version of its Spectacles AR glasses (aka 'Specs') in 2026. This updated version is teased to have a smaller form factor, lighter weight, and increased capabilities compared to the developer-focused Spectacles '24. Significant updates to Snap OS will also be introduced, including integrations with OpenAI and Google Cloud's Gemini to enable multimodal AI-powered Lenses.

title: Meta Losing AI Spending War To Google, Microsoft, Warns 'Big Short' Investor Steve Eisman
summary: Steve Eisman, the investor known for predicting the 2008 collapse, warns that Meta Platforms Inc. is losing the AI spending war against Alphabet Inc.'s Google and Microsoft Corp. He attributes this to Meta's lack of a cloud business, which prevents it from generating revenue from its AI investments unlike Google and Microsoft, whose cash reserves are increasing while Meta's is plummeting. Despite this, Eisman believes the massive spending from all three tech giants indicates the AI boom is far from over.

title: HP Dimension with Google Beam Takes Virtual Collaboration to the Next Level at InfoComm 2025
summary: HP Inc. unveiled HP Dimension with Google Beam at InfoComm 2025, an AI-powered 3D video communication solution designed to enhance virtual collaboration by replicating in-person experiences without wearables. Developed with Google, this technology aims to create more authentic connections in distributed workforces through features like 3D imaging, natural eye contact, and spatial audio. Additionally, HP introduced Poly Studio A2 Audio Solutions, providing advanced audio for various meeting room sizes, with both products becoming available to customers in late 2025.

title: Forget Star Trek, HP Just Made 3D Hologram Calls A Reality With Google Beam
summary: HP has partnered with Google to launch the HP Dimension with Google Beam, an AI-powered 3D video communications solution designed to make virtual meetings feel more in-person. This system, which evolved from Project Starline, uses volumetric video and specialized light field displays to create lifelike 3D representations of participants without requiring headsets. While promising enhanced engagement and focus, the technology comes with a significant cost, priced at $25,000 per unit plus separate licensing fees for Google Beam.

title: Databricks launches free tool to streamline enterprise data warehouse migrations
summary: Databricks has introduced Lakebridge, a free migration tool designed to simplify and accelerate the process of moving data from legacy data warehouses. The tool automates up to 80% of the migration lifecycle, featuring components like Analyzer, Converter, and Validator to facilitate transitions to Databricks SQL. Additionally, Databricks announced expanded partnerships with Google Cloud and Microsoft to integrate Gemini models and enhance Azure AI Foundry integrations, respectively.

title: GGLL Bulls Watch Willow—GGLS Bears Watch DC
summary: This article discusses the current performance of Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) stock, which has seen a significant drop after reaching an all-time high in February. It explores two potential catalysts for the stock's future: Alphabet's new Willow quantum computing chip, which could drive growth and innovation, and looming antitrust risks and U.S. export controls, which could negatively impact the company. The article also highlights Direxion's leveraged ETFs, GGLL and GGLS, for traders looking to capitalize on bullish or bearish movements in GOOGL.

title: Google's First 'Beam' Videoconferencing Device is 'HP Dimension', Coming Late 2025 at $25,000
summary: HP has unveiled 'Dimension', its first hardware offering based on Google Beam's 3D videoconferencing platform, targeting enterprise clients at a price of $25,000. The device, which uses six cameras and AI to create realistic 3D video on a 65-inch light field display, aims to enhance human connection in virtual meetings. While marketed as a light-field display, some commentators suggest it's a sophisticated auto-stereoscopic system with advanced rendering rather than a true light-field technology.

title: Introducing Quartz AI Factor, a new streaming video series set at the Nasdaq MarketSite
summary: Quartz has launched a new streaming video series, "Quartz AI Factor," filmed at the Nasdaq MarketSite, to explore how AI is transforming business. The series features interviews with industry leaders, including Doug Clinton, CEO of Intelligent Alpha, an investment firm utilizing advanced generative AI models for its strategies. Clinton emphasizes the importance of continuous innovation to stay ahead in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

title: Synopsys Stock Falls on China Ban, But Long-Term Outlook Holds
summary: Synopsys (NASDAQ: SNPS) shares dropped after the Trump administration ordered a halt to EDA software sales to China, impacting about 10% of the company's revenue, though sales in the region were already declining. This ban also adds complexity to Synopsys's pending $35 billion acquisition of ANSYS (NASDAQ: ANSS), which still requires Chinese regulatory approval. Despite near-term uncertainty and a moderate "Hold" rating from analysts, the company's long-term outlook remains strong due to its indispensable role in chip design and the enduring secular trends in advanced chip development.

title: Solve the AI Stock-Picking Burden With This ETF
summary: The article suggests that the Invesco Top QQQ ETF (QBIG) can help investors seeking exposure to artificial intelligence (AI) without the burden of individual stock-picking. QBIG primarily holds the "Magnificent Seven" and Broadcom, providing significant AI exposure. Morningstar analysts also highlight Alphabet as an undervalued stock within this group, offering good AI exposure for investors.

title: Solve the AI Stock-Picking Burden With This ETF
summary: The Invesco Top QQQ ETF (QBIG) offers a solution for investors seeking AI exposure without the burden of individual stock picking. It holds the Magnificent Seven stocks and Broadcom, providing strong ties to artificial intelligence. According to Morningstar, while all Magnificent Seven stocks offer long-term growth and innovation, Alphabet (GOOGL) is highlighted as an undervalued pick providing good AI exposure.

title: Broadcom Delivers the Modern Private Cloud with VMware Cloud Foundation 9.0
summary: Broadcom has announced the general availability of VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) 9.0, a platform designed for the modern private cloud. This new version aims to accelerate innovation, control cloud costs, and enhance sovereignty and security for customers across data centers, edge, and managed cloud infrastructure. VCF 9.0 introduces a new architecture with a unified interface for operations, frictionless consumption, and a unified platform supporting traditional, cloud-native, and AI applications.

title: Foreign direct investment falls but Scotland still second outside London for market share, survey shows
summary: Scotland maintained its position as the second-biggest destination for foreign direct investment (FDI) in the UK, outside of London, despite a slight decline in projects last year. The EY survey noted that while Scotland saw a 4.9% dip in new projects, this was less severe than the UK's overall 13% fall. Major cities like Aberdeen, Edinburgh, and Glasgow continue to attract significant investment, with a quarter of investors targeting the UK also considering Scotland.

title: Intuit Inc. Unveils Suite Of Tools For Mailchimp To Help Marketers Better Understand Customer Data
summary: Intuit Inc. has rolled out new tools and integrations for Mailchimp, designed to help marketers and small-to-mid-sized businesses better understand and leverage customer data for sustainable growth. These AI-powered features, built on the Intuit platform, aim to streamline lead generation, enhance customer engagement through personalized campaigns, and provide deeper insights through custom reports. Upcoming features include new custom audience tools for ad targeting and SMS contact import via API, alongside Intuit's AI Agents for QuickBooks to offer predictive business insights.

title: $24.8 Bn Digital Circular Economy Market Opportunities and Strategies to 2034: SaaS Innovations Set to Revolutionize Waste Management and Boost Sustainability
summary: The global digital circular economy market is projected to grow from $2.9 billion in 2024 to $24.8 billion by 2034, driven by smart city initiatives and AI adoption. Key opportunities include software, AI, and digital resale, with North America currently leading but Asia-Pacific and Eastern Europe expected to show the fastest growth. The report also highlights the market's fragmentation, with SAP SE holding the largest share among numerous small players, and outlines strategies for future growth, including SaaS solutions for waste management and leveraging AI and machine learning.

title: Sundar Pichai Sales Alphabet Stock Worth $5.6 Million
summary: Sundar Pichai, CEO of Alphabet Inc., filed a notice to sell 32,500 shares of the company's Class C common stock, valued at nearly $5.76 million. This transaction, conducted under Rule 144, is part of a series of regular stock sales by Pichai and his family foundation under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. Over the past three months, Pichai has personally sold 130,000 shares totaling approximately $21.78 million, with an additional $3.24 million from the Pichai Family Foundation's sale of 20,000 shares.

title: Pichai Family Foundation to Sell 4,000 Alphabet Shares Worth $708K
summary: The Pichai Family Foundation has filed a notice to sell 4,000 shares of Alphabet Inc.'s Class C common stock, valued at approximately $708,920. This transaction is part of a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted in December 2024, and the foundation has made similar sales totaling 4,000 shares on five previous occasions in the last three months. The shares were originally acquired through restricted stock units granted in April 2018.

title: Who Will Buy TikTok? And Why it Matters
summary: This article discusses the uncertain future of TikTok in the US, specifically focusing on the mandated divestiture of its US operations due to government concerns over data security and Chinese influence. It explores potential buyers like Microsoft, Amazon, and Oracle, estimating a deal value of over $50 billion and analyzing the implications for investors and competitors if a sale, ban, or prolonged limbo occurs. The article emphasizes that the nature of the buyer is critical due to TikTok's advanced algorithm and market share in social media advertising.

title: Market Update: The Section 899 “revenge tax”
summary: The article discusses Section 899, a new foreign tax provision in the proposed One Big Beautiful Bill Act, aimed at discouraging what the U.S. considers "unfair foreign taxes" on American companies. This "revenge tax" would impose a levy on entities from nations with tax policies deemed discriminatory towards U.S. businesses, potentially impacting dividends and corporate profits starting in 2027. While it could raise revenue and create a more level playing field, the tax might also deter investment in the U.S. and shift equity investors to international markets, with specific implications for high-dividend U.S. companies and those with significant U.S. operations.

title: Investing in the ASX's VanEck Wide Moat ETF (MOAT)? Here's what you're buying
summary: The VanEck Morningstar Wide Moat ETF (ASX: MOAT) is a popular ETF that invests in companies with a "wide economic moat," a concept popularized by Warren Buffett. These companies possess intrinsic competitive advantages like strong brands or low-cost production. MOAT's current holdings include Boeing, Walt Disney, Alphabet, Campbell's Company, Nike, and Altria, demonstrating these characteristics, and the ETF has returned an impressive 14.59% per annum since its 2015 founding.

title: Faraday Future Launches FF AI 2.0 Software Update for the FF 91 and Future FX Models: Represents the Biggest AI Leap Yet, Built with OpenAI
summary: Faraday Future announced the debut of FF AI 2.0, a significant software update built with OpenAI, for its FF 91 and future FX models. This intelligent in-car OS offers enhanced natural language understanding, real-time multilingual switching in over 50 languages, and a multi-agent AI architecture. The update aims to provide a new class of intelligent driving experiences and is designed to integrate large language models across core vehicle systems.

title: World’s Most Expensive Stocks: What Makes Them So Pricey?
summary: This article explores what makes some stocks extraordinarily expensive, like Berkshire Hathaway, Lindt & Sprüngli, and NVR Inc. It explains that a high per-share price doesn't necessarily equate to a high market valuation; rather, it often reflects a company's intentional strategy to avoid stock splits, attract long-term investors, and maintain an exclusive brand image. The piece highlights the top 10 most expensive stocks and advises investors to focus on fundamental value rather than just share price.

title: Zacks Investment Ideas feature highlights: Tesla, eBay, PayPal and Alphabet
summary: Zacks Investment Ideas spotlights Tesla, eBay, PayPal, and Alphabet, focusing on Tesla's robotaxi debut. Tesla shares spiked over 6% after launching its robotaxi service in Austin, Texas, using modified Model Y vehicles. The article highlights Elon Musk's measured approach to this high-stakes launch and acknowledges skepticism while noting the potential for significant growth if the service scales successfully.

title: Nuclear startup Terrestrial Energy inks a deal to use gas as a ‘bridge’ to SMRs
summary: Terrestrial Energy, a nuclear startup, has partnered with Ameresco to utilize natural gas as an interim power source for data centers and industrial plants before transitioning to small modular reactors (SMRs). This strategy aims to address the immediate need for steady electricity for data centers while nuclear SMR technology is still in development and regulatory processes. The deal involves Ameresco exploring about half a dozen projects, positioning gas as a "bridge" fuel to future nuclear power, giving Terrestrial Energy a potential edge in the competitive SMR market.

title: Better Home Hit With Lawsuit Over Google, LinkedIn Web Trackers
summary: Better Home & Finance Holding Co. is facing a proposed class action lawsuit for allegedly using web trackers from Google and LinkedIn to collect customer data without consent. The lawsuit claims that the real estate and mortgage company shared sensitive personal and financial information, such as home addresses and credit scores, which customers expected to remain private. The complaint was filed in the US District Court for the Central District of California, on behalf of potentially millions of affected individuals.

title: Meta Platforms is nearing new highs on a wave of potential acquisition reports 📈
summary: Meta Platforms is nearing new highs as reports surface about its potential acquisition of PlayAI, an AI startup specializing in voice imitation. This move is seen as part of Meta's broader strategy to lead in artificial intelligence development, following a significant investment in Scale AI and bringing its CEO into Meta's "superintelligence" team in mid-June. The company's stock prices are consequently approaching all-time highs as it strives to outpace competitors like Alphabet, Microsoft, and Amazon in the AI race.

title: 3 Top Fund Managers on What’s Next for the Magnificent 7
summary: Three top large-growth fund managers discussed the opportunities and risks facing the "Magnificent Seven" tech giants at the 2025 Morningstar Investment Conference. They offered insights on Alphabet's regulatory threats, Amazon's successful risk-taking, Apple's AI challenges, Meta's questionable metaverse investments, Microsoft's leading AI revenue, Nvidia's cyclical business risks, and Tesla's potential beyond car manufacturing. The fund managers shared their varying allocations and perspectives on each company's future.

title: Unilever to pay $1.5 bln to acquire men’s grooming brand Dr Squatch – FT
summary: Unilever is set to acquire the U.S.-based men's personal care brand Dr Squatch for $1.5 billion, as reported by the Financial Times. This acquisition aims to expand Unilever's premium personal care offerings globally and aligns with its strategy to pivot towards higher-margin personal care products. Dr Squatch is known for its natural products, direct-to-consumer sales, and successful viral ad campaigns.

title: Alphabet's Strong Free Cash Flow Makes GOOG Stock a Value Buy
summary: Alphabet (GOOG) demonstrated strong Q1 free cash flow (FCF), surpassing expectations, and maintaining stable FCF margins despite increased capital expenditure. The analysis suggests GOOG stock is undervalued by approximately 19%, projecting a target price of $212 per share based on expected FCF and a 3.50% FCF yield. Investors could also generate additional income and set a lower buy-in point by selling short out-of-the-money put options.

title: In market rally to new record, best tech trade inside S&P 500 hasn't been 'Mag 7'
summary: While tech stocks have led the market rally, the Communication Services sector of the S&P 500, particularly driven by Meta and Netflix, has outperformed even the tech sector itself. This outperformance is attributed to the "service"-based nature of the communication services, with significant inflows into related ETFs. In contrast, major tech players like Apple and Alphabet have faced headwinds, making the "Magnificent 7" ETFs lag behind.

title: Google Ads: How Do They Work?
summary: This article explains Google Ads, formerly Google AdWords, detailing its various formats like Search, Display, Shopping, Video, and App Promotion Ads, including the newer Performance Max Campaigns. It describes how Google Ads operates through a real-time auction system, emphasizing keyword selection, bidding, and ad relevance. The piece also covers best practices for campaigns, optimization, budgeting, and ad formats, while discussing when Google Ads is most effective and offering alternatives for marketers.

title: Mark Zuckerberg Once Predicted That This Technology Would Replace Your Phone — A Decade Later, How Far Is Meta From Delivering On A 'More Natural' Future
summary: Mark Zuckerberg's 2015 prediction that virtual and augmented reality would replace mobile phones is revisited, nearly a decade later. The article examines Meta's progress and challenges in achieving this vision, detailing investments in Quest headsets and Ray-Ban smart glasses, along with financial performance and competition from Apple, Google, and Samsung. Despite substantial losses in Reality Labs, Meta continues to invest in AR/VR, while also facing struggles like the discontinuation of the Quest Pro.

title: Multi-Cloud Strategies: How Federal Government Can Master Them
summary: Federal agencies are increasingly adopting multi-cloud strategies to enhance cost efficiency, resilience, and innovation, as nearly all federal IT leaders plan to expand cloud solution usage. While beneficial, this approach introduces complexity, necessitating clear use cases and careful consideration of vendor capabilities, data sensitivity, existing applications, staff skillsets, and budget. Partnering with experienced technology providers can help agencies navigate these challenges and align with industry best practices and compliance standards.

title: Alphabet’s Google Reaches $50M Settlement Over Claims of Systemic Racial Discrimination Against Black Employees
summary: Google has agreed to a $50 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit alleging systemic racial discrimination against Black employees, following a prior $28 million settlement for discrimination against non-white employees. The lawsuit claimed Google placed Black workers in lower-level roles, paid them less, and denied promotions, while using "coded language" like "Googley-ness" to marginalize them. These back-to-back settlements bring Alphabet's total discrimination-related payouts to $78 million in two months.

title: Magnite Retail Chatter Spikes After Stock Hits Multi-Year Highs As Analyst Flags ‘Substantial’ Benefits From Google Adtech Antitrust Ruling
summary: Magnite (MGNI) stock saw a significant surge and increased retail chatter after Rosenblatt Securities raised its price target to $39, citing substantial benefits from the recent Google AdTech antitrust ruling, which found Google guilty of monopolizing ad markets. Analysts anticipate earnings improvements from behavioral remedies and potential settlements as early as 2026. The stock hit a multi-year high, reflecting bullish sentiment among investors despite a slight dip in early premarket trading.

title: Elon Musk's $71 Billion Wealth Wipeout Dwarfs Next 7 Billionaire Decliners Combined
summary: Elon Musk, CEO of Tesla, has experienced a $71.2 billion decrease in his net worth in 2025, primarily due to the volatile performance of Tesla stock amidst his public disputes with President Donald Trump.
