This decline is greater than the combined wealth loss of the next seven billionaires on Bloomberg's list. The article highlights how political tensions and stock performance have significantly impacted Musk's financial standing and could continue to do so.

title: New Buy Rating for International Business Machines (IBM), the Technology Giant
summary: Goldman Sachs analyst James Schneider maintained a Buy rating on International Business Machines (IBM) with a $310.00 price target. This aligns with a Moderate Buy analyst consensus, although the average price target of $269.46 suggests a potential downside from current levels. The report follows IBM's latest quarterly earnings, which showed $14.54 billion in revenue and $1.06 billion in net profit.

title: Nvidia's $4T Countdown: Will Next Week's Chart Print A Trillion-Dollar Breakout?
summary: Nvidia is on the verge of reaching a $4 trillion market capitalization, potentially becoming only the second U.S. company to hit this milestone. Despite competitive whispers, Nvidia maintains its dominance due to strong demand for its AI chips, with strong European partnerships offsetting China sales pressure. Its valuation remains reasonable compared to peers, making a $164 breakout a key target for investors.

title: In Sonos v. Google , the Federal Circuit Has a Chance to Fix Its Prosecution Laches Doctrine
summary: The article discusses the upcoming Federal Circuit oral arguments in Google v. Sonos, focusing on the doctrine of prosecution laches. Author Gene Quinn argues that prosecution laches is an inappropriate doctrine, especially when patent applicants operate within Congressionally authorized timeframes, citing the Supreme Court's decision in SCA Hygiene Prods. He criticizes the Federal Circuit's retroactive application of this common law doctrine, which can render patents unenforceable without prior warning, despite compliance with statutory deadlines.

title: The intelligent data blueprint at NetApp, in detail
summary: NetApp is focusing on intelligent data infrastructure to unify cloud and AI, distinguishing itself by offering native storage integration across major public cloud providers. The company's strategy involves bringing AI capabilities directly to the storage layer, rather than moving massive datasets, and is collaborating with Nvidia on the Nvidia AI Data Platform. NetApp is also evolving its market focus to include data engineers and security leaders alongside traditional CIOs and storage administrators.

title: Garmin smartwatches add turn-by-turn directions with Google Maps
summary: Garmin has announced that the Google Maps app is now available for free in the Connect IQ Store, bringing turn-by-turn directions to compatible Garmin smartwatches. This new feature allows users to receive navigation prompts directly on their wrist while walking, running, or cycling, keeping their hands free and phone safely in their pocket. The integration also enables fitness enthusiasts to track their activities alongside navigation, syncing data to other health and fitness apps.

title: SuRo Capital Corp. Second Quarter 2025 Preliminary Investment Portfolio Update
summary: SuRo Capital Corp. announced a strong second quarter for 2025, driven by investments in AI infrastructure companies like CoreWeave and OpenAI, leading to an anticipated NAV per share of $9.00 to $9.50. This performance led the Board of Directors to declare an initial cash dividend of $0.25 per share, with expectations of further dividends throughout the year. The company also made a new $5.0 million investment in Plaid and exited positions in CoreWeave and ServiceTitan, leveraging its publicly traded common stock to provide access to high-growth private companies.

title: Gorilla Technology stock soars after becoming NVIDIA Solutions Partner
summary: Gorilla Technology Group Inc. (NASDAQ:GORR) stock surged 14.6% after the company announced its new status as an NVIDIA Solutions Partner. This partnership is set to enhance Gorilla's capabilities in AI, smart city technologies, and cybersecurity by providing access to NVIDIA's resources and technologies, fostering innovation and accelerating market growth. The collaboration aims to develop advanced AI-powered solutions, particularly in next-generation cybersecurity and smart city infrastructure.

title: Ria Money Transfer and Xe Join Forces with Google to Collaborate on Seamless, Cross-Border Money Transfers
summary: Ria Money Transfer and Xe, both segments of Euronet, have partnered with Google to enhance access to cross-border money transfer services. This collaboration aims to make it easier for Google users to discover and utilize Ria and Xe's services, tapping into the rapidly growing digital remittance market, projected to reach $83.2 billion by 2034. The initiative seeks to expand the reach of digital payment solutions to millions globally, leveraging Google's platform for greater accessibility.

title: A Practical Guide to AI ETFs
summary: Rene Reyna of Invesco discusses strategic investment in AI ETFs, emphasizing it as a satellite strategy (5-10% of a portfolio) to capitalize on AI's potential without overexposure. He notes that despite growth, AI valuations are not overheated, with companies like Nvidia showing fluctuating but reasonable P/E ratios. The Invesco AI and Next Gen Software ETF (IGPT) offers diversified exposure across AI, robotics, and next-generation software, highlighting opportunities in semiconductors and back-office software sectors.

title: Google Chrome to Distrust Chunghwa & Netlock Certificates: How Qualys Certificate View Helps You Respond
summary: Google Chrome will distrust new digital certificates from Chunghwa Telecom and Netlock starting July 31, 2025, due to repeated compliance failures. This change will cause security warnings for users visiting affected websites and necessitates immediate action by organizations to identify and replace these certificates. Qualys Certificate View can assist organizations in inventorying, monitoring, and remediating certificates issued by these CAs before the deadline to ensure web security and compliance.

title: FDRR: Look At FCPI Instead
summary: The Fidelity Dividend ETF for Rising Rates (FDRR) invests in large-cap dividend stocks correlated with U.S. Treasury yields, but has underperformed its benchmark despite strong dividend growth during a period of rising interest rates. The article suggests considering FCPI, another Fidelity ETF focused on inflation, which has a similar expense ratio and trading volume but a more convincing track record. This update reviews FDRR's performance and holdings since a previous review in September 2024.

title: BBM, bling, and boardrooms: The Blackberry that ruled the world
summary: BlackBerry, once a cultural icon ruling over 20% of the global smartphone market in 2009-2010, symbolized success and connectivity for professionals and celebrities alike. Its "CrackBerry" era was driven by features like secure email, a tactile QWERTY keyboard, and the indispensable BlackBerry Messenger (BBM). However, its downfall was heralded by the rise of touchscreens, app stores, and its own reluctance to adapt, ultimately leading to its decline in the face of iPhone and Android dominance.

title: Oakmark Global Select Fund: Second Calendar Quarter 2025
summary: The Oakmark Global Select Fund experienced a higher level of activity in the second calendar quarter of 2025, utilizing market volatility to reposition its portfolio. During this period, a health care holding was the top contributor to the fund's performance, while another health care holding acted as the top detractor. The information presented summarizes the views and investments of the portfolio managers and Harris Associates L.P. as of the date written.

title: China startup Moonshot AI rivals U.S. with cheap open model
summary: Chinese startup Moonshot AI has released its Kimi K2 open-source large language model, which claims to outperform some Western competitors like OpenAI's GPT 4.1 in coding and agentic tasks, and is priced significantly lower. This move intensifies the AI competition between the U.S. and China, with analysts noting Kimi K2's potential to attract a global developer base but also raising concerns about data security and market dumping from some critics.

title: Brookfield and Google Sign Hydro Framework Agreement to Deliver up to 3,000 MW of Homegrown Energy in the United States
summary: Brookfield Asset Management and Google have signed a Hydro Framework Agreement (HFA) to provide up to 3,000 megawatts (MW) of carbon-free hydroelectric capacity across the United States. This marks the world's largest corporate clean power deal for hydroelectricity, with initial contracts worth over $3 billion for 670 MW from Brookfield's Holtwood and Safe Harbor facilities in Pennsylvania. The HFA supports Google's goal of 24/7 carbon-free operations and Brookfield's strategy to deliver flexible clean energy to the technology sector.

title: Meta, Google unveil massive AI data center investment plans
summary: Meta and Google are significantly increasing their investments in AI infrastructure, focusing on massive data center builds and power acquisition. Meta plans to invest "hundreds of billions of dollars" in AI data "superclusters" like Prometheus and Hyperion, aiming for compute supremacy. Google, in contrast, will invest $25 billion in data centers and AI infrastructure, along with $3 billion to update hydropower plants, balancing aggressive expansion with long-term clean energy procurement.

title: Tech layoffs 2025: How Microsoft, Google, and Meta are plotting for the AI era
summary: Major tech companies like Microsoft, Google, and Meta have initiated significant layoffs in 2025, not primarily because AI is directly replacing human workers, but to free up capital for massive investments in AI initiatives and to hire AI-focused talent. Experts suggest these layoffs are a strategic restructuring to fund the development and implementation of artificial intelligence, leading to a shift in hiring towards more technical and creative roles. This trend indicates a reshaping of the tech workforce as automation increasingly impacts white-collar jobs.

title: AI Learning Accelerates in MENA: Coursera Adds Over 900 GenAI Courses and Tools
summary: Coursera has significantly expanded its Generative AI (GenAI) course catalog to over 925 offerings, aiming to meet the accelerating demand for AI skills in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region. This expansion addresses the rapid skills transformation, with MENA seeing over 500,000 GenAI enrollments and a pressing need to advance beyond beginner-level skills and improve gender inclusivity. The platform is also enhancing learning through AI-powered tools like Coursera Coach and offering new Professional Certificates and Specializations from major tech companies and universities.

title: Top Research Reports for Alphabet, Oracle & Shell
summary: This article highlights top research reports from Zacks, featuring Alphabet, Oracle, and Shell, along with micro-caps Fossil Group and ImmuCell. It provides detailed analysis on the performance, growth drivers, and challenges for each company, offering insights for investors. The reports cover cloud growth, AI initiatives, energy sector trends, and turnaround strategies for the featured stocks.

title: Trump unveils $92B in energy and AI investments for Pennsylvania
summary: President Donald Trump announced $92 billion in energy and AI investments for Pennsylvania from companies like Google, Blackstone, and FirstEnergy at a recent summit. These investments include new natural gas plants by Blackstone to power data centers and significant infrastructure upgrades by FirstEnergy. Environmental groups, however, criticized the plan for focusing on fossil fuels and potentially increasing costs and grid instability.

title: QQQ Is Ready for Modern Makeover
summary: The Invesco QQQ ETF (QQQ) is seeking shareholder approval to convert from a Unit Investment Trust (UIT) to an open-end fund, which would lead to a lower expense ratio, increased liquidity, and potential for securities lending and dividend reinvestment. This modernization aims to benefit shareholders by reducing costs and enhancing the fund's operational capabilities, further cementing its appeal for long-term investors despite a slightly higher fee than its younger sibling, QQQM. The fund's strong performance and diverse holdings, beyond just tech stocks, are highlighted, reinforcing its impressive track record.

title: Corning staves off antitrust fine as EU regulators accept concessions to mobile phone makers
summary: The EU antitrust regulators have accepted concessions from glass maker Corning, averting a potential fine. Corning agreed to waive exclusive dealing clauses with mobile phone makers and glass processing companies and to scrap purchasing clauses, ending an eight-month investigation. This ensures that original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) are not required to buy more than 50% of their demand from Corning, a measure valid for nine years.

title: Studio LICO launches Mythic Item Obtained: Idle RPG globally
summary: Studio LICO, a WEBTOON Entertainment Inc. game developer, has launched "Mythic Item Obtained: Idle RPG" globally in North America, Japan, and Taiwan. The mobile game adapts a popular webnovel and WEBTOON into an idle RPG format, featuring strategic progression and characters from the original series. Having already gained success in South Korea, the global launch includes a bonus code for in-game Gems and marks WEBTOON Entertainment's strategy to expand its IP across various media platforms.

title: Alphabet's AI Moment: Trading Q2 Results With 2X Bull and 1X Bear ETFs
summary: Alphabet (GOOGL) is set to report Q2 2025 earnings on July 23rd, with traders anticipating volatility due to heavy AI investments, competitive pressures, and ongoing legal battles. Despite outperforming earnings estimates previously, the stock has lagged the S&P 500. Traders can utilize Direxion's Single Stock Daily Leveraged & Inverse ETFs (GGLL and GGLS) to capitalize on potential price swings around the earnings release.

title: Adobe Trades 38% Below 52-Week High: Right Time Buy the ADBE Stock?
summary: Adobe's stock has dropped 38% from its 52-week high and 11.5% since its Q2 fiscal 2025 report, largely due to stiff competition in the AI space from companies like Microsoft and Alphabet. However, the company is expanding its AI portfolio with solutions like GenStudio and Firefly Services, and its AI-first products are tracking ahead of their annual recurring revenue target. Despite overvaluation concerns, Adobe offers positive guidance for fiscal 2025, anticipating revenue growth and indicating a "Buy" with a Zacks Rank #2.

title: AI Bubble Is More Unhinged Than Dot-Com—A Chilling Warning From The Inside
summary: Apollo's chief economist Torsten Slok warns that the current AI-driven market frenzy is more dangerously inflated than the 1990s internet bubble, primarily due to its concentrated nature within the S&P 500's largest names. He highlights that while the top 10 companies' P/E ratio is similar to 2000, the rest of the market is not as stretched, indicating a structural imbalance. Investors can use ETFs like Global X Robotics & Artificial Intelligence ETF (BOTZ) for exposure or Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF (RSP) and ProShares Short QQQ (PSQ) for hedging.

title: Composio raises $25M in funding to ease AI agent development
summary: Composio, a startup specializing in AI agent development platforms, has secured $25 million in Series A funding led by Lightspeed Venture Partners, bringing its total funding to $29 million. The company's cloud platform aims to significantly reduce the time and effort required to build AI agents, particularly by automating complex authentication workflows. Composio's technology supports integration with over 3,000 cloud applications and allows agents to improve capabilities over time through a reinforcement learning layer, benefiting its current base of 200+ companies.

title: Alphabet Inc. Reports Robust Q2 2025 Growth
summary: Alphabet Inc. announced robust Q2 2025 earnings, with consolidated revenues increasing by 14% to $96.4 billion. Google Services revenue grew by 12% and Google Cloud saw a significant 32% increase, largely due to demand for AI solutions. The company anticipates raising capital expenditures to $85 billion in 2025 to further invest in cloud infrastructure and AI innovations.

title: Consumers are putting more trust in AI searches
summary: A new survey by Yext reveals that 62% of consumers trust AI to guide their brand decisions, putting it on par with traditional search methods, although many still prefer traditional search for personal, medical, or financial topics. Nearly half of consumers use AI search tools daily, reflecting AI's growing integration into routine online behavior, yet only 10% fully trust the first result, leading 48% to cross-check information. Generational differences exist, with Gen Z using AI for brainstorming, Millennials for quick insights, and Boomers still relying heavily on traditional search for general knowledge.

title: Alphabet Earnings: Google Search Remains Resilient as AI Adoption Drives Cloud Sales
summary: Alphabet reported strong second-quarter earnings, with sales up 14% and Google Cloud growing by 32% due to AI-driven demand. The core Google Search business showed resilience, growing 12%, leading Morningstar to maintain its $237 fair value estimate and view Alphabet as undervalued despite increased capital expenditure guidance.

title: ETFs to Soar Post Alphabet's Strong Q2 Earnings Results

While the partnership presents opportunities for new revenue streams and validates Alphabet's AI infrastructure investment, investors will be monitoring how effectively these initiatives scale and generate returns given the high capital expenditure involved.

title: Longview Partners trims Alphabet Class A stake by 698K
summary: Longview Partners (Guernsey) Ltd, managed by Marina Lund, significantly reduced its stake in Alphabet Inc. Class A (GOOGL) by selling 698,090 shares. Despite this trim, Alphabet Class A stock has seen substantial growth over the past year, with Wall Street analysts maintaining a Strong Buy rating due to strong Q4 2025 results, re-accelerating Search revenue, and a significant jump in Google Cloud, fueled by aggressive AI development and a surging cloud backlog. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, also rates GOOGL as "Outperform" due to its financial quality, although it notes weaker technical momentum and a premium valuation.

title: After Earnings, Is Alphabet Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar maintains its fair value estimate of USD 340 for Alphabet (GOOGL) following strong fourth-quarter earnings, considering the stock to be fairly valued. The company's AI integration, particularly in Google Cloud and Search, is highlighted as a significant driver of sales and a competitive advantage. Despite a projected substantial increase in capital expenditures for AI in 2026, Alphabet's robust financial position and wide economic moat provide a stable outlook, although antitrust concerns and AI investment uncertainties remain notable risks.

title: Meet the Most Profitable Company in America (It’s Not Apple or Microsoft)
summary: Alphabet has emerged as the most profitable company in America, surpassing Apple and Microsoft, largely due to the rapid growth of Google Cloud, fueled by AI demand. The company's investments in emerging technologies like the Gemini App and Waymo, alongside strong financial performance (including a 48% year-over-year revenue increase in Google Cloud for Q4), position it for continued margin expansion and make it a top-performing Magnificent Seven stock.

title: Alphabet (GOOG) Jumps 4% as Dividend Looms
summary: Alphabet Inc. (GOOG) saw its shares jump by 4.01% as investors positioned themselves for the company's next quarterly dividend payment of $0.21 per share. This surge follows strong earnings performance for 2025, with net income increasing by 32% to $132 billion and revenues climbing 15% to $402.8 billion. The company's CEO highlighted the success of Gemini 3, YouTube's growth, and Google Cloud's strong performance, driven by AI investments.

title: Sea Limited (SE) Enters a Strategic Partnership with Google, Here's What You Need to Know
summary: Sea Limited (NYSE: SE) has announced a strategic partnership with Google to develop AI tools for e-commerce and gaming in Southeast Asia. This collaboration will focus on creating an AI agentic shopping prototype for Sea Limited's Shopee platform, capable of autonomous shopping and order management, and will also integrate Google's AI to boost game development for Garena. This move aligns with Google's strategy to monetize AI by expanding collaborations beyond chatbots, giving Shopee an added edge in the competitive Southeast Asian e-commerce market.

title: Neha Parikh appointed as new Waze CEO
summary: Neha Parikh, former President of Hotwire, has been appointed as the new CEO of Waze, replacing Noam Bardin. She will start her role next month and plans to focus on growing the company. Bardin, who led Waze for eight years and oversaw its acquisition by Google, cited being "worn down by the nature of the beast" as his reason for leaving.

title: Wells Fargo upgrades Google-parent Alphabet, says it has '3 key traits of AI winner'
summary: Wells Fargo has upgraded Alphabet to overweight, citing the company's strong position to dominate in AI due to its data, distribution, and computing capacity. Analyst Ken Gawrelski increased the price target to $387, projecting a 22% upside. The report highlights Alphabet's ambitious expansion of AI compute capacity and expected tripling of Gemini average recurring revenue by 2027.

title: Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL Shares Acquired by German American Bancorp Inc.

Cambridge also explains his investment philosophy centered on companies that generate cash and return it to shareholders, while reiterating his belief in the U.S. market as a global leader, particularly in AI.

title: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) Deep Dive: Navigating the Agentic Era and the $180 Billion AI Bet
summary: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) is transitioning from a search-engine dominant company to an "AI-first" agentic platform, with revenues surpassing $400 billion in 2025. The company faces high capital expenditures for AI infrastructure in 2026, alongside intense competition and regulatory scrutiny. Despite these challenges, Alphabet's strong financial performance, proprietary AI chips, and the successful integration of Gemini AI have maintained investor confidence, with many analysts bullish on its long-term prospects.

title: Alphabet Inc. Shares Dip on Heavy AI Spending Outlook Despite Strong Earnings Momentum
summary: Alphabet Inc. shares dipped by 1.76% to $307.38 following concerns over aggressive capital expenditure plans for AI infrastructure, despite strong fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 earnings that surpassed analyst expectations. The company projects 2026 capital expenditures between $175 billion and $185 billion, primarily for AI data centers and related infrastructure, which tempered investor enthusiasm despite an initial earnings beat. Analysts largely remain bullish, maintaining "Buy" ratings due to Alphabet's comprehensive AI stack, but investors are weighing short-term spending pressures against long-term AI monetization potential.

title: [144] Alphabet Inc. SEC Filing
